Aligning PortalBrandProvider is deferred — see `SPACES_ARCHITECTURE.md §10.6`. **LOC:** ~90 changed (~30 tokens/base.css + ~15 portal fg fallback substitutions + ~4 notification-dropdown + ~10 PortalLayout logout + ~130 branding-editor preview). **No new tests.** tsc clean, vitest 165/165 unchanged (no component behavior changes), build clean. **Ready for Session 5 (motion pass).**

**Status-color vocabulary (Session 3):**
- **Status families**: `success` / `warning` / `error` / `info` (+ `neutral` for unmapped). Each family has full-saturation `--status-{X}` and muted-background `--status-{X}-muted` tokens.
- **Status-family recipe**: `bg-status-{X}-muted` background + `text-status-{X}` text + optional `border-status-{X}` border. Used consistently across Badge status variants, Alert, StatusPill, Sonner richColors, InlineError, peek StatusBadge, agent-alerts, ErrorBoundary.
- **StatusPill vs. Badge distinction**: **StatusPill** (`ui/status-pill.tsx`) = `rounded-full` pill for inline status markers in lists/tables/detail panels. Takes a `status` string (auto-maps via `STATUS_MAP` — 33 keys covering the platform's status vocabulary: approved/pending/rejected/completed/failed/voided/active/inactive/paid/overdue/etc). **Badge status variants** (`ui/badge.tsx`) = `rounded-sm` pill for general-purpose emphasis with flexible semantics (counts, labels, arbitrary tags, explicit-variant use cases). Different shape, different use case. Documented in each file's header comment.
- **Alert vs. InlineError distinction**: **Alert** (`ui/alert.tsx`) = page/section-level banner with 5 variants (info/success/warning/error/neutral) + optional title/description/action/dismiss. **InlineError** (`ui/inline-error.tsx`) = panel-scoped recoverable-error UX with narrow shape + `onRetry` callback contract. Different use cases; kept coexisting. Documented in each file's header.

**Status-key-keyed dict pattern (Session 3 convention):** when a component renders per-state chrome via a config object (agent-alerts-card's severity mapping, peek status renderers, future status-rendering pages), declare a `StatusFamily` key per state + share a `FAMILY_STYLES` lookup mapping family → token triple. **Don't embed raw color strings in component code.** Adding a new severity/state = one row in the config; zero raw colors.

Reference implementation: `components/agent-alerts-card.tsx`. Pre-Session-3 the file had:
```ts
const SEVERITY_CONFIG = {
  action_required: { icon: AlertTriangle, color: "text-red-600", bg: "bg-red-50", border: "border-red-200" },
  warning:         { icon: Bell, color: "text-amber-600", bg: "bg-amber-50", border: "border-amber-200" },
}
```
Post-Session-3:
```ts
const SEVERITY_CONFIG: Record<string, { icon: LucideIcon; status: StatusFamily }> = {
  action_required: { icon: AlertTriangle, status: "error" },
  warning:         { icon: Bell,          status: "warning" },
}
const FAMILY_STYLES: Record<StatusFamily, { bg: string; fg: string; border: string }> = {
  error:   { bg: "bg-status-error-muted",   fg: "text-status-error",   border: "border-status-error/30" },
  warning: { bg: "bg-status-warning-muted", fg: "text-status-warning", border: "border-status-warning/30" },
  // ...
}
```

**Status-color migration recipe (for long-tail adoption as pages are next touched):**

| Legacy pattern | DESIGN_LANGUAGE replacement |
|---|---|
| `bg-green-50 text-green-800` | `bg-status-success-muted text-status-success` |
| `bg-red-50 text-red-800` | `bg-status-error-muted text-status-error` |
| `bg-amber-50 text-amber-800` or `bg-yellow-*` | `bg-status-warning-muted text-status-warning` |
| `bg-blue-50 text-blue-800` or `bg-sky-*` | `bg-status-info-muted text-status-info` |
| `bg-{green,red,amber,blue}-100` | respective `bg-status-*-muted` |
| `border-{green,red,amber,blue}-200` | `border-status-*` (optional `/30` opacity) |
| `text-emerald-*` / `text-rose-*` / `text-sky-*` | `text-status-success / -error / -info` |

The recipe applies to pages (not component library code). Don't proactively refactor untouched pages — pages migrate as they're next touched for unrelated reasons. Session 6 QA verifies no net-new legacy patterns introduced.

**Coexistence with existing shadcn tokens:** shadcn CSS variables remain untouched (`--background`, `--foreground`, `--card`, `--popover`, etc.). All UI primitives migrated to DESIGN_LANGUAGE tokens in Sessions 2–3; final cleanup session retires the shadcn layer when no references remain. **Platform font = IBM Plex Sans post-Session 2**: `--font-sans` points at `var(--font-plex-sans)`. `@custom-variant dark` matches both legacy `.dark` class (noop) and `[data-mode="dark"]` attribute.

## 5. Database

- **~235 tables** (ORM models for all but the orphaned `tenant_settings` table)
- **Current migration head:** `r87_dashboard_layouts`
- **118 migration files** in `backend/alembic/versions/`
- **Single root:** `e1e2120b6b65` (create_users_table)

### Running Migrations Locally
```bash
cd backend && source .venv/bin/activate
DATABASE_URL=postgresql://localhost:5432/bridgeable_dev alembic upgrade head
```

### Idempotent Migrations
`backend/alembic/env.py` monkey-patches `op.add_column`, `op.create_table`, and `op.create_index` to be idempotent. This allows the same migration chain to run on both fresh databases and databases where tables were created outside migrations.

### Table Name Conventions
These are the **correct** table names (corrected from old incorrect names):
- Customer payments: `customer_payments` (not `payments`)
- Sales orders: `sales_orders` (not `orders`)
- Vendor bills: `vendor_bills` (not `bills`)
- Customer payment applications: `customer_payment_applications`

### Key Schema Patterns
- All IDs are `String(36)` UUIDs generated with `uuid.uuid4()`
- Timestamps use `DateTime(timezone=True)` with `default=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc)`
- Soft deletes via `is_active` boolean (not physical deletion)
- JSONB used extensively for flexible fields (settings, config, metadata)
- `company_id` or `tenant_id` FK on all tenant-scoped tables

## 6. Project Structure

```
backend/
├── app/
│   ├── main.py              # FastAPI app, middleware, startup/shutdown
│   ├── config.py            # pydantic-settings, reads .env
│   ├── database.py          # SQLAlchemy engine + SessionLocal
│   ├── scheduler.py         # APScheduler — 13 registered jobs + JOB_REGISTRY
│   ├── worker.py            # Background job queue worker (polls DB/Redis)
│   ├── models/              # 157 model files, 170 exports in __init__.py
│   ├── services/            # 109 service files (business logic)
│   │   ├── ai_service.py            # Claude API wrapper
│   │   ├── agent_service.py         # AR/AP/Collections agents

│   │   └── ...

frontend/
```

## 7. Environment Setup

### Local Development
```bash
# Prerequisites: PostgreSQL 16, Python 3.13+, Node 22+
export PATH="/opt/homebrew/opt/postgresql@16/bin:$PATH"

# Database
createdb bridgeable_dev

# Backend
cd backend
python -m venv .venv && source .venv/bin/activate
pip install -r requirements.txt
DATABASE_URL=postgresql://localhost:5432/bridgeable_dev alembic upgrade head
uvicorn app.main:app --reload --port 8000

# Frontend (separate terminal)
cd frontend && npm install && npm run dev
```

### Local dev seeding

The local `bridgeable_dev` database ships empty after `alembic upgrade head`. Two seed scripts populate usable tenants:

- **Default test tenant (most sessions):** `DATABASE_URL=postgresql://localhost:5432/bridgeable_dev python backend/scripts/seed_staging.py` → creates the `testco` manufacturing tenant ("Test Vault Co", id `staging-test-001`) with 7 users, 8 company entities, 25 products, 10 orders, 3 invoices, 1 price list, 5 KB categories. Idempotent via slug-adoption (re-running cleans + re-seeds).
- **Default admin credentials:** `admin@testco.com` / `TestAdmin123!` (admin role, all permissions). Full roster in `backend/scripts/seed_staging.py:350-356` (accountant, office, driver, production roles seeded alongside).
- **FH demo tenant (Phase B onward + September demo):** `python -m scripts.seed_fh_demo --apply` (from `backend/`) → creates `hopkins-fh` funeral-home tenant + `st-marys` cemetery + pre-seeded Hopkins↔Sunnycrest cross-tenant connection + demo case FC-2026-0001 (John Michael Smith, veteran). Admin: `admin@hopkinsfh.test` / `DemoAdmin123!`. Director: `director1@hopkinsfh.test` / `DemoDirector123!`.
- **Browser tenant bootstrap:** on `localhost`, `getCompanySlug()` reads from `localStorage.company_slug`. Visit `http://localhost:5173/?slug=testco` on first load — the module at `lib/tenant.ts` persists the slug and strips the query param. Subsequent visits resolve the tenant from localStorage.

### Staging deploy + auto-seed (Phase R-1.6)

Staging auto-deploys from `main` via `.github/workflows/deploy.yml` → `railway up`. The Railway start script `backend/railway-start.sh` runs after every deploy:

1. `alembic upgrade head` — apply pending migrations.
2. `python -m scripts.seed_staging --idempotent` — ensure testco tenant + demo data.
3. `python -m scripts.seed_fh_demo --apply --idempotent` — ensure Hopkins FH + St. Mary's + cross-tenant connection + FC-2026-0001.
4. `uvicorn` — start server.

Both seed scripts are idempotent (the `--idempotent` flag is the explicit-intent alias for the default ensure-or-skip behavior). Re-running on existing data is a no-op for `seed_fh_demo`; `seed_staging` cleans + re-seeds for end-state consistency. Both refuse to run if `ENVIRONMENT=production` — production has no test seed.

**R-1.6.3: seed failures FAIL the deploy.** Pre-R-1.6.3 the start script logged a warning and continued on seed errors. That swallowed a TypeError in `seed_fh_demo._ensure_user` (passing `role` string into a `User` model that has only `role_id` FK) for six R-* phases — Hopkins FH was half-seeded (company row exists, no users) and the deploy went green anyway. The CI bot investigation in R-1.6.3 surfaced the cascade. New contract: any seed failure exits the start script non-zero, Railway dashboard goes red, ops sees the failure immediately. Half-seeded tenants are not acceptable. Production still skips all staging seeds via the `ENVIRONMENT` guard.

`/api/health` includes a `commit` field sourced from Railway's `RAILWAY_GIT_COMMIT_SHA` env var. CI workflows poll the health endpoint to verify staging deployed the just-pushed SHA before running Playwright specs (closes the deploy-timing race).

### CI bot platform admin (Phase R-1.6)

Staging has a dedicated `ci-bot-runtime-editor@bridgeable.internal` PlatformUser with `support` role (the minimum role that can call `/api/platform/impersonation/impersonate`). The Playwright workflow at `.github/workflows/playwright-staging.yml` authenticates as this user — never as a human admin — so audit-log rows from CI activity are distinguishable by email pattern.

**The credential lives in GitHub Secrets, NOT in the repo, NOT in `.env.example`:**

- `STAGING_CI_BOT_EMAIL` = `ci-bot-runtime-editor@bridgeable.internal`
- `STAGING_CI_BOT_PASSWORD` = (32-char random; printed once by `provision_ci_bot.py` at provision time)

To rotate the password (e.g. quarterly hygiene): re-run `python -m scripts.provision_ci_bot --rotate` on staging, capture the new password, update `STAGING_CI_BOT_PASSWORD` in GitHub Secrets in lockstep. Failing to update the secret invalidates the Playwright CI run on next push.

All jobs use `_run_per_tenant()` or `_run_global()` wrappers with per-session DB isolation and error logging. All are manually triggerable via `JOB_REGISTRY` dict and the agent trigger API endpoint.

### Per-user Scheduled Jobs (new pattern — Phase 6)

Phase 6 (Briefings) introduced the first per-user scheduled pattern. APScheduler doesn't register one trigger per user (that would scale poorly with 100+ users per tenant); instead:

- **Single global sweep trigger**: `CronTrigger(minute="*/15")` — one APScheduler job registration.
- **Sweep function queries users whose per-user time preference fell in the trailing window**: e.g. `briefing_preferences.morning_delivery_time ∈ [local_now - 15min, local_now]`. Per-user timing runs in application code, not in the scheduler.
- **Idempotent via DB unique constraint**: the briefings table has a partial unique on `(user_id, briefing_type, DATE(generated_at AT TIME ZONE 'UTC'))`. Even if a sweep ran twice inside the same 15-min window, the second insert raises; the sweep pre-checks with `func.date(...) == today` for a cheap short-circuit.
- **Tenant timezone aware**: the sweep resolves `Company.timezone` (fallback `America/New_York`) + computes `local_now` per tenant before the window check. One tenant's 7am is a different wall-clock than another's.
- **Best-effort per user**: failures log and continue; one user's error never blocks the rest of the sweep.

Future per-user scheduled features (e.g. individual reminder digests, per-user inbox notifications, personal KPI pulls) reuse this same pattern: write a sweep function that queries users by a per-user preference, register a global cron trigger in `scheduler.py`. Reference implementation: `app/services/briefings/scheduler_integration.py`.

## 12. Coding Conventions

### API Routes
```python
# backend/app/api/routes/{module}.py
router = APIRouter()

@router.get("/endpoint")
def get_something(
    current_user: User = Depends(get_current_user),
    db: Session = Depends(get_db),
):
    """Docstring."""
    return db.query(Model).filter(Model.company_id == current_user.company_id).all()
```
Register in `backend/app/api/v1.py`:
```python
v1_router.include_router(module.router, prefix="/module", tags=["Module"])
```

### SQLAlchemy Models
```python
class Entity(Base):
    __tablename__ = "entities"
    id: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(36), primary_key=True, default=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
    tenant_id: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(36), ForeignKey("companies.id"), nullable=False, index=True)
    created_at: Mapped[datetime] = mapped_column(DateTime(timezone=True), default=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc))
```
Import in `backend/app/models/__init__.py` and add to `__all__`.

### Frontend Pages
```tsx
export default function FeaturePage() {
  const [data, setData] = useState(null)
  useEffect(() => { apiClient.get("/endpoint").then(r => setData(r.data)) }, [])
  return <div className="space-y-6 p-6">...</div>
}
```
Register route in `frontend/src/App.tsx`. Add nav item in `frontend/src/services/navigation-service.ts`.

### Nightly Agent Jobs
Add function in the appropriate service file. Add wrapper in `backend/app/scheduler.py`. Add to `JOB_REGISTRY`. Per-tenant jobs use `_run_per_tenant()`.

### Accounting Agents (Phase 1 Infrastructure)

**Architecture:** Shared infrastructure for 13 accounting agents (month-end close, AR collections, unbilled orders, etc.). Agents run as background jobs with a human-in-the-loop approval gate before committing changes.

**Key files:**
- `backend/app/services/agents/base_agent.py` — Abstract base class all agents extend
- `backend/app/services/agents/agent_runner.py` — Job creation, validation, execution orchestrator
- `backend/app/services/agents/approval_gate.py` — Token-based email approval workflow (simple path for weekly agents, full path with period lock for month-end)
- `backend/app/services/agents/period_lock.py` — Financial period locking service
- `backend/app/services/agents/ar_collections_agent.py` — AR Collections Agent (Phase 3)
- `backend/app/services/agents/unbilled_orders_agent.py` — Unbilled Orders Agent (Phase 4)
- `backend/app/services/agents/cash_receipts_agent.py` — Cash Receipts Matching Agent (Phase 5)
- `backend/app/services/agents/expense_categorization_agent.py` — Expense Categorization Agent (Phase 6)
- `backend/app/services/agents/estimated_tax_prep_agent.py` — Estimated Tax Prep Agent (Phase 7)
- `backend/app/services/agents/inventory_reconciliation_agent.py` — Inventory Reconciliation Agent (Phase 8)
- `backend/app/services/agents/budget_vs_actual_agent.py` — Budget vs. Actual Agent (Phase 9)
- `backend/app/services/agents/prep_1099_agent.py` — 1099 Prep Agent (Phase 10)
- `backend/app/services/agents/year_end_close_agent.py` — Year-End Close Agent (Phase 11)
- `backend/app/services/agents/tax_package_agent.py` — Tax Package Compilation Agent (Phase 12)
- `backend/app/services/agents/annual_budget_agent.py` — Annual Budget Agent (Phase 13)
- `backend/app/schemas/agent.py` — All Pydantic schemas (enums, request/response models)
- `backend/app/api/routes/agents.py` — API endpoints (under `/api/v1/agents/accounting`)
- `frontend/src/pages/agents/AgentDashboard.tsx` — Run agents, view history, manage period locks
- `frontend/src/pages/agents/ApprovalReview.tsx` — Review anomalies, approve/reject with period lock

**Database tables:** `agent_jobs` (extended), `agent_run_steps`, `agent_anomalies`, `agent_schedules`, `period_locks`

**Job lifecycle:** `pending` → `running` → `awaiting_approval` → `approved` → `complete` (or `rejected`/`failed`)

**Creating a new agent (Phase 2+):**
```python
from app.services.agents.base_agent import BaseAgent
from app.schemas.agent import StepResult, AnomalySeverity

class MonthEndCloseAgent(BaseAgent):
    STEPS = ["validate_balances", "post_accruals", "reconcile"]

    def run_step(self, step_name: str) -> StepResult:
        if step_name == "validate_balances":
            # Read-only analysis — always safe
            issues = self._check_balances()
            for issue in issues:
                self.add_anomaly(
                    severity=AnomalySeverity.WARNING,
                    anomaly_type="balance_mismatch",
                    description=issue["desc"],
                    amount=issue["amount"],
                )
            return StepResult(message=f"Found {len(issues)} issues", data={"issues": issues})
        elif step_name == "post_accruals":
            self.guard_write()  # Raises DryRunGuardError if dry_run=True
            # ... commit financial writes ...
            return StepResult(message="Posted accruals", data={})
```
Register in `AgentRunner.AGENT_REGISTRY`:
```python
from app.schemas.agent import AgentJobType
AgentRunner.AGENT_REGISTRY[AgentJobType.MONTH_END_CLOSE] = MonthEndCloseAgent
```

**Period lock integration:** `PeriodLockService.check_date_in_locked_period()` is called in `sales_service.py` before creating invoices or recording payments. Returns `PeriodLockedError` (HTTP 409) if the date falls in a locked period.

**Dry-run mode:** Default for all agent runs. `guard_write()` prevents any database mutations. Produces the same report/anomalies as a live run without committing changes.

**Approval gate:** After agent completes, generates a `secrets.token_urlsafe(48)` token, sends HTML email with approve/reject buttons. Token expires after 72 hours. No auth required — the token IS the auth. Approval locks the period; rejection does not.

### shadcn/ui v4
Uses `@base-ui/react` — **no `asChild` prop**. Use `render={<Component />}` instead. Use `buttonVariants()` for styling Links as buttons.

### Focus Canvas tier-renderer pointer-events contract

All three Focus Canvas tier renderers (`canvas`, `stack`, `icon`) are uniformly `pointer-events: none`. Interactive descendants self-assert `pointer-events: auto` on their own outer containers — `WidgetChrome` for canvas-tier widgets, `StackRail` for the right-rail Smart Stack, `IconButton` + `BottomSheet` (4 elements) for icon tier. CSS pointer-events is not inherited; descendants with explicit `pointer-events: auto` receive events normally even when the parent renderer is `pointer-events: none`.

This contract enables full-viewport renderer placement (`absolute inset-0`) without intercepting events meant for surfaces beneath. Canvas is a sibling of the focus-core-positioner inside `Dialog.Portal` but renders AFTER (Phase 4.2.3 DOM-order swap so accessories paint on top of core); at equal z-index this would otherwise mean every viewport coordinate gets captured by the topmost tier renderer, breaking kanban interactions in the popup beneath.

Established in **Phase 4.2.1** for canvas tier; extended to stack + icon tiers in **Phase 4.3b.3.1** after the `AncillaryPoolPin` widget triggered the latent bug — pre-pin the stack tier was rarely active in production (no widgets → canvas tier always passed), so the pin's 260px width forcing fall-through to stack tier exposed the asymmetric pointer-events contract for the first time.

**Future tier renderers + interactive children must follow this pattern.** Tier renderer = pointer-events: none. Each interactive surface inside self-asserts pointer-events: auto on its OWN container. New widgets that wrap their interactive content in another auto layer don't break anything; widgets that rely on the renderer's pointer-events would (silently — the bug surfaces only when widgets actually need to be active in production tier).

### Spec-Override Discipline

When user specification appears to conflict with design system canons, implementation conventions, or Sonnet's preferred interpretation:

1. **Flag the conflict** during pre-build investigation phase.
2. **Articulate both interpretations** clearly.
3. **Wait for user decision.**
4. **Execute per confirmed direction.**

Do not execute opposite of stated spec and justify after the fact. User spec is the primary source of direction. Canonical docs inform the approach but do not override explicit user direction. When tension exists between the two, resolution happens through conversation, not unilateral interpretation.

This rule caught an instance in Phase 3.3 where three explicit spec items were not executed due to Sonnet's preference for canonical doc interpretation (Dispatch Monitor rename skipped; empty driver columns shipped as "always render" when spec said "hide by default reveal on drag"; rotation physics left untouched when user specified "needs polish"). Rework was required in Phase 3.3.1. The discipline prevents this pattern from recurring.

Scope: applies to build prompts where the user has stated explicit direction. Exploratory/design conversations where the user is asking Sonnet to think alongside them are different — those invite interpretation. Build prompts with spec lists do not.

## 13. Business Context

### Tenant Types
- **Manufacturer** (Wilbert licensee) — produces burial vaults, manages deliveries, bills funeral homes monthly
- **Funeral Home** — orders vaults, pays on charge account, receives monthly statements
- **Cemetery** — orders cemetery products, interacts with manufacturers
- **Crematory** — specialized funeral service operations

### Product Lines
- **Funeral Service** (core) — burial vaults, urn vaults, cemetery equipment
- **Urn Sales** (extension) — cremation urns (stocked + Wilbert drop-ship), engraving workflow, Wilbert catalog ingestion
- **Wastewater** (extension) — septic tanks, precast wastewater products
- **Redi-Rock** (extension) — retaining wall blocks for contractors
- **Rosetta Hardscapes** (extension) — hardscape products
- **NPCA Audit Prep** (extension) — compliance and audit readiness; auto-enabled when `npca_certification_status = "certified"`

### Key Business Workflows
1. **Monthly Statement Billing** — funeral homes get consolidated statements, not individual invoices
2. **Finance Charges** — calculated on overdue balances, reviewed before posting
3. **Early Payment Discounts** — 2% if paid by 15th of month
4. **Cross-Licensee Transfers** — NY licensee transfers burial to NJ licensee; billing chains automatically
5. **Spring Burial Season** — March–May peak, requires delivery capacity management

## 14. Recent Changes

- **Phase R-1.6 — Staging Hardening: Auto-Seed + CI Bot + Deploy Gate + Behavioral Specs (code complete, ops handoff pending, 2026-06-06):** Six-part substrate phase wiring R-1.5's 13 Playwright validation gates against an actually-validatable target. No editor features, no schema changes, no migrations. Migration head unchanged at `r87_dashboard_layouts`. **Investigation-first** (per `/tmp/r_1_6_staging_investigation.md`): verified staging exists + healthy + R-1.5 deployed; testco seeded but Hopkins FH NOT seeded; platform admin route mounted but no CI credential in repo; Playwright not in CI today. R-1.6 closes those gaps in code; ops runs the one-time provisioning steps to activate. **Part 1 — Seed idempotency + Railway start integration.** `seed_fh_demo.py` + `seed_staging.py` gain `--idempotent` flag (explicit alias for default ensure-or-skip behavior; verbose-mode marker for railway-start.sh re-invocation). `seed_fh_demo` was already ensure-or-skip in core paths; `--force` continues to deletes + recreate when explicit. `seed_staging` was already cleanup + re-seed; the flag adds consistent log output. **Slug alignment:** seed_fh_demo creates `hopkins-fh` + `st-marys` slugs (was `hopkinsfh` + `stmarys` — drifted from CLAUDE.md canonical docs). Email domains stay `@hopkinsfh.test` (independent of tenant slug). **`railway-start.sh` extended**: after `alembic upgrade head`, runs `seed_staging --idempotent` then `seed_fh_demo --apply --idempotent`. Both refuse `ENVIRONMENT=production` per existing safety guards. Failures log warnings + continue — server boot must complete even if seeding hits transient issues. **Part 2 — `provision_ci_bot.py` one-time provisioner.** Creates `ci-bot-runtime-editor@bridgeable.internal` PlatformUser with `support` role (minimum that can call impersonation API). Generates 32-char URL-safe random password via `secrets.choice`; prints once to stdout. Operator captures the password + adds to GitHub Secrets as `STAGING_CI_BOT_EMAIL` + `STAGING_CI_BOT_PASSWORD`. Re-running with `--rotate` rotates the password (idempotent at user-record level); without `--rotate`, second run aborts to prevent accidental credential invalidation. **Part 3 — `/api/health` exposes deployed commit hash.** New `commit` field sources from `RAILWAY_GIT_COMMIT_SHA` env var (Railway injects on every deploy). Field is `null` for non-Railway hosts (local dev). Existing health-check consumers see only additive field; response shape backward-compatible. CI workflow polls this field to gate Playwright runs on deploy completion. **Part 4 — `.github/workflows/playwright-staging.yml`.** New workflow triggers on `push` to `main` + `workflow_dispatch`. Job sequence: checkout → install Playwright + cache browsers → **deploy gate** (poll `/api/health` until `commit` matches `${{ github.sha }}`, 15-min timeout, 5-min bootstrap fallback for pre-R-1.6 deploys lacking the commit field) → run `npx playwright test tests/e2e/runtime-editor/` → upload traces + screenshots + HTML report on failure. Concurrency group `playwright-staging-${{ github.ref }}` with `cancel-in-progress: true` so back-to-back pushes don't race. Browser binaries cached via `actions/cache@v4` keyed on `package-lock.json` hash — first run installs (~1 min), cached runs skip (~10s). **Does NOT run on PRs or feature branches** — staging is single-tenant for `main`; PR-time validation continues via existing `ci.yml` (vitest + tsc + build). **Part 5 — 10 specs promoted from structural to behavioral.** Real `loginAsPlatformAdmin` in `_shared.ts` POSTs to `/api/platform/auth/login`, stashes token under `bridgeable-admin-token-staging` localStorage key (canonical adminApi key per `bridgeable-admin/lib/admin-api.ts` — NOT under tenant `access_token`; cross-realm boundary preserved). New `impersonateHopkinsDirector` calls tenants/lookup → impersonation API → persists impersonation token under `localStorage.access_token` + `company_slug` + `runtime_editor_session` marker (mirrors TenantUserPicker post-success). New `openEditorForHopkins` orchestrates the full flow + navigates to editor URL with active session. New `loginAsHopkinsDirector` for the tenant-side regression spec (Gate 10). Per-spec rewrites: **Gate 1** drives picker → editor mount → asserts ribbon + tenant slug visible. **Gate 2** asserts `?edit=1` URL sync + `data-active="true"` toggle attribute + brass indicator + clean toggle-off (no unsaved drafts). **Gate 3** clicks first `[data-component-name]` widget → asserts brass selection overlay + 3 inspector tabs visible + Props default-active. **Gate 4** opens Theme tab → fills accent token input → asserts `--accent` on `documentElement` reflects staged value within 200ms. **Gate 5** opens Class tab → interacts with first prop control. **Gate 6** stages on Props tab → asserts staged-count > 0. **Gate 7** stages theme override → commits → reloads page → reads `--accent` post-reload + asserts persisted value (test-residue accepted at vertical_default scope per spec acceptance). **Gate 8** stages override + toggles off → asserts confirm dialog appears with discard/commit/cancel buttons → clicks discard → URL drops `?edit=1`. **Gate 9** stubs `/api/platform/admin/visual-editor/themes/**` PATCH/POST to return 500 → commits → asserts `commitError` text surfaces in footer + staged drafts retained for retry. **Gate 10** logs in as Hopkins director1 directly at `/login` (NOT through runtime editor) → asserts no toggle, no shell, no `?edit=1` URL artifacts. **Part 6 — bundle-baseline.json fixture.** New `tests/e2e/runtime-editor/fixtures/bundle-baseline.json` captures R-1.5 ship sizes for non-runtime-editor chunks (main_bundle 5,521,976 bytes; css 235,767 bytes; chart_renderer 408,934 bytes). Spec 11 reads the fixture, scans `frontend/dist/assets/`, asserts each pattern-matched chunk is within +5% of baseline. Runtime-editor chunks (`RuntimeEditorShell-*`, `TenantUserPicker-*`, etc.) excluded — they're EXPECTED to change as R-2+ ships. **Spec 11 verified locally**: `npx playwright test 11-bundle-delta.spec.ts` passes against current build. Fixture updates require commit-message justification when intentional bundle growth lands. **Files added** (4 new + 1 fixture): `backend/scripts/provision_ci_bot.py`, `.github/workflows/playwright-staging.yml`, `frontend/tests/e2e/runtime-editor/fixtures/bundle-baseline.json`. **Files modified**: `backend/scripts/seed_fh_demo.py` (--idempotent flag + slug alignment to canonical hopkins-fh/st-marys + dry-run prints), `backend/scripts/seed_staging.py` (--idempotent flag + production safety guard), `backend/railway-start.sh` (auto-seed both scripts post-migration), `backend/app/main.py` (commit field on /api/health), `frontend/tests/e2e/runtime-editor/_shared.ts` (real platform admin login + impersonation helper + openEditorForHopkins), 10 spec files in `frontend/tests/e2e/runtime-editor/0{1-9}-*.spec.ts` + `10-tenant-operator-regression.spec.ts` + `11-bundle-delta.spec.ts` (rewritten from structural to behavioral), `CLAUDE.md` (this entry + new staging deploy section + CI bot section). **Final regression posture**: tsc clean, vitest **1409/1409 passing** (unchanged from R-1.5 baseline — no vitest changes in R-1.6), vite build clean (5.20s). Migration head unchanged at `r87_dashboard_layouts`. **OPS HANDOFF REQUIRED to activate** — see CLAUDE.md "Staging deploy + auto-seed" + "CI bot platform admin" sections. Steps: (1) merge R-1.6 to main → triggers Railway deploy → railway-start.sh runs the new auto-seed → Hopkins FH appears on staging. (2) On staging, run `python -m scripts.provision_ci_bot` once → capture printed password. (3) Add `STAGING_CI_BOT_EMAIL` + `STAGING_CI_BOT_PASSWORD` to GitHub Secrets. (4) Manually trigger `playwright-staging.yml` via `workflow_dispatch` to verify deploy gate + 13 specs run end-to-end. **Architectural patterns established this phase**: (1) **Auto-seed on every deploy** — staging is now self-bootstrapping; no manual `seed_fh_demo --apply` needed after deploys. Production has the env-guard escape hatch. (2) **Deploy-gate via `/api/health` commit hash** — closes the timing race the investigation flagged. CI workflows can now reliably ensure staging matches the just-pushed SHA before running specs. (3) **Dedicated CI bot user with audit-distinguishable email** — `*@bridgeable.internal` TLD pattern lets audit log queries filter human vs CI activity. The bot has minimum-required role (`support`), not super_admin. (4) **GitHub Secrets as the canonical credential store** — credentials NEVER in repo, NEVER in `.env.example`. Provision script prints once + relies on operator to capture. (5) **Hybrid local + staging Playwright** — spec 11 (bundle-delta) runs locally against `frontend/dist/`; specs 1-10 + 13 run against staging. Spec 12 is a CI gate anchor with no body. (6) **Behavioral specs over structural** — R-1.6 specs assert what the editor DOES (URL state, computed CSS variables, dialog mounting, error-text surfaces) rather than what test-ids EXIST. (7) **Test residue accepted at vertical_default scope** — spec 7 mutates Hopkins FH's theme; subsequent runs see the prior staged value as new baseline. Future ops can run a periodic `vertical_default` reset script if needed (deferred). **What R-1.6 explicitly did NOT ship**: editor features (no R-2 entity-card click-to-edit, no R-3 page-chrome editing, no R-4 unification); local docker-compose full-stack runner (deferred — not needed when staging works); PR-time Playwright runs (staging is single-tenant for `main`); audit-log cleanup script for accumulating CI bot rows (flagged as known future work; not blocking R-1.6). **Sequencing forward**: ops handoff completes R-1.6 activation; first Playwright run against post-handoff staging validates all 13 gates pass; flaky specs (>1 retry) are R-2 architectural debt to address. After R-1.6 activation, R-2 (entity cards + selection borders + drag handles) opens with a proven CI-validated runtime editor surface.

- **Phase R-1.5 — Tightening: Operational Safety + Playwright Gates + Registry Audit (complete, 2026-06-06):** Three-part defense-in-depth phase tightening R-1's foundations. No new features, no schema changes, migration head unchanged at `r87_dashboard_layouts`. **Part 1 — Operational-handler safety pass on the 6 R-1 registered widgets.** Each of `today`, `operator-profile`, `recent-activity`, `anomalies`, `vault-schedule`, `line-status` now reads `useEditMode().isEditing` (from `@/lib/runtime-host/edit-mode-context`) AND accepts a `_editMode?: boolean` prop, combines them into `editModeActive`, and gates every operational handler (navigate, acknowledge, click-through) behind `if (editModeActive) return` at handler entry. **Defense-in-depth over SelectionOverlay's capture-phase suppression**: the architectural gate remains the document-level capture-phase click handler in `SelectionOverlay`, which calls `stopPropagation` to prevent React's root delegation listener from invoking the widget's bubble-phase `onClick`. R-1.5's handler-level gates are the belt-and-suspenders layer. Each widget gains a top-of-file comment marker `// R-1.5: handlers gated behind _editMode for runtime editor safety` so future authors understand the discipline. **Custom ESLint rule** at `frontend/eslint-rules/widget-handlers-edit-mode-gated.js` registered as `bridgeable/widget-handlers-edit-mode-gated` (warning level): heuristic — fires on any FunctionDeclaration or VariableDeclarator named `handle*` or `on*` inside one of the 6 R-1 widget files when the file's source doesn't reference `_editMode`. **Scope narrowed to the 6 R-1 widgets specifically** per spec discipline ("Better an opinionated narrow rule than a noisy broad one that gets disabled") — broader widget directory has many additional widgets (Calendar, Email, vault subfolder, ops-board) outside R-1.5 scope; gating those is future-phase work. The rule passes 0 violations on the 6 R-1 widgets post-Part-1; firings against other widgets surface forward-debt without blocking. Future phases that promote additional widgets to the runtime registry must extend the rule's allowlist + gate the new widget's handlers in lockstep. **6 vitest widget safety specs** in `frontend/src/components/widgets/__tests__/widget-edit-mode-safety.test.tsx` — one per registered widget plus a control test confirming handlers DO fire when `_editMode={true}` is omitted (sanity check that the test plumbing is wired correctly). All 7 tests passing. **Part 2 — 13 Playwright validation gate specs** in `frontend/tests/e2e/runtime-editor/01-…` through `13-…` covering R-1's user-facing behavior end-to-end. Spec-Override Discipline note (CLAUDE.md §12): R-1's prompt specified `frontend/playwright/runtime-editor/`; the project's canonical Playwright `testDir` is `frontend/tests/e2e/` (per playwright.config.ts), so specs land at `tests/e2e/runtime-editor/` to integrate with existing CI. **Gates 1-13 verbatim from R-1 prompt**: picker-lands-on-dashboard, toggle-edit-mode + URL sync, click-widget-opens-inspector with 3 tabs, theme-token-live-preview via `--accent` root CSS variable, class-config-live-preview, component-prop-live-preview, commit-overrides-persist + reload, discard-on-toggle-off + confirm dialog, partial-commit-failure with 2-of-5 stub + retry, tenant-operator-regression (Hopkins FH director1 unchanged), bundle-delta within +5%, test-suite-health (CI gate documenting cross-suite contract), migration-head-unchanged at r87_dashboard_layouts. Specs are staging-deploy-dependent — they assert the test-id contract surface + reachable endpoints; full interactive flows wire when impersonation + click-to-select drive against staging. **Part 3 — Registry contract audit** documented at repo-root `R_1_5_REGISTRY_AUDIT.md` (~150 lines). R-1's `registerComponent` HOC now wraps every registered component in a `display: contents` boundary div carrying `data-component-name` data attributes. The `RegistryEntry.component` field points at the wrapped boundary, NOT the original component reference. **Audit covered 63 callsites** across `frontend/src/` outside the registry package — every call to `getByName`, `getAllRegistered`, `getByType`, `getByVertical`, `getCanvasPlaceableComponents`, `getCanvasMetadata`, `isCanvasPlaceable`, `getEffectiveComponentClasses`, `getComponentsInClass`, `getTokensConsumedBy`, `getComponentsConsumingToken`, `getKnownTokens`, `getCountByType`, `getCoverageByVertical`, `getRegistrationVersion`, `getAcceptedChildrenForSlot`, plus `RegistryEntry` type usages and `entry.component` / `reg.component` / `registration.component` reads. **Conclusion: 0 callsites depend on the old contract.** No `===` / `!==` ref-identity comparisons; no Map keys using component reference; no `useMemo` / `useCallback` deps using component reference; no JSX rendering of `<entry.component />` that depends on the wrapper-vs-original distinction. Every consumer reads `metadata` fields (name, type, configurableProps, canvasMetadata, verticals) for routing or rendering decisions, then renders via dedicated per-kind preview components or stand-ins. The HOC change is structurally non-breaking; the only asserter that broke was `registry.test.ts`, fixed in R-1. The audit doc preserves the per-callsite catalog as the architectural artifact. **Tests shipped Phase R-1.5**: 7 vitest in widget-edit-mode-safety.test.tsx + 13 Playwright (staging-deploy-dependent) + 1 ESLint rule registered as warning. **Final regression posture**: tsc clean (zero errors); vitest **1409/1409 passing** (1402 R-1 baseline + 7 new R-1.5); vite build clean (4.76s); main bundle 5,521.72 kB (+0.09% vs R-1's 5,516.80 kB — well under +5% ceiling); RuntimeEditorShell chunk lazy-split at 25.13 kB / gzip 7.22 kB; ESLint passes 0 violations on the 6 R-1 widgets. **Files added** (10): `R_1_5_REGISTRY_AUDIT.md` at repo root, `frontend/eslint-rules/widget-handlers-edit-mode-gated.js`, `frontend/src/components/widgets/__tests__/widget-edit-mode-safety.test.tsx`, 13 Playwright spec files at `frontend/tests/e2e/runtime-editor/{01-13}-….spec.ts` + shared helpers `_shared.ts`. **Files modified**: 6 widget files (TodayWidget, OperatorProfileWidget, RecentActivityWidget, AnomaliesWidget, VaultScheduleWidget, LineStatusWidget) — added `_editMode` prop + `useEditMode()` consumption + handler gates + R-1.5 comment marker; `frontend/eslint.config.js` (registered bridgeable plugin + the rule); `CLAUDE.md` (this entry). **Architectural patterns established this phase**: (1) **Defense-in-depth via widget-level `_editMode` gates** — capture-phase suppression in SelectionOverlay is the architectural gate; widget-level gates are the belt-and-suspenders layer. Both must be present for the 6 R-1 widgets; future registered widgets follow the same pattern. (2) **Heuristic ESLint rule with allowlist scope** — narrow rule that lints only the 6 R-1 widget files, not the entire widget directory. Future phases that promote additional widgets to the registry extend the allowlist. (3) **Audit document as the registry-contract artifact** — `R_1_5_REGISTRY_AUDIT.md` preserves the per-callsite catalog so future callers know which patterns are safe (none today depend on ref-identity). Future phases that introduce ref-identity dependence add a `RegistryEntry.originalComponent` field exposing the un-wrapped reference. (4) **R-1.5 ships 0 schema changes + 0 new tables** — purely tightening; the editor's user-facing surface is unchanged from R-1. **What R-1.5 explicitly did NOT ship**: no per-key partial-commit retry buttons (still deferred to R-1.5+ polish); no expansion of widget coverage beyond the 6 already registered; no entity card click-to-edit (R-2); no page chrome editing (R-3); no undo/redo (R-4); no visual editor merge (R-4). **Sequencing forward**: R-1.6 — staging-integration run hardening the 13 Playwright gates (drive picker → impersonation → click-to-select → stage → commit/reload/discard end-to-end against the post-R-1.5 staging deploy); R-2 — entity cards (DeliveryCard, AncillaryCard, OrderCard) + selection borders + drag handle UI; R-3 — page-chrome + form-layout editing with `page_overrides` table; R-4 — `/visual-editor/*` ↔ `/runtime-editor/*` unification + performance + polish.

- **Phase R-1 — Runtime Editor V1: Click-to-Edit on Registered Widgets (complete, 2026-06-05):** First user-facing runtime editor. Platform admins navigate any tenant route while impersonating a tenant user, click any of the 6 currently-registered widgets, and edit theme tokens / component class config / per-component prop overrides through an inspector panel that commits through the dual-token client to the existing platform services. Live preview against the impersonated tenant render. Builds on R-0's substrate (TenantProviders, TenantRouteTree, dual-token client, EditModeProvider, dashboard_layouts table) — no migration this phase, no schema changes, no new backend services or endpoints (everything reuses existing platform_themes / component_configurations / component_class_configurations services from prior visual editor phases). **`registerComponent` HOC contract change**: every registered component now renders inside a `display: contents` div carrying `data-component-name` + `data-component-type` + `data-component-version` data attributes. The wrapper is invisible in layout (display: contents removes it from the box tree while keeping it queryable via `document.querySelector`); the wrapped component is stored in the registry so introspection consumers (preview canvas, editor) see the same DOM the runtime renders — no drift between editor markup and production markup. Tenant operators see these attributes too in production (harmless metadata; chose this over editor-only injection to avoid the "edit mode injects different markup" anti-pattern). The HOC swap from "returns original component reference" → "returns wrapped component" is the load-bearing R-1 contract change. Existing registry test updated to assert `Wrapped !== Cmp` and the new `Registered(displayName)` displayName pattern. **`page-contexts.ts` registry**: 20-route `PAGE_CONTEXT_MAP` covering the most-trafficked tenant routes (/dashboard, /home, /scheduling, /cases, /triage, /briefing, /inbox, /production, /agents, /financials/board, /calls, /safety, /vault, /saved-views, /tasks, /calendar, /order-station, FH dashboard variants). `resolvePageContext(pathname)` returns `{pageContext, label, mapped}`. Pattern matching supports `:param` segments (e.g. `/cases/:id`) and `*` wildcards. Unmapped routes fall through to `unmapped:{path}` page_context — theme + class edits still work, per-component prop overrides scoped placeholder. `usePageContext()` hook composes `useLocation` + the registry. **`EditModeToggle.tsx`**: floating fixed-position toggle (top-right, z-index 91). URL parameter `?edit=1` syncs ↔ `isEditing` via `useSearchParams` so edit-mode is shareable + page-reload-preserved. Brass top-edge highlight (h-0.5 bar) when active. Page-label readout shows the current `pageContext` + display label + warning icon for unmapped routes. Unsaved-drafts confirm dialog gates toggle-off (3 actions: discard / commit / stay in edit mode). All test-ids stable: `runtime-editor-toggle`, `runtime-editor-staged-count`, `runtime-editor-page-label`, `runtime-editor-edit-indicator`, `runtime-editor-unmapped-warning`, `runtime-editor-confirm-{dialog,cancel,discard,commit}`. **`SelectionOverlay.tsx`**: capture-phase click handler at `document` walks up from `event.target` to the nearest `[data-component-name]`, stopping at the `[data-runtime-host-root]` boundary OR skipping when inside `[data-runtime-editor-chrome]` regions. On match: `preventDefault + stopPropagation + selectComponent(name)`. On miss inside the tenant content region: `selectComponent(null)`. `stopPropagation` from a capture-phase handler at `document` prevents React's root delegation listener from receiving the event — so widget-level bubble-phase `onClick` handlers (the ones that call `navigate()`) do NOT fire. This is the architectural gate for **operational-handler safety on R-1's 6 widgets**. None of the 6 widgets install capture-phase listeners of their own, so document-level capture is sufficient — verified by 4 vitest specs in `SelectionOverlay.test.tsx`. Brass border around selected element via fixed-position overlay sized to `getBoundingClientRect()`, updated on scroll + resize via `ResizeObserver` + scroll listener. Hover-affordance CSS injected once: 30%-opacity brass outline on `[data-runtime-editor-mode="edit"] [data-component-name]:hover`. **Inspector panel + 3 tabs** under `lib/runtime-host/inspector/`: `InspectorPanel.tsx` (380px wide right-rail, fixed position, z-index 95; renders only when `isEditing && selectedComponentName !== null`; header shows `kind:displayName` + close affordance; 3-tab strip Theme | Class | Props with default tab Props; footer with staged-count badge + Discard + Commit buttons + commit-error inline display + per-failed-key retry surface). `PropsTab.tsx` auto-resolves `componentConfigurationsService.resolve` for the selected widget, builds 4-layer ConfigStack via `stackFromResolvedConfig` + draft layer from `editMode.draftOverrides`, calls `composeEffectiveProps` for effective values, renders `CompactPropControl` per `configurableProps` entry; edits stage to `component_prop` overrides keyed by `${kind}:${name}::${prop}`. `ClassTab.tsx` resolves component classes via `getEffectiveComponentClasses(entry)` (V1: each component belongs to exactly one class — its ComponentKind), fetches class registration's `configurableProps` from `class-registrations.ts`, calls `componentClassConfigurationsService.resolve(className)` for class defaults, renders `CompactPropControl` per class-prop with source badge `D / C / draft`. `ThemeTab.tsx` curated 20-token V1 subset (accent + surface + content + status + radius families) with "Show all tokens" expansion to full 80-token catalog; lookup via `themesService.resolve({mode, vertical, tenant_id})`; live preview via `applyThemeToElement(effective, document.documentElement)` — same mechanism the standalone theme editor uses, scoped to root. Source badges D/P/V/T/draft per token. **`runtime-writers.ts`**: registers real `OverrideWriter` implementations for the 4 canonical override types via `buildRuntimeWriters(ctx)`. Theme writer fetches existing `vertical_default` row at the impersonated tenant's vertical via `themesService.list`, merges the new token override, calls `update` if active row exists or `create` if not. Component-prop writer parses `${kind}:${name}` target, similarly merge-or-create at `vertical_default` scope keyed on (kind, name). Component-class writer ships at `class_default` scope (single canonical scope per CLAUDE.md §4 Component Class Configuration). Dashboard-layout writer throws (R-1 inspector doesn't expose layout editing; layouts continue to be authored at the Widget Editor's Dashboard Layouts tab — no contract leak via runtime editor in this phase). All writers route through the existing visual editor services (which themselves use `adminApi` → `/api/platform/admin/visual-editor/*`); commits flow PlatformUser-authenticated via the standard admin route auth. **`RuntimeEditorShell.tsx`** (replaces R-0's `RuntimeHostTestPage` as the canonical R-1 entry): mounts `<TenantProviders>` + `<TenantRouteTree>` + `<EditModeProvider writers={buildRuntimeWriters(ctx)}>` + `<EditModeToggle>` + `<SelectionOverlay>` + `<InspectorPanel>` in the correct DOM hierarchy. Inner `ShellWithTenantContext` runs INSIDE TenantProviders so `useAuth().company` resolves the impersonated tenant's vertical for the writer context. Auth gate widened from R-0's super_admin-only to `super_admin OR platform_admin OR support` — matches the impersonation API's role gate (R-0's narrower gate was phase scope, not architectural constraint). Loading / unauth / forbidden / missing-params states each carry stable test-ids. Warning ribbon at top with tenant slug + impersonated user id + admin email. Tenant content rendered inside `data-runtime-host-root` boundary so SelectionOverlay knows when to stop walking. **`TenantUserPicker.tsx`** at `/runtime-editor` (entry surface): canonical TenantPicker (the visual editor's existing component) + optional `user_id` text input + `reason` field. Submitting POSTs to `/api/platform/impersonation/impersonate`, persists access_token + company_slug + a `runtime_editor_session` marker to localStorage, then navigates to `/runtime-editor/?tenant=<slug>&user=<id>`. When `user_id` is blank, the impersonate API picks the tenant's first admin (per `ImpersonateRequest` schema). Listing impersonatable users explicitly is deferred — no backend endpoint exists today for tenant-user lookup under platform auth, and the auto-pick-first-admin behavior is sufficient for V1; a scoped endpoint can be added in a follow-up. **Routes**: `/runtime-editor` and `/bridgeable-admin/runtime-editor` route to `TenantUserPicker`; `/runtime-editor/*` and `/bridgeable-admin/runtime-editor/*` route to `RuntimeEditorShell`. Both lazy-loaded; both bypass admin/visual-editor layouts. R-0's `/_runtime-host-test/*` paths preserved as backward-compat aliases for existing test references — they continue to mount the original `RuntimeHostTestPage`. **Visual Editor Index** gains a new "Edit live tenant pages" card with `MousePointerClick` icon deep-linking to `/runtime-editor`; sits alongside the 7 visual editor cards. **Vitest setup polyfill**: `globalThis.ResizeObserver` shimmed via stub class so `SelectionOverlay`'s ResizeObserver consumption doesn't crash jsdom tests. Documented inline in `src/test/setup.ts` alongside the existing matchMedia + localStorage shims. **Tests shipped Phase R-1**: 8 new vitest tests in `runtime-host/SelectionOverlay.test.tsx` (4 — selects on click, capture-phase blocks bubble onClick in edit mode, view-mode does NOT block, chrome clicks not hijacked) + `runtime-host/EditModeToggle.test.tsx` (4 — View label when not editing, Editing label + indicator when active, unmapped warning surfaces, click toggles into edit mode) + 1 update to `registry/registry.test.ts` ("returns the same component reference" → "returns a wrapped component that injects data-component-name" + displayName regression guard). **Final regression posture**: tsc clean (only pre-existing `CalendarAccountsPage.tsx` `asChild` violation predates R-1); vitest **1402/1402 passing** (1394 baseline + 8 new R-1, including the registry.test.ts update); vite build clean (4.97s); RuntimeEditorShell chunk lazy-split at 29.01 kB / gzip 8.68 kB; TenantUserPicker chunk at 3.46 kB / gzip 1.34 kB. Migration head unchanged at `r87_dashboard_layouts`. **Files added** (8): `frontend/src/lib/runtime-host/page-contexts.ts`, `use-page-context.ts`, `EditModeToggle.tsx`, `EditModeToggle.test.tsx`, `SelectionOverlay.tsx`, `SelectionOverlay.test.tsx`, `runtime-writers.ts`, `inspector/{PropsTab.tsx, ClassTab.tsx, ThemeTab.tsx, InspectorPanel.tsx}`, `frontend/src/bridgeable-admin/pages/runtime-editor/{RuntimeEditorShell.tsx, TenantUserPicker.tsx}`. **Files modified**: `frontend/src/lib/visual-editor/registry/register.ts` (HOC now wraps components), `registry/registry.test.ts` (HOC contract test update), `frontend/src/test/setup.ts` (ResizeObserver polyfill), `frontend/src/bridgeable-admin/BridgeableAdminApp.tsx` (4 new lazy routes for runtime-editor + picker; R-0 alias preserved), `frontend/src/bridgeable-admin/pages/visual-editor/VisualEditorIndex.tsx` (Edit live tenant pages card + MousePointerClick icon import), `CLAUDE.md` (this entry). **Architectural patterns established this phase**: (1) **`display: contents` wrapper for data-attribute injection** preserves layout while keeping DOM queryable; chosen over React.cloneElement (which can't handle fragments) and over editor-only conditional injection (which would create runtime-vs-editor markup drift). (2) **Capture-phase handler at `document` is sufficient gate for arbitrary widget operational handlers** — `stopPropagation` from a capture handler at document level prevents React's root delegation listener from receiving the event, so bubble-phase `onClick` handlers don't fire. Verified architecturally + tested. Widget-level `_editMode` reads remain available as belt-and-suspenders for any widget that opts in, but are not required for safety. (3) **Page-context registry as pragmatic V1 — 20 routes, fall-through for the rest** — rather than gate the entire editor on full route coverage, unmapped routes get a placeholder page_context that lets theme + class edits work today; per-component prop overrides at unmapped routes are scoped. New routes opt in by adding to PAGE_CONTEXT_MAP (no migration). (4) **EditMode contract scoped to the runtime editor shell only** — outside-provider `useEditMode()` returns the no-op stub from R-0; tenant components that want to be edit-aware call `useEditMode()` unconditionally and read `isEditing`. R-1 doesn't retrofit any tenant component to read edit-mode (operational-handler safety is the SelectionOverlay capture phase, not opt-in widget-level gates). (5) **R-1 reuses every existing visual editor backend endpoint** — themes service, component_configurations service, component_class_configurations service are all called via the `adminApi` axios instance from the runtime writers. No new platform endpoints. The dual-token client's PlatformUser write contract is honored because the platform services themselves authenticate as PlatformUser via `get_current_platform_user`. (6) **Cross-realm boundary preserved** — runtime editor commits run as PlatformUser auth. Impersonation token handles tenant-data reads only (the tenant route tree's data fetches). The two never cross. **What R-1 explicitly did NOT ship**: per-key partial-commit retry buttons (the inspector footer surfaces `commitError` text but not granular per-key retry — deferred to R-1.5 polish); entity-card click-to-edit (DeliveryCard, AncillaryCard, OrderCard — deferred to R-2 alongside selection borders + drag handle UI for canvas-positioned widgets); page-chrome + form-layout editing (deferred to R-3 with `page_overrides` table); operational-handler safety pass beyond the 6 registered widgets (R-1.5 — adds `_editMode` reads to the 50-80 components per the existing widget convention as belt-and-suspenders); inline-picker dropdown for selecting a saved view as a widget config target inside the inspector (deferred to R-2); audit-log enrichment with runtime-editor session metadata (deferred to a separate phase; current impersonation audit row captures session_id which is sufficient). **Sequencing forward**: R-1.5 — operational-handler safety pass extending `_editMode` opt-ins across the broader component surface + per-key partial-commit retry UX in inspector footer + Playwright spec coverage for the full editor flow. R-2 — entity cards (DeliveryCard, AncillaryCard, OrderCard) + selection borders + drag handle UI + Hopkins FH director1 regression. R-3 — page-chrome + form-layout editing with new `page_overrides` table. R-4 — unify `/visual-editor/*` and `/runtime-editor/*` into a single editor surface + performance + polish.

- **Phase R-0 — Runtime Editor Foundation (complete, 2026-06-04):** Foundation phase for the Runtime-Aware Editor that R-1+ phases build on. Ships ZERO user-facing runtime editor UI; the deliverable is the four substrate pieces R-1 needs: shared-bundle refactor, dashboard_layouts 3-tier table + authoring UI, dual-token API client, EditModeProvider context plumbing. Architectural surprises surface NOW rather than three phases in. **Migration `r87_dashboard_layouts`** creates `dashboard_layouts` table parallel to platform_themes / component_configurations / focus_compositions — `(scope, vertical, tenant_id, page_context, layout_config, version, is_active, audit)` with CHECK constraints for scope-key shape + partial unique on `is_active=true`. Migration head advances `r86_document_template_vertical_tier → r87_dashboard_layouts`. Closes the pre-existing scope-inheritance gap: dashboard widget arrangements were per-user only via `user_widget_layouts` with no platform/vertical/tenant default tier; defaults came from each WidgetDefinition's in-code `default_position`. **`app/services/dashboard_layouts/layouts_service.py`** mirrors `theme_service` shape — `list_layouts / get_layout_by_id / create_layout / update_layout / resolve_layout` with write-side versioning (each save deactivates prior + inserts at version+1; partial unique enforces "at most one active row per tuple"; inactive rows accumulate as audit trail). Validation rejects scope-key mismatches, empty page_contexts, malformed layout_config (must be list of widget config dicts), duplicate widget_ids in same layout. **`resolve_layout`** walks `platform_default → vertical_default(vertical) → tenant_default(tenant_id)` first-match-wins (deepest non-empty wins because layouts are complete arrangements, not partial overlays — same semantics as `focus_compositions`). Returns `{layout_config, source, source_id, source_version, sources, page_context, vertical, tenant_id}`. **`widget_service.get_user_layout` refactored** to consume `dashboard_layouts.resolve_layout` for default-layout generation: when no UserWidgetLayout row exists for a fresh user, walks the new 3-tier inheritance chain first, falls back to in-code `WIDGET_DEFINITIONS.default_position` only when no row exists at any scope. **5 admin endpoints** at `/api/platform/admin/visual-editor/dashboard-layouts/*` (PlatformUser-gated): list / get / resolve / create (201 + scope-key validation 400) / patch (with version bump). **Frontend dual-token API client** at `frontend/src/lib/runtime-host/dual-token-client.ts`. Establishes the contract every R-1+ runtime editor write uses: PlatformUser token (from `getAdminToken()` per existing admin-api.ts) for `/api/platform/*` endpoints, impersonation token (from `localStorage.access_token` set by existing impersonation flow) for `/api/v1/*` reads. **Strict no-fallback discipline**: tenant writes through this client are FORBIDDEN — POST/PATCH/PUT/DELETE against `/api/v1/*` throw `tenant_write_forbidden` error pre-flight before the request fires. Cross-realm boundary enforced TWICE — frontend refuses tenant writes; backend `get_current_platform_user` / `get_current_user` reject cross-realm tokens at the dependency layer (CLAUDE.md §4 load-bearing security boundary). Unsupported paths (anything outside `/api/platform/` or `/api/v1/`) throw `unsupported_path` rather than guess routing. Helper functions (`platformGet/Post/Patch/Delete` + `tenantGet`) make routing explicit at call site. Token refresh follows existing platform refresh flow + impersonation banner countdown UX. Module-level singleton via `useRuntimeHostClient()` hook. **EditModeProvider + useEditMode hook** at `frontend/src/lib/runtime-host/edit-mode-context.tsx`. Context plumbing for R-1+ consumers (no UI yet — R-0 ships the contract). Provider props: `tenantSlug`, `impersonatedUserId`, `initialMode`, `initialPageContext`, optional `writers` (per-type override registry, test-injectable). Context surface: `isEditing` boolean, `pageContext`, `selectedComponentName`, `draftOverrides` (Map keyed by `${type}::${target}::${prop}`), `isCommitting`, `commitError` + actions `setEditing / setPageContext / selectComponent / stageOverride / clearStaged / commitDraft / discardDraft`. Four canonical override types: `token` → platform_themes_service, `component_prop` → component_configurations_service, `component_class` → component_class_config_service, `dashboard_layout` → dashboard_layouts_service. **Outside-provider stub**: `useEditMode()` returns a no-op stub when called outside an EditModeProvider, matching the existing widget `_editMode` convention so R-1+ tenant components can call it unconditionally without crashing. **commitDraft routing**: provider holds an `OverrideWriter` per type (R-0 ships stub writers; R-1+ wires real platform-token writes through the dual-token client). Commit collects per-key results + errors, surfaces `RuntimeCommitOutcome { succeeded, failed, results, errors }`; all-success clears staged state, partial-failure retains staged for retry. **`setEditing(false)` clears per-element selection** so the next edit-mode entry starts clean. **`TenantProviders` extracted from App.tsx** at `frontend/src/lib/runtime-host/TenantProviders.tsx`. The 9-deep tenant context chain (Auth → FeatureFlag → Extension → Location → Layout → AuthDevice → Focus → CommandBar → CallContext → Tooltip) lifted into a shared module. App.tsx consumes it via `<TenantProviders>{children}</TenantProviders>` for the tenant boot path; the admin tree's runtime-host-test surface mounts the same chain. Single source of truth — adding a provider in App.tsx without adding it here would now break the runtime host (test gate). Inline `AuthDeviceProvider` (which bridges `useAuth()` → `DeviceProvider`) lives inside TenantProviders. The provider chain composes the same way as pre-R-0; no behavioral change to tenant boot path. **`renderTenantSlugRoutes` exported from App.tsx + `TenantRouteTree` re-export module.** Rather than a 1,300-line physical move (high tenant-operator regression risk), the tenant slug-set Routes block stays inside App.tsx but is wrapped in an exported function `renderTenantSlugRoutes()` — App.tsx still calls it inline (zero behavioral change) AND `frontend/src/lib/runtime-host/TenantRouteTree.tsx` re-exports it as a self-contained `<Routes>{renderTenantSlugRoutes()}</Routes>` component. Admin tree imports `TenantRouteTree` to mount tenant content. **Vite chunk splitting**: `RuntimeHostTestPage` is `React.lazy()`-loaded inside BridgeableAdminApp at `/_runtime-host-test/*`, so Vite emits a separate chunk that admin pages NOT visiting the runtime-host-test path don't pull. Build report shows the new `RuntimeHostTestPage-*.js` chunk at 2.42 kB / gzip 0.84 kB (separate from main bundle); main bundle delta vs pre-R-0 is +0.2% (5503 → 5514 kB), well within the 5% budget. **`/_runtime-host-test/*` admin route** (`bridgeable-admin/pages/RuntimeHostTestPage.tsx`). Path-based + subdomain-based entry parallel to visual editor pattern. Super_admin gate enforced inside the page (`useAdminAuth().user.role === "super_admin"` required); other admin roles see "Forbidden" copy. Loading / unauth / forbidden / shell states each carry a stable test-id. Render shell: warning ribbon at top (orange status-warning palette, 32px h-8 distinctive from tenant chrome) + tenant content below mounted in `<TenantProviders><Suspense><TenantRouteTree /></Suspense></TenantProviders>`. Hidden from VisualEditorIndex per spec. **Widget Editor "Dashboard Layouts" tab** ships at `/visual-editor/widgets` (extends existing 2-mode toggle to 3-mode: `individual / class / layouts`). Single-pane authoring: top toolbar with scope picker (platform_default / vertical_default / tenant_default) + vertical or tenant picker (gated by selected scope) + page_context dropdown (5 canonical contexts: dashboard / home / ops_board / vault_overview / financials) + source-trail badge ("source: Platform default" / "Vertical default (funeral_home)" / "Tenant default (abc12345)" / "Inherits in-code defaults"). Widget list with per-row position + size selector + enabled toggle + up/down arrows + remove button. Add-widget picker at bottom shows registered widgets not yet in the layout. Save / Discard buttons; autosave deferred (manual save semantics). Frontend service at `bridgeable-admin/services/dashboard-layouts-service.ts` mirrors `focus-compositions-service` shape (uses existing `adminApi` helper, not the new dual-token client — the existing visual editor surfaces stay on adminApi for R-0; the runtime editor proper consumes the dual-token client in R-1). **Tests shipped Phase R-0**: 23 backend tests in `test_dashboard_layouts_r0.py` across 5 test classes — TestServiceValidation × 6 (scope-key mismatch, invalid scope, empty page_context, layout_config must be list, widget_id required, duplicate widget_id), TestVersioning × 3 (initial v=1, resave bumps version + deactivates prior, update versions + replaces), TestInheritance × 5 (empty no rows, platform default resolves, vertical overrides platform, tenant overrides vertical, tenant in unauthored vertical falls back to platform), TestApiAdmin × 5 (list+create+resolve happy path, scope-key 400, anonymous 401, tenant token rejected 401 cross-realm, update existing layout), TestWidgetServiceIntegration × 2 (fresh user with no override inherits vertical_default, no authored layout falls back to in-code defaults), TestMigrationHead × 2 (table exists, columns present). 19 vitest tests in `dual-token-client.test.ts` across 4 describe blocks — classifier × 3 (platform / tenant / unknown paths), routing × 9 (platform read/write attaches PlatformUser token, tenant read attaches impersonation token, tenant POST/PATCH/DELETE all throw, unsupported path throws, missing platform token throws, missing impersonation token throws), helpers × 4 (path-prefix enforcement, platformGet routes correctly, tenantGet routes correctly), error-code × 3 (`getImpersonationToken` null/set behavior + DualTokenError code on tenant write attempt). 18 vitest tests in `edit-mode-context.test.tsx` across 6 describe blocks — stub × 2 (outside-provider returns stub, commitDraft on stub resolves empty), provider populates × 5 (tenantSlug + impersonatedUserId from props, initialMode toggle, initialPageContext, setEditing toggles, setEditing(false) clears selection), staging × 5 (stageOverride adds, re-staging same key replaces, differing prop on same target accumulates, clearStaged removes only matching, discardDraft drops without backend), commit routing × 2 (routes each type to writer, retains staged + surfaces commitError on partial failure), component consumption × 2 (provider state + responds to actions, stub when outside provider), overrideKey helper × 2. 4 Playwright smoke tests in `runtime-host-r0.spec.ts` (admin runtime-host-test page mounts; dashboard-layouts API rejects anonymous; resolve endpoint requires auth; Widget Editor exposes Dashboard Layouts mode toggle). **Final regression posture**: backend visual-editor regression **132/132 passing** (109 baseline + 23 new R-0); vitest **1394/1394 passing** (1357 baseline + 37 new R-0); tsc clean (only pre-existing `CalendarAccountsPage.tsx` `asChild` violation predates R-0); vite build clean (5.13s); RuntimeHostTestPage chunk separately split at 2.42 kB. **Migration head advances**: `r86_document_template_vertical_tier` → **`r87_dashboard_layouts`**. **Files added** (8): `backend/alembic/versions/r87_dashboard_layouts.py`, `backend/app/models/dashboard_layout.py`, `backend/app/services/dashboard_layouts/{__init__.py, layouts_service.py}`, `backend/app/api/routes/admin/visual_editor_dashboard_layouts.py`, `backend/tests/test_dashboard_layouts_r0.py`, `frontend/src/lib/runtime-host/{TenantProviders.tsx, TenantRouteTree.tsx, dual-token-client.ts, dual-token-client.test.ts, edit-mode-context.tsx, edit-mode-context.test.tsx}`, `frontend/src/bridgeable-admin/pages/RuntimeHostTestPage.tsx`, `frontend/src/bridgeable-admin/services/dashboard-layouts-service.ts`, `frontend/tests/e2e/runtime-host-r0.spec.ts`. **Files modified**: `backend/app/models/__init__.py` (DashboardLayout import + __all__), `backend/app/api/platform.py` (route registration), `backend/app/services/widgets/widget_service.py` (`_resolve_default_layout_config` helper + `get_user_layout` consumes new resolver), `frontend/src/App.tsx` (provider chain → TenantProviders; slug-set Routes wrapped in `renderTenantSlugRoutes` exported function; ~290 imports unchanged), `frontend/src/bridgeable-admin/BridgeableAdminApp.tsx` (lazy RuntimeHostTestPage + 2 new routes path-based + subdomain-based), `frontend/src/bridgeable-admin/pages/visual-editor/WidgetEditorPage.tsx` (3-mode toggle + DashboardLayoutsEditor inline + dashboard-layouts-service consumption), `frontend/package.json` (added `axios-mock-adapter@^2.1.0` for dual-token-client tests), `CLAUDE.md` (this entry). **Architectural patterns established this phase**: (1) **3-tier scope inheritance now consistent across visual editor data types** — themes / component_configurations / component_class_configurations / focus_compositions / workflow_templates / document_templates / **dashboard_layouts**. Same write-side versioning + READ-time resolution pattern across all seven. (2) **Dual-token contract** for runtime-aware editor sessions — PlatformUser token for writes, impersonation token for reads; cross-direction enforcement at frontend (interceptor) + backend (realm check). The strict no-fallback discipline (tenant writes throw pre-flight, unsupported paths throw, missing tokens throw with explicit `code` field) prevents accidental cross-talk + keeps debug-paths clear. (3) **EditMode contract is presence-of-provider, not opt-in flag** — the existing widget `_editMode` convention extends naturally; consumers call `useEditMode()` unconditionally + read `isEditing` to decide affordances. Outside-provider returns the stub. (4) **Shared-bundle refactor preserved tenant operator behavior exactly** — App.tsx's tenant boot path is structurally identical post-R-0 (provider chain composed by `<TenantProviders>` instead of inline; slug-set Routes inlined via `renderTenantSlugRoutes()` function call; same JSX produced). The admin tree mounts the same content via `<TenantRouteTree />`; the architectural goal (mountable from anywhere) is met without a 1,300-line physical movement. (5) **Lazy chunk split at React.lazy boundary in admin tree** — Vite emits separate chunk for `RuntimeHostTestPage` so admin pages NOT visiting the runtime-host-test path don't pull the lazy chunk's overhead. Main bundle delta is +0.2% (within 5% budget). True per-tree chunk separation (separate AdminApp + TenantApp Vite entries) is a follow-up optimization, not R-0 scope. (6) **Pragmatic refactor discipline (CLAUDE.md §12 Spec-Override)** — physically moving 290+ tenant page imports + 1,344 lines of Routes into TenantRouteTree.tsx was the literal spec instruction; chose the low-risk wrapper-export-from-App.tsx pattern instead. Same architectural outcome (tenant content mountable from anywhere via shared module), zero tenant-operator regression risk. Documented inline in App.tsx + TenantRouteTree.tsx so future sessions understand the choice. **What R-0 explicitly did NOT ship**: runtime editor UI (no click-to-edit, no inspector panel, no theme panel, no overlay layer); `data-component-name` auto-injection on registered components (R-1); tenant-component consumers of `useEditMode()` (R-1); operational-handler safety pass — `editMode === true` gating across 50-80 components (R-1.5); `page_overrides` table for page-level chrome (R-3); the actual runtime editor write-path wiring through the dual-token client (R-1; R-0 ships stub writers + the routing contract). **Sequencing forward**: R-0 foundation locked. R-1 implements the runtime editor proper — `data-component-name` auto-injection on `registerComponent`, click-to-edit gestures, inspector panel + theme panel + class panel reusing existing `CompactPropControl` + `PropControlDispatcher`, page-level edit-mode toggle, real `OverrideWriter` implementations through the dual-token client. R-2 extends component coverage to entity cards (DeliveryCard, AncillaryCard, OrderCard) + selection borders + drag handle UI. R-3 extends to page-chrome + form-layout editing with new `page_overrides` table. R-4 unifies `/visual-editor/*` and `/runtime-editor/*` into a single editor surface + performance + polish pass.

- **Admin Visual Editor — Focus Editor Preview Faithfulness for Funeral Scheduling (complete, 2026-06-03):** Replaces the Focus Editor preview pane's generic placeholder with a faithful representation of the Funeral Scheduling Focus when that template is selected. Editor preview now shows the kanban shell (driver lanes + DateBox header + delivery cards) using **real production sub-components** (DeliveryCard, DateBox) fed by mock data, alongside the live composition draft rendered through CompositionRenderer. Editor edits on the Composition tab propagate to the preview in real time without saving. **Pattern: Phase 2 stand-in canonical** — per the spec's "same pattern as Phase 2's preview harness for tokens" cue, the harness uses real visual atoms inside a structurally-faithful kanban shell rather than mounting `SchedulingKanbanCore` (1,714 LOC) with its full provider tree (`useFocus`, `useSchedulingFocus`, `dispatch-service` network calls, dnd-kit, URL params). Spec-Override Discipline scope-tightening per CLAUDE.md §12: the 1,714-LOC orchestrator's runtime dependencies aren't viable in editor context without binding preview to live API or duplicating substantial mock-provider machinery; the Phase 2 stand-in pattern is the canonical answer (matches `lib/visual-editor/themes/preview-data.tsx`'s structurally-faithful stand-ins). DeliveryCard + DateBox + composition substrate ARE real — same visual fidelity as production at the atomic level. **New harness module** at `frontend/src/bridgeable-admin/pages/visual-editor/focus-editor/`: (1) `mock-data/funeralSchedulingMockData.ts` (~270 LOC) — typed DTOs matching `dispatch-service.ts` (`DeliveryDTO` / `DeliveryTypeConfig` / `TenantTimeDTO` / `DriverDTO` / `ScheduleStateDTO`). Three sample scenarios — `default` (mid-week, 6 deliveries / 3 drivers), `high-volume` (busy day, 10 deliveries / 5 drivers), `empty` (no kanban content). All deceased family names use a `Sample {Surname}` pattern (Sample Anderson, Sample Brennan, etc.) for unambiguous "this is mock data" appearance in editor screenshots. Stable preview "today" = 2026-06-05. Realistic vault types + service types + cemeteries. Exports `buildMockBundle(scenario)` returning `{tenant_time, schedule, drivers, deliveries, pool_items, target_date, scenario}` plus `SAMPLE_SCENARIO_OPTIONS` for the picker. (2) `FuneralSchedulingPreviewHarness.tsx` (~340 LOC) — exports the harness component + `SampleScenarioPicker` + `compositionDraftAsResolved(placements, canvas_config, vertical)` helper. Wraps in `<DndContext onDragEnd={() => {}}>` so DeliveryCard's `useDraggable` hook fires events visually but mutations don't persist. Renders `PreviewHeader` (DateBox flanking H2 day label), `PreviewKanban` with `PreviewLane` components (220px wide, alphabetical by driver display name, Unassigned lane first with `border-accent/30` emphasis), DeliveryCard with `density="compact"` (production Focus uses this density), accessory rail to the right rendering `<CompositionRenderer composition={draft} editorMode={true} />` when the draft has placements. Empty kanban renders centered empty state `"No cases scheduled"`. **`compositionDraftAsResolved` helper** synthesizes a `ResolvedComposition` shape from the editor's draft state — `focus_type: "scheduling"`, `vertical: <selected>`, `tenant_id: null`, `source: "vertical_default"` when placements exist (so renderer treats it like a real resolution) or null when empty, `placements + canvas_config` flow through verbatim. **FocusEditorPage refactor**: lifted `draftPlacements` + `draftCanvasConfig` + load logic from `CompositionTab` into a new `useCompositionDraft(compositionFocusType, scope, vertical, tenantId)` custom hook called at the FocusEditorPage level. Hook returns a `CompositionDraftState` bundle (draftPlacements + setter, draftCanvasConfig + setter, activeRow + setter, resolved, isLoading, loadError, isSaving + setter, saveError + setter, hasUnsaved). Lifting was load-bearing: CompositionTab unmounts on tab switch (Composition → Preview Settings), so without lifting, the in-progress draft would reset every tab change. CompositionTab now consumes the bundle via a new `state` prop and stays purely presentational + handles save/palette/canvas interactions. `selectedIds` (multi-select for delete) stays local to CompositionTab — only relevant during composition edit, not across tabs. `FocusTemplatePreview` rewritten: dispatches on `template.metadata.name === "funeral-scheduling"` to render the harness with `compositionDraft={compositionDraftAsResolved(draftPlacements, draftCanvasConfig, vertical)}` synthesized from lifted state; other Focus templates (Triage, Arrangement Scribe + future templates) continue rendering the generic placeholder card with the existing accessory-layer placement summary. Tag explicitly notes "Faithful editor preview will land when this Focus core ships" so the deferred state is visible to operators. `tenantId` prop dropped from `FocusTemplatePreview` (was only used for the now-removed inline API resolve). **PreviewSettingsTab** extended with `scenario` + `setScenario` + `templateName` props; conditionally renders the `SampleScenarioPicker` (only for funeral-scheduling, gated on `templateName === "funeral-scheduling"`). Future per-template harnesses extend this gate as their cores ship — the picker is harness-shape-specific. New `scenario: SampleScenario` state added to FocusEditorPage with default `"default"`. **Tests shipped**: 16 new vitest tests in `FuneralSchedulingPreviewHarness.test.tsx` across 4 describe blocks — TestHarness × 7 (renders header + kanban; renders empty state for empty scenario; includes Unassigned lane; renders accessory rail when draft has placements; hides accessory when draft null; hides accessory when draft has zero placements; data-scenario attribute reflects active scenario), TestSampleScenarioPicker × 1 (renders all options + correct value), Test compositionDraftAsResolved × 2 (synthesizes ResolvedComposition correctly; null source for empty placements), TestMockData × 6 (default has kanban deliveries + drivers; high-volume strictly more than default; empty zero deliveries; preview today is stable 2026-06-05; driver display names present; Sample-prefix discipline asserts at least one Sample-prefixed family_name in default scenario). **Final regression**: tsc clean; vitest **1357/1357 passing** (1341 baseline + 16 new); vite build clean (4.93s).
