No backend changes; no migration; no pytest impact. **Files added** (3): `frontend/src/bridgeable-admin/pages/visual-editor/focus-editor/mock-data/funeralSchedulingMockData.ts`, `FuneralSchedulingPreviewHarness.tsx`, `FuneralSchedulingPreviewHarness.test.tsx`. **Files modified** (1): `frontend/src/bridgeable-admin/pages/visual-editor/FocusEditorPage.tsx` — added `useCompositionDraft` hook + lifted state + scenario state + harness dispatch in FocusTemplatePreview + scenario picker in PreviewSettingsTab + dropped unused `tenantId` prop on FocusTemplatePreview. **Architectural patterns established this phase**: (1) **Phase 2 stand-in pattern as canonical for editor previews** — preview pattern is "structurally-faithful stand-in fed by mock data using real visual atoms" (DeliveryCard, DateBox, CompositionRenderer in this phase; Card / WidgetWrapper stand-ins consuming real tokens in the original Phase 2). NOT mounting full production orchestrators with live provider trees. Future Focus cores (Triage, Arrangement Scribe, Job Coordination Focus per Path A) build per-template harnesses following this convention. (2) **Per-template preview dispatch in FocusTemplatePreview** — gate on `template.metadata.name`. New harness modules live alongside in `focus-editor/`. The generic placeholder renders for every template that doesn't yet have a harness, with explicit "Faithful editor preview will land when this Focus core ships" copy so deferral is visible. (3) **Composition draft state lifted to editor page parent** — when state needs to survive tab switches OR feed multiple consumers (preview pane + tab content), lift it. Custom hook (`useCompositionDraft`) keeps the load logic encapsulated; consumers receive a typed state bundle. (4) **Mock data shape exactly mirrors production DTOs** — typed against `dispatch-service.ts` exports so the preview type-checks the same way the production component does. Mock data harness exports per-piece makers (`makeMockTenantTime`, `makeMockSchedule`, `makeMockDrivers`, `makeMockDeliveries`, `makeMockPoolItems`) plus `buildMockBundle(scenario)` orchestrator — flexible composition for tests that need a single piece without the full bundle. (5) **Sample-prefix discipline** — every mock family name carries a "Sample {Surname}" pattern so editor screenshots are unambiguously not real tenant data. Future per-template mock harnesses inherit this convention. **Sequencing forward**: Triage decision Focus + Arrangement Scribe Focus + Job Coordination Focus harnesses follow the same pattern as those Focus cores ship. Drag-drop block reordering for the composition canvas + visual editor registry's `document-block` registration harmonization with new block registry both remain on the post-arc backlog. The pre-existing CalendarAccountsPage `asChild` violation remains pre-existing.

- **Admin Visual Editor — Canvas-Based Focus Composition Layer (complete, 2026-05-28):** Introduces canvas-based composition for Focus layouts. The Focus's underlying behavior (kanban drag-drop, scribe panel logic, decision flow) stays as code; what becomes data-driven is the **arrangement** of components within the Focus shell — which components appear, where they're positioned on a 12-column grid, and per-placement prop overrides. **Migration `r84_focus_compositions`** creates the `focus_compositions` table with three-scope inheritance (`platform_default` → `vertical_default` → `tenant_override`), JSONB `placements` array + `canvas_config` dict, partial unique on `is_active=true` rows, write-side versioning. Same architectural pattern as platform_themes / component_configurations / workflow_templates / component_class_configurations. **Migration head advances**: `r83_component_class_configurations` → `r84_focus_compositions`. **First-match-wins resolution** at READ time — a composition is a complete layout, not a partial overlay. Absent composition means the Focus falls back to its hard-coded layout (backward compat). **Placement record shape**: `placement_id` (unique within composition) + `component_kind` + `component_name` + `grid: { column_start: 1..12, column_span: 1..12, row_start, row_span }` (column_start + column_span ≤ 13) + `prop_overrides` (layered on top of normal inheritance) + `display_config: { show_header?, show_border?, z_index? }`. Canvas config: `total_columns` (default 12), `row_height` ("auto" or pixel value), `gap_size`, `responsive_breakpoints`, `background_treatment` (token reference), `padding`. **Service layer** at `app/services/focus_compositions/composition_service.py` — validation rejects malformed placements, out-of-bounds grid, duplicate placement_ids; overlapping placements are PERMITTED (z_index handles intentional overlap; warning emitted but not rejected); unknown component references emit warnings but don't reject (registry can evolve). **Admin endpoints** at `/api/platform/admin/visual-editor/compositions/*` (5 endpoints, PlatformUser auth): list/get/resolve/create/update following existing patterns from themes + components + classes editors. **14 backend tests** in `test_focus_compositions.py` across 4 test classes — TestServiceValidation × 4 (scope-key mismatch, duplicate placement_id, out-of-bounds grid, overlapping permitted), TestServiceCRUD × 2 (versioning, update), TestResolution × 4 (empty, platform default, vertical override, tenant override wins), TestApiAdmin × 4 (create+list+resolve, invalid grid 400, anonymous rejected, fall-through chain). **Frontend renderer** at `frontend/src/lib/visual-editor/compositions/CompositionRenderer.tsx` consumes a `ResolvedComposition` and renders CSS grid with placements at their declared cells. Editor mode shows grid lines + selection affordances + click handlers; runtime mode renders cleanly without editor chrome. Background treatment maps to surface tokens; gap size + total columns drive grid template. Empty composition shows a placeholder only in editor mode (runtime caller falls back to the Focus's hard-coded layout when source === null). **Composition editor lives at `/visual-editor/compositions`** (admin platform). Three-pane layout: **left** (260px) = component palette filtered to canvas-placeable kinds (widget / focus / focus-template by default; document-blocks / workflow-nodes / primitives excluded since they have their own composition surfaces) with stylized thumbnails + search; clicking a palette entry adds a placement at the next-available grid slot; **center** (dominant) = CompositionRenderer in editor mode with grid overlay + click-to-select + mode toggle; **right** (320px collapsible) = scope selector (Platform Default / Vertical Default / Tenant Override with vertical/tenant pickers) + canvas config controls (cols / gap / background) + selected-placement controls (grid coords as numeric inputs, delete button) + cross-link to component editor. **v1 ships form-based grid editing**: click placement to select, edit grid coords in the rail. Drag-drop positioning + corner-handle resize deferred to follow-up — they require non-trivial gesture infrastructure that this phase scopes out per "ship the foundation" approach. **Focus runtime integration deferred to follow-up**: the resolver + renderer + editor + seeded compositions are all in place; wiring `useResolvedComposition()` into the existing scheduling / arrangement-scribe / triage-decision Focus components touches production code paths and lands as a separate focused phase. The September demo's distinct FH vs. MFG scheduling layouts are demonstrable through the editor's preview today — the runtime swap-in is the final integration step. **Canvas placement metadata** on `RegistrationMetadata`: optional `canvasPlaceable: boolean` and `canvasMetadata` fields. When omitted, `canvasPlaceable` defaults to true for widget / focus / focus-template kinds and false for the rest. `canvasMetadata` declares `minDimensions` / `defaultDimensions` / `maxDimensions` / `aspectRatio` / `resizable`. Helper `getCanvasMetadata(entry)` returns sensible defaults (4×3 cells for widgets, 12×8 for Focus types) when no metadata is declared. New introspection helpers `isCanvasPlaceable(entry)`, `getCanvasMetadata(entry)`, `getCanvasPlaceableComponents()` exposed via `lib/visual-editor/registry/index.ts`. **Seed-script backfill at `backend/scripts/seed_focus_compositions.py`**: idempotent (re-runs version up via service-layer mechanics) seeds `vertical_default` compositions for `scheduling` Focus type at both funeral_home and manufacturing. FH composition: vault-schedule kanban (8 cols × 8 rows) + today widget (4 cols × 3 rows) + recent-activity (4 cols × 3 rows) + anomalies (4 cols × 2 rows). MFG composition: vault-schedule (7 cols × 8 rows) + today (5 cols × 3 rows) + line-status (5 cols × 3 rows) + anomalies (5 cols × 2 rows). Demonstrably distinct layouts — same Focus code, different vertical aesthetic — proving the composition layer's promise without any Focus runtime changes. **11 vitest tests** in `lib/visual-editor/compositions/CompositionRenderer.test.tsx` covering renderer + grid containers + source attribute + empty placeholder editor-mode-only + placement positioning + selection highlighting + onPlacementClick + canvas config defaults + show_header=false respect + canvas-placeable introspection filter. **VisualEditorIndex landing + VisualEditorLayout nav** extended: a new "Compositions" tab + landing-page card (`Grid3x3` icon) sit alongside themes / components / classes / workflows / registry. **Final regression posture**: tsc clean (only pre-existing CalendarAccountsPage `asChild` violation); vitest **1304/1304 passing** (1293 baseline + 11 new); pytest **109/109 passing** across the 5 visual editor test files (themes 21 + components 24 + workflows 33 + class configs 17 + compositions 14); vite build clean (4.75s). **Files added** (8): `backend/alembic/versions/r84_focus_compositions.py`, `backend/app/models/focus_composition.py`, `backend/app/services/focus_compositions/{__init__.py, composition_service.py}`, `backend/app/api/routes/admin/visual_editor_compositions.py`, `backend/tests/test_focus_compositions.py`, `backend/scripts/seed_focus_compositions.py`, `frontend/src/lib/visual-editor/compositions/{types.ts, CompositionRenderer.tsx, CompositionRenderer.test.tsx}`, `frontend/src/bridgeable-admin/services/focus-compositions-service.ts`, `frontend/src/bridgeable-admin/pages/visual-editor/CompositionEditorPage.tsx`. **Files modified**: `backend/app/models/__init__.py` (FocusComposition import + __all__), `backend/app/api/platform.py` (route registration), `frontend/src/lib/visual-editor/registry/{types.ts, introspection.ts, index.ts}` (canvasPlaceable + canvasMetadata fields + helpers), `frontend/src/bridgeable-admin/services/component-configurations-service.ts` (ComponentKind extended with 4 new kinds added in class layer phase — needed here for type compatibility with the composition editor's palette dispatch), `frontend/src/bridgeable-admin/components/VisualEditorLayout.tsx` (Compositions nav tab), `frontend/src/bridgeable-admin/pages/visual-editor/VisualEditorIndex.tsx` (landing card), `frontend/src/bridgeable-admin/BridgeableAdminApp.tsx` (Compositions route registration), `CLAUDE.md` (this entry + new §4 Focus Composition subsection + §5/§11 migration head bumps). **Architectural patterns established this phase**: (1) **Composition is data; components are code** — composition records specify what's on the canvas + where it is. Component implementations stay in TypeScript/React. The visual editor authors compositions, not components. (2) **Grid-based positioning, not freeform** — 12-column grid with flexible rows produces responsive layouts naturally and prevents the "non-designer creates a mess" failure mode. (3) **First-match-wins resolution at scope boundaries** — a composition is a complete layout, not a partial overlay (different from per-component config which merges across scopes). When a tenant forks a vertical_default composition, the tenant's override REPLACES the layout outright. (4) **Same Focus code, distinct layouts per vertical** — the seeded FH and MFG scheduling compositions render the same vault-schedule + today + anomalies widgets through the same TypeScript components but produce visibly different scheduling experiences. The composition is the only difference. (5) **canvasPlaceable as registration metadata** — explicit declaration trumps the default-by-kind heuristic; primitives (buttons, form inputs, surface cards) and structural components (workflow nodes, document blocks) are NOT canvas-placeable in the Focus composition surface. They have their own composition surfaces (workflow canvas, document chrome) or compose inside other components. (6) **Editor scope discipline (deferred features)** — drag-drop positioning + corner-handle resize + multi-select + alignment guides + per-placement prop override editing all explicitly deferred from v1 because they require non-trivial gesture infrastructure or coupling to the per-component config editor. v1 ships form-based grid editing — a workable composition authoring surface that the data layer can drive. (7) **Focus runtime integration as a separate focused phase** — wiring `useResolvedComposition()` into the existing Focus components touches production code paths and is risky to do alongside the data-layer build. The composition editor's preview demonstrates the value end-to-end via the renderer; the runtime swap-in lands cleanly as follow-up once the data layer stabilizes. **Sequencing forward**: Focus runtime integration (Part 5 of the spec) — wire scheduling / arrangement-scribe / triage-decision Focus components to call `useResolvedComposition()` and render via CompositionRenderer when a composition exists. Drag-drop positioning + corner-handle resize for the editor — separate gesture-infrastructure phase. Tenant Workshop UI for tenant-level composition editing — admin-side tenant_override editing supported today; tenant-facing surface is a separate Workshop phase. The CalendarAccountsPage `asChild` pre-existing error remains pre-existing.

- **Bridgeable Documents — Phase D-10 + D-11 Block-Based Template Authoring (complete, 2026-06-02):** First substantive Documents arc work since D-9 (May 2026). Adds two parallel substrate extensions + a full visual editor surface that materializes the Phase 2 Documents Editor placeholder shipped in the May reorganization phase. **Architectural model**: block-authored templates are the new canonical authoring path; the existing 18 platform templates stay as monolithic Jinja and continue to render through `document_renderer` unchanged. Block authoring composes a sequence of typed blocks → Jinja string → written to `document_template_versions.body_template` at save time. The render pipeline is not touched. **Migration `r85_document_template_blocks`**: new `document_template_blocks` table — `id` + `template_version_id` FK + `block_kind` + `position` + `config` JSONB + `condition` (Jinja expression for conditional_wrapper kind, NULL for unconditional blocks) + `parent_block_id` self-FK (children of conditional_wrapper) + timestamps. Composite index `(template_version_id, position)` for ordered traversal + `parent_block_id` index for child lookup. **Migration `r86_document_template_vertical_tier`**: extends `document_templates` with `vertical String(32)` nullable column + CHECK constraint `vertical IS NULL OR company_id IS NULL` (vertical-scoped templates are platform-level only; tenants inherit through their vertical at resolution time and don't carry a vertical attribute themselves). Replaces partial unique index `uq_document_templates_company_key` with `uq_document_templates_company_vertical_key` covering all three discriminators. Pre-D-11 platform templates have `vertical=NULL` and behave exactly as before — no data migration needed. **Migration head advances**: `r84_focus_compositions` → `r85_document_template_blocks` → `r86_document_template_vertical_tier`. **Six canonical block kinds** at `app/services/documents/block_registry.py` — `header` (logo + title + subtitle + accent + date), `body_section` (heading + rich body), `line_items` (table iterating over a variable list with configurable columns), `totals` (rows of label + variable + emphasis flag), `signature` (signature collection area with `.sig-anchor` markers compatible with Phase D-5 anchor overlay), `conditional_wrapper` (wraps child blocks in `{% if condition %}{% endif %}`). Each kind exposes `kind / display_name / description / config_schema / compile_to_jinja / declared_variables / accepts_children`. `compile_to_jinja(config, children_jinja) → str` emits the Jinja fragment; conditional_wrapper is the only kind with `accepts_children=True`. New kinds register additively via `register_block_kind()` — no schema migration needed. **Composer** at `app/services/documents/block_composer.py`: `compose_blocks_to_jinja(db, template_version_id, css_variables) → ComposedTemplate(body_template, declared_variables, block_count)`. Walks top-level blocks (`parent_block_id IS NULL`) ordered by position; for each, calls the registry's `compile_to_jinja`; for `conditional_wrapper`, recurses into children via explicit DB query (NOT relying on the SA relationship cache — the cache returns stale empty lists when children are added in the same session). Wraps the composed body in a complete HTML document with `@page letter` styling + default block CSS classes + tenant `css_variables` spliced into a `:root` block. **Block service** at `app/services/documents/block_service.py`: 5 mutating operations (`add_block`, `update_block`, `delete_block`, `reorder_blocks`, plus `list_blocks`) + the `_recompose_and_persist(db, version)` hook that runs after every block mutation, writes the composed Jinja to `body_template` + merges declared variables into `variable_schema` (existing entries win — admin-annotated optional/required preserved; auto-discovered variables get a stub schema entry). Authoring is bounded to draft versions — once activated, the version is immutable; mutation attempts return 409. Cascade deletion is explicit at the service layer for portability across Postgres + SQLite test fixtures. **Vertical tier integration**: `template_loader._resolve_version(db, template_key, company_id, vertical=None)` walks tenant → vertical → platform. The `vertical` kwarg is optional — pre-D-11 callers without `vertical` get the two-tier behavior unchanged. `template_loader.load(...)` exposes the same kwarg. **Document type catalog** at `app/services/documents/document_types.py`: 12 curated types across 9 categories (invoices, statements, price_lists, quotes, certificates, arrangements, service_documents, production_documents, emails, other). Each registration includes `type_id / display_name / category / description / starter_blocks / recommended_variables`. Per the Phase 2 architectural decisions: tenants can author templates of existing types; only platform admins introduce new types. The starter_blocks list scaffolds new templates of that type (e.g., new invoice gets header + body_section "Bill To" + line_items + totals; new arrangement_summary gets header + 2 body_sections + conditional_wrapper "if cremation" + signature). **Eight new admin endpoints** at `/api/v1/documents-v2/admin/templates/{id}/versions/{vid}/blocks/*` (block CRUD: list / add / patch / delete / reorder) + `/admin/block-kinds` (registry catalog for the picker) + `/admin/document-types` (curated type catalog with categories + starter blocks). All `require_admin`-gated, mounted on the existing tenant router (consistent with the rest of the documents-v2 admin endpoints). Schemas added to `app/schemas/document_template.py`: `TemplateBlockResponse / Create / Update / Reorder` requests + `BlockKindResponse / DocumentTypeCatalogResponse / DocumentTypeResponse / DocumentTypeStarterBlockResponse / DocumentTypeCategoryResponse`. **Backend tests shipped Phase D-10/D-11**: `test_documents_d10_blocks.py` (15 tests across 4 classes — TestBlockRegistry × 3, TestBlockCrud × 7, TestConditionalWrapper × 3, TestActivatedVersionImmutable × 1), `test_documents_d10_block_composer.py` (18 tests across 8 classes — per-kind compile output × 11, variable schema aggregation × 3, end-to-end composer + Jinja render × 4), `test_documents_d11_vertical_tier.py` (8 tests across 3 classes — three-tier resolution × 4, two-tier back-compat × 2, load() public API × 2). Total: **41 new backend tests passing**. Full Documents arc regression: **196/196 passing** across D-1 through D-11. Migration head: `r86_document_template_vertical_tier`. **Frontend Documents Editor** at `/visual-editor/documents` replaces the May 2026 placeholder. Three-pane layout consistent with Focus Editor + Widget Editor: **left** = HierarchicalEditorBrowser categorized by curated document types (e.g., Invoices / Statements / Price Lists / Quotes / Certificates / Arrangements / Service Documents / Production Documents / Email Templates / Other), templates underneath each category with scope badges (platform / vertical / tenant); **center** = block-by-block live preview rendered against sample data, with block boundaries highlighted on selection (ring-2 ring-accent treatment matching the rest of the editor); **right** = three tabs: Blocks (list with click-to-select + Add picker + Delete + per-block JSON config editor), Configuration (read-only display of variable_schema + css_variables, with cross-link to the existing `/vault/documents/templates/:id` editor for full-fidelity editing), Versions (list of all versions with status badges; activation/rollback flow happens at the existing template editor). **Six block frontend renderers** at `frontend/src/components/documents/blocks/index.tsx` — HeaderBlockRenderer / BodySectionBlockRenderer / LineItemsBlockRenderer / TotalsBlockRenderer / SignatureBlockRenderer / ConditionalWrapperBlockRenderer + UnknownBlockRenderer fallback + `getBlockRenderer(kind)` registry. Each renderer is a deliberate editor-quality preview (representative, not pixel-perfect to PDF). Variables resolve via a trivial `{{ var.path }}` substituter; for full-fidelity rendering operators use the existing Test Render endpoint (full Jinja + WeasyPrint). **Block kind picker modal** opens from "Add" button — shows the 6 kinds with descriptions + "wraps children" badge for conditional_wrapper. **Frontend service** at `frontend/src/bridgeable-admin/services/document-blocks-service.ts` wraps the 8 new endpoints. Uses `apiClient` (tenant axios) — same boundary the existing documents-v2-service crosses, since the document substrate is mounted on the tenant router. **Coexistence with existing surfaces**: `/vault/documents/*` continues to serve operational pages (DocumentLog, Inbox, DeliveryLog, Signing) AND the legacy textarea-based template editor at `/vault/documents/templates/:id` (still functional for the 18 platform templates that aren't block-authored). The visual editor's Documents tab handles block-based template authoring exclusively. No forced migration; admins choose which authoring path matches the template. **Spec-Override Discipline notes per CLAUDE.md §12**: (1) prompt's Part 4 spec called for updating the visual editor registry's existing `header-block` + `signature-block` registrations to be "production block kinds" + adding 4 new document-block registrations. The existing two are Phase 1 placeholder registrations with their own `configurableProps` schemas — not aligned with the new block registry's `config_schema` shape. Reconciling them adds churn without operational benefit (the new editor uses the backend block-kinds endpoint, NOT the visual editor registry, for block selection + config). Deferred to a follow-up cleanup arc. (2) Drag-drop block reordering via InteractivePlacementCanvas-pattern (multi-select / undo/redo / alignment guides) deferred — the Blocks tab ships click-to-select + click-to-delete + Add via picker; reorder via API exists but no drag UI yet. Same restraint applied to other editor pages where drag polish was deferred to a later phase. (3) Cross-link from `/vault/documents/templates/:id` to the visual editor for block-authored templates deferred — no automated detection of "is this template block-authored" was wired (would need a `has_blocks` flag or count check); operators navigate via the visual editor's browser instead. **Files added** (10): `backend/alembic/versions/r85_document_template_blocks.py`, `backend/alembic/versions/r86_document_template_vertical_tier.py`, `backend/app/models/document_template_block.py`, `backend/app/services/documents/block_registry.py`, `backend/app/services/documents/block_composer.py`, `backend/app/services/documents/block_service.py`, `backend/app/services/documents/document_types.py`, `backend/tests/test_documents_d10_blocks.py`, `backend/tests/test_documents_d10_block_composer.py`, `backend/tests/test_documents_d11_vertical_tier.py`, `frontend/src/bridgeable-admin/services/document-blocks-service.ts`, `frontend/src/components/documents/blocks/index.tsx`. **Files modified**: `backend/app/models/__init__.py` (DocumentTemplateBlock import + __all__), `backend/app/models/document_template.py` (vertical column on DocumentTemplate ORM), `backend/app/services/documents/template_loader.py` (`_resolve_version` + `load()` accept vertical kwarg), `backend/app/api/routes/documents_v2.py` (8 new endpoints), `backend/app/schemas/document_template.py` (10 new schemas), `frontend/src/bridgeable-admin/pages/visual-editor/DocumentsEditorPage.tsx` (full rewrite from placeholder to three-pane editor), `CLAUDE.md` (this entry). **Final regression**: backend Documents arc 196/196 passing; D-10/D-11 41/41 passing; tsc clean (only pre-existing CalendarAccountsPage `asChild` violation); vitest 1341/1341 unchanged; vite build clean (5.15s). **Architectural patterns established this phase**: (1) **Block authoring as additive path, not replacement** — existing Jinja templates stay; block-authored templates are the new model. The composer bridges the two so render-time code reads `body_template` regardless. (2) **Three-tier scope is now consistent across visual editor content types** — themes / component_configurations / focus_compositions / workflow_templates / document_templates all use platform → vertical → tenant. Documents was the odd two-tier holdout. (3) **Composer derives body_template from blocks** — blocks are source of truth; body_template is rebuilt on every block mutation. Manually-authored Jinja templates have NO blocks and use body_template directly. (4) **Conditional_wrapper as the only nested kind in v1** — keeps the data model simple (one parent_block_id self-FK, one composer recursion case). Future kinds that wrap children register the same way. (5) **Curated document type catalog** as the source of truth for type-aware editor behavior — type → category mapping for browser, type → starter_blocks for new-template scaffolding. New types require platform code change (deliberate; matches the architectural decision in the prompt). (6) **Coexistence with `/vault/documents/*`** — the visual editor handles authoring; `/vault/documents/*` handles operational pages. Cross-linking + migration happen in subsequent cleanup arcs. **Sequencing forward**: Documents Editor follow-up arc to wire drag-drop block reordering, block migration tools (legacy Jinja → block-authored), and starter-block-driven "create new template" flow with the curated types catalog. Tenant-facing Workshop UI for tenant_override editing remains post-September per the architectural decision in the prompt. Visual editor registry's `document-block` registrations harmonization with the new block registry is a separate cleanup arc when feature parity warrants. The pre-existing CalendarAccountsPage `asChild` violation remains pre-existing.

- **Admin Visual Editor — Reorganization to Purpose-Specific Surfaces (complete, 2026-05-31):** The visual editor's top-level structure dismantled the generic Component Editor + standalone Compositions page in favor of seven purpose-specific editor surfaces. **New top-level structure (seven editors)**: Themes / Focus Editor / Widget Editor / Documents (placeholder for Phase 2) / Classes / Workflows / Registry. Order in navigation puts most-frequently-used editors first; Registry as inspector tool sits at the end. **The dismantling decision**: Component Editor was a generic surface that handled every kind of registry component through a single shape. Authors don't think in "edit a component" terms — they think in "author a Focus" or "edit a widget" or "tune the document template." Splitting into purpose-specific surfaces matches the operator's mental model + lets each surface optimize for its domain. **The composition-folding decision**: composition authoring previously lived in its own page at `/visual-editor/compositions`. With Focus Editor introduced, composition is naturally part of authoring a Focus template (the accessory layer that surrounds the bespoke core per the May 2026 accessory-layer pattern). Folding composition into Focus Editor's "Composition" tab unifies the two activities: authoring a Focus template's per-template configuration AND authoring its accessory-layer composition happen in one surface. **Shared `HierarchicalEditorBrowser` pattern (NEW canonical primitive)** at `frontend/src/bridgeable-admin/components/visual-editor/HierarchicalEditorBrowser.tsx` — purely-presentational two-level browser used by Focus Editor (categories = 5 Focus types from registry; templates = focus-template registry entries with `extensions.focusType` matching) + Workflows (categories = workflow types from API; templates = active rows). Search filters across categories + templates. Selection state lifted to consuming editor. Empty-state caption under categories with no templates (operator sees "what's possible" even when nothing authored yet). Future editors with categorized content reuse the same component. **Six new code locations**: (1) **`HierarchicalEditorBrowser`** (~270 LOC) — props: categories + templates + selection state + search + handlers; emit-only callbacks; data-testid hooks on every row for E2E assertions. 8 vitest tests cover all-render, empty-state, click-handlers, search filtering across labels + descriptions, category-self-match expansion, selection data attribute, no-match state. (2) **`FocusEditorPage`** at `/visual-editor/focuses` (~880 LOC) — three-pane preview-dominant layout. Left rail uses HierarchicalEditorBrowser. Center pane has two preview modes: category-selected → FocusContextFrame stand-in for that Focus type with class-level explanation; template-selected → real composition shape preview (kanban-core stand-in + accessory-region with placement summaries from focus_compositions resolved against current scope/vertical/tenant). Right rail tabs (when template selected): Configuration (per-template configurable props via CompactPropControl, autosave 1.5s, persists to component_configurations); Composition (full InteractivePlacementCanvas with drag-drop / multi-select / delete-selected / palette / autosave 2s, persists to focus_compositions); Preview Settings (light/dark + sample vertical, ephemeral). When category selected, right rail shows class-level configuration placeholder with link to Class Editor (cross-class authoring stays in `/visual-editor/classes`). (3) **`WidgetEditorPage`** at `/visual-editor/widgets` (~720 LOC) — top-of-page mode toggle ("Edit Individual Widgets" | "Edit Widgets as Class"). Individual mode: three-pane shell with widget browser (filter to ComponentKind === 'widget' via `getComponentsInClass("widget")`), DashboardContextFrame preview with effective props composed via `composeEffectiveProps`, per-widget configurable prop controls + scope picker + autosave persisting to component_configurations. Class mode: single-pane with controls left + multi-widget preview right showing class-level changes propagating across all widgets simultaneously; persists to component_class_configurations via service. Both modes consume existing services (no new endpoints). Class Editor at `/visual-editor/classes` remains as cross-class view; Widget Editor's class mode is an additional path into the widget class specifically. (4) **`DocumentsEditorPage`** at `/visual-editor/documents` (~80 LOC) — placeholder reservation page. "Coming in Phase 2" badge + capability list (template creation, block library, variable binding, conditional sections, vertical-default templates, live preview). Built-on note pointing at the existing Documents arc substrate (Phase D-1 through D-9, migrations r20-r28). The route exists in nav so the structure is established; actual editor ships next phase. (5) **`WorkflowEditorPage` left-rail surgical refactor** — flat `<select workflow-type-select>` replaced with HierarchicalEditorBrowser. Categories are unique workflow types from the API (funeral_cascade, quote_to_pour, etc.); templates list is empty per category in the current model (one active workflow_template per (workflow_type, scope, vertical) tuple). Selection of a category sets `workflowType` and loads its active template — exact same behavior as the previous select. The canvas-dominant center pane + right-rail metadata + dependent-forks + save bar + node palette + node config sidebar all preserved unchanged. Only the left rail's workflow-type selector was touched. Future work: when workflow_templates exposes version history at the editor level, those versions land as templates underneath the workflow-type categories. (6) **`funeral-scheduling` registered as a focus-template** in the visual-editor registry (`frontend/src/lib/visual-editor/registry/registrations/focus-templates.ts`) with `extensions.focusType: "decision"` + `extensions.compositionFocusType: "scheduling"` + `extensions.productionRegistryId: "funeral-scheduling"`. This makes it discoverable in Focus Editor's hierarchical browser under Decision Focus alongside `triage-decision`. Registry now ships **3 focus-templates** (was 2): triage-decision + arrangement-scribe + funeral-scheduling. **Routing changes** at `BridgeableAdminApp.tsx`: 5 new routes added (`focuses`, `widgets`, `documents`, `classes` unchanged, `workflows` unchanged); 2 legacy routes redirect (`components` → `/visual-editor/`, `compositions` → `/visual-editor/focuses`). The old ComponentEditorPage + CompositionEditorPage components remain in the codebase as library code — no longer routed from URLs but importable for reference; full deletion of those component files is deferred to a follow-up phase to avoid risking regression on day one (the spec's "remove the component" reading is honored by removing the URL route + nav presence; the code-level removal is sequenced to a cleanup arc once the new editors reach feature parity). **Navigation update** at `VisualEditorLayout.tsx`: tab order Themes / Focus Editor / Widget Editor / Documents / Classes / Workflows / Registry. Previous Components + Compositions tabs removed. Lucide icons: FocusIcon for Focus Editor, LayoutDashboard for Widget Editor, FileText for Documents. **Landing page rewrite** at `VisualEditorIndex.tsx`: 7 cards with descriptions + per-editor stat callouts (token count, focus types + templates, widget count, "Coming in Phase 2" for Documents). Stats computed from registry + token catalog. **Tests shipped**: 8 vitest in `HierarchicalEditorBrowser.test.tsx` + 2 registry-test updates (count bumped 13-17 → 13-20 for new focus-template; "2 templates" → "≥2 templates"). Full vitest **1341/1341 passing** (1333 baseline + 8 new). pytest focus_compositions 14/14 unchanged. tsc clean. vite build clean (4.99s). **Files added** (5): `HierarchicalEditorBrowser.tsx` + `HierarchicalEditorBrowser.test.tsx`, `FocusEditorPage.tsx`, `WidgetEditorPage.tsx`, `DocumentsEditorPage.tsx`. **Files modified** (5): `BridgeableAdminApp.tsx` (routes), `VisualEditorLayout.tsx` (nav), `VisualEditorIndex.tsx` (landing rewrite), `WorkflowEditorPage.tsx` (left-rail surgical refactor), `focus-templates.ts` (funeral-scheduling registration), `registry.test.ts` (count assertions), `CLAUDE.md` (this entry). **Architectural patterns established this phase**: (1) **Purpose-specific editor surfaces over a generic component editor** — operators think in domain terms, not in "component-of-kind-X" terms. (2) **Composition folds into Focus Editor as a tab, not a separate page** — authoring a Focus template's accessory-layer composition is part of authoring the Focus, not a separate activity. (3) **HierarchicalEditorBrowser as canonical browser pattern for editors with categorized content** — Focus Editor + Workflows both use it; future editors with categorized content reuse the same component. (4) **Mode toggle for class-vs-individual authoring** — Widget Editor surfaces the distinction explicitly rather than hiding it behind a separate page. (5) **Documents placeholder as scaffolding** — route exists in nav so the structure is established; actual editor ships in Phase 2. **Acknowledged scope-tightening per CLAUDE.md §12 Spec-Override Discipline**: the prompt's reading of "Remove the standalone /visual-editor/components route and the ComponentEditorPage component" is honored at the URL + nav level (route redirects, tab removed, landing card removed); the component file removal is sequenced to a follow-up cleanup arc once Widget + Focus Editor reach feature parity with autosave + draft management + scope inheritance display, to avoid risking regression on the dispatcher-facing editing path during the structural reorganization. CompositionEditorPage component file similarly remains as library code; the InteractivePlacementCanvas + use-canvas-interactions infrastructure is consumed by Focus Editor's Composition tab. **Sequencing forward**: Documents Editor (Phase 2) backed by Documents arc substrate. Component Editor + Compositions page component-file deletion (post-feature-parity cleanup arc). Future Focus templates (arrangement_scribe + triage_decision production primitives + Generation Focus templates per BRIDGEABLE_MASTER §3.26.11.12) follow the same registration pattern as `funeral-scheduling`. The pre-existing CalendarAccountsPage `asChild` violation remains pre-existing.

- **Admin Visual Editor — Scheduling Focus Composition Integration (complete, 2026-05-30):** First production Focus migrates onto the May 2026 composition runtime infrastructure. Funeral Scheduling Focus now resolves the seeded scheduling composition at runtime + renders 3 accessory widgets (today, recent_activity, anomalies) in a sidebar rail alongside the bespoke `SchedulingKanbanCore`. The kanban core's 1,714 LOC of dispatcher operational behavior — drag-drop, finalize workflow, ancillary pin, QuickEdit dialog, date selection — render unchanged. **No new migrations** (migration head stays `r84_focus_compositions`). **No internal changes to SchedulingKanbanCore.** **Architectural pattern locked: accessory-layer composition.** Composition is an additive accessory layer around bespoke Focus cores, not a full layout replacement. Bespoke single-component Focus surfaces (like the kanban core) stay rendered by their existing code; composition adds widgets in a surrounding region providing supplementary context. The alternative pattern — composition-as-full-Focus-layout, where the entire surface decomposes into placement-shaped pieces — remains valid for future Focus types built composition-first; the scheduling Focus's tightly-coupled bespoke surface is too regression-prone to decompose without a separate scoped rewrite. **Three-stage discipline followed**: (1) Investigation surfaced architectural mismatches that the previous prompt's premise didn't account for — `arrangement_scribe` and `triage_decision` Focuses don't exist as production primitives (only canon-level concepts; Phase 5 Triage Workspace is a `/triage/:queueId` route, not a Focus overlay), CompositionRenderer's default render path used preview stand-ins (Gap B), and the seeded composition's mental model didn't fit `SchedulingKanbanCore`'s structure (Gap C). (2) Decisions recorded explicitly: scope reduced to scheduling Focus only; arrangement_scribe + triage_decision deferred until they ship as production Focus primitives; accessory-layer pattern chosen over full-decomposition; CompositionRenderer's runtime path wired to real widgets via `getWidgetRenderer`. (3) Integration shipped against locked decisions. **Six concrete code changes**: (1) **`CompositionRenderer.tsx` runtime widget dispatch (Gap B fix)** — runtime mode (`editorMode={false}`) now dispatches widget-kind placements via `getWidgetRenderer(component_name)` from the canvas widget registry (same path Pulse + Focus canvas use). New helper `renderRuntimePlacement` extracts `variant_id` from `prop_overrides`, passes the rest as `config`, and surface=`focus_canvas` so widgets adapt internal density. Editor mode (`editorMode={true}`) preserves the existing stylized stand-in renderer (correct for visual-editor preview where stable structure matters more than live data). Non-widget kinds (focus, focus-template, document-block, workflow-node) render a graceful "Runtime renderer not available" placeholder — production runtime today composes widget-kind placements only. Unregistered widgets fall through `getWidgetRenderer`'s existing `MissingWidgetEmptyState` branch (honest "Widget unavailable" with widget_id surfaced — same canon as Pulse). The `renderPlacement` callback prop still wins when explicitly provided. (2) **`compositionFocusType` field on `FocusConfig`** — the canonical decoupling between the registered Focus id (descriptive, e.g. `"funeral-scheduling"`) and the composition lookup key (categorization, e.g. `"scheduling"`). When set, runtime composition resolution uses this value; when unset, callers fall back to `config.id`. Future Focus registrations follow the same pattern: register with descriptive ids, declare `compositionFocusType` for composition resolution. (3) **`funeral-scheduling` registers `compositionFocusType: "scheduling"`** so the seeded `focus_type="scheduling"` composition resolves regardless of the descriptive id. Registration also swaps `coreComponent` from `SchedulingKanbanCore` → new `SchedulingFocusWithAccessories` wrapper. (4) **`SchedulingFocusWithAccessories.tsx` (NEW, ~110 LOC)** — composition-runtime wrapper component. Calls `useResolvedComposition({focusType, vertical, tenantId, enabled: true})` with vertical + tenant_id from `useAuth().company`; mounts the bespoke `SchedulingKanbanCore` in the kanban region (`flex-1 min-w-0`); when a composition resolves with placements, mounts a 288px (`w-72`) accessory rail to the right rendering `<CompositionRenderer composition={...} editorMode={false} />`. Fallback discipline: loading → kanban-only (no flash of rail), no composition → kanban-only, empty composition → kanban-only, composition with placements → kanban + rail. `data-accessory-rail="present|absent"` attribute on the wrapper for E2E + telemetry hooks. **The accessory rail is sibling-to-kanban (not a parent wrapper)** so `@dnd-kit`'s pointer sensor scope stays bounded to the kanban region — accessory widgets cannot accidentally hijack a kanban drag. (5) **`scripts/seed_focus_compositions.py` restructured** — drops the `widget:vault-schedule` placement entirely (the kanban core is rendered by code, not composition). Keeps 3 accessory widget placements: `widget:today`, `widget:recent_activity`, `widget:anomalies`. Snake_case widget_ids match the canvas widget registry (`registerWidgetRenderer("today", ...)` etc.) so runtime dispatch resolves correctly. Single-column canvas (`total_columns=1`) so each placement fills the accessory rail's full width; widgets stack vertically at row_start 1, 4, 8 with row_span 3, 4, 3. Inline doc explains why the kanban core is NOT a placement and what the seeded composition represents. Seed remains idempotent — re-running deactivates the prior active row + inserts a new row at version+1 (per existing service-layer versioning). (6) **`CompositionRenderer.test.tsx` regression-test fix** — pre-existing test asserted "card text doesn't contain 'widget'" when `show_header=false`; that assumed the editor-mode preview body. With runtime dispatch now using `MissingWidgetEmptyState` on unregistered widgets, the empty state's body text mentions "widget" — making the loose string assertion brittle. Test rewritten to assert directly on header-element absence (`card.querySelector(".border-b") === null`) — resilient to body-text changes across editor and runtime paths, preserves original intent. **Spec-Override Discipline note** (per CLAUDE.md §12): the prompt's example seed positions widgets at `column_start=9, column_span=4` (a sidebar-of-12-col model). Override applied to use `total_columns=1, column_start=1, column_span=1`: the accessory rail is its own ~288px region, NOT a sidebar within a 12-col Focus canvas — placing widgets at col 9-12 of a 12-col grid rendered into a 288px region would compress them to ~96px wide each, illegible. Single-column canvas matches the actual rendering shape. Documented inline in the seed comments. **Tests shipped this phase**: 6 vitest in `CompositionRenderer.runtime.test.tsx` (runtime dispatch via getWidgetRenderer + prop_overrides config thread + variant_id extraction + editor-mode-preview-not-runtime-dispatch + missing-widget fallback + non-widget-kind graceful placeholder + explicit renderPlacement override) + 6 vitest in `SchedulingFocusWithAccessories.test.tsx` (kanban core mount + no-composition kanban-only + empty-composition kanban-only + loading kanban-only + populated composition shows accessory rail as sibling + compositionFocusType fallback to config.id) = **12 new tests**. Plus the rewritten regression-test in `CompositionRenderer.test.tsx`. Full vitest **1333/1333 passing** (1321 baseline + 12 new); pytest focus_compositions **14/14 passing**; tsc clean (only pre-existing CalendarAccountsPage `asChild` violation); vite build clean (4.87s). **Live resolution verification on dev DB**: `resolve_composition(focus_type="scheduling", vertical="funeral_home")` returns `source="vertical_default"` + 3 placements (today / recent_activity / anomalies); `vertical="manufacturing"` returns the same 3-widget shape; `vertical="cemetery"` returns `source=None placements=0` → wrapper correctly falls back to kanban-only. Seed idempotency confirmed: first run creates v1, second run creates v2 (deactivating v1 per service-layer versioning). **Acknowledged drift (out of scope)**: backend's `composition_service` validation emits a soft warning `"references unknown component 'widget:recent_activity'"` because the visual-editor's component registry uses kebab-case names (`recent-activity`) while the canvas widget renderer registry uses snake_case (`recent_activity`). The two registries are conceptually overlapping but not unified. The runtime dispatch path uses the **canvas widget renderer registry** (snake_case), so seeds use snake_case for operational correctness. The validation warning is benign noise; consolidating the two registries is a separate cleanup. **Files added** (3): `frontend/src/components/dispatch/scheduling-focus/SchedulingFocusWithAccessories.tsx`, `frontend/src/components/dispatch/scheduling-focus/SchedulingFocusWithAccessories.test.tsx`, `frontend/src/lib/visual-editor/compositions/CompositionRenderer.runtime.test.tsx`. **Files modified** (5): `frontend/src/lib/visual-editor/compositions/CompositionRenderer.tsx` (runtime widget dispatch + renderRuntimePlacement helper + import getWidgetRenderer), `frontend/src/lib/visual-editor/compositions/CompositionRenderer.test.tsx` (regression-test fix), `frontend/src/contexts/focus-registry.ts` (compositionFocusType field on FocusConfig), `frontend/src/components/dispatch/scheduling-focus/register.ts` (compositionFocusType registered + coreComponent swap), `backend/scripts/seed_focus_compositions.py` (full rewrite for accessory-layer pattern), `CLAUDE.md` (this entry). **Architectural patterns established this phase**: (1) **Accessory-layer pattern as alternative to full-decomposition** — composition can author the periphery around a bespoke Focus core, preserving operational behavior. Future Focus types built composition-first will use full-decomposition; bespoke surfaces use accessory-layer. (2) **`compositionFocusType` decouples descriptive id from composition lookup key** — Focus registrations stay descriptive; composition data stays canonical. (3) **CompositionRenderer two-mode contract** — editor mode renders preview stand-ins, runtime mode renders real widgets via the canvas widget registry. The `renderPlacement` callback overrides both. (4) **Sibling-to-kanban accessory rail preserves dnd-kit scope** — wrapping the kanban in a parent that contains the rail would put the rail inside the dnd context boundary; sibling-to-kanban keeps the pointer sensor focused on the kanban region naturally. (5) **Loading state renders fallback, not partial composition** — prevents flash-of-rail-then-disappear if composition fetch fails or returns empty. **Sequencing forward**: arrangement_scribe + triage_decision Focus integrations land when those Focus primitives ship as production code (separate arcs, not wiring tasks). Production tenant-readable composition resolver endpoint (parallel to `/api/platform/admin/visual-editor/compositions/resolve`) — current `useResolvedComposition` calls the admin path. Tenant runtime authentication scope is the next hardening step before any non-admin user invokes composition resolution. Visual-editor + canvas-widget-registry consolidation cleanup (the kebab-case vs snake_case drift surfaced this phase) — separate refactor. Drag-drop integration tests for the composition editor (Playwright with real pointer events) — deferred for further interactive editor refinement. The pre-existing CalendarAccountsPage `asChild` violation remains pre-existing.

- **Admin Visual Editor — Composition Runtime + Drag-Drop Interaction Layer (complete, 2026-05-29):** Two-part follow-on to the canvas-based composition phase. Part 1 ships the runtime infrastructure that lets production Focus components consume composition rows at render time; Part 2 turns the form-based composition editor into a real direct-manipulation canvas (drag-drop, resize handles, multi-select, marquee, undo/redo, keyboard shortcuts). **No backend changes**; migration head unchanged at `r84_focus_compositions`. **Part 1 — Runtime composition infrastructure (3 new files at `frontend/src/lib/visual-editor/compositions/`)**: (a) `useResolvedComposition.ts` hook fetches the resolved composition for a `(focus_type, vertical, tenant_id)` triple via `focusCompositionsService.resolve()`, returns `{composition, isLoading, error, hasComposition}`, gracefully handles concurrent calls via cancelled-flag closure. Currently calls the admin path; production tenant runtime will need a parallel tenant-readable resolver endpoint when production Focus components migrate (documented inline). (b) `FocusContextBridge.tsx` is the runtime context provider that lets composition placements read operational data (case rows, drag handlers, save callbacks, current date) without the underlying components knowing they're being rendered via composition. Pattern: production Focus React component populates the bridge via `buildOperationalProps([{componentKind, componentName, props}, ...])` keyed by `${component_kind}:${component_name}`, then placements call `useOperationalProps(kind, name)` to receive the operational data merged with their `prop_overrides`. Decoupling rationale: kanban implementation doesn't know it's being rendered via composition. The composition layer is invisible to the underlying components — they see normal React props. (c) `ComposedFocus.tsx` is the canonical wrapper. On mount resolves the composition; if a row exists at any scope, renders via `CompositionRenderer` wrapped in `FocusContextBridge` so placements consume operational props; if no composition exists, renders `fallback` (the Focus's existing hard-coded layout). During the loading window it renders `loadingFallback` (defaults to `fallback` so the user never sees a flash of empty state — same content shows whether composition resolves or fallback fires). `forceFallback` prop supports tenant opt-out and emergency rollback. **Production Focus refactor explicitly DEFERRED** per scope discipline — `SchedulingKanbanCore.tsx` (1,714 LOC), `arrangement_scribe`, `triage_decision` each carry substantial operational-props mapping work + careful regression coverage. Phase ships the runtime architecture; production wiring is incremental. The pattern is the contract; future production refactors call `buildOperationalProps([...])` + `<ComposedFocus focusType="..." operational={ops} fallback={<HardCodedLayout />} />`. Convenience `useMergedPlacementProps(kind, name, configuredProps)` returns `{...configuredProps, ...operationalProps}` so placements see one prop bag (operational wins on key conflict — operational data is dynamic state, configured is static design). **Part 2 — Drag-drop interaction layer (3 new files under `frontend/src/bridgeable-admin/components/visual-editor/composition-canvas/`)**: (a) `use-canvas-interactions.ts` is the drag/resize state-machine hook. State machine: `idle → maybe-dragging → dragging → idle` (committed) with parallel resize variant. 3px movement threshold prevents click-as-drag false positives. Window-level pointermove + pointerup + pointercancel handlers so dragging works even when the cursor leaves the placement element. Pure helpers `snapPxToCells / gridDeltaFromPx / resizeDeltaFromPx` exported for testing via `_internals`. Multi-placement drag: when anchor placement is in `selectedIds`, all selected placements move together preserving relative grid positions; otherwise just the anchor moves. Resize delta translation per handle: `n` adjusts row_start + row_span (tugging top edge up grows the placement upward); `s` adjusts row_span only; `e` adjusts column_span only; `w` adjusts column_start + column_span; corner handles (`ne / nw / se / sw`) combine both axis effects. Per-placement bounds via `getBounds(placementId)` callback consults registry `getCanvasMetadata` for min/max columns + rows; spans clamp first, then column_start clamps to keep placement inside totalColumns. Marquee selection ships as approximate (canvas-relative coordinate threading via canvas ref deferred for production polish). (b) `InteractivePlacementCanvas.tsx` — the interactive canvas component. Distinct from `CompositionRenderer` (which stays for runtime + non-interactive preview): adds the interaction layer while reusing the same grid math + token-based styling. Renders 12-col CSS grid + dotted background showing cell boundaries (toggleable via `showGrid`) + each placement at declared grid coords + 8 resize handles (n/s/e/w + 4 corners) **only when single-selected** (multi-select disables resize for unambiguous gestures) + marquee rectangle during drag-select. Live drag uses CSS `transform` (GPU-composited, 60fps) on dragged elements; live resize visualizes via grid coord adjustment. Pointer-down on grid background fires marquee-select; pointer-down on placement fires select + drag; pointer-down on resize handle fires resize (via stopPropagation to avoid drag/marquee interference). Click on canvas (`e.target === e.currentTarget`) deselects all. Each placement gets `data-testid="interactive-placement-{id}"` + `data-component-kind` + `data-component-name` + `data-selected="true|false"` for E2E. Failed-preview-render gracefully degrades to "Preview unavailable" caption rather than crashing. (c) `use-canvas-interactions.test.ts` covers the pure helpers — 9 tests across snapPxToCells (5 — round-to-nearest, defensive 0/negative-cellSize), gridDeltaFromPx (2 — 2D translation, sub-threshold no-op), resizeDeltaFromPx (8 — every handle direction including 4 corners), DRAG_THRESHOLD_PX (1 — bounded sanity). State-machine portion is integration-tested via Playwright (deferred — real pointer events on a rendered canvas require browser context). **CompositionEditorPage.tsx refactored** (~870 LOC, was ~790 LOC): converts `selectedPlacementId: string | null` → `selectedIds: Set<string>`; replaces inline `<CompositionRenderer ... editorMode={true} ...>` with `<InteractivePlacementCanvas ...>`; wires `onSelect (handle shift-click toggle), onDeselectAll, onPlacementsChange, onCommitDrag, onCommitResize, onMarqueeSelect`; adds undo/redo stack (`undoStack: DraftSnapshot[]` + `undoPointer: number`, capped at 50 entries via slice on overflow); `pushSnapshot` invariant — every committed mutation (drag end, resize end, add, delete, duplicate, nudge, manual grid edit, canvas config change) pushes a fresh snapshot; replay on undo/redo via `isReplayingRef` flag preventing snapshot push during replay; canvas config changes also push snapshots so cols/gap/background are part of history. Top-bar canvas controls: undo button (`Cmd+Z`), redo button (`Cmd+Shift+Z` or `Cmd+Y`), grid-overlay toggle (`Grid3x3` icon, `aria-pressed`), preview-mode toggle (light/dark, scoped to canvas-area `data-mode`), right-rail collapse. Right rail handles three states: (1) multi-select (>1 placement) — shows count + bulk Duplicate + bulk Delete buttons + drag-together hint; (2) single-select — shows component identity + grid position form + Delete button; (3) no selection — shows shift-click + marquee-select hints. Keyboard shortcuts on `window`-level listener that ignores `INPUT / TEXTAREA / SELECT / contentEditable` targets: `Cmd+Z` undo, `Cmd+Shift+Z` or `Cmd+Y` redo, `Cmd+D` duplicate selected, `Backspace` / `Delete` delete selected, `Arrow{Left,Right,Up,Down}` nudge by 1 cell, `Shift+Arrow` nudge by 3 cells, `Escape` clears selection. Selection count badge appears in canvas top bar when ≥1 selected (`data-testid="selection-count-badge"`). Bulk duplicate intelligently finds empty grid slots for each duplicate (avoids overlap with the original + with previously-placed duplicates in the same operation) by re-running `findEmptyGridSlot` against the running working copy. **Tests shipped**: 9 vitest in `use-canvas-interactions.test.ts` (pure helpers) + 4 vitest in `FocusContextBridge.test.tsx` (3 op-prop threading scenarios + 1 buildOperationalProps key-format invariant) + verification of CompositionEditorPage tsc cleanliness. Production-Focus refactor + production tenant resolver endpoint + Playwright drag-drop integration tests deferred to a future focused phase. **Final regression posture**: tsc clean (only pre-existing `CalendarAccountsPage.tsx` `asChild` violation predates the visual editor work); vitest **1321/1321 passing** (1293 baseline + 13 new — 9 canvas interactions + 4 FocusContextBridge); pytest unaffected (focus_compositions 14/14 passing); vite build clean (4.96s). **Files added** (6): `frontend/src/lib/visual-editor/compositions/{useResolvedComposition.ts, FocusContextBridge.tsx, ComposedFocus.tsx, FocusContextBridge.test.tsx}`, `frontend/src/bridgeable-admin/components/visual-editor/composition-canvas/{use-canvas-interactions.ts, InteractivePlacementCanvas.tsx, use-canvas-interactions.test.ts}`. **Files modified** (2): `frontend/src/bridgeable-admin/pages/visual-editor/CompositionEditorPage.tsx` (refactor to integrate InteractivePlacementCanvas + undo/redo + keyboard shortcuts + multi-select rail), `CLAUDE.md` (this entry). **Architectural patterns established this phase**: (1) **Two-tier composition rendering** — `CompositionRenderer` is the runtime path (read-only render against operational props), `InteractivePlacementCanvas` is the editor path (drag-drop + resize + selection state). Same grid math, different concerns, no shared mutable state. (2) **Operational-props-via-bridge decouples composition from runtime data** — placements receive operational props via `FocusContextBridge` context lookup keyed on `componentKind:componentName`. Underlying components don't know they're being rendered via composition. (3) **Coexist-with-fallback default** — `ComposedFocus` always falls back to a hard-coded layout when no composition exists. Composition is opt-in per-tenant; absence is the canonical state during rollout. (4) **Snapshot-based undo/redo with isReplayingRef gate** — every committed mutation pushes; replays don't push (gated via flag). Stack capped at 50 to bound memory; overflow drops oldest. (5) **Multi-select disables resize handles** — when selectedIds.size > 1 the canvas hides resize affordances; gestures stay unambiguous (drag = move group; click = select). (6) **Pure helpers extracted via `_internals` for testing** — drag/resize state machine is integration-tested (Playwright deferred), but the pure math (snapPxToCells, gridDeltaFromPx, resizeDeltaFromPx) ships with full unit coverage. **Sequencing forward**: production Focus refactor (SchedulingKanbanCore + arrangement_scribe + triage_decision) is the next focused phase — each Focus call site adopts `<ComposedFocus operational={buildOperationalProps([...])} fallback={...} />` with its operational-props mapping carefully validated against the existing hard-coded behavior. Tenant-readable composition resolver endpoint (parallel to `/api/platform/admin/visual-editor/compositions/resolve`) lands alongside the first production Focus migration. Playwright drag-drop integration tests (real pointer events on rendered canvas) ship alongside any further interactive editor refinement. Alignment-guide rendering (snap-to-edge in addition to grid snap) deferred — current grid snap is sufficient for Apple-Pro-app-grade direct manipulation; alignment guides become valuable when canvases routinely have ≥6 placements (current scheduling fallback has 3-4). The pre-existing `CalendarAccountsPage.tsx` `asChild` violation remains pre-existing across this phase.

- **Admin Visual Editor — Component Class Configuration Layer (complete, 2026-05-28):** Introduces a class-level configuration scope into the inheritance chain. Real use of the per-component editor surfaced a gap — many visual properties are properly scoped to a class of components ("all widgets float more"), not to individual components. This phase ships class configuration as a new layer, expands ComponentKinds to cover cross-cutting patterns that should be classifiable (entity-card, button, form-input, surface-card), and ships a class editor at `/visual-editor/classes`. **Migration `r83_component_class_configurations`**: new `component_class_configurations` table with single `class_default` scope (no vertical/tenant variant — class defaults apply platform-wide) + class-name CHECK enumerating the 9 v1 classes. Same write-side versioning pattern as platform_themes + component_configurations (partial unique on `is_active=true`, version increments on save). The migration also extends the existing `component_configurations.component_kind` CHECK constraint to permit the four new ComponentKinds (`entity-card`, `button`, `form-input`, `surface-card`) — these classifiable patterns now appear alongside `widget` / `focus` / `focus-template` / `document-block` / `pulse-widget` / `workflow-node` / `layout` / `composite`. **Migration head advances**: `r82_workflow_templates` → `r83_component_class_configurations`. **Inheritance chain post-class-layer**: registration_default → class_default(component_class) → platform_default(component_kind, component_name) → vertical_default → tenant_override → draft. Class defaults apply to every component in the class; per-component scopes override at matching keys. Resolution at `app/services/component_config/config_service.py::resolve_configuration` walks the chain in this order; the response's `sources` array distinguishes class-default sources via a `scope: "class_default"` discriminator + a `component_class` field. **Class membership = ComponentKind for v1.** Each component belongs to exactly one class; storage is array-typed (`componentClasses: string[]`) so multi-class extension lands without schema migration. Frontend `RegistrationMetadata` gains an optional `componentClasses?: string[]` field defaulting to `[type]`; backend `lookup_component_classes(kind, name)` mirrors the same default in the snapshot. **Single source of truth for class definitions** at `frontend/src/lib/visual-editor/registry/class-registrations.ts` — 9 classes × 4–8 configurable props each, ~55 props total. Each class declares its own narrow set of class-level props (widget: shadowToken/surfaceToken/radiusToken/density/borderTreatment/hoverElevation/headerStyle/showFooter; entity-card: similar + accentTreatment/imagePosition/actionBarStyle; focus: header/action-bar/transition/dismiss/accent-border behavior; etc.). Backend mirror at `backend/app/services/component_class_config/class_registry_snapshot.py` validates writes against the same schema. **6 admin endpoints** at `/api/platform/admin/visual-editor/classes/*` (all `get_current_platform_user`): GET / (list with filters), POST / (create / version), PATCH /{id} (update), GET /resolve?component_class= (resolved class default), GET /registry (class registry snapshot for the editor's controls), GET /{id} (single row). **17 backend tests** in `test_component_class_configurations.py` across 5 test classes — TestServiceCRUD × 4 (create, version-on-resave, update-versions, unknown-class-rejected), TestValidation × 4 (unknown-prop-rejected, enum-out-of-bounds, wrong-type, number-out-of-bounds), TestResolution × 2 (empty-when-no-config, returns-active-row), TestComponentResolverIntegratesClassLayer × 2 (class-default-appears-in-component-resolution, platform-default-overrides-class-default-source-ordering), TestApiAdmin × 5 (create+list, resolve, registry, anonymous-rejected, invalid-prop-400). **Class editor frontend** at `bridgeable-admin/pages/visual-editor/ClassEditorPage.tsx` ships the same three-pane preview-dominant layout as the redesigned component editor — left = class browser with search + member-count + prop-count; center = multi-component preview in the class's appropriate context frame (3-column dashboard for widgets, Focus shell for Focus types, document page for document-blocks, workflow canvas for workflow-nodes, etc.) so editing the widget shadow visibly updates all widgets in the preview together; right = compact class-level prop controls + autosave + reset-all + cross-link to component editor. New `bridgeable-admin/services/component-class-configurations-service.ts` wraps the 6 endpoints. **Component editor integration**: the existing `/visual-editor/components` page now shows a `class-default` source badge (letter `C`) for props inherited from the class layer + a class-membership pill in the right-rail component-identity card with a "Edit class defaults →" link to `/visual-editor/classes`. **Frontend resolver extended**: `ConfigStack` gains `classLayer` + `classNames` slots; `mergeConfigStack` merges in canonical order `class → platform → vertical → tenant → draft`; `stackFromResolvedConfig` maps backend class_default sources into the classLayer; `resolvePropSource` returns `"class-default"` for class-only props. **13 vitest tests** in `lib/visual-editor/registry/class-registrations.test.ts` covering well-formedness of all 9 class registrations, getEffectiveComponentClasses default-to-`[type]` semantics, resolver integration of class-default sources, and merge-order verification (class < platform < vertical < tenant < draft). **VisualEditorIndex landing + VisualEditorLayout nav** extended: a new "Classes" tab + landing-page card (`Boxes` icon) sit alongside themes/components/workflows/registry. **Final regression posture**: tsc clean (only pre-existing CalendarAccountsPage `asChild` violation); vitest **1293/1293 passing** (1280 baseline + 13 new); pytest **95/95 passing** across the 4 visual editor test files (themes 21 + components 24 + workflows 33 + class configs 17); vite build clean (4.84s). **Files added** (8): `backend/alembic/versions/r83_component_class_configurations.py`, `backend/app/models/component_class_configuration.py`, `backend/app/services/component_class_config/{__init__.py, class_config_service.py, class_registry_snapshot.py}`, `backend/app/api/routes/admin/visual_editor_classes.py`, `backend/tests/test_component_class_configurations.py`, `frontend/src/lib/visual-editor/registry/class-registrations.ts`, `frontend/src/bridgeable-admin/services/component-class-configurations-service.ts`, `frontend/src/bridgeable-admin/pages/visual-editor/ClassEditorPage.tsx`, `frontend/src/lib/visual-editor/registry/class-registrations.test.ts`. **Files modified**: `backend/app/models/__init__.py` (ComponentClassConfiguration import + __all__), `backend/app/api/platform.py` (route registration), `backend/app/api/routes/admin/visual_editor_components.py` (Literal extended with 4 new kinds), `backend/app/services/component_config/{config_service.py, registry_snapshot.py}` (resolver class-layer integration + lookup_component_classes helper), `frontend/src/lib/visual-editor/registry/{types.ts, introspection.ts, index.ts}` (ComponentKind expansion + componentClasses field + getEffectiveComponentClasses + getComponentsInClass), `frontend/src/lib/visual-editor/components/config-resolver.ts` (ConfigStack extended with classLayer + classNames), `frontend/src/bridgeable-admin/components/visual-editor/CompactPropControl.tsx` (class-default source badge), `frontend/src/bridgeable-admin/components/VisualEditorLayout.tsx` (Classes nav tab), `frontend/src/bridgeable-admin/pages/visual-editor/{VisualEditorIndex.tsx, ComponentEditorPage.tsx}` (landing card + class-membership pill), `frontend/src/bridgeable-admin/BridgeableAdminApp.tsx` (Classes route registration), `CLAUDE.md` (this entry + new §4 Component Class Configuration subsection + §5/§11 migration head bumps). **Architectural patterns established this phase**: (1) **Class layer slots between registration default and per-component scopes** — class layer is a sibling to platform_default in the inheritance chain, NOT a parent; per-component overrides correctly win over class defaults at matching keys. (2) **Single-class for v1, array storage for forward-compat** — `componentClasses: string[]` with v1 invariant of exactly one entry; multi-class extension drops in without schema migration. (3) **Class registrations file as single source of truth** — frontend canonical, backend snapshot mirror for write-time validation; same pattern as `registry_snapshot.py` for per-component configs. (4) **Class editor preview shows the class effect** — multi-component preview rendered in the class's appropriate context frame so the operator sees "tune the widget shadow at class level → all widgets update" together. The whole point of the class layer becomes visible in the editor surface. (5) **Audit attribution limitation carries forward** — `created_by`/`updated_by` FK to `users.id` on the new `component_class_configurations` table; PlatformUser ids cannot satisfy that FK, so `actor_user_id=None` for now (same limitation flagged in the relocation phase entry). Future migration to drop the FK or add a `platform_user_id` column would resolve uniformly across all 4 visual editor tables. **Sequencing forward**: backfill class_default records to encode the current platform-wide aesthetic — this proves the class editor works for real authoring. New-kind component registrations (entity-card / button / form-input / surface-card) ship piecewise as components become classifiable (Phase 9 of the original spec scoped this as optional/recommended; can be done as a follow-up). The CalendarAccountsPage `asChild` pre-existing error remains pre-existing.

- **Admin Visual Editor — Component Editor Layout Redesign (complete, 2026-05-27):** UX-correcting layout refactor for the relocated component editor at `/bridgeable-admin/visual-editor/components` (and the admin-subdomain entry at `/visual-editor/components`). The Phase 3 editor shipped with the property controls dominating the viewport and the preview as a secondary surface — backwards: the preview is what the operator manipulates, the controls are how they manipulate it. This phase inverts the priority. Capability is identical — same registry, same configuration data model, same three-scope inheritance, same prop editors, same backend endpoints. Only the visual hierarchy + contextual preview rendering change. **Migration head unchanged at `r82_workflow_templates`** (no backend work). **New layout** is three-pane preview-dominant: left rail (320px) is the component browser with category tabs (`All / Widgets / Focus / Templates / Doc Blocks / Workflow Nodes`) + search + vertical filter + stylized per-kind thumbnails; center pane dominates the viewport with the preview rendered in appropriate context per component kind; right rail (320px, collapsible to a 48px strip) holds the compact property controls + scope selector + tenant picker. **Four context frames** ship at `bridgeable-admin/components/visual-editor/context-frames/`: `DashboardContextFrame` wraps widget previews in a 3-column dashboard grid with placeholder surrounding cards (so the widget under edit is seen alongside what would normally surround it); `FocusContextFrame` renders the full Focus shell — header with title + close affordance, content area, action bar at the bottom — and adapts the action bar to the inferred Focus type (Decision = Decline/Approve, Generation = Save Draft/Commit, Coordination = Close, Execution = Mark Complete, Review = Reject/Approve); `DocumentContextFrame` renders sample document chrome (page boundary, mock letterhead, mock paragraphs) with the block in its appropriate position (header blocks at top, signature blocks at bottom); `WorkflowCanvasContextFrame` renders a 3-node canvas snippet with subtle grid background + connection arrows, the node under edit highlighted as the selected middle node, and inferred upstream/downstream placeholder labels per node-type. Each frame supports a `showAllInstances` mode that renders three variants side-by-side. **Stylized thumbnails** at `bridgeable-admin/components/visual-editor/ComponentThumbnail.tsx` — 80×60 stand-ins per kind that use design tokens to convey shape (Widget thumbnails differ by inferred shape: metric / list / schedule / default; Focus thumbnails vary in proportion by type — Generation tall, Decision/Execution short; Document blocks position content at top vs bottom per name; Workflow nodes show input/output dots with a labeled rounded rectangle). NOT real renders; just enough visual signature for scannability. Override-dot indicator in the corner when the component has overrides at the active scope. **CompactPropControl** at `bridgeable-admin/components/visual-editor/CompactPropControl.tsx` is the dense wrapper around `PropControlDispatcher`. Each prop row: label + source-icon + reset-button (hover-revealed when overridden) on one line. Boolean controls render inline next to the label; longer controls render below. Description text is collapsed behind an info icon. Source badge is a single-letter circle (D=registration default, P=platform, V=vertical, T=tenant, • = unsaved draft). Reset-to-inherited is a small icon button visible only when the prop is overridden at the active scope. Props group by inferred category (Layout / Appearance / Behavior / General) via a name-pattern heuristic; group headers are collapsible. **Component browser tab filtering** + search + vertical filter all compose; the list is virtualization-ready (not virtualized at current 17-component scale, per spec). **Right rail collapse**: a `PanelRightClose`/`PanelRightOpen` toggle in the preview top-bar collapses the right rail to a 48px strip showing only an expand affordance + a vertical "Properties" label. Useful for inspection-focused work. **Empty state** when no component selected: preview shows "Select a component to begin editing" centered in the canvas; right rail shows "No component selected." **Save semantics preserved**: 1.5s autosave debounce + manual save button + sticky save bar at the top of the right rail showing unsaved-count badge + saving spinner + error state. Discard reverts to the persisted overrides. Reset-all clears the draft (confirmation dialog). Per-prop reset removes that prop from the draft so it falls back through the inheritance chain. **Tests shipped**: 23 new vitest tests in `bridgeable-admin/components/visual-editor/component-editor-redesign.test.tsx` covering all four context frames (target rendering, show-all-instances variant slots, position handling for documents, adjacency labels for workflow), thumbnail dispatch per kind (widget / focus / focus-template badge / document-block / workflow-node + override dot conditional rendering), CompactPropControl (label + source badge + inline boolean + reset button visibility + description toggle + reset callback), and the inferPropGroup heuristic. **Final regression posture**: tsc clean (only pre-existing CalendarAccountsPage `asChild` violation); vitest **1280/1280 passing** (1257 baseline + 23 new); vite build clean (4.91s). **Files added** (5): `bridgeable-admin/components/visual-editor/context-frames/DashboardContextFrame.tsx`, `FocusContextFrame.tsx`, `DocumentContextFrame.tsx`, `WorkflowCanvasContextFrame.tsx` + `ComponentThumbnail.tsx` + `CompactPropControl.tsx` + `component-editor-redesign.test.tsx`. **Files modified** (1): `bridgeable-admin/pages/visual-editor/ComponentEditorPage.tsx` rewritten as preview-dominant three-pane layout (~900 lines → ~960 lines including the contextual preview routing). **Patterns established this phase**: (1) **Preview-dominant editor layout** — when an editor is fundamentally about visualizing what's being edited, the preview should occupy the dominant share of the viewport (~60-65% width here); controls become a thin rail. Inverts the form-style "controls-first" pattern. (2) **Context-frame wrappers per component kind** — generic preview renderers are inadequate for editing components that exist in specific contexts. Render widgets inside dashboard chrome, Focus types inside Focus shells, document blocks inside document pages, workflow nodes on workflow canvases. The frame is stylistic mock; the renderer fills the central slot. (3) **Stylized thumbnails over real previews** in dense list contexts — at thumbnail scale (80×60) full renders are illegible. A token-styled silhouette conveying "this is a widget vs Focus vs node" is more scannable. (4) **Compact prop controls with progressive disclosure** — descriptions collapsed by default behind an info icon; reset-to-inherited only visible when applicable; source badges are single letters not text labels. Prioritizes density for power-user editing without losing affordance for new users. **Sequencing forward**: theme editor at `/visual-editor/themes` and workflow editor at `/visual-editor/workflows` will get analogous redesigns once this redesign validates in staging. The audit-attribution limitation (PlatformUser ids can't satisfy users.id FK) carries forward unchanged from the relocation phase. The CalendarAccountsPage `asChild` pre-existing error remains pre-existing.

- **Admin Visual Editor — Corrective Relocation Phase (complete, 2026-05-27):** The Phase 1-4 visual editors were originally wired into the tenant App tree with tenant-admin auth, when they belong in the BridgeableAdminApp tree under PlatformUser auth. This phase relocates the editors to the correct tree, swaps the auth realm, and resolves the inconsistency between "what is being edited" (platform/vertical defaults that no tenant should be authoring) and "who is editing it."
