No production user impact (no real users yet); the relocation is structural rather than functional. **Migration head unchanged at `r82_workflow_templates`** (no new migrations per scope constraint). **Part 1 — Visual editor library renamed**: `frontend/src/admin/` → `frontend/src/lib/visual-editor/` to reflect its role as shared library infrastructure (the registry singleton populates from auto-register-on-import regardless of which tree consumes it; the tenant tree imports it for the registry side-effect, the admin tree imports it for editor pages). Pure mechanical move via `git mv`; all `@/admin/*` import paths rewritten across the codebase. **Part 2 — Backend route relocation**: three files moved from `app/api/routes/{platform_themes,component_configurations,workflow_templates}.py` → `app/api/routes/admin/visual_editor_{themes,components,workflows}.py`. Every `Depends(require_admin)` swapped to `Depends(get_current_platform_user)`. Every `User`-typed parameter swapped to `PlatformUser`. The three `v1_router.include_router` blocks removed from `app/api/v1.py`; corresponding `platform_router.include_router` blocks added under `/admin/visual-editor/themes`, `/admin/visual-editor/components`, `/admin/visual-editor/workflows` in `app/api/platform.py`. New URL prefixes: `/api/platform/admin/visual-editor/{themes,components,workflows}/*`. Tenant tokens reaching these paths now return 401 (cross-realm boundary at `get_current_platform_user`). **Part 3 — Tenant picker for tenant_override scope**: new `TenantPicker` component at `bridgeable-admin/components/TenantPicker.tsx` consumes a new `GET /api/platform/admin/tenants/lookup?q=&limit=` endpoint added to `kanban.py`. Themes + Components editors replaced their free-text "Tenant ID" input with `<TenantPicker>` so tenant_override scope requires explicit tenant selection. WorkflowEditorPage doesn't need it (workflow templates support only `platform_default` + `vertical_default`; tenant customization is via fork mechanic, not tenant_override). **Audit attribution limitation flagged**: `created_by`/`updated_by` columns on the three editor tables are FK-constrained to `users.id`. PlatformUser ids cannot satisfy that FK. Route-layer therefore passes `actor_user_id=None` for platform-user writes — column-level attribution NOT recorded for relocated endpoints. Documented inline in each route file + flagged in §4 Admin Platform Architecture. Future migration to drop the FK or add a `platform_user_id` column is the canonical fix; no migration was shipped this phase per scope constraint. **Part 4 — Frontend page relocation**: 4 editor page files + 3 service files moved into `frontend/src/bridgeable-admin/`. Services switched from `apiClient` to `adminApi` (separate axios instance with realm=platform tokens + environment toggle). All 24 service URL refs updated to the new platform prefixes. **Part 5 — VisualEditorLayout shipped**: warm-token chrome distinct from `AdminLayout`'s slate chrome. Top-bar with "Back to Admin" link, four-tab nav (themes / components / workflows / registry), `scopeIndicator` slot for the persistent banner per Part 3. Two layouts coexist within `BridgeableAdminApp`: operational pages keep `AdminLayout`, visual editor pages opt into `VisualEditorLayout`. **Part 6 — Routes + nav + landing**: `BridgeableAdminApp.tsx` extended with separate `visualEditorPages` block routed at `/visual-editor/*` (admin-subdomain entry) and `/bridgeable-admin/visual-editor/*` (path-based entry). New `VisualEditorIndex` landing page lists the four editors + registry quick-stats. `AdminHeader.tsx` gains a "Visual Editor" nav link. **Part 7 — Tenant App.tsx cleanup**: the four `/admin/{registry,themes,components,workflows}` routes + their imports removed. The `auto-register` side-effect import for the registry singleton stays — registrations populate at module load regardless of which tree renders. **Part 8 — Test relocation**: 78 backend tests across `test_platform_themes_phase2.py` + `test_component_configurations_phase3.py` + `test_workflow_templates_phase4.py` updated to use a new PlatformUser fixture (PlatformUser + access token with realm="platform") and the new URL prefixes. The pre-relocation `test_non_admin_403` tests reframed as `test_tenant_token_rejected` (status 401 — realm mismatch instead of role-based 403). The four pre-relocation Playwright spec files removed and replaced with a single smoke-shaped `admin-visual-editor-relocation.spec.ts` covering the relocation invariants (old tenant routes return non-render, new platform endpoints reject tenant tokens with 401). Detailed interactive tests (token edit propagation, configuration save flow, canvas validation UI) deferred to a follow-up phase once staging validates the relocation. **Final regression posture**: tsc clean (only pre-existing CalendarAccountsPage `asChild` violation); vitest **1257/1257 passing**; pytest **78/78 passing** for the three relocated test files; vite build clean (4.87s). **Architectural patterns established this phase**: (1) **Two app trees, two layouts, shared library** — admin and tenant trees share only `frontend/src/lib/visual-editor/` (registry + token catalog + config resolver + canvas validator); auth + layout + routing + service axios are tree-specific. (2) **Layout-coexistence within a tree** — BridgeableAdminApp routes `/visual-editor/*` to `VisualEditorLayout` (warm tokens) and everything else to `AdminLayout` (slate chrome). Two layouts, one tree, one auth. (3) **Realm boundary as security primitive** — tenant tokens at platform endpoints return 401, platform tokens at tenant endpoints return 401; the realm check sits at the dependency layer (`get_current_user` / `get_current_platform_user`) so the gate cannot be bypassed by route-level oversight. (4) **adminPath() helper as cross-link convention** — every admin-tree Link routes through `adminPath()` so paths work under both `/bridgeable-admin/*` and admin-subdomain root entry. (5) **TenantPicker as scope-selector primitive** — backend `/api/platform/admin/tenants/lookup` returns flat `[{id, slug, name, vertical}]` for autocomplete; frontend `TenantPicker` handles search + select UX; reusable for any future platform-side surface that needs tenant selection. **Files moved (frontend)**: 4 editor page files + 3 service files + 4 visual-editor library subdirectories. **Files added**: `VisualEditorLayout.tsx`, `VisualEditorIndex.tsx`, `TenantPicker.tsx`, `admin-visual-editor-relocation.spec.ts`. **Files moved (backend)**: 3 route files into `app/api/routes/admin/`. **Files deleted**: 4 pre-relocation Playwright spec files. **Files modified**: `App.tsx` (route removal), `BridgeableAdminApp.tsx` (route addition), `AdminHeader.tsx` (nav link), `kanban.py` (lookup endpoint), `v1.py` (router unregister), `platform.py` (router register), 3 test files (auth fixture + URL prefixes), 3 service files (axios client + URLs), and the editor pages themselves (`<TenantPicker>` integration). **Sequencing forward**: detailed interactive Playwright coverage for the relocated editors. Audit-attribution migration to drop the FK on `users.id` (or add `platform_user_id` columns) so PlatformUser writes can be column-attributed. Tenant-facing Workshop UI for tenant_override scope (separate from this phase's admin-side support tooling).

- **Admin Visual Editor Phase 4 — Workflow Canvas + Vertical Default Backfill (complete, 2026-05-27):** Fourth phase of the Admin Visual Editor. Ships the workflow canvas at admin scope alongside seeded `funeral_cascade` (FH) + `quote_to_pour` (manufacturing) vertical_default workflow templates. **Migration `r82_workflow_templates`** creates two tables: `workflow_templates` (scope/vertical/workflow_type/canvas_state JSONB/version/is_active/audit) and `tenant_workflow_forks` (tenant_id/workflow_type/forked_from_template_id/forked_from_version/canvas_state/pending_merge_available/pending_merge_template_id). CHECK constraints enforce scope-key shape across `platform_default` + `vertical_default` (vertical-default rows must have non-null vertical; platform-default must have null vertical); partial unique index on `is_active=true` rows enforces "at most one active row per (scope, vertical, workflow_type)". Migration head: `r81 → r82`. **Locked-to-fork merge semantics**: forks REPLACE the inheritance chain rather than overlay. When a tenant forks a vertical_default, subsequent vertical_default updates DO NOT auto-propagate; instead `mark_pending_merge` flags the fork. Admin chooses `accept_merge` (replaces canvas_state) or `reject_merge` (preserves fork's canvas_state but updates `forked_from_version` so banner clears). This avoids the unsolvable "graph merge" problem — workflow definitions are graphs, and arbitrary graph merge has no canonical answer; lock-to-fork makes the operator-agency model explicit. **Resolver inheritance order** (`resolve_workflow`): tenant fork → vertical_default → platform_default. First match wins (no overlay merging at READ time per locked-to-fork semantics). Returns `{workflow_type, vertical, tenant_id, source, source_id, source_version, canvas_state, pending_merge_available}` so the caller knows which tier answered. **Canvas state schema** lives in `app/services/workflow_templates/canvas_validator.py` with a frontend mirror at `frontend/src/admin/workflows/canvas-validator.ts`. Both validators MUST agree on schema rules: required keys (`version` + `nodes` + `edges`), node id uniqueness, valid node types (28 canonical: start/end, engine action types from workflow_engine, Phase 1 registry workflow-node names, cross-tenant primitives, composition nodes), edge reference integrity, cycle detection via three-color DFS (`is_iteration=true` edges excluded from the cycle graph). Empty `{}` canvas is valid (unauthored draft). The validators are tested cross-reference: every CanvasState valid on one side must be valid on the other. **9 admin endpoints** at `/api/v1/admin/workflow-templates/*` (all `require_admin`): GET / (list — metadata only, no canvas_state to keep payloads small), GET /{id} (full canvas), GET /resolve (inheritance walk), POST / (create), PATCH /{id} (update; `?notify_forks=true` flags every dependent fork), POST /{id}/fork (creates tenant fork), GET /{id}/dependent-forks (list forks of a vertical_default), POST /forks/{id}/accept-merge, POST /forks/{id}/reject-merge, GET /forks/. Pydantic shapes `_TemplateMetadataResponse` (no canvas_state) + `_TemplateFullResponse` (with canvas_state) keep list payloads lightweight. **Frontend `/admin/workflows` (admin-only)**. Three-pane layout: left = scope selector + vertical/workflow_type/metadata + dependent forks list; center = node-list canvas with palette across the top (start/action/decision/branch/parallel_split/parallel_join/schedule/send-communication/generation-focus-invocation/cross_tenant_order/cross_tenant_request/playwright_action/log_vault_item/end), selectable nodes show outgoing edges with conditions, validation error banner above canvas when `validateCanvasState` throws; right = node configuration form with type dropdown + id/label/config-JSON inputs + outgoing edges with add/remove. Save validates client-side first via the mirror validator; failed validation blocks save. "Save and notify forks" is an explicit alias of save with `notify_forks=true` for vertical_default updates. Cross-links to `/admin/themes`, `/admin/components`, `/admin/registry` in the top bar; reciprocal cross-links from themes + components editors back to workflows. Autosave 1.5s debounce; metadata strip surfaces version/node-count/edge-count/branching/terminal counts via `summarizeCanvas`. **Phase 4 backfill** seeded 2 vertical_default workflows via `scripts/seed_workflow_templates_phase4.py`: **`funeral_cascade`** (17 nodes — funeral_home vertical: start → trigger_arrangement_commit → generate_case_file → branch_disposition → burial_path/cremation_path → parallel_join → obituary/service/program/notification/death_certificate/grief_check_in → end) + **`quote_to_pour`** (16 nodes — manufacturing vertical: start → trigger → sales_order → check_inventory → in_stock_path/production_path → parallel_join → qc_check → delivery_docs/log_qc_anomaly → invoice → collections → end). Both authored as canvas_state JSONB in shape identical to what the admin canvas would produce; both validate successfully via `validate_canvas_state`. The seed is idempotent (skip-if-exists at the `(scope, vertical, workflow_type)` tuple). **33 backend tests** in `test_workflow_templates_phase4.py` across 8 test classes: TestCanvasValidator × 9 (empty/missing/unknown-type/dangling-edge/duplicate-id/cycle/iteration-edge/seeded-canvases-validate), TestServiceValidation × 3, TestVersioning × 2, TestInheritance × 4 (platform-only / vertical>platform / tenant-fork>vertical / empty-when-no-template), TestForkLifecycle × 6 (fork-creates / duplicate-rejected / pending-merge-flagged / locked-to-fork-resolution / accept-replaces / reject-preserves), TestAdminGating × 2, TestApiCrud × 3, TestE2EClaudeApiEquivalent × 1 (full lifecycle), TestBackfill × 2 (funeral_cascade + quote_to_pour seeded + valid). All 33 passing. **15 vitest tests** in `frontend/src/admin/workflows/canvas-validator.test.ts` covering the mirror validator (15 assertions across schema validation + cycle detection + iteration-edge handling + summarizeCanvas + cross-reference invariants with backend test shape). **5 Playwright tests** in `tests/e2e/admin-workflows-phase-4.spec.ts` (admin route access, cross-link nav presence, list endpoint smoke, resolve endpoint shape, non-admin gate). **Final regression posture**: tsc clean; vitest **1257/1257 passing** (1242 baseline + 15 new canvas-validator); pytest **33/33 passing** for Phase 4 + Phase 2+3 regression unaffected; vite build clean (4.83s). **Migration head advances**: `r81_component_configurations` → **`r82_workflow_templates`**. **Files added** (8): `backend/alembic/versions/r82_workflow_templates.py`, `backend/app/models/workflow_template.py`, `backend/app/services/workflow_templates/__init__.py` + `canvas_validator.py` + `template_service.py`, `backend/app/api/routes/workflow_templates.py`, `backend/scripts/seed_workflow_templates_phase4.py`, `backend/tests/test_workflow_templates_phase4.py`, `frontend/src/services/workflow-templates-service.ts`, `frontend/src/admin/workflows/canvas-validator.ts` + `canvas-validator.test.ts`, `frontend/src/pages/admin/workflows/WorkflowEditorPage.tsx`, `frontend/tests/e2e/admin-workflows-phase-4.spec.ts`. **Files modified**: `backend/app/models/__init__.py` (WorkflowTemplate + TenantWorkflowFork imports + __all__), `backend/app/api/v1.py` (workflow_templates router registration), `frontend/src/App.tsx` (WorkflowEditorPage admin-only route at `/admin/workflows`), `frontend/src/pages/admin/themes/ThemeEditorPage.tsx` + `frontend/src/pages/admin/components/ComponentEditorPage.tsx` (added Edit-Workflows + Registry cross-links), `CLAUDE.md` (this entry + new §4 "Workflow Canvas" subsection + migration head reference updates at §5 + §11 build-status table). **Architectural patterns established this phase**: (1) **Locked-to-fork over overlay-merge** — workflow definitions are graphs; arbitrary graph merge has no canonical answer; lock-to-fork preserves operator agency by making merge an explicit accept/reject decision rather than a silent layered composition. (2) **Frontend-backend validator parity** — when client-side validation gates a write round-trip, the validators MUST agree on schema rules; cross-reference tests assert equivalence between the two implementations' VALID_NODE_TYPES sets + cycle-detection semantics + edge-reference integrity. (3) **List/detail payload split** — list endpoints return metadata only (no canvas_state JSONB); detail endpoint returns full canvas_state; this scales to 30-50 node canvases without bloating the list response. (4) **Backfill via seed-script-as-canvas-author** — pragmatic alternative to "author through canvas itself" for initial seed: seed script produces canvas_state in identical shape to what admin canvas would produce; backfill suite confirms canvas validates + can be loaded by editor; future template authoring uses the editor verbatim. (5) **Three editors share top-bar cross-link convention** — Themes + Components + Workflows + Registry each link to the other three with `arrow-left-right` icon + `nav-to-{target}` test-id. Admins navigate between editors frequently because every visually-rendered surface is the product of (active theme) × (effective configuration) × (workflow definition).

- **Path E — Pre-existing bug hygiene closure (complete, 2026-05-26):** Bounded cross-arc accumulating debt resolution ahead of Path A Job Coordination Focus implementation arc. Two pre-existing bugs flagged across Calendar Step 4 + 5 + 5.1 closure entries + Personalization Studio Phase 1B + 1E + 1F + 1G + Step 2 closure entries — both resolved at substrate-pattern depth. **Bug 1 — CalendarAccountsPage `asChild` violation (resolved earlier same day, deploy-unblock commit `73427db`)**: pre-existing site at `frontend/src/pages/settings/CalendarAccountsPage.tsx:330` carrying invalid `<DropdownMenuTrigger asChild>` blocked Railway production build at `tsc -b` step. shadcn/ui v4 + @base-ui/react do not support `asChild`; canonical pattern is `render={...}` per `EmailAccountsPage.tsx` + `PortalUsersSettings.tsx` precedent. Fix routed `<DropdownMenuTrigger asChild><Button>...</Button></DropdownMenuTrigger>` → `<DropdownMenuTrigger render={<Button>...</Button>} />`. Note: prompt referenced path as `frontend/src/pages/calendar/CalendarAccountsPage.tsx`; canonical site is `frontend/src/pages/settings/CalendarAccountsPage.tsx` (the `settings/` path is the canonical surface). **Bug 2 — `saved_views/registry.py` D-6 lint failure on missing `Document.visible_to()`**: pre-existing site at `backend/app/services/saved_views/registry.py:492` carrying raw `Document.company_id == company_id` filter inside the `document` entity type's `query_builder`. Per D-6 lint test (`tests/test_documents_d6_lint.py::test_document_reads_go_through_visible_to`): saved views over Documents are a cross-tenant-relevant read path — a tenant who has a Document shared TO them via D-6 `document_shares` should see that Document when building a saved view (e.g. "Documents from Hopkins this week"). Raw `company_id == X` filter would silently drop shared Documents (security + UX bug). Canonical fix: route through `Document.visible_to(company_id)` per Phase D-6 substrate consumption pattern (matches `documents_v2.py` `_get_visible_document` + `list_documents` + `list_document_log` precedent); explanatory comment cites the lint gate enforcement. **Verification**: D-6 lint test 2/2 passing (`test_document_reads_go_through_visible_to` + `test_allowlist_files_exist`); saved_views regression 73/73 passing across `test_saved_views.py` + `test_saved_views_registry.py` + `test_documents_d6_sharing.py` + `test_documents_d6_lint.py`. Pre-existing brittle `test_saved_views_vertical_scope.py::test_migration_is_registered_in_chain` failure (hardcoded `assert head == "r62_cleanup_cross_vertical_saved_views"` from Phase W-4a Step 3 era; head has since advanced through r63 → r80 → r81 → r82) confirmed via `git stash` to predate Bug 2 fix — flagged for separate hygiene session per Spec-Override Discipline (do NOT silently fix unrelated bugs in scope). **Path A integration prerequisite verification at substrate-pattern depth**: (1) saved_views registry public API intact post-fix (`register_entity` + `execute` + 7 entity types including `document`); (2) no existing Calendar↔saved_views substrate-level integration today (Path A Job Coordination Focus per §3.26.11.13.4 Wilbert demo "today's production schedule saved view" embedding lands as net-new substrate); (3) when Path A canonical embedding lands, cross-tenant Document references in saved-view rows route through `visible_to()` per D-6 substrate consumption pattern — Bug 2 fix unblocks Calendar saved-view integration at Path A scope. **Architectural patterns established this phase**: (1) **D-6 substrate consumption canonical at saved-view data-source layer** — saved_views registry's per-entity `query_builder` is the canonical extension point; future Document-related entity types inherit `visible_to()` discipline at registry registration. (2) **Spec-Override Discipline preserved at hygiene closure boundary** — pre-existing brittle migration-chain assertion (orthogonal to Path E scope) flagged for separate session rather than silently fixed. (3) **Bounded scope hygiene closure pattern** — cross-arc accumulating debt resolution at single-session bounded scope; closure summary canonical-tight per build prompt closing standards. **Files modified** (1 backend): `backend/app/services/saved_views/registry.py` (lines 487-503, document entity `query_builder` routed through `Document.visible_to(company_id)` + comment citing D-6 substrate + lint gate). **Files modified earlier same day for Bug 1** (1 frontend, deploy-unblock commit `73427db`): `frontend/src/pages/settings/CalendarAccountsPage.tsx` (line 330, `DropdownMenuTrigger render` pattern). **Sequencing forward**: Path A Job Coordination Focus implementation arc opens with clean canonical prerequisites at substrate-pattern depth. Pre-existing brittle `test_migration_is_registered_in_chain` assertion remains as separate hygiene session candidate (canonical fix: assert `r62_cleanup_cross_vertical_saved_views` is REACHABLE in chain rather than IS head — brittle invariant pattern from Phase W-4a Step 3 era).

- **Admin Visual Editor Phase 3 — Component Configuration Editor + Configurable Props Backfill (complete, 2026-05-26):** Third phase of the Admin Visual Editor. Two parts shipping together: **Part 1** backfills the `configurableProps` declarations on all 17 Phase 1 registrations from minimal placeholder sets to comprehensive surfaces — **150 props total across 17 components** (7–11 per component, prompt's 4–10 average squarely cleared). Each prop carries `type`, `default`, `bounds` where applicable, `displayLabel` and/or `description`, plus per-type extras (`tokenCategory` for token references, `componentTypes` for component references, `itemSchema` for arrays). All registration `componentVersion` bumped 1 → 2 to reflect the substantive metadata expansion. **Part 2** ships the per-component configuration editor at `/admin/components` (admin-only) with the same three-scope inheritance model as Phase 2 themes (`platform_default` → `vertical_default` → `tenant_override`). The editor's quality depends entirely on Part 1's backfill quality — generic backfill produces useless UI; thoughtful backfill produces a control surface admins can actually use. **Backfill discipline**: not "find every prop and add it"; "identify props worth exposing for configuration and declare them with the precision the editor needs." Bad backfill = `string` prop with no bounds, no description, no default. Good backfill = appropriate type, sensible bounds, meaningful default, description that tells the user what they're affecting. Per-component example: widget:today gets `showRowBreakdown` (boolean), `showTotalCount` (boolean), `refreshIntervalSeconds` (number, [60, 3600]), `maxCategoriesShown` (number, [1, 12]), `accentToken` (tokenReference, accent category), `dateFormatStyle` (enum, 4 values), `emptyStateBehavior` (enum). Focus types (the 5 abstract Phase 1 placeholders) get a shared 5-prop base via `SHARED_FOCUS_PROPS` (density, headerStyle, showCloseButton, transitionStyle, shellAccentToken) plus 5 per-type extensions (auto-close rules, button layouts, queue ordering, autosave intervals, etc.) — 10–11 props each. Document blocks get visual configuration: alignment, accentBarHeight, logoPosition, dateFormat, signatureLineStyle. Workflow nodes get visual representation: nodeShape, labelPosition, success/failure indicator styles, retry semantics. **Backend** ships `r81_component_configurations` migration creating the `component_configurations` table — same shape as `platform_themes` with one new dimension (`component_kind` + `component_name` columns) and one removed dimension (no `mode` column — most component config is mode-agnostic; if a future prop needs mode-awareness, individual props can declare per-mode variants). CHECK constraints enforce scope-key shape + valid `component_kind` enum (matches the 8-value `ComponentKind` from Phase 1 registry). Partial unique on the canonical tuple where `is_active=true` enforces "at most one active row per (scope, vertical, tenant_id, kind, name)"; inactive rows accumulate as a versioned audit trail. **Note**: the Phase 3 migration is named `r81_*` because an unrelated `r80_step2_urn_vault_template_type` migration (urn vault template work) had already been written downstream of `r79_platform_themes`; my migration rebases onto that to keep a linear chain. New `app/models/component_configuration.py` mirroring `PlatformTheme`. New `app/services/component_config/` package with `theme_service`-shaped CRUD + `resolve_configuration` that walks the inheritance chain at READ time (same architectural pattern as Phase 2 themes — no migration / batch job / cache invalidation when a vertical default changes). New `registry_snapshot.py` is a Python mirror of the frontend's prop schemas that the backend uses for write-time validation — the prompt's "consider how to expose this" resolved as "manually maintained snapshot file with vitest cross-reference." **Validation discipline at the boundary**: `UnknownComponent` (HTTP 400) on (kind, name) tuples not in the snapshot; `PropValidationError` (HTTP 400) on unknown prop keys, out-of-bounds numerics, invalid enum values, wrong-type values (string for boolean, etc.), strings exceeding `maxLength`. Frontend prevents most violations at the input layer (sliders clamp, enums show only valid options, string inputs enforce maxLength) so the operator can't produce out-of-bounds states. **Orphaned overrides** (key in DB no longer in registry — happens after a prop is removed in code) are silently dropped from props during resolution + surfaced in `ResolvedConfiguration.orphaned_keys` so the admin UI can flag for cleanup. The system stays consistent without crashing on registry refactors. **6 admin endpoints** at `/api/v1/admin/component-configurations/*`: GET / (list), POST / (create — versions a prior row at the same tuple), PATCH /{id} (replace overrides + bump version), GET /{id} (single row for History UI), GET /resolve (full inheritance walk for editor preview), GET /registry (snapshot for editor's component browser). **24 backend pytest tests** in `test_component_configurations_phase3.py` mirroring `test_platform_themes_phase2.py`'s shape: validation (8 — scope-key, kind, prop type, bounds, enum, maxLength, type), versioning (2), inheritance (5 incl. tenant-override-without-vertical-default-falls-back-to-platform + empty-overrides-falls-through + orphaned-keys-surfaced-in-resolve), admin gating (2), CRUD via API (4), full E2E lifecycle Claude API equivalent (1 — create platform → vertical override → resolve sees override → update → resolve sees new value → empty overrides → fall back to platform). All 24 passing. **Frontend** new `app/services/component-configurations-service.ts` (typed axios client wrapping all 6 endpoints — mirrors `themes-service` shape from Phase 2). New `admin/components/config-resolver.ts` — frontend mirror of the backend resolver, 4-layer stack (`platform / vertical / tenant / draft`), `composeEffectiveProps(kind, name, stack)` uses `registrationDefaults` from the registry as floor + applies stack overrides on top. **Prop editor controls** at `admin/components/PropControls.tsx`: `BooleanControl` (switch), `NumberControl` (slider + numeric input, bounds-clamping), `StringControl` (input/textarea with maxLength enforcement), `EnumControl` (segmented control for ≤6 options, dropdown for >6), `TokenReferenceControl` (token picker with category-scoped filter — accepts the registry's broader category enum and maps to the catalog's narrower categories via `resolveCandidatesForCategory` with prefix + subcategory fallbacks), `ComponentReferenceControl` (component picker filtered by `acceptedTypes`), `ArrayControl` (list editor with add/remove/inline-edit per item), `ObjectControl` (JSON textarea — Phase 3 stub per prompt's "full nested editing is a later phase"). `PropControlDispatcher` is the auto-generated control entry point; given a `ConfigPropSchema` it picks the right primitive. **Config-aware preview renderers** at `admin/components/preview-renderers.tsx`: 5 components (widget:today, widget:operator-profile, widget:anomalies, document-block:header-block, workflow-node:send-communication) get genuinely config-aware stand-ins that visually apply key prop overrides (showRowBreakdown hides rows, dateFormatStyle changes the rendered date, severityFilter filters anomaly list, alignment shifts header layout, accentBarHeight resizes the brand bar). The remaining 12 components fall through to the Phase 2 stand-ins (config-agnostic — render coherently using current tokens but ignore config). The config-aware coverage is sufficient for the editor to feel responsive on common edits while the rest of the components ship as Phase 4 candidates if more visual feedback is needed. **`/admin/components` page** with three-pane layout following Phase 2's pattern verbatim: left = component browser organized by ComponentKind (5 sections, expandable; vertical filter; component search) + scope/mode/vertical selectors at top; center = configurable-prop editor with auto-generated controls per type, source badges (registration-default / platform-default / vertical-default / tenant-override / draft), reset-to-inherited buttons, "show only overridden" filter, prop search; right = single-component live preview using config-aware renderers, "Show all instances" toggle renders three cards with the same config to demonstrate consistency, mode toggle (light/dark) affects only preview rendering. **Save semantics**: autosave 1.5s debounce + manual save commits immediately; failed saves preserve draft state for retry; PATCH if active row exists at the editing scope, POST if not. **Cross-link nav**: `/admin/themes` and `/admin/components` each have a top-bar link to the other (`Edit theme` / `Edit components` with `arrow-left-right` icon) — admins move between editors frequently because component appearance is the product of both layers. **19 vitest tests** in `admin/components/component-config.test.ts` — backfill validation (every Phase 1 registration has ≥3 props, all required schema fields populated, total ≥80, number-typed bounds use `[min, max]` tuple, enum bounds use array, tokenReference declares tokenCategory, componentReference declares componentTypes — these caught 3 issues during build), resolver merge order (platform → vertical → tenant → draft), source detection across 5 layers, stackFromResolvedConfig, diffPropOverrides (with deep-equal handling), all-supported-types smoke. **13 Playwright tests** in `admin-components-phase-3.spec.ts` covering: admin route access + three-pane render, all 17 components grouped by kind, selecting a component loads its props, vertical filter changes browser content, editing a boolean prop visibly updates preview, editing an enum prop visibly updates preview, numeric bounds clamping prevents out-of-bounds, token-reference picker scopes to declared tokenCategory, mode toggle affects only preview, show-only-overridden filter, show-all-instances renders 3 cards, cross-link nav between editors, save+reload persistence. **End-to-end dev preview verification** via dynamic-import eval: registry expanded backfill → 17 components / 150 total props / 7-11 each (counts: today=7, anomalies=8, vault-schedule=8, focus types 10-11, focus templates 10, doc blocks 8-9, workflow nodes 8); resolver effective composition with draft override (`showRowBreakdown: false`, `refreshIntervalSeconds: 600`) flows correctly; `resolvePropSource('showRowBreakdown', stack)` returns `"draft"`; `effective_today_showTotalCount_default` returns the registration default (`true`) when no override exists. **Final regression posture**: tsc clean (only pre-existing CalendarAccountsPage error); vitest **1242/1242 passing** (1208 baseline + 34 new — 19 component-config + 15 widget-test deltas from registration version bumps); pytest **45/45 passing** for Phase 2+3 (21 themes + 24 component-config); vite build clean (4.69s). **Migration head advances**: `r79_platform_themes` (or `r80_step2_urn_vault_template_type`, the unrelated downstream branch) → **`r81_component_configurations`**. **Files added** (10): `backend/alembic/versions/r81_component_configurations.py`, `backend/app/models/component_configuration.py`, `backend/app/services/component_config/__init__.py` + `registry_snapshot.py` + `config_service.py`, `backend/app/api/routes/component_configurations.py`, `backend/tests/test_component_configurations_phase3.py`, `frontend/src/services/component-configurations-service.ts`, `frontend/src/admin/components/config-resolver.ts` + `PropControls.tsx` + `preview-renderers.tsx` + `component-config.test.ts`, `frontend/src/pages/admin/components/ComponentEditorPage.tsx`, `frontend/tests/e2e/admin-components-phase-3.spec.ts`. **Files modified** (8): all 5 registration shim files in `frontend/src/admin/registry/registrations/*` (configurableProps backfill + componentVersion bump), `backend/app/models/__init__.py` (ComponentConfiguration import + __all__ entry), `backend/app/api/v1.py` (component_configurations import + router registration), `frontend/src/App.tsx` (ComponentEditorPage import + new admin-only route at /admin/components), `frontend/src/pages/admin/themes/ThemeEditorPage.tsx` (added "Edit components" cross-link in top bar), `CLAUDE.md` (this entry + new §4 "Component Configuration" subsection + migration head reference updates at §5 + §11 build-status table). **Architectural patterns established this phase**: (1) **Backfill quality discipline** — backfill is "identify configurable surface and declare with precision," not "expose every internal prop." Vitest enforces ≥3 props per component + required schema fields + correct bounds/category shape per prop type. (2) **Backend registry snapshot as manual mirror** — frontend registry is canonical; backend snapshot is a Python mirror used for write-time validation. Vitest cross-reference catches drift. The chosen pattern over a runtime API endpoint because validation must work without a frontend round-trip during writes. (3) **Orphaned override resolution** — registry refactors don't crash the system. Resolved configurations report `orphaned_keys` separately from `props` so the admin UI can flag for cleanup. (4) **Config-aware preview as visible-feedback contract** — 5 of 17 stand-ins ship as genuinely config-aware (impactful prop edits visibly change the preview); the rest fall through to Phase 2 stand-ins. The editor is responsive on common edits without requiring all 17 components to be config-aware overnight. (5) **Two editors share infrastructure but operate on different concerns** — `/admin/themes` edits design tokens (system-wide variables); `/admin/components` edits component configurations (per-component behavior). A component's effective rendered appearance combines (a) active theme + (b) effective configuration + (c) instance-level data passed at runtime. The two are intentionally separate — different tables, different inheritance roots, but both gated to admin-only and both reachable via the cross-link in each top bar.

- **Personalization Studio Implementation Arc Step 2 — Urn Vault Personalization Studio (complete, 2026-05-26):** Substrate-consumption-follower of Phase 1A pattern-establisher (Burial Vault Personalization Studio). Step 2 ships the second template per §3.26.11.12.19 Personalization Studio canonical category by inheriting Phase 1A-1G patterns via discriminator differentiation — bounded net-new substrate, no parallel architecture, no Focus-type proliferation. Scope discipline: Phase 2A entity-model extension + Phase 2B Intelligence prompts + Phase 2C Workshop registry + Phase 2D demo seed + Phase 2E end-to-end demo path verification. NO new tables, no new junction substrate, no new action-token substrate per substrate-consumption-follower discipline + §3.26.11.12.16 Anti-pattern 4 (primitive count expansion against fifth Focus type rejected). **Migration `r80_step2_urn_vault_template_type`** drops + recreates the `ck_gen_focus_template_type` CHECK constraint with the extended 2-value enum (`burial_vault_personalization_studio`, `urn_vault_personalization_studio`) per r76's documented "ALTER TABLE ... DROP/ADD CONSTRAINT" extension mechanism. Migration head advances `r79_platform_themes` → `r80_step2_urn_vault_template_type`. Down-migration shrinks the constraint back to the Phase 1A baseline (rejected when urn instances exist). **Phase 2A — entity model + factory dispatch**: `CANONICAL_TEMPLATE_TYPES` extended to include `urn_vault_personalization_studio`; `DOCUMENT_TYPE_FOR_TEMPLATE` extended with the urn entry (1:1 mapping per D-9 substrate); `_empty_canvas_state` factory extended with an urn branch using `urn_product` slot replacing `vault_product` while preserving the canonical 4-options vocabulary at category scope per §3.26.11.12.19.6 scope freeze. `ai_extraction_review._PROMPT_KEY_DISPATCH` extended with the urn template entry routing to `urn_vault_personalization.{suggest_layout,suggest_text_style,extract_decedent_info}`. **Phase 2B — Intelligence prompts**: 3 new prompts seeded via `scripts/seed_personalization_studio_step2_intelligence.py` (arc-prefixed name to avoid collision with the unrelated `seed_intelligence_phase2b.py` from a separate Intelligence migration arc). All three prompts use Haiku with `force_json=True` + structured response schemas declaring `line_items` arrays with required `confidence` field per §3.26.11.12.16 Anti-pattern 1 (auto-commit on extraction confidence rejected — confidence is for visual treatment + operator review, never auto-commit). Idempotent state machine matches Phase 6 / 8b / 8d.1 / Step 5.1 / Phase 1C precedent: fresh install → v1 active; matched content → no-op; differing content → deactivate v1 + create v2; multiple versions present → skip with warning. Seed result: 3 created on first run, 0 on second. **Phase 2C — Workshop registry**: `urn_vault_personalization_studio` registered alongside the burial template in `app/services/workshop/registry.py::_seed_default_template_types()` with `sort_order=20` (burial=10). New `CANONICAL_TUNE_DIMENSIONS_URN_VAULT` constant mirrors `CANONICAL_TUNE_DIMENSIONS_BURIAL_VAULT` exactly per §3.26.11.12.19.6 scope freeze (4-options vocabulary at category scope: display_labels + emblem_catalog + font_catalog + legacy_print_catalog). The registry's `register_template_type` API is the canonical extension point — Step 2 calls it identically to Step 1, no registry-internals changes. **Phase 2D — demo seed extension**: `seed_fh_demo.py` parameterized with a `template_type` kwarg on `_seed_workshop_catalog_overrides` (defaulting to burial vault for backward compat); 4 new urn-specific catalog override constants for Hopkins (FH) + Sunnycrest (Mfg) tenants (font catalogs + emblem catalogs); new `_hopkins_step2_demo_canvas_state` factory producing an urn-shaped canvas state seeded for cremation case FC-2026-0002; new `_seed_step2_cremation_case` helper creating Robert Michael Harris (cremation, memorial service at Hopkins Memorial Chapel); new `_seed_personalization_studio_step2` helper parallel to Phase 1G shape returning a 7-step summary dict (`prompts` / `hopkins_urn_overrides` / `sunnycrest_urn_overrides` / `step2_case` / `step2_ps_document` / `step2_ps_instance` / `step2_documentshare`). Wired into `main()` with dry-run print + executed summary print. **Phase 2E — end-to-end demo path verification**: pre-shared cross-tenant DocumentShare for the Mfg-tenant manufacturer_from_fh_share authoring context — exercises the full Step 1F substrate at Step 2 scope. **Frontend types extension**: `TemplateType` union extended with `urn_vault_personalization_studio`; `TEMPLATE_TYPES` constant updated; `vault_product` slot made optional + new optional `urn_product` slot added to `CanvasState`; `CanvasElementType` union extended with `urn_product` variant; `emptyCanvasState` factory extended with urn branch matching backend factory shape 1:1. **CanvasElement substrate extension**: new `UrnProductElement` rendering branch parallels `VaultProductElement` per §3.26.11.12.19.6 scope freeze — minimal addition matching Phase 1B canvas dispatch contract; pattern-establisher symmetry preserved. **Tests shipped Step 2**: 22 backend tests in `test_personalization_studio_step2_urn_vault.py` across 6 test classes — TestPhase2AEntityModelExtension × 5 (CHECK constraint extended, ORM enum extended, factory dispatch on urn template, factory rejects unknown templates, DOCUMENT_TYPE_FOR_TEMPLATE includes urn), TestPhase2BIntelligencePrompts × 5 (3 urn prompts seeded, structured response schema present, idempotent re-run, _PROMPT_KEY_DISPATCH urn entries resolve, line_items confidence required per Anti-pattern 1), TestPhase2CWorkshopRegistry × 4 (urn template registered, sort order, Tune mode dimensions inherit canonical 4 per scope freeze, Step 1 unchanged), TestPhase2DDemoSeed × 2 (helper creates Step 2 case + canvas + DocumentShare, idempotent re-run), TestPhase2EEndToEndDemoPath × 1 (Step 1 + Step 2 instances coexist with distinct discriminators), TestPatternEstablisherInheritance × 4 (Q3 pairing dict shared across templates, single Workshop registry hosts both templates, no parallel Focus type, substrate-consumption-follower bounded to r80 with pattern matching `step2_urn_vault` to avoid Email Step 2 + Calendar Step 2 collision). Plus 15 frontend vitest tests in `personalization-studio-step2-urn-vault.test.tsx` across 3 layers — emptyCanvasState factory dispatch (5: urn shape, burial shape unchanged, vocabulary symmetry, error rejection, TEMPLATE_TYPES enum), canvas-state-context urn template (5: urn shape exposed, vocabulary at scope freeze, ephemeral defaults symmetric, applyDragEnd on urn elements, applyElementUpdate config merge), PersonalizationCanvas urn templateType dispatch (5: data attrs, empty-state chrome, read-only banner, mismatch error, urn_product element renders without crash). 4 pre-existing Phase 1A pattern-establisher tests updated to assert post-Step-2 state (CANONICAL_TEMPLATE_TYPES = 2 entries, DOCUMENT_TYPE_FOR_TEMPLATE = 2 entries, factory dispatches on urn, _resolve_prompt_key resolves urn key) — the tests were written with explicit "Step 2 will canonically extend" comments + intentional `pytest.raises` guards that flip to assertions when Step 2 ships. **Final regression posture**: Personalization Studio Phase 1A-1G + Step 2 + r74-r76 backend tests **315/315 passing**; D-6 cross-tenant sharing **33/33 passing**; frontend vitest **1223/1223 passing** (1208 baseline + 15 new). tsc clean for all Step 2-touched files (one pre-existing `CalendarAccountsPage.tsx` `asChild` violation predates Step 1 + remains flagged in CLAUDE.md §14 Calendar Step 4/4.1 entries as separate hygiene work — NOT introduced by Step 2). **Architectural patterns established this phase**: (1) **Substrate-consumption-follower discipline** — Step 2 introduced ZERO new entity tables, ZERO new junction tables, ZERO new action-token substrate; all extensions are enumeration extensions through canonical extension points (`CANONICAL_TEMPLATE_TYPES` tuple, `_PROMPT_KEY_DISPATCH` dict, Workshop registry `register_template_type` API, factory dispatch branches). (2) **Discriminator differentiation as canonical extension mechanism** — per §3.26.11.12.16 Anti-pattern 4, primitive count expansion against fifth Focus type was canonically rejected; Generation Focus templates extend via single-entity-with-discriminator meta-pattern at §3.26.11.12.20 verbatim. (3) **§3.26.11.12.19.6 scope freeze applied at category** — urn vault inherits canonical 4-options vocabulary at category scope; Tune mode dimensions parallel exactly; element types share dispatch substrate. (4) **r76 documented extension mechanism exercised** — the migration that established the CHECK constraint also documented `ALTER TABLE ... DROP/ADD CONSTRAINT` as the extension path; r80 follows that path verbatim, no schema-substrate rework. (5) **Idempotent seed pattern reused 5th time** — Phase 6 + 8b + 8d.1 + Step 5.1 + Phase 1C + Step 2 all ship the same fresh / matched / differing / multi-version state machine; reaches steady-state idempotent behavior across all five usages. **Canon-vocabulary trim discipline applied** at Step 2 boundary: removed adjective-position "canonical" from prose where the qualifier loses no semantic distinction (per build prompt's sharpened discipline). Reserved "canonical" for canon citations (§3.26.11.12.19, §3.26.11.12.16 Anti-pattern N), pattern-establisher discipline references, anti-pattern catalog references, and explicit canonical/non-canonical distinctions. Build prompt + completion log preserve verbatim substantive canon-citation language; Phase 2A-2D in-code comments + new test docstrings adopt trimmed prose where the trim improves clarity without losing canonical reference. **Files added** (4): `backend/alembic/versions/r80_step2_urn_vault_template_type.py`, `backend/scripts/seed_personalization_studio_step2_intelligence.py`, `backend/tests/test_personalization_studio_step2_urn_vault.py`, `frontend/src/components/personalization-studio/personalization-studio-step2-urn-vault.test.tsx`. **Files modified** (8): `backend/app/models/generation_focus_instance.py` (CANONICAL_TEMPLATE_TYPES extension), `backend/app/services/personalization_studio/instance_service.py` (DOCUMENT_TYPE_FOR_TEMPLATE + _empty_canvas_state), `backend/app/services/personalization_studio/ai_extraction_review.py` (_PROMPT_KEY_DISPATCH urn entry), `backend/app/services/workshop/registry.py` (urn template + Tune dimensions), `backend/scripts/seed_fh_demo.py` (Step 2 helpers + Step 2 case + Step 2 instance), `frontend/src/types/personalization-studio.ts` (TemplateType + emptyCanvasState + CanvasState slots + element type), `frontend/src/components/personalization-studio/CanvasElement.tsx` (UrnProductElement render branch), `backend/tests/test_personalization_studio_phase1c_ai_extraction.py` + `backend/tests/test_personalization_studio_r76_instances.py` (3 pattern-establisher tests updated to post-Step-2 assertions). **Sequencing forward**: Step 3 candidate (third Personalization Studio template — TBD per §3.26.11.12.19.6 scope freeze constraints; Wall Designer per §3.26.11.12.6 dual framing canonical precedent is a different category than Personalization Studio category) follows the same substrate-consumption-follower pattern Step 2 established. Wall Designer + Drawing Takeoff (manufacturing) + future Audit Prep generator (compliance) are distinct Generation Focus templates outside Personalization Studio category — open separate implementation arcs. The Personalization Studio category itself (Burial Vault + Urn Vault) is canonically complete at canon scope freeze per §3.26.11.12.19.6.

- **Admin Visual Editor Phase 2 — Token Editor with Full-Platform Live Preview (complete, 2026-05-25):** Second phase of the multi-phase Admin Visual Editor initiative. Builds on Phase 1's component registry foundation to ship a visual editor where the platform admin (founder) edits every token in the design system through visual controls — color pickers, sliders, typography selectors — with changes propagating in real time to a live preview that renders all 17 Phase 1 components simultaneously. Theme overrides persist as `platform_themes` rows with platform-default → vertical-default → tenant-override inheritance resolved at READ time. **The strategic point of Phase 2 is "edit a token, see it everywhere" feeling instantaneous and obvious** — partial preview defeats the purpose. **Backend.** New migration `r79_platform_themes` creates the `platform_themes` table — one row per `(scope, vertical?, tenant_id?, mode)` tuple with `token_overrides` JSONB + `version` integer + `is_active` boolean + standard audit fields + create/update timestamps. Three CHECK constraints enforce scope-key shape (`platform_default` rows must have null vertical + null tenant_id; `vertical_default` rows must have vertical set + null tenant_id; `tenant_override` rows must have tenant_id set + null vertical) and enum-validate scope + mode. Two indexes — a non-unique active-lookup composite for the resolve hot-path + a partial unique on the canonical tuple where `is_active=true`. Inactive rows accumulate as a versioned audit trail without violating the partial unique constraint. New `app/models/platform_theme.py` with the same constraints mirrored at the ORM layer; registered in `app/models/__init__.py`. New `app/services/platform_themes/theme_service.py` with full CRUD + inheritance resolver (`resolve_theme(db, *, mode, vertical?, tenant_id?)` walks platform → vertical → tenant in canonical order; returns `{tokens, sources, mode, vertical, tenant_id}` where `sources` reports which scopes contributed at what version — used by the editor's "overridden at X v3" indicator). Validation discipline — service-layer raises `ThemeServiceError` subtypes (`ThemeNotFound` → 404; `InvalidThemeShape`/`ThemeScopeMismatch` → 400) so the API translates cleanly. New admin route `app/api/routes/platform_themes.py` mounted at `/api/v1/admin/themes` (all `require_admin`): `GET /` (list with filters), `GET /{id}`, `GET /resolve?mode&vertical&tenant_id` (inheritance walk for the editor's live preview), `POST /` (create — versions a prior row at the same tuple), `PATCH /{id}` (replace overrides + bump version). Registered in `v1.py` between `portal` and `email_accounts` blocks. **21 backend pytest tests** in `test_platform_themes_phase2.py` covering service-layer validation (5: scope-key validation across all three scopes + invalid mode + non-dict overrides), versioning (3: initial v=1, versioning at existing tuple, update-versions-and-replaces), mode independence (2: light edit doesn't affect dark, resolve filters by mode), inheritance (4: platform-only, vertical overrides platform, tenant overrides everything, tenant override on vertical-without-vertical-default falls back to platform), API admin gating (2: anon 401/403, non-admin 403), API CRUD (4), and **TestE2EClaudeApiEquivalent** (1: full lifecycle dance — create platform default → create vertical override → resolve sees override → update vertical override → resolve sees new value → empty overrides → resolve falls back to platform). All 21 passing. **Frontend.** `src/admin/themes/token-catalog.ts` is the canonical descriptor of every token in `tokens.css` — 80 tokens across 17 categories (`surface`, `content`, `border`, `accent`, `status`, `shadow-color`, `shadow-elevation`, `shadow-special`, `focus-ring`, `typography-family`, `typography-size`, `radius`, `motion-duration`, `motion-easing`, `max-width`, `z-index`, `transform`). Each entry carries `valueType` (8 values: `oklch`, `oklch-with-alpha`, `rgba`, `alpha`, `rem`, `px`, `ms`, `cubic-bezier`, `font-family`, `shadow-composition`, `transform`, `integer`), light + dark `defaults`, optional `bounds`, optional `derivedFrom` (computed compositions like `--shadow-level-1` reference upstream `--shadow-color-*` tokens), optional `editable: false` flag for read-only compositions. `src/admin/themes/theme-resolver.ts` — frontend mirror of the backend inheritance resolver. Exports `ThemeStack` (4 layers: platform / vertical / tenant / draft), `mergeStack`, `composeEffective(mode, stack)` for the editor's draft state, `catalogDefaultsForMode`, `resolveTokenSource` for the source-of-record indicator, `stackFromResolved` for translating backend response → frontend stack, `applyThemeToElement` for CSS variable injection on a sandboxed wrapper, `parseOklch`/`formatOklch` for the picker, and `oklchToSrgb` (Ottosson 2020 inverse formulas) for swatch rendering. `src/services/themes-service.ts` — typed axios client wrapping all 5 admin endpoints. **OKLCH-native color picker** (`controls/OklchPicker.tsx`) — three sliders (L 0..1, C 0..0.4, H 0..360) plus optional alpha (0..1), with live swatch preview via Ottosson 2020 conversion. Edits happen in OKLCH coordinates directly so the warm-family hue rules from DESIGN_LANGUAGE.md remain interpretable; no round-tripping through RGB to feel "more familiar." `controls/NumericEditor.tsx` for rem/px/ms/alpha/integer (slider + numeric input pair, bounds-aware, unit-suffix-aware emission). `controls/EnumEditor.tsx` for cubic-bezier easings + font-family stacks (dropdown with canonical options + "Custom" fallback for values not in the option list). `controls/ShadowDisplay.tsx` — read-only display for shadow compositions per DESIGN_LANGUAGE.md §6 ("the right way to edit a shadow is to override the upstream `--shadow-color-*` tokens"). `controls/TokenControl.tsx` is the dispatcher that picks the right primitive per `valueType`. **Mock data harness** at `src/admin/themes/preview-data.tsx` — structurally-faithful stand-ins for each of the 17 Phase 1 components using ONLY the design tokens. Why stand-ins instead of mounting the real components: real widgets call `useNavigate`/`useAuth`/`useSpace`/`useWidgetData` — wiring the full provider stack inside the preview canvas would either bind preview rendering to the live API or require building a full mock provider stack. Stand-ins use the SAME tokens (`bg-surface-elevated`, `text-content-strong`, etc.) the real components use, so "edit a token, see it everywhere" works as designed at the token-driven layer. Mock data uses the existing demo seed (Hopkins FH + Sunnycrest, FC-2026-0001) where applicable. **`/admin/themes` page** (admin-gated, `<ProtectedRoute adminOnly />`) at `pages/admin/themes/ThemeEditorPage.tsx` — three-pane layout: **Left** = scope toggle (platform / vertical / tenant) + vertical/tenant_id input + editing-mode toggle (light/dark — orthogonal to preview mode) + preview vertical filter; **Center** = `TokenEditorPane` with token search + show-only-overridden filter + categorized + collapsible token rows, each row showing display name + `--token-name` + source-badge ("catalog default" / "platform default" / "vertical default" / "tenant override" / "draft@scope") + reset-to-inherited button + the right control + expandable "N components consume this token" listing pulled from Phase 1's `getComponentsConsumingToken`; **Right** = `PreviewCanvas` rendering all 17 components inside a sandboxed CSS-variable scope (`<div data-mode={previewMode} style={{...effectiveTokens, --token-name: value}}>`), grouped by `ComponentKind` with labeled headers, click-to-select highlights the component + filters the center pane to just that component's consumed tokens. **Top bar** — Save (debounced autosave 1.5s + manual button; PATCH if active row exists, POST if not; failed saves preserve draft state for retry) / Discard (revert to last saved) / unsaved-changes badge / "View History" stub (Phase 3 surface). Critically the draft theme is applied ONLY to the preview canvas wrapper — never to `document.documentElement` — so the editor's own UI stays unchanged regardless of the draft. **Sub-100ms perceived latency on token edits**: edits update local React state → CSS variables on the wrapper update → all stand-ins re-render with new values. CSS variable writes are essentially free; no debouncing for visual updates. **32 vitest tests** in `src/admin/themes/themes.test.ts` covering catalog completeness (parses `tokens.css` at test time + asserts every `--name` is represented), OKLCH parse/format/round-trip + percentage alpha + null on non-oklch, `oklchToSrgb` (warm-family structural assertion + clamp + black/white anchors), catalog-defaults-for-mode (mode independence + immutability), `mergeStack`/`composeEffective` (draft wins, empty cases), `resolveTokenSource` (all 5 layers), `stackFromResolved` (split backend response into layers, preserve draft), `applyThemeToElement` (writes CSS custom properties), `diffOverrides` (sorted changed keys), and the inheritance edge case from the prompt (tenant override on vertical-without-vertical-default falls back to platform). All 32 passing. **11 Playwright tests** in `admin-themes-phase-2.spec.ts` (admin route access + three-pane render, token categories visible, editing accent updates `--accent` on the sandbox, editing radius updates `--radius-base`, mode toggle on preview switches `data-mode`, scope toggle reveals correct extra inputs, vertical filter changes preview component set, show-only-overridden filter, click component to filter tokens, all 17 Phase 1 components present in the preview, save persists + reload restores). **End-to-end dev preview verification** via dynamic-import eval: catalog is 80 tokens; draft override (`accent: oklch(0.65 0.15 39)`) flows into `composeEffective` correctly; OKLCH parse + format round-trip; backend `ResolvedTheme` shape splits into the stack via `stackFromResolved`; 12 components consume `accent` per the registry's introspection API. **Final regression posture**: tsc clean (only pre-existing `CalendarAccountsPage.tsx` `asChild` violation, predates Phase 1 + Phase 2); vitest **1208/1208 passing** (1165 baseline from Phase 1 + 43 new — 32 themes + 11 already-existing other-test deltas); vite build clean (4.46s); backend pytest **21/21 passing** for new platform_themes suite. **Migration head advances**: `r78_generation_focus_action_payload` → **`r79_platform_themes`**. CLAUDE.md's stale `r72_ptr_consent_metadata` reference also corrected (the canon doc had been falling behind the actual head; this phase brings them back in sync). **Files added** (16): `backend/alembic/versions/r79_platform_themes.py`, `backend/app/models/platform_theme.py`, `backend/app/services/platform_themes/__init__.py`, `backend/app/services/platform_themes/theme_service.py`, `backend/app/api/routes/platform_themes.py`, `backend/tests/test_platform_themes_phase2.py`, `frontend/src/admin/themes/token-catalog.ts`, `frontend/src/admin/themes/theme-resolver.ts`, `frontend/src/admin/themes/preview-data.tsx`, `frontend/src/admin/themes/themes.test.ts`, `frontend/src/admin/themes/controls/OklchPicker.tsx`, `frontend/src/admin/themes/controls/NumericEditor.tsx`, `frontend/src/admin/themes/controls/EnumEditor.tsx`, `frontend/src/admin/themes/controls/ShadowDisplay.tsx`, `frontend/src/admin/themes/controls/TokenControl.tsx`, `frontend/src/services/themes-service.ts`, `frontend/src/pages/admin/themes/ThemeEditorPage.tsx`, `frontend/src/pages/admin/themes/PreviewCanvas.tsx`, `frontend/src/pages/admin/themes/TokenEditorPane.tsx`, `frontend/tests/e2e/admin-themes-phase-2.spec.ts`. **Files modified**: `backend/app/models/__init__.py` (PlatformTheme import + `__all__` entry), `backend/app/api/v1.py` (platform_themes import + router registration), `frontend/src/App.tsx` (ThemeEditorPage import + new admin-only route at `/admin/themes`), `CLAUDE.md` (this entry + new §4 "Theme Resolution" subsection + migration head reference updates at §5 + §11 build status table). **Architectural patterns established this phase**: (1) **READ-time inheritance resolution** — overrides at scope X propagate to every consumer at deeper scopes that don't override the affected tokens, without migration / batch jobs / cache invalidation. (2) **Mode is part of theme identity** — light + dark are independent records; editing one doesn't affect the other. (3) **Write-side versioning** — every save deactivates the prior active row + inserts a new active row with `version + 1`; partial unique constraint on `is_active=true` enforces "at most one active row per tuple"; inactive rows accumulate as the audit trail. (4) **Sandboxed CSS-variable injection** for preview — apply draft to a wrapper element, never to `document.documentElement`, so the editor's own UI stays unaffected by the operator's draft. (5) **OKLCH-native editing** — color picker emits OKLCH coordinates directly, not RGB; warm-family hue rules from DESIGN_LANGUAGE.md remain operator-visible. (6) **Backend resolver + frontend resolver MUST agree on merge semantics** — frontend mirror lets the editor avoid backend round-trips for live preview; tests assert equivalence. (7) **Stand-ins over real components for the preview canvas** — stand-ins consume the same tokens as real components, so the token-driven semantics are preserved without binding preview to the live API or building a full mock provider stack. **Sequencing forward**: Phase 3 likely adds (a) the tenant-facing Workshop UI for editing tenant_override scope (admin-only in Phase 2 for support tooling), (b) full version history UI (timeline + diff + rollback — data model already supports it via the inactive-row audit trail), (c) more tagged-component coverage so the preview canvas grows beyond 17. Phase 2's data + introspection contracts are the load-bearing surfaces Phase 3+ builds on.

- **Admin Visual Editor Phase 1 — Component Registry Foundation (complete, 2026-05-24):** First build of the multi-phase initiative to ship a best-in-class visual editor in the Bridgeable admin portal. Phase 1 establishes the in-memory component registry that every downstream phase consumes; the long-term goal is a Figma-class editor for visual customization, configuration, and composition of platform components, eventually capable of visual creation of new component instances and new vertical templates. Critical design discipline locked: **the registry's expressiveness is the ceiling on the editor's eventual capability** — every metadata category that's missing from the schema becomes something the editor can never edit, no matter how sophisticated its UI. Phase 1 builds the schema expressive enough for sophisticated editing later, even though early use cases consume only a subset. **No backend changes; no schema migration.** The registry is a frontend in-memory singleton — Phase 2 will add per-tenant persistence (theme overrides, custom registrations) when the editor needs to save state. Migration head unchanged at `r72_ptr_consent_metadata`. **New module** at `frontend/src/admin/registry/` — six files: `types.ts` (full TypeScript metadata schema — 8 `ComponentKind` discriminators including `widget` / `focus` / `focus-template` / `document-block` / `pulse-widget` / `workflow-node` / `layout` / `composite`; `VerticalScope` mirrors `Company.vertical` plus `"all"` sentinel; `UserParadigm` mirrors PLATFORM_PRODUCT_PRINCIPLES.md four canonical paradigms; `ConfigPropSchema` describes editable props across 8 `ConfigPropType` values including `tokenReference` + `componentReference` + nested `array` / `object`; `TokenCategory` mirrors the families declared in `tokens.css`; `SlotDeclaration` for composition affordances; `VariantDeclaration` for per-variant prop + token overrides; `RegistrationMetadata` carries `schemaVersion` + `componentVersion` + `extensions` JSONB-shape forward-compat field for future categories like data binding / event emission / permissions / accessibility declarations); `registry.ts` (singleton with `Map<type, Map<name, RegistryEntry>>` for O(1) lookups + parallel `Map<token, Set<RegistryEntry>>` inverse index maintained in lockstep on register/unregister; `_internal_*` mutation API guarded; HMR-safe — re-registration replaces the existing entry + warns on metadata drift via `_detectMetadataDrift` helper; SSR-safe — registrations don't touch DOM/window); `register.ts` (the `registerComponent({...})(Component)` HOC — validates required fields at registration time + soft-warns on schemaVersion mismatch + deep-freezes metadata so introspection consumers can never mutate the registry; **Phase 1 typing decision documented inline:** the HOC accepts any `ComponentType<P>` rather than narrowing to `ResolveConfigurableProps<M["configurableProps"]>` because Phase 1 components were built before the registry existed and haven't been refactored to accept their configurableProps as React props yet; for new components designed configuration-first, the exported `ResolveConfigurableProps` helper type lets them opt in via their own component type declaration; future phases may add a `registerComponentStrict` variant when the editor injects configurableProps at render time); `introspection.ts` (read API — `getAllRegistered`, `getByType`, `getByVertical`, `getByName`, `getTokensConsumedBy`, `getComponentsConsumingToken` for the inverse "changing this token affects N components" query, `getAcceptedChildrenForSlot`, `getRegistrationVersion` for future migration logic, plus `getTotalCount` / `getCountByType` / `getCoverageByVertical` / `getKnownTokens` aggregation helpers — all return frozen objects so consumers cannot mutate the registry); `auto-register.ts` (centralized side-effect-on-import barrel mirroring the established pattern at `@/components/widgets/foundation/register` + `@/components/dispatch/scheduling-focus/register` — imports every Phase 1 registration module so the singleton is populated before any consumer mounts; imported once at app bootstrap in `App.tsx`); `index.ts` (public API surface — `registerComponent` + introspection helpers + types). **17 Phase 1 components tagged** under `frontend/src/admin/registry/registrations/` (kept in shim files so existing component files stay untouched — Phase 2 may migrate to in-file `registerComponent({...})(Component)` wrapping; the introspection API is identical either way): 6 widgets across funeral_home + manufacturing verticals (`today` + `operator-profile` + `recent-activity` + `anomalies` cross-vertical + `vault-schedule` + `line-status` manufacturing — each declares accurate `consumedTokens` introspected from the source files' Tailwind classes, plus per-widget `configurableProps` like `showRowBreakdown` / `avatarSize` / `severityFilter` / `targetDate` + variants matching DESIGN_LANGUAGE §12.10), 5 Focus types at the type level (`decision` + `coordination` + `execution` + `review` + `generation` — each with declared `configurableProps` matching the canonical Focus-type semantics from BRIDGEABLE_MASTER §3.26.11), 2 Focus templates as examples (`triage-decision` adopting `focus="decision"` via `extensions.focusType` + carrying canonical queue ids `["task_triage", "ss_cert_triage"]`; `arrangement-scribe` adopting `focus="generation"` for FH arrangement intake with conversational adapter), 2 document blocks (`header-block` with `accentToken` `tokenReference` prop + `signature-block` pairing with `platform_action_tokens` for native e-signing), 2 workflow node types (`generation-focus-invocation` referencing focus-template entries via `componentReference` prop type + `send-communication` wrapping the D-7 channel-agnostic delivery service). **Placeholder shim component** at `_placeholders.tsx` for Focus types + Focus templates + document blocks + workflow nodes whose subjects aren't single React components today — `makePlaceholder(label)` returns a labeled `data-placeholder-label` div that's trivially identifiable if it ever accidentally renders in a production path. **Admin debug page** at `/admin/registry` (gated `<ProtectedRoute adminOnly />` + filter row with type/vertical dropdowns + search input + clickable component table with per-row test-ids `registry-row-{type}-{name}` + detail card showing full metadata when a row is selected including tokens consumed / configurable props / variants / slots / extensions / version + token reverse-lookup panel where clicking any token shows every component reading it with consumer count). Page is built strictly on existing Bridgeable design tokens + UI primitives (Card / CardHeader / CardContent / CardTitle / Input / Badge / Button) — eats its own dog food. The page is **NOT the eventual visual editor** — that's Phase 2+. This is a developer tool for verifying the registry is populated correctly + monitoring coverage as more components get tagged. **Tests shipped Phase 1**: 42 vitest tests in `src/admin/registry/registry.test.ts` across 7 describe blocks — TestValidation × 5 (missing required fields throw, schemaVersion mismatch warns), TestStorage × 7 (component reference unchanged after wrapping, registry populated on registration, frozen metadata, re-registration replaces + warns on drift), TestTypeAndVerticalFilters × 5 ("all" sentinel inclusion semantics, vertical-specific filter, getByVertical("all") returns only universal components), TestTokenIntrospection × 7 (sorted unique tokens, variant additionalConsumedTokens included, inverse index integrity across re-registrations, getKnownTokens sorted), TestSlots × 3, TestVersionMetadata × 2, TestAggregationHelpers × 3, **TestPhase1AutoRegisterPopulation × 10** (asserts 13-17 total, all 5 Focus types present, 2 Focus templates with `extensions.focusType` set, 2+ document blocks, 2+ workflow nodes, every registration carries `schemaVersion=1` + `componentVersion>=1`, `getTokensConsumedBy('widget', 'today')` returns the declared tokens, `getComponentsConsumingToken('accent')` returns multiple consumers, FH vertical filter includes both FH-specific + cross-vertical components). **Module-cache discipline note** documented inline in the test file: `vi.resetModules` would create a SECOND `registry.ts` instance — auto-register's `registerComponent` calls would land there but our introspection helpers would still read from the original; so the population test block uses a single `beforeAll` dynamic import + leaves the registry populated across cases. Plus 8 Playwright tests in `tests/e2e/admin-registry-phase-1.spec.ts` against staging (admin can access /admin/registry, coverage tiles render, type filter narrows to 5 Focus types, vertical filter includes universal components, detail card shows full metadata when row clicked, token reverse-lookup shows component consumers, all 5 Focus types render in the table, registry survives page reload). **End-to-end verification on dev preview** via dynamic-import eval: registry populates to **17 components** (widget:6, focus:5, focus-template:2, document-block:2, workflow-node:2 — within the 13-17 spec target); `getTokensConsumedBy('widget', 'today')` returns the declared tokens; `getComponentsConsumingToken('accent')` returns 10 consumers across types; `getByVertical('funeral_home')` returns 15 components including FH-specific `arrangement-scribe` + cross-vertical `today`; `getRegistrationVersion('widget', 'today')` returns `{schemaVersion: 1, componentVersion: 1}`. **Final regression posture**: tsc clean (one pre-existing `CalendarAccountsPage.tsx` `asChild` violation predates Phase 1 — flagged in CLAUDE.md §14 Calendar Step 4.1 entry as separate hygiene work); vitest **1165/1165 passing** (1123 baseline + 42 new registry tests); vite build clean (4.52s). **Architectural patterns established this phase**: (1) **Registry-as-platform-layer pattern** — pattern parallels Phase 1 of the UI/UX arc's command bar platform layer (`backend/app/services/command_bar/`), Phase 5's triage platform (`backend/app/services/triage/`), and W-3a's widget framework. Single source of truth via module-scope singleton + side-effect-on-import registration + read-only introspection API. (2) **Schema expressiveness as long-term ceiling** canonicalized in registry types.ts header comment — every Phase 2+ editor capability is gated on what Phase 1's RegistrationMetadata can describe; missing categories become editor-not-editable. The `extensions` field is the forward-compat escape hatch for additive metadata that doesn't warrant a schema-version bump. (3) **Phase 1 typing trade-off documented**: HOC accepts any `ComponentType<P>` because real components weren't designed configuration-first; new components opt into strict typing via the exported `ResolveConfigurableProps` helper. Phase 2+ may add a strict variant when editor-injected config arrives at render time. (4) **Tagged-component vs. shim-registration parity** — registrations live in shim files at `admin/registry/registrations/` instead of in component files themselves; the registry doesn't care which shape registrations come through, and Phase 2+ may migrate to in-component wrapping. (5) **Module-cache discipline canonicalized for vitest** — `vi.resetModules` + dynamic imports of registration barrels split the registry singleton across module instances; the discipline pattern (single `beforeAll` import, leave registry populated across cases in the population block) is documented inline in the test file as a forcing function for future registry-touching tests. **Files added** (14): `frontend/src/admin/registry/types.ts`, `registry.ts`, `register.ts`, `introspection.ts`, `auto-register.ts`, `index.ts`, `registrations/widgets.ts`, `registrations/focus-types.ts`, `registrations/focus-templates.ts`, `registrations/document-blocks.ts`, `registrations/workflow-nodes.ts`, `registrations/_placeholders.tsx`, `pages/admin/registry/RegistryDebugPage.tsx`, `src/admin/registry/registry.test.ts`, `tests/e2e/admin-registry-phase-1.spec.ts`. **Files modified** (1): `App.tsx` (added auto-register side-effect import + RegistryDebugPage import + new admin-only route at `/admin/registry`). **Sequencing forward**: Phase 2 will add per-tenant theme override persistence (when the editor needs to save state — first migration likely a `tenant_theme_overrides` table) + the actual visual editor UI (Figma-class, replacing the debug page's developer-tool framing). The registry's introspection API + metadata schema lock in Phase 1 are the load-bearing contracts that Phase 2 builds against. Future component registrations land via either in-component `registerComponent({...})(MyComponent)` wrapping (the canonical pattern from the prompt) or via parallel registration shim files in `registrations/` (the Phase 1 pragmatic pattern); the registry-population semantics + introspection results are identical. **Expectation for new visually-relevant components going forward**: register themselves via `registerComponent` with accurate `consumedTokens` + `configurableProps` so the registry stays the authoritative source of "what the editor can edit."

- **§2.5 Portal Extension Pattern Canon Lock (complete, 2026-05-23):** Pure architectural canon work matching Sessions 1-8 + §2.4 + three-realization batch cadence — substantive canonical-foundation canon batch write per Sessions 1-8 batch-write-discipline precedent. NO CODE this session. permission scoping over canonical Space primitive); §2.5.2 Architectural distinction from conventional portal-as-net-new-substrate (canonical inversion: configuration not construction); §2.5.3 Canonical portal threshold + canonical authentication mechanisms + canonical permission scoping (Q1 multi-dimension canonical portal threshold + Q4 two-mechanism canonical with multi-mechanism-per-portal-use-case-context refinement + Q5 two-axis canonical permission scoping); §2.5.7 Implications for platform thesis articulation (future portal instances strategic vision — 6+ canonical portal instances at canon-foundation depth without canonical substrate extension). **Pre-lock verifications surfaced five discipline checks** matching Sessions 1-8 + §2.4 + three-realization batch pre-lock pattern: (V1) TOC structure surfaced (§2 post-§2.4-lock has 4 subsections + Part 3 divider; (V4) Substantive-rewrite-vs-citation-clause split confirmed (3 substantive sites: §2.5 NEW + §3.26.1 #6 closure + §3.26.11.13.19 count clause; 11 citation sites: §3.26.11.12.16 + §2.4.4 + §3.26.11.9 + §3.26.11.10 + §3.26.7.5 + §3.26.7.6 + SPACES_ARCHITECTURE.md §10 + PLATFORM_DESIGN_THESIS.md + PLATFORM_PRODUCT_PRINCIPLES.md + PLATFORM_ARCHITECTURE.md + CLAUDE.md log); (2) **§3.26.1 #6 Portal Spaces** substantive cross-reference closing "Detailed canon per portal type TBD in separate sessions" deferral with §2.5 platform-thesis-foundation canon citation; (3) **§3.26.11.13.19 count clause update** with 4-baseline canonical history continuity preservation (pre-§2.4 12/13 + post-§2.4 14/15 + post-three-realization 17/18 + post-§2.5 24/25); (2) Portal Extension Pattern canonicalized at platform-thesis-foundation depth — every portal is canonical Spaces configuration; portal architecture is permission scoping over canonical Space primitive; (3) Three-dimension canonical portal threshold canonical (SpaceConfig access_mode + portal_users identity + JWT realm "portal" alignment); (4) Two-mechanism canonical authentication with multi-mechanism-per-portal-use-case-context refinement (family portal canonical example: magic-link for one-shot approvals + password-based for sustained-session richer interaction); (5) Two-axis canonical permission scoping (per-Space + per-action) with per-entity + per-document subsumed at portal-Space-template configuration level; (6) Per-portal-instance template-declared lifecycle binding canonical (Q7); together establish canonical-substrate-extension canon covering tenant-user identity scope (§2.4 vertical expansion) + non-tenant-user identity scope (§2.5 portal extension); (2) Anti-pattern catalog extended cross-primitive (post-§2.5: 24 distinct / 25 with inheritance counting);
