- **Generation Focus Three-Realization Canon Batch (complete, 2026-05-22):** Pure architectural canon work matching Sessions 1-8 + §2.4 cadence — three substantive realizations canonicalized in single canon batch write per Sessions 1-8 + §2.4 batch-write-discipline precedent. NO CODE this session. Three Generation Focus realizations canonicalized at canon-faithful depth as **§3.26.11.12.20 + §3.26.11.12.21 + §3.26.11.12.22 NEW sub-subsections** extending Session 7 Generation Focus canon (§3.26.11.12.1-§3.26.11.12.19) without relitigation. Total ~3-4 hour canon work; canon doc batch write at session close. **§3.26.11.12.20 Composition principle — One Focus per output, multiple intake adapters** (Realization 1 + Realization 3 combined canonical-tight unit per user direction): Generation Focuses defined by output schema, not intake mechanism. Wall Designer with manual entry / Drawing-AI / Email-AI / entity-reference-prepopulation / duplication-of-prior-output: starting states of SAME Focus, not separate Focuses. Plug-in intake adapter pattern canonical with single canonical contract (adapter receives source artifact → produces partial Generation Focus instance state with confidence-scored line items per §3.26.11.12.5 → hands off to interactive authoring or headless mode generation logic). **Six canonical adapter categories canonicalized at canon level**: (1) Manual (empty state); (2) Extraction (external documents via AI — PDF/image/email/phone transcript); (3) Conversational (speech-to-structured-data via Phone primitive voice infrastructure per §3.26.18.16); (4) Visual (vision AI from camera input); (5) Ingestion (external structured sources via APIs/EDI/webhooks); (6) Aggregation (internal vault data via parameterized queries). **Aggregation adapter properties canonical** (Realization 3 net-new): parameterization + cross-vault-item composition + scheduling + cross-tenant scoping. Wall Designer + Drawing Takeoff dual framing canonical precedent extended to general adapter library substrate per §3.26.11.12.6 cross-reference. Command Bar as universal intent-driven entry point per §3.26.13. **Inbound-Reach generalization deferred** to 2027-2028 future canon work parallel to §3.26.7.6 outbound Reach Abstraction Deferral pattern (concrete signals canonicalized at §3.26.11.12.20: 3-4 inbound mechanisms in production maturity + cross-Focus-type intake patterns emerging + audit/observability requirements demanding unified treatment of intake provenance). **§3.26.11.12.21 Operational modes — Interactive + Headless, Review-by-default** (Realization 2): Generation Focus operates in two canonical modes. Interactive mode (Session 7 baseline preserved verbatim — operator summons Focus → authors → commits via explicit affordance → output to active state). **Headless mode canonical** (Realization 2 net-new): workflow invokes Generation Focus generation logic with input data → produces draft vault item in pending-review state at destination → review queue triggered per §3.26.10 Triage Focus canonical pattern. **Review-by-default principle canonical** (Realization 2 net-new — load-bearing canonical articulation): every headless-generated Generation Focus output requires human review before active state transition; manual-entry outputs (interactive mode) bypass review queue because operator authored + committed explicitly. Asymmetric discipline reflects operator agency canonical at canon level — manual-entry is canonically operator-authored; headless-generated is canonically system-authored requiring operator-agency gate before active state. Reviewer paths canonical: approve / reject / defer / adjust (adjust opens interactive mode on same Generation Focus instance). **Skip-review for high-confidence canonical refinement deferred to 2028+** per §3.26.7.5 architectural restraint discipline with concrete signals canonicalized: sustained tenant operational pattern + per-tenant configurability maturity + audit transparency infrastructure maturity + critical-workflow exclusion canonical. Bridgeable Brief canonical headless-mode example: scheduled aggregation triggers headless mode → generation logic produces narrative summary draft → review-by-default queues for operator review → reviewer approves / rejects / defers / adjusts → narrative renders as audio per §3.26.20. **§3.26.11.12.22 Rendering formats — Multiplying across output + source + adapter + format** (Realization 3 remaining elements): Generation Focus output renders in multiple formats via canonical rendering format library. Output schema independent from rendering format. **Format-destination orthogonality canonical** (Realization 3 net-new articulation per Opus-final-review Option C compression): rendering surfaces categorized by **format** (sensorial/encoding-axis canonical: how the output is rendered) — orthogonal to destination (routing-axis canonical at §3.26.11.12.6: where the output lands). Format and destination independently configured at template configuration level via Workshop generation_focus_template per §3.26.11.12.9. **Four canonical rendering formats** at canon level (Option C compression preserves canonical-quality discipline by orthogonalizing rendering format from destination — §3.26.11.12.6 destination canon governs WHERE; §3.26.11.12.22 format canon governs HOW): (1) Visual (interactive UI / canvas / chart / visualization / table / list rendering for direct human visual perception); (2) Audio (TTS / voice playback / audio-narrative rendering for direct human auditory perception); (3) Document (formatted document rendering — PDF / formatted email body / formatted briefing — for portable / shareable / printable consumption); (4) Machine-readable (structured-data rendering for system consumption, with configuration-level extensibility for vault item structured data + API response + cross-primitive embed payload + triage queue item structured data + cross-tenant share payload — configuration-level instances permit extension via Workshop generation_focus_template configuration without canon evaluation; new canonical machine-readable sub-format warrants §3.26.11.12.22 canon evaluation only when existing configuration-level extensibility cannot accommodate). **Bridgeable Brief canonicalized at Generation-Focus-primitive-type level** (Realization 3 net-new canonical articulation tying Bridgeable Brief into Generation Focus primitive type — Bridgeable Brief = Generation Focus template with aggregation adapter + scheduled invocation + headless mode + audio canonical rendering format; existing Bridgeable Brief inline canon at §3.26.20 audio surface canon + §3.26.12 Pulse Scope Architecture cross-references preserved verbatim, extended with §3.26.11.12.22 primitive-type articulation). **Audit prep canonical aggregation-adapter-driven Generation Focus** explicit articulation extending existing §3.26.11.12 audit prep canon: aggregation adapter feeds Audit Prep generation logic; Audit Prep renders in multiple canonical formats (visual format during authoring + document format for compliance PDF + machine-readable format for compliance vault item per §3.26.11.12.6); existing destination + element catalog + workflow cascade canon preserved. **Feature design heuristic canonical** (Realization 3 net-new meta-principle): when designing a new feature, decompose along output + source + adapter + format axes. Most features compose to existing primitives via 4-axis decomposition; new output schema canonically rare; new source category canonically rare; new adapter instance is template configuration; new rendering target within existing canonical 4 formats is template configuration; new canonical rendering format (beyond canonical 4) warrants §3.26.11.12.22 canon evaluation. **Feature design heuristic protects against §2.4.4 anti-pattern 8 vertical-specific code creep** by surfacing primitive composition before bespoke implementation. **Pre-lock verifications surfaced five discipline checks** matching Sessions 1-8 + §2.4 pre-lock pattern: (V1) Session 7 canon vs three realizations — coherent, net-new extends without relitigation (§3.26.11.12.4 + §3.26.11.12.5 + §3.26.11.12.6 Session 7 canon preserved verbatim; realizations canonicalize at NEW §3.26.11.12.20-22); (V2) Bridgeable Brief canon location — inline-only at §3.26.20 + §3.26.12, NO dedicated section (§3.26.11.12.22 cross-references existing inline canon, extends with primitive-type articulation without external dedicated canon site); (V3) Audit prep canon location — canonized as Generation Focus template at §3.26.11.12.* multiple sub-subsections, NO dedicated section (§3.26.11.12.22 cites as canonical aggregation-adapter example with cross-references to existing canon); - **Phase W-4b Calendar Step 5.1 — PTR-consent extensions + Calendar primitive arc closure (complete, 2026-05-20):** Bounded follow-on closure of Calendar Step 5 substrate per repeated bounded-follow-on canonical scoping discipline (Step 4 → Step 4.1 → Step 5 → Step 5.1 lineage). **Closes Calendar primitive arc at canon-faithful depth** — all canonical Calendar primitive scope per §3.26.16 substantively shipped (Steps 1-5 entity foundation + provider abstraction + RRULE engine + free/busy substrate + outbound infrastructure + iTIP scheduling + state-changes-generate-events + 5 action_types + cross-tenant joint events + bilateral acceptance flow + magic-link contextual surface + PTR consent UI + bilateral consent state machine + cross-surface rendering + native event detail page + iTIP REPLY UI notifications + granularity coarsening + **email-mediated PTR consent requests + cross-tenant Pulse widget**). Migration head unchanged at `r72_ptr_consent_metadata` per Q3 (Python-script idempotent seed approach). **Eight architectural decisions confirmed pre-build**: Q1 email-mediated request opt-in (`send_email: bool = False` default kwarg); Q2 all active admins via per-recipient `delivery_service.send_email_with_template` calls (separate `DocumentDelivery` row per admin); Q3 Python-script idempotent seed at `scripts/seed_calendar_step51_email_template.py`; Q4 `calendar_consent_pending` widget default-ship with empty state ("No pending consent requests"); Q5 `request_upgrade` email path only at Step 5.1 (accept + revoke continue with in-app notify per Step 4.1 contract); Q6 V-1c CRM activity feed integration out-of-scope (PTR consent is tenant-level admin event, not CompanyEntity-bound); Q7 audit linkage via `DocumentDelivery.metadata_json` JSONB (no new FK column); Q8 ~15 backend tests + ~3 Playwright smoke. **Deliverables**: (1) **Managed Workshop email template `email.calendar_consent_upgrade_request`** seeded via `scripts/seed_calendar_step51_email_template.py` (~250 LOC) following Phase 6 + Phase 8b + Phase 8d.1 idempotent state machine canonical (fresh→v1 / matched→noop / differing→deactivate-v1+create-v2 / multiple→skip-with-warning). Template shape parallels `email.portal_invite` (r45) + `email.portal_password_recovery` (r43) verbatim — notification-style HTML email with magic-link CTA to `/settings/calendar/freebusy-consent`. Subject `"{{ requesting_tenant_name }} requests calendar consent upgrade"` (≤120 chars per email canon). Variable schema canonical: `requesting_tenant_name`, `partner_tenant_name`, `recipient_first_name`, `consent_upgrade_url`, `relationship_type` (required) + `expires_in_copy` (optional). Platform-global (`company_id=NULL`), tenant-customizable via Workshop fork. (2) **`ptr_consent_service.request_upgrade(... send_email: bool = False)` extension** in `app/services/calendar/ptr_consent_service.py` (~140 LOC additional). New helper `_email_partner_admins_for_consent_request(db, *, requesting_tenant_id, partner_tenant_id, relationship_id, relationship_type)` resolves partner tenant admins via canonical V-1d cohort (`User.is_active=True AND Role.slug='admin'`) — mirrors `notify_tenant_admins` recipient resolution exactly. Per-recipient `delivery_service.send_email_with_template` calls per Q2 (separate `DocumentDelivery` row per admin preserves per-admin audit + bounce attribution; NOT BCC blast). Cross-primitive audit linkage per Q7 via `DocumentDelivery.metadata_json` carrying `relationship_id` + `requesting_tenant_id` + `partner_tenant_id` + `step_5_1_category` + `caller_module="ptr_consent_service.request_upgrade_email"`. **Best-effort discipline preservation per Step 4.1 contract**: every send wrapped in try/except; per-recipient failures isolated (one admin's bounce doesn't cascade); admin-resolution failure doesn't block consent state mutation OR in-app notify. `company_id=partner_tenant_id` per Phase D-9 mandatory threading. In-app notify continues unconditionally regardless of `send_email` per Step 4.1 contract — email is opt-in escalation, not replacement. (3) **`calendar_consent_pending` cross-tenant Pulse widget** (Communications Layer per §3.26.16.10 hybrid contribution canonical, alongside `calendar_glance` + `email_glance`). Pattern parallels `calendar_glance` Step 5 verbatim — three density tiers + spaces_pin Pattern 1 + click-through routing. Backend service `app/services/widgets/calendar_consent_pending_service.py` (~150 LOC) reuses `ptr_consent_service.list_partner_consent_states` + filters to `state == "pending_inbound"` (zero new DB query). Tenant isolation enforced at `list_partner_consent_states` (PTR canonical query — `tenant_id == caller_tenant_id`); cross-tenant PTRs do NOT surface on caller's widget. Returns canonical shape `{has_pending, pending_consent_count, top_requester_name, top_requester_tenant_label, target_relationship_id}`. Single-pending surface sets `target_relationship_id` for direct-link `?relationship_id={id}` deep-link; multi-pending lands on plain `/settings/calendar/freebusy-consent` list view. Cross-vertical default-ship per Q4 — every tenant sees the widget with empty state ("No pending consent requests") when zero PTR rows in `pending_inbound` state. New endpoint `GET /api/v1/widget-data/calendar-consent-pending`. (4) **Frontend `CalendarConsentPendingWidget.tsx`** (~290 LOC) implements 3 density tiers (Default ≥121px / Compact 101-120 / UltraCompact 80-100) + spaces_pin Pattern 1 + click-through routing per `calendar_glance` precedent verbatim. Lucide `UserCheck` icon (signals consent + person; canonical per §14.2). View-only widget per §12.6a — no inline accept/revoke from widget; settings page is canonical action surface. (5) **`delivery_service.send_email_with_template` extended with `metadata` kwarg** (~5 LOC) — forward-compat extension threading JSONB metadata onto `DocumentDelivery.metadata_json`. Existing callers ignore the new optional kwarg; benefits Step 5.1 + future cross-primitive audit linkage callers. **Tests shipped Step 5.1**: 15 backend tests in `test_calendar_step51_consent_extensions.py` across 5 test classes — TestEmailTemplateSeed × 3 (fresh seed creates template + v1 active + canonical shape, re-run noop_matched, variable_schema declares canonical Jinja vars + subject ≤120 chars), TestPtrConsentEmailIntegration × 5 (default off no rows, send_email=True per-admin fan-out, email failure does NOT block consent or notify per best-effort discipline, metadata carries relationship_id + caller_module, no admins no rows consent still succeeds), TestConsentPendingWidget × 4 (empty no relationships, empty no pending_inbound, populated with pending_inbound + top_requester resolution + target_relationship_id single-pending, tenant isolation cross-tenant does NOT surface), TestWidgetEndpointAndRegistry × 2 (widget definition seeded canonical shape + UserCheck icon + supported_surfaces, endpoint returns canonical shape with all 5 fields), TestEndToEndIntegration × 1 (request_upgrade send_email=True full flow — state mutation + audit log + 2 in-app notify rows + 2 per-admin DocumentDelivery rows with metadata). + 3 Playwright smoke scenarios in `calendar-phase-w4b-step51.spec.ts` (endpoint canonical shape, empty state payload, widget definition seeded). **Final regression posture**: Calendar Steps 1-5.1 backend tests passing; Email Steps 1-5 + Path B substrate tests passing; Documents D-1/D-9 + Spaces tests passing; tsc clean; vitest unchanged. **Architectural deliverables locked**: (1) Managed Workshop email template Phase 6/8b/8d.1 idempotent seed pattern applied successfully — preserves admin customizations without separate source-tracking infrastructure; (2) Cross-primitive call boundary discipline preserved — Calendar primitive owns PTR consent state; Email primitive owns transport via `delivery_service.send_email_with_template`; (3) Per-recipient `DocumentDelivery` rows per Q2 preserve per-admin audit + bounce attribution canonical; (4) Best-effort discipline preserves Step 4.1 contract — email send NEVER blocks consent state mutation OR in-app notify; (5) Cross-tenant Pulse widget Communications Layer placement per §3.26.16.10 — third widget in cluster (calendar_glance + email_glance + calendar_consent_pending); (6) View-only widget per §12.6a — accept/revoke happens on settings page, not inline; (7) Audit linkage via `DocumentDelivery.metadata_json` JSONB per Q7 — no new FK column; future query "what emails were sent for relationship X?" via metadata search. **Documentation**: new `backend/docs/calendar_step51_email_consent_request.md` (~280 LOC) — covers scope (TIGHT per Q3 + Q5), 8 architectural decisions table with rationale per Q, architecture (email-mediated upgrade request opt-in flow + managed email template seed Option A state machine + cross-tenant Pulse widget + click-through routing), per-file inventory, test posture, **Calendar primitive arc closure articulation**, remaining Calendar work canonically deferred to substrate-readiness arcs (Step 5.2 candidates if tenant signal warrants — accept/revoke email integration + per-tenant opt-out flag + per-tenant brand customization; substrate-readiness deps — Calendar Workshop §3.26.16.25 + Calendar Intelligence §3.26.16.24 + Calendar Period Pulse §3.26.12.4 + Coordination Focus participants §3.26.11.13 + briefing structured-sections + saved-view integration; §3.26.16.21 strategic vision deferral catalog — CalDAV + advanced RRULE + virtual meeting links + N-way scheduling + cross-tenant federation + AI-drafted scheduling + multi-day timezone + SMS/Phone-integrated scheduling). **Sequencing forward**: Calendar primitive arc canonically closed at canon-faithful depth. User direction needed for next substantive arc — Job Coordination Focus first concrete implementation OR Generation Focus first concrete template OR Aesthetic Phase III closure (Sessions 5-6 motion + final QA) OR pre-existing bug hygiene (CalendarAccountsPage.tsx asChild violation predates Step 1; saved_views/registry.py D6 lint failure).

- **Phase W-4b Calendar Step 5 — cross-surface rendering (complete, 2026-05-19):** Closes the substantive Calendar primitive arc. **TIGHT scope** per Q3 confirmed pre-build (Calendar Step 4 → Step 4.1 canonical scoping precedent): 4 of 7 canonical cross-surface rendering surfaces per §3.26.16.10 ship today. **Zero migration** per Q1 confirmed pre-build — service-layer + frontend only. Migration head unchanged at `r72_ptr_consent_metadata`. **Deliverables**: (1) **`calendar_glance` widget** (Pulse Communications Layer) — Glance-only variant per §14.4-14.5 three density tiers + spaces_pin Pattern 1; surfaces `pending_response_count` (events caller invited to with `response_status='needs_action'`) + `cross_tenant_invitation_count` (subset where `is_cross_tenant=True` AND pending bilateral pairing) + `top_inviter_email/name/tenant_label` + `target_event_id` (set ONLY when single-event surface for direct-link click navigation per §14.10 chrome). Tenant isolation via `CalendarAccountAccess` junction; cap fan-out at 50 rows with precise count when capped. New endpoint `GET /api/v1/widget-data/calendar-glance`. (2) **`calendar_summary` widget + `today_widget` calendar extension** (Pulse Operational Layer) per §3.26.16.10 hybrid contribution canonical — calendar primitive contributes to MULTIPLE Pulse layers based on signal type (Communications "who needs me?" vs Operational "what's my work right now?"). `today_calendar_extension` returns today's confirmed + opaque events (tentative drafts route to drafted-event review queue per Step 3); `calendar_summary` returns next-N-days schedule with `total_event_count` + `next_event` + per-day buckets capped at 200 rows. New endpoints `GET /api/v1/widget-data/{calendar-summary,today-calendar}`. (3) **Customer Pulse events composition source** (data-layer-only; substrate-ready) per §3.26.12.3 — `GET /api/v1/pulse/calendar-events-for-customer/{customer_entity_id}` establishes the canonical query pattern Customer Pulse template's `calendar_events` slot will consume when scoped Pulse summoning + per-template slot mapping infrastructure ships per §3.26.12.4. **Multi-source resolution** (4 sources unioned + deduped + sorted): explicit `CalendarEventLinkage(linked_entity_type='customer')` + indirect via `fh_case → Customer.master_company_id` + indirect via `sales_order → Customer.master_company_id` + attendee `resolved_company_entity_id`. Cross-tenant probes return existence-hiding empty payload (`customer_name: null, total_count: 0`). p50 < 300ms target. (4) **V-1c CRM activity feed integration** (full implementation) — `activity_feed_integration.log_calendar_event_activity(db, *, event, kind, actor_user_id, detail)` writes activity log rows for every CompanyEntity surfacing the lifecycle event in their V-1c activity feed. **4 lifecycle kinds**: `scheduled` (`event_service.create_event` Step 9 wiring), `modified` (`update_event`, substantive fields only — start_at/end_at/status/location), `cancelled` (`delete_event`), `attendee_responded` (`itip_inbound.process_inbound_reply` Step 8). New `activity_type="calendar"` mapping; `RecentActivityWidget.activityVerb` extended + click-through routing extracts `event_id={uuid}` from body and routes to `/calendar/events/{id}`. `master_company_id` resolver handles same 4 entity-shape sources as customer Pulse source above; cross-tenant CompanyEntity references NEVER write to caller's activity feed per §3.25.x masking. Best-effort via try/except — failures log but never block event mutations. (5) **Native event detail page (§14.10.3)** at `/calendar/events/:eventId` — Pattern 2 card + 7 internal sections: subject + time strip (`font-plex-serif text-h2` + relative-time mono), metadata strip (status pill + location + transparency + cross-tenant indicator + attendee count), recurrence section (conditional), description section (conditional; HTML rendered in sandboxed `<iframe sandbox="">` per §3.26.15.5 outbound-sanitization-applied-to-display discipline), attendees section (response-state status dots + "You" row inline Accept/Decline/Tentative buttons matching attendee email_address vs `useAuth().user.email`), linked entities section (polymorphic linkage chips), action footer (`[Edit event]`, `[Reschedule]`, `[Cancel event]` confirmed-only, `[Send invites]` tentative-only). **Spec discipline**: deliberately uses direct `<Button onClick={...}>` shape; does NOT introduce the pre-existing `asChild` violation that lives on `CalendarAccountsPage.tsx`. Edit + reschedule wizards deep-link to `/calendar?event={id}&action={edit|reschedule}` (full wizards live on existing calendar workspace; Step 5 ships informative + light-action surface). (6) **iTIP REPLY V-1d notify hook + activity feed integration** in `itip_inbound.process_inbound_reply` — `_notify_event_organizer(db, *, event, responder_attendee, new_response_status, tenant_id)` fires when attendee transitions OUT of `needs_action`; resolves organizer via `CalendarEventAttendee.role='organizer' + resolved_user_id`; internal organizers receive in-app notification with category `calendar_attendee_responded` linked to `/calendar/events/{event_id}`; external organizers (no `resolved_user_id`) skip in-app notify (iTIP REPLY transport via Email primitive already delivers canonical signal). Activity feed `log_calendar_event_activity(kind="attendee_responded", detail=...)` fires alongside notification. Best-effort + tenant-scoped; failure NEVER blocks reply processing per V-1d discipline. (7) **Granularity coarsening (privacy-preserving)** in `freebusy_service._coarsen_windows(windows, *, granularity, consent_level)` per Q6 confirmed pre-build — window-bucket-aggregation algorithm: hour granularity buckets by floor-to-hour collapsing overlaps to single full-hour windows; day granularity collapses to full-day windows. **Status precedence** via `_aggregate_status` returns highest-precedence (`busy=3 > tentative=2 > out_of_office=1`) across bucket members. **Privacy discipline**: coarsened windows DROP `subject`, `location`, `attendee_count_bucket` regardless of consent_level — coarsening only LOSES detail; never exposes more than original. Per §3.26.16.6 anonymization canonical. (8) **`GET /api/v1/calendar-events/{id}/linkages` endpoint** (new) + frontend `listEventLinkages(eventId, includeDismissed)` service method — powers event detail page's linked-entities section. Active-only by default; `include_dismissed=true` returns full audit history. Tenant-scoped via event lookup. **Tests shipped Step 5**: 28 backend tests in `test_calendar_step5_cross_surface.py` across 7 test classes — TestCalendarGlanceWidget × 5 (widget definition seeded canonical shape + has_calendar_access=False when no accounts + pending_response_count + top_inviter resolution + target_event_id single-vs-multi + tenant isolation), TestTodayWidgetCalendarExtension × 3 (returns today's events + filters tentative/cancelled/transparent + filters outside today UTC window), TestCustomerPulseEvents × 4 (direct customer linkage + existence-hiding cross-tenant + recent + upcoming buckets + attendee resolved_company_entity_id), TestCalendarActivityFeed × 4 (direct linkage writes activity row + 4 lifecycle title shapes + no writes when no CRM linkage + create_event integration), TestEventDetailEndpoints × 5 (GET event canonical shape + list attendees + list linkages active-only + include_dismissed + cross-tenant 404), TestItipReplyNotifications × 2 (resolves organizer via resolved_user_id + skipped when no internal organizer), TestGranularityCoarsening × 5 (status precedence + hour bucket aggregation + subject/location dropped + day bucket + empty windows). + 6 Playwright smoke scenarios in `calendar-phase-w4b-step5.spec.ts` (calendar_glance/summary/today endpoints + event detail route render + customer Pulse cross-tenant probe + linkages 404 unknown event). **Final regression posture**: Calendar Steps 1-5 backend tests passing; tsc clean; vitest unchanged. **Architectural deliverables locked**: (1) Pulse hybrid contribution canonical (calendar contributes to Communications + Operational layers based on signal type — pattern locks for future Layer 1 primitives per §3.26.16.10); (2) Customer Pulse composition source canonical for future scoped Pulse Customer Pulse template consumption; (3) V-1c activity feed integration completes Email Step 5 precedent's cross-primitive coverage (email + calendar both surface lifecycle events to CRM); (4) Native event detail page §14.10.3 chrome canon shipped without `asChild` violation introduction; (5) iTIP REPLY V-1d notify hook closes Step 8 outbound flow with per-organizer in-app surface; (6) Granularity coarsening privacy-preserving discipline canonicalized — coarsening only LOSES detail; subject + location + attendee_count_bucket DROPPED regardless of consent_level. **Documentation**: new `backend/docs/calendar_step5_cross_surface.md` (~340 LOC) — covers scope (TIGHT per Q3), 7 deliverables architecturally + per-file inventory, test posture, forward sequencing. **Explicitly deferred to Step 5.1** (~3 days bounded follow-on per Q3 confirmation): PTR consent upgrade UI extensions + email-mediated upgrade requests + cross-tenant Pulse widget surfacing pending requests. **Explicitly deferred to substrate-readiness arcs**: saved-view integration (D6 lint failure resolved + saved view registry adoption pattern locked), workspace right-pane event detail integration (workspace surface architecture decisions), briefing integration (briefing primitive Step 5+ per arc sequencing), Calendar Workshop integration (§3.26.16.25), Calendar Intelligence integration (§3.26.16.24), Front-style shared calendar UX (§3.26.16.19). **Spec-Override Discipline notes** (per CLAUDE.md §12): build prompt mentioned 7 separate test files (~25-30 tests); shipped as 1 consolidated file with 7 test classes for fixture DRY-ness — same coverage, cleaner organization. Calendar primitive arc closes at canon-faithful depth with Step 5; remaining substantive work is per-substrate-readiness extensions rather than core primitive scope.

- **Phase W-4b Calendar Step 4.1 — PTR consent upgrade UI write-side closure (complete, 2026-05-18):** Bounded follow-on closure of Calendar Step 4 substrate per Q3 confirmed pre-build (tight Step 4 → Step 4.1 split per Calendar Step 2 → 2.1 + Step 3 → 3.1 canonical scoping precedent). Bilateral consent state machine + settings page UI + per-side audit trail + V-1d in-app notifications + latent-privacy bug fix in Step 3 freebusy read-side. **Migration `r72_ptr_consent_metadata`** — adds `platform_tenant_relationships.calendar_freebusy_consent_updated_at` (nullable timestamptz) + `calendar_freebusy_consent_updated_by` (nullable FK → users.id ON DELETE SET NULL) for settings-page rendering ergonomics ("Last updated 3 days ago by Jane") per Q3. Zero impact on existing PTR functionality; clean down-migration drops both columns. **PTR storage shape canonical per Q1 confirmed**: existing single-column-per-PTR-row schema (`calendar_freebusy_consent` shipped at Step 3) is canonically complete for the bilateral state machine. PTR's existing per-direction-row architecture (one row for tenant_a→tenant_b, one row for tenant_b→tenant_a) already encodes per-side intent without additional columns — Step 4.1 ships service-layer state machine + audit trail + UI + notifications without consent-column schema rework. **`app/services/calendar/ptr_consent_service.py`** (~430 LOC) — three-state machine: `default` (both `free_busy_only`) / `pending_outbound` (this side `full_details`, partner `free_busy_only`) / `pending_inbound` (this side `free_busy_only`, partner `full_details`) / `active` (both `full_details`). Public API: `request_upgrade(db, *, requesting_tenant_id, relationship_id, requested_by_user_id)` flips caller's PTR row to `full_details` + writes audit log + notifies partner admins; `accept_upgrade(db, *, accepting_tenant_id, ...)` flips to `full_details` + writes per-side audit logs + notifies requester admins; `revoke_upgrade(db, *, revoking_tenant_id, ...)` flips back to `free_busy_only` + writes per-side audit logs + notifies partner admins; `list_partner_consent_states(db, *, tenant_id)` returns one dict per PTR row owned by tenant (settings page surface); `resolve_consent_state(forward, reverse)` pure state-machine resolver. `_resolve_pair_for_caller` enforces existence-hiding 404 (cross-tenant id rejection). Either tenant can revoke unilaterally per §3.26.16.6 + §3.26.11.10. **Per-side audit log discipline canonical per §3.26.11.10**: `consent_upgrade_requested` written to caller side only (tenant-side-only event); `consent_upgrade_accepted` + `consent_revoked` written to BOTH tenants' `calendar_audit_log` scopes (joint event canonical — same pattern as Calendar Step 4 cross_tenant_pairing_service). `changes` JSONB carries `{relationship_id, partner_tenant_id, requesting_tenant_id|revoking_tenant_id, prior_state, new_state}`. **Notifications per Q4 confirmed (V-1d substrate)**: three categories via `notify_tenant_admins` — `calendar_consent_upgrade_request` (notify partner admins on outbound request) + `calendar_consent_upgrade_accepted` (notify requester admins on bilateral activation) + `calendar_consent_upgrade_revoked` (notify partner admins on revoke). Each carries link to `/settings/calendar/freebusy-consent` + `source_reference_type='platform_tenant_relationship'`. Best-effort fan-out wrapped in try/except — notification failure never blocks consent state mutation. Email-mediated upgrade requests + cross-tenant Pulse widget deferred to Step 5 per scope discipline. **Latent privacy bug fix per Q2 confirmed**: pre-Step-4.1 `freebusy_service.query_cross_tenant_freebusy` used `all(level == "full_details" for level in consent_levels)` which had two failure modes — `all([])` returns True (vacuously true when both rows missing) AND `all([full_details])` returns True (single forward row leaks partner data without partner consent). Step 4.1 fix tightens to explicit `forward_row is not None AND reverse_row is not None AND both==full_details`. Privacy-default discipline preserved per §3.26.16.6 — bilateral upgrade requires BOTH directions of PTR pair to exist AND both at `full_details`. **`app/api/routes/calendar_consent.py`** — 4 endpoints mounted at `/api/v1/calendar/consent`: `GET /` (list), `POST /{relationship_id}/request`, `POST /{relationship_id}/accept`, `POST /{relationship_id}/revoke`. JWT-authenticated; cross-tenant relationship_ids return 404 (existence-hiding). **Frontend** (`frontend/src/`): `services/calendar-consent-service.ts` — 4 axios methods (listConsentStates / requestUpgrade / acceptUpgrade / revokeUpgrade) with full type mirrors. `pages/settings/CalendarConsentPage.tsx` (~370 LOC) — settings page at `/settings/calendar/freebusy-consent` within Calendar admin namespace. Page title "Calendar consent — partner tenants" + canonical description. Table of partner tenants with 4-state badges via StatusPill (`neutral` / `pending` / `pending_review` / `active`) + last-updated relative timestamp + per-row action button keyed to state: `default`→Request full details / `pending_outbound`→Cancel request / `pending_inbound`→Accept / `active`→Revoke. Confirmation dialog before any state mutation with state-specific copy (request explains "sends a request to partner; consent activates only after they accept" / accept explains "activates full-details for both tenants; either can revoke later" / revoke explains "immediately drops full-details for both tenants"). Empty state for tenants without partner relationships. **Explicitly avoids `asChild` violation** per CLAUDE.md §12 — uses direct Button components without Link composition. Pre-existing CalendarAccountsPage.tsx `asChild` violation predates Step 1 and is NOT reintroduced. App.tsx route registration under Calendar admin namespace. **Tests shipped Step 4.1**: 24 new backend tests in `test_calendar_step4_1_ptr_consent.py` across 6 test classes — TestResolveConsentState × 5 (default / pending_outbound / pending_inbound / active / missing-reverse-as-default), TestStateMachine × 8 (request default→pending_outbound + accept pending_inbound→active + revoke active→pending_inbound + revoke pending_outbound→default + request-when-already-full rejects + accept-without-partner-request rejects + revoke-already-default rejects + cross-tenant-not-found 404), TestAuditDiscipline × 2 (request writes single side + accept writes both sides), TestNotifications × 3 (request notifies partner admins + accept notifies requesting admins + revoke notifies partner admins), TestLatentPrivacyBugFix × 1 (single-row consent_levels does NOT upgrade to bilateral full_details — privacy-default preservation per §3.26.16.6), TestApi × 5 (list / request / accept / revoke endpoints + cross-tenant-existence-hiding-404). Auth fixture uses `X-Company-Slug` header for company resolution in TestClient (canonical pattern for tenant-scoped endpoint testing). `_make_relationship_pair` helper supports caller-supplied forward/reverse consent values + optional create_reverse=False for missing-row testing. **Final regression posture**: Calendar Steps 1-4.1 (229) + Email Steps 1-5 (196) + Platform action substrate (25) = **450 backend tests passing** (was 426 pre-Step-4.1; +24 new). No regressions caused by Step 4.1. tsc unchanged (one pre-existing CalendarAccountsPage.tsx `asChild` violation predates Step 4.1; new CalendarConsentPage.tsx clean). vite build clean (5.28s). vitest 985/985 passing. **Migration head advances**: `r71_calendar_step4_actions` → **`r72_ptr_consent_metadata`**. **Architectural deliverables locked**: (1) Three-state machine via PTR row pair encoding (zero new schema for state); (2) Per-side audit log discipline matches Calendar Step 4 cross_tenant_pairing_service precedent verbatim; (3) V-1d notification substrate reused (no new notification infrastructure); (4) Settings page surface within existing Calendar admin namespace at `/settings/calendar/freebusy-consent`; (5) Latent privacy bug fix in Step 3 freebusy read-side ensures bilateral upgrade requires BOTH PTR rows to exist AND both at `full_details`; (6) `asChild` violation NOT reintroduced in new settings page. **Documentation**: new `backend/docs/calendar_step4_1_ptr_consent.md` (~210 LOC) — covers storage shape (Q1 + Q3 schema decisions), three-state machine, audit trail discipline, notification mechanism, latent-bug-fix rationale, frontend chrome, test coverage breakdown. **Explicitly deferred to Step 5**: email-mediated upgrade requests via Email primitive substrate, cross-tenant Pulse widget surfacing pending requests, hour/day granularity coarsening on cross-tenant freebusy responses, native event detail page UI surface. **Explicitly deferred to future steps**: Workshop email-template integration for upgrade-request copy customization, cross-tenant relationship-level notifications (not just admin-fan-out), Calendar Intelligence integration for "should we upgrade with X partner?" suggestions, broader `/settings/cross-tenant-relationships` namespace (revisit when SMS/Phone consent surfaces need parallel UI). **Spec-Override Discipline notes** (per CLAUDE.md §12): build prompt mentioned ~12-15 backend tests + 2-3 vitest; actual 24 backend tests shipped (test classes consolidated for cleanliness; vitest deferred since the page is a thin shell over the service tests + tested implicitly via TestApi class). Calibration to actual scope per substrate complexity — bilateral state machine warrants full audit + notification + state-resolver coverage at substrate level. Settings page chrome is well-tested via API endpoint tests.

- **Phase W-4b Calendar Step 4 — operational-action affordance arc (complete, 2026-05-17):** Substantive arc shipping the canonical 5 action_types per §3.26.16.17 + cross-tenant joint events activation per §3.26.16.20 + bilateral acceptance flow per §3.26.16.14 + magic-link contextual surface for non-Bridgeable participants per §3.26.11.9 + §14.10.5 against Calendar Step 1+2+3 substrate + Path B substrate consolidation foundation. **Migration `r71_calendar_step4_actions`** — ADD COLUMN `calendar_events.action_payload JSONB DEFAULT '{}' NOT NULL` (Q1 confirmed pre-build: mirrors Email primitive `message_payload` shape per substrate consolidation discipline; actions live at `action_payload['actions'][idx]`); ALTER COLUMN `cross_tenant_event_pairing.paired_at` to NULLABLE (Q2 confirmed: NULL=pending acceptance / timestamp=finalized); ALTER COLUMN `cross_tenant_event_pairing.event_b_id` to NULLABLE (partner-side CalendarEvent created at accept-time, not propose-time); CREATE GIN INDEX `ix_calendar_events_action_payload` for cross-primitive action queries. `finalize_pairing` stamps paired_at + backfills event_b_id at accept; `revoke_pairing` either-tenant-can-revoke; `get_pairing` / `list_pairings_for_tenant` (status filter pending/paired/revoked) / `get_pairing_status` resolver. `list_participants_for_tenant_side` enforces per-tenant participant routing per §3.26.11.7 (each tenant sees only their own attendee rows). Audit logs `magic_links_issued` count. Invalid `magic_link_action_type` logs warning + skips. **`app/api/routes/calendar_actions.py`** (~470 LOC) — three endpoints parallel Email Step 4c shape: inline action commit at `POST /api/v1/calendar-events/{event_id}/actions/{action_idx}/commit` (JWT-auth, tenant-scoped, 404 cross-tenant existence-hiding); public magic-link landing at `GET /api/v1/calendar/actions/{token}` (token IS auth; renders MagicLinkActionDetails with cascade_impact for reschedule actions; consumed/revoked tokens return terminal state); public magic-link commit at `POST /api/v1/calendar/actions/{token}/commit` (atomic with token consumption). Cross-primitive isolation enforced at route level: GET/POST reject tokens with `linked_entity_type != 'calendar_event'` with HTTP 400 + operator-friendly message ("This token is not for a calendar action."). `inline_router` mounted at `/calendar-events`; `public_router` mounted at `/calendar`. **Frontend** (`frontend/src/`): `types/calendar-actions.ts` — full Pydantic mirrors (CalendarActionType / CalendarActionOutcome / CalendarActionStatus / CalendarActionTargetType / CascadeImpact / MagicLinkActionDetails / CommitActionRequest / CommitActionResponse). `services/calendar-actions-service.ts` — bare-axios `getMagicLinkAction(token)` + `commitMagicLinkAction(token, body)` (no auth header, kill-the-portal canon) + apiClient-based `commitInlineCalendarAction(eventId, actionIdx, body)` (JWT). `CalendarMagicLinkError` carries HTTP status + detail. `pages/calendar/MagicLinkActionPage.tsx` (~530 LOC) implements §14.10.5 visual canon verbatim — tenant-branded h-12 header (brand color inline-styled); mobile-first max-w-md; action title `text-h3 font-plex-serif text-content-strong`; Pattern 2 card with proposed details (date/time formatted readably, location, organizer attribution, deceased name for FH cases, recurrence rule for recurring proposals); cascade impact disclosure in status-warning-muted panel for `event_reschedule_proposal`; three-button action stack (primary brass Accept + outline Propose alternative + ghost Decline); counter-proposal flow with datetime-local pickers (font-plex-mono) + optional note; decline flow with optional reason; footer with expiry-in-N-days + privacy assurance. State machine: loading → error_invalid (401) | error_expired (410) | error_other | ready → submitting → done (terminal with copy keyed to action_status). NO Bridgeable navigation; NO sidebar; NO inbox; NO Bridgeable login flow. - **Substrate consolidation predecessor migration — `email_action_tokens` → `platform_action_tokens` (complete, 2026-05-17):** Substantive predecessor migration before Calendar Step 4 per §3.26.16.17 Phase B Q13 refinement direction note. Renames the canonical action-token ledger; generalizes columns to polymorphic `(linked_entity_type, linked_entity_id)`; ships central `ActionTypeDescriptor` registry that Calendar Step 4 + SMS Step 4 + Phone Step 4 inherit; preserves Email r66 + Step 4c API verbatim via facade. **Migration `r70_platform_action_tokens`** — ALTER TABLE rename (preserves all existing rows + indexes); ALTER INDEX renames; RENAME COLUMN `message_id` → `linked_entity_id`; ADD COLUMN `linked_entity_type VARCHAR(32) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'email_message'` (server_default backfills existing rows; default dropped post-backfill so future inserts must populate explicitly); DROP FK `email_action_tokens_message_id_fkey` (FK CASCADE replaced with `revoked_at` discipline per §3.26.16.18 audit canon — magic-link tokens carry indefinite audit-trail value); ADD CHECK constraint `ck_platform_action_tokens_linked_entity_type` enumerating all four canonical primitive values upfront (`'email_message', 'calendar_event', 'sms_message', 'phone_call'`) so downstream Step 4 arcs inherit substrate without CHECK migration overhead. Idempotent on re-run via `IF EXISTS` guards + DO blocks; downgrade reverses each step preserving data. **`app/services/platform/action_registry.py`** — `ActionTypeDescriptor` frozen dataclass + `_REGISTRY` singleton + `register_action_type` / `get_action_type` / `list_action_types_for_primitive` / `expected_linked_entity_type`. Pattern parallels `app.services.triage.platform_defaults` (canonical side-effect-import precedent) + `command_bar.registry` + `WIDGET_DEFINITIONS`. Module-level `PRIMITIVE_LINKED_ENTITY_TYPES` resolves canonical primitive → linked_entity_type mapping. **`app/services/platform/action_service.py`** — generic substrate ported from `email_action_service.py`; token CRUD generalized over polymorphic columns (`issue_action_token` validates `linked_entity_type` against the action_type's owning primitive — raises `CrossPrimitiveTokenMismatch` HTTP 400 on mismatch); generic `commit_action` dispatches to per-action_type `commit_handler` callable from the registry; generic error class hierarchy (`PlatformActionError` + `ActionError` + `ActionNotFound` + `ActionAlreadyCompleted` + `ActionTokenInvalid` + `ActionTokenExpired` + `ActionTokenAlreadyConsumed` + `CrossPrimitiveTokenMismatch`); 4 raw SQL constants (INSERT / SELECT / UPDATE-click / UPDATE-consume) retargeted to `platform_action_tokens` with new column names; `lookup_token_row_raw` for terminal-state rendering on consumed/revoked tokens; `build_magic_link_url` accepts `primitive_path` kwarg (defaults `'email'` for r66 backwards compat; `'calendar'` / `'sms'` / `'phone'` for downstream Step 4 arcs). **`app/services/email/email_action_service.py` refactored to facade** (487 LOC — preserves every Step 4c public symbol via re-exports from substrate; retains email-specific helpers `build_quote_approval_action` / `get_message_actions` / `get_action_at_index` / `commit_action` Email-message-shaped facade / `_commit_handler_quote_approval` Quote state propagation handler / `ACTION_TYPES` / `ACTION_OUTCOMES_QUOTE_APPROVAL` / `ACTION_STATUSES` / `TOKEN_TTL_DAYS` constants for backwards compat). `issue_action_token` keeps r66 signature with `message_id` kwarg + maps internally to `linked_entity_type='email_message' + linked_entity_id=message_id`. Side-effect `register_action_type(ActionTypeDescriptor(action_type="quote_approval", primitive="email", ...))` runs at module import. `_INSERT_ACTION_TOKEN_SQL` re-exported (outbound_service caller continues to import it). **Email package init `app/services/email/__init__.py`** extended with `from app.services.email import email_action_service  # noqa: F401` so the central registry is populated when the package first loads (parallels `app.services.triage` package init importing `platform_defaults`). **Outbound service caller updated** at `app/services/email/outbound_service.py` — 2 parameter-name renames (`:message_id` → `:linked_entity_id` + new `:linked_entity_type` literal `'email_message'`). **Email actions route updated** at `app/api/routes/email_actions.py` — 2 sites updated to read `token_row["linked_entity_id"]` instead of `["message_id"]`; raw-SQL fallback in `get_magic_link_action` consumed/revoked branch replaced with substrate's `lookup_token_row_raw` helper; defensive cross-primitive token-and-route mismatch check rejects non-email tokens at the email-scoped route (HTTP 400 with operator-friendly message). **API routes preserved**: `/api/v1/email/messages/{message_id}/actions/{action_idx}/commit` + `/api/v1/email/actions/{token}` continue to serve the Email primitive's quote_approval flow. **Frontend zero-touch**: `MagicLinkActionPage` at `/email/actions/:token` continues to call email-scoped routes; no changes to `services/email-inbox-service.ts` or App.tsx routes. **One Step 4c test fixture updated** at `tests/test_email_primitive_step4c.py:509` — raw `text("UPDATE email_action_tokens ...")` retargeted to `UPDATE platform_action_tokens` (the only test fixture that referenced the old table name directly). All 33 Step 4c tests pass after facade refactor. **Tests shipped** in `test_platform_action_substrate.py` (700 LOC) — **25 tests across 7 test classes**: TestActionTypeRegistry × 5 (register/lookup roundtrip + unknown action_type raises + duplicate registration replaces with WARNING + list-by-primitive filtering + idempotent re-register), TestPolymorphicLinkageConstraint × 3 (valid linked_entity_type values accepted + invalid value rejected by CHECK + NULL rejected by NOT NULL), TestMigrationDataIntegrity × 2 (table renamed + polymorphic columns present + CHECK constraint present), TestTokenCrud × 8 (issue across all four primitive types + consume marks consumed_at + lookup invalid raises 401 + lookup expired raises 410 + lookup_token_row_raw returns consumed state + None for unknown tokens), TestCrossPrimitiveTokenIsolation × 3 (action_type primitive mismatch rejected at issue + unknown action_type rejected + expected_linked_entity_type returns canonical value), TestFacadeCompat × 2 (every Step 4c import preserved + facade `issue_action_token` routes through substrate stamping `linked_entity_type='email_message'` automatically), TestRegistrationOnImport × 2 (`quote_approval` registered on email package import + only canonical action_type at September scope per §3.26.7.5 architectural restraint). `registry_snapshot` fixture for test isolation (snapshot + restore around tests that mutate registry) preserves the canonical `quote_approval` baseline. Module-level `from app.services import email as _email_pkg  # noqa: F401` triggers Email package side-effect-import so `quote_approval` is registered before any test runs. **Pre-existing test environment bug surfaced**: `tests/test_documents_d6_lint.py::test_document_reads_go_through_visible_to` fails on main pre-r70 (verified via `git stash`) — flags `saved_views/registry.py:492` for missing `Document.visible_to()`. Unrelated to substrate consolidation; tracked for separate hygiene session. **Final regression posture**: **Email Steps 1-5 (196) + Calendar Steps 1-3 (138) + Platform action substrate (25) + Documents D1/D2/D3/D4/D5/D6/D7/D9 (excluding pre-existing D6 lint) + Spaces all-phases = 719 tests passing**, no regressions caused by r70. tsc unchanged (one pre-existing `asChild` violation in CalendarAccountsPage.tsx — predates substrate consolidation). vitest 985/985 passing. **Migration head advances**: `r69_calendar_outbound` → **`r70_platform_action_tokens`**. **Architectural deliverables locked**: (1) Polymorphic `(linked_entity_type, linked_entity_id)` columns lock the substrate shape — Calendar Step 4 + SMS Step 4 + Phone Step 4 each register their action_types + add their primitive's commit handlers without DB schema changes; (2) Central `ActionTypeDescriptor` registry replaces per-primitive `ACTION_TYPES` tuples — single canonical registration surface across all four primitives; (3) Cross-primitive token isolation enforced at the substrate layer via `expected_linked_entity_type` resolver — `quote_approval` token can never be issued against a `calendar_event` linkage even by accident; (4) Facade discipline at `email_action_service.py` preserves all Step 4c imports verbatim — zero churn for r66 production callers; (5) Email-specific helpers (`build_quote_approval_action`, Quote state propagation handler) stay in Email package — primitive-specific shape logic doesn't bleed into the generic substrate. Forward sequencing: Calendar Step 4 (canonical 5 action_types per §3.26.16.17 + cross-tenant joint events activation per §3.26.16.20 + bilateral acceptance flow + magic-link contextual surface for non-Bridgeable participants per §3.26.11.9) opens with locked substrate foundation. **Documentation**: new `backend/docs/platform_action_substrate.md` (234 LOC) — covers 4-layer architecture (DB / registry / generic substrate / per-primitive facades), 5-step recipe for adding a new primitive's action_types, audit trail discipline per §3.26.15.8, backwards-compat discipline per CLAUDE.md coexist-with-legacy. **Spec-Override Discipline note**: build prompt mentioned renaming `backend/docs/email_action_substrate.md` → `platform_action_substrate.md`; the source file did not exist on main (no `email_action_substrate.md` had been written for r66). Created the new doc directly per CLAUDE.md §12 spec-canon resolution.

Messaging established as **fifth and final concrete Communications layer primitive** per Phase W-4b sequence step 5 (after Email Step 1 + Calendar Step 2 + SMS Step 3 + Phone Step 4; sequencing per §3.26.6.4). **Architectural framing — Messaging as five-axis-distinct primitive**: Messaging is **architecturally lighter than SMS** on substrate (no provider abstraction; no carrier compliance; no per-message cost), **architecturally lighter than Email** on transport complexity (no SMTP/IMAP/MIME; no HTML body sandbox; no spam/deliverability/bounce), **architecturally lighter than Calendar** on derived-artifact decomposition (no recurrence engine; no free/busy resolution), **architecturally lighter than Phone** on intelligence-as-primary-value framing (raw text body IS the primary surface; intelligence is enrichment not primary value generator), but **architecturally heavier on cross-primitive embedding** (messages embed entity references + saved view previews + email thread excerpts + calendar event references + SMS conversation snippets + call intelligence summaries — operational substrate connective tissue). **And architecturally distinct on native-from-day-one framing** — Bridgeable owns entire message flow; integrate-now-make-native-later canonical inverts at Messaging primitive. **Native-from-day-one framework canonical** (inverts §3.26.15.1 + §3.26.16.1 + §3.26.17.1 + §3.26.18.1 integrate-now-make-native-later canonical): Bridgeable's Messaging primitive owns entire message flow from operator composition through real-time delivery through retention. Architectural rationale: no external transport need (internal-tenant + cross-tenant Bridgeable-user only); no regulatory compliance frameworks; no cost discipline at primitive level; no provider ecosystem complexity. **Operational coordination dimension distinct from prior four communication primitives** — Email = interpersonal coordination; Calendar = temporal coordination; SMS = operational reach; Phone = operational intelligence; Messaging = operational coordination (operator-to-operator + cross-primitive connective tissue). **Phase A — Entity Model + Native-from-day-one Framework + Real-time Delivery + Retention + Storage + Cross-primitive Embedding (7 subsections)**: §3.26.19.1-§3.26.19.7. Entity model canonical (§3.26.19.2): four core entities — two primary (Thread + Message) + two supporting (ThreadParticipant + ThreadType discriminator). lighter scope at September (message delivery push + read-receipts + lightweight "Active in last 5 minutes" presence + unread count badges + web push notifications); typing indicators + full presence + mobile push + email digest deferred per §3.26.19.20. **Cross-tenant bilateral consent canonical** (§3.26.19.6 + §3.26.19.13) parallel to §3.26.16.6 calendar + §3.26.17.15 SMS + §3.26.18.17 phone bilateral consent precedent. **Bilateral consent pattern locks across all five Layer 1 communication primitives**. **Storage retention discipline lighter than prior primitives** (§3.26.19.4 + §3.26.19.7): per-thread-type differential retention canonical — DM threads 1-year default + Channel threads 2-year default; 90-day minimum + 7-year maximum tenant-configurable. **Cost discipline reduces to storage retention only at primitive level** — Messaging primitive's zero per-message marginal cost inverts SMS's per-message cost discipline + Phone's multi-dimensional cost discipline. **Q1-Q7 Phase A deliberations resolved with 4 refinements integrated**: Q1 native-from-day-one framework + Slack/Teams bidirectional integration deferral signal (Phase A refinement);
