Phone established as **fourth and final concrete Communications layer primitive** per Phase W-4b sequence step 4 (after Email Step 1 + Calendar Step 2 + SMS Step 3; sequencing per §3.26.6.4). **Architectural framing — Phone as four-axis-distinct primitive**: Phone is **architecturally heavier than SMS** on substrate (calls are real-time interactions PLUS asynchronous derived artifacts — recording + transcript + intelligence), **architecturally heavier than Calendar** on derived-artifact decomposition, **architecturally heavier than Email** on intelligence-as-primary-value (call recordings are operationally meaningless without transcription + extraction + summarization), and **architecturally heavier on compliance complexity** (state-by-state two-party consent jurisdictional variability + HIPAA recording mode + FCC outbound discipline + CFPB collections discipline). **Real-time + asynchronous duality canonical at architectural model**: Call entity owns real-time identity + outcome; CallRecording + CallTranscript + CallIntelligenceArtifact entities own asynchronous derived content. **Call intelligence as primary value framework**: raw audio is compliance + audit artifact; transcription + summarization + intent extraction + entity resolution + action item extraction + sentiment + compliance review are primary operator surfaces. **Existing Call Intelligence implementation as foundation**: the platform shipped Call Intelligence (April 2026 — RingCentral OAuth + Deepgram Nova-2 + Claude extraction + after-call orchestrator + CallOverlay UI) as a working feature with substantial customer tuning. Phone primitive canon does NOT discard this implementation — it formalizes it as canonical Phone-primitive architectural model + extends with multi-provider abstraction + canonical entity decomposition + cross-primitive integration. Existing `ringcentral_*` tables map to canonical Phone primitive entity model — implementation arc executes provider-rename + entity-decomposition + extension migrations, not greenfield rebuild. **Phase A — Entity Model + Provider Abstraction + Call Intelligence + Compliance + Cost (10 subsections)**: §3.26.18.1-§3.26.18.10. Native-vs-integration deliberation reaffirmed verbatim — native phone implementation requires SIP trunking + carrier compliance + STIR/SHAKEN + 911 + voicemail platform + international roaming + carrier-direct billing reconciliation. Integrate-now-make-native-later commitment canonical — Bridgeable owns architectural model; provider abstraction handles transport (RingCentral canonical primary + Twilio Voice deferred alternate per §3.26.18.3 + concrete signal). Future native carrier integration deferred indefinitely. **Entity model canonical** (§3.26.18.2): six core entities — two primary (Call + CallParticipant) + three derived-artifact (CallRecording + CallTranscript + CallIntelligenceArtifact) + supporting (PhoneAccount + PhoneAccountAccess junction) + cross-tenant pairing via `cross_tenant_call_pairing`. CallTranscript carries `superseded_by_transcript_id` FK self-reference (Phase A refinement integrated) for explicit version chain audit history. **Real-time architectural canon** (§3.26.18.4 + §3.26.18.6, Phase A refinement integrated): Deepgram real-time API (WebSocket connection per active call; partial transcripts at ~250ms cadence); WebSocket/SSE channel for live intelligence delivery to CallOverlay; provider-side real-time webhook subscriptions; reconciliation discipline at call end (partial transcripts → canonical CallTranscript via batch API re-processing; intelligence pipeline re-execution; superseded versions audit-preserved via `superseded_by_artifact_id`). **Multi-framework compliance discipline canonical at primitive level** (§3.26.18.9, Phase A refinement integrated): state-by-state two-party consent enforcement (13-state jurisdictional config) + recording disclosure mechanics (TTS prompt + 5-second delay) + HIPAA-compliant recording mode + FCC TCRA compliance + CFPB Regulation F discipline + cross-primitive opt-out coordination registry (caller-side opt-out detected via call intelligence pipeline propagates to all communication primitives — email + SMS + calendar + phone outbound suspension). **Multi-dimensional cost discipline canonical at primitive level** (§3.26.18.10, Phase A refinement integrated): per-call + per-minute + per-transcription + per-intelligence-prompt cost dimensions. Total per-call cost magnitude $0.20-0.50 for typical 30-minute call. Concrete competitor comparison canonicalized: standalone call intelligence platforms (Gong + Chorus + Fireflies + Otter) charge per-seat licensing ($60-150/seat/month); Bridgeable's Phone-as-platform-primitive framing eliminates context-switch + per-primitive cost discipline replaces per-seat licensing. **Q1-Q12 Phase A deliberations resolved with 5 refinements integrated**: Q1 RingCentral canonical primary + Twilio Voice three documented signals canonicalized; Q2 6-entity decomposition + CallTranscript `superseded_by_transcript_id` FK self-reference; Q3 3-value `recording_default` enum + operator-disclosure-on-call-start TTS prompt + 5-second delay; Q4 Deepgram Nova-2 canonical primary + Whisper alternate deferred + real-time transcription canonical at September; Q5 7 managed prompts canonical + `phone.call_quality_review` deferred with concrete signal; Q6 differential retention (Call indefinite + CallRecording 7-year + CallTranscript 10-year + CallIntelligenceArtifact indefinite/superseded-90-day); Q7 comprehensive multi-framework compliance + cross-primitive opt-out coordination; Q8 multi-dimensional cost discipline + competitor comparison closing prose; Q9 voicemail as canonical sub-state of Call; Q10 simple per-account routing + complex IVR-equivalent routing tree deferred with three documented signals; Q11 real-time call surface canonical at September + real-time architectural canon; Q12 cross-tenant call bilateral consent + per-tenant audit + per-tenant intelligence + per-tenant recording. **Phase B — Surfacing + Composition + Voice-Mediated Workflows + Strategic Vision Integration (13 subsections)**: §3.26.18.11-§3.26.18.23. Three-shape surface decomposition: unified call surface (call-workspace-shape at `/calls` distinct from Space architecture per §3.26.18.11) + Pulse Phone rendering across multiple layers (Communications + Operational layers per §3.26.18.12 hybrid contribution pattern) + real-time call surface canonical at September per §3.26.18.22. **Hybrid layer contribution pattern canonical** (§3.26.18.12, parallel to §3.26.16.10 calendar + §3.26.17.11 SMS): Phone contributes to MULTIPLE Pulse layers — Communications layer (customer-facing call signals) + Operational layer (operational-team-facing call signals). Pattern continuity: Email + Calendar + SMS + Phone all use hybrid layer contribution per §3.26.9 canonical pattern. **Customer-facing vs operational-team-facing conversation discipline** (§3.26.18.21): single primitive with `conversation_orientation` parameter; orientation is composition parameter, not primitive split. **Five canonical action_types** (§3.26.18.20): `outbound_call_attempt_acknowledgment` + `inbound_call_action_item_review` + `compliance_review_required` + `voicemail_follow_up` + `cross_tenant_operational_acknowledgment`. Phone primitive's intelligence-mediated coupling is structurally different — when customer accepts quote on phone call, intelligence extracts action item via `inbound_call_action_item_review` substrate; operator reviews + commits state change. Routes through unified action item substrate, not parallel direct-confirmation action_types. All reuse `platform_action_tokens` substrate. **Total cross-primitive action_types canonical post-Session-5**: Email 1 + Calendar 5 + SMS 5 + Phone 5 = **16 action_types** all reusing `platform_action_tokens` substrate. **Communications + scheduling + reach + intelligence = operations strategic framing locked** (§3.26.18.18, four-part operational substrate completion): Phone primitive's strategic role completes operational substrate four-part composition. Email primitive ships interpersonal coordination; Calendar primitive ships temporal coordination; SMS primitive ships operational reach; Phone primitive ships **operational intelligence** — call recordings + transcripts + intelligence-derived artifacts feed operational state directly via call-intelligence-as-primary-value framework. Three architectural justifications preserved at canon level (Phone-distinctive intelligence-as-primary-value among Layer 1 primitives; voice modality is platform substrate not Phone-specific dimension; real-time + asynchronous duality is architectural model concern not operator-experiential concern). **Voice-mediated workflows canon at September scope** (§3.26.18.16): Phone primitive ships voice input infrastructure consumed by other primitives. Voice input infrastructure (Web Speech API + Deepgram fallback + push-to-talk affordance + audio waveform visualization) + voice-mediated Command Bar (existing SmartPlantCommandBar pattern) + voice-mediated entity reference + hands-free operational use cases (driver console + production floor terminals + dispatcher kiosks + FH director arrangement scribe) + voice input as platform substrate per architectural-elegance discipline. **Voice input cost attribution discipline canonical** (§3.26.18.16, Phase B refinement integrated): voice input transcription cost attribution canonical: voice input consumed by primitive X attributes transcription cost to primitive X's cost discipline. Cross-primitive voice input substrate preserves per-primitive cost attribution clarity. **Q13-Q19 Phase B deliberations resolved with 1 refinement integrated**: Q13 separate /calls route + split-pane call detail composition; Q14 hybrid layer contribution; Q15 "Communications + scheduling + reach + intelligence = operations" + three architectural justifications + concrete competitor comparison; Q16 voice-mediated workflows canonical + voice input cost attribution discipline; Q17 5 canonical action_types + parallel direct-confirmation action_types rejected; Q18 single primitive with conversation_orientation parameter; Q19 real-time call infrastructure canonical at full depth. **Phase C — Focus + Workshop + Intelligence Integration + Visual Canon (4 BRIDGEABLE_MASTER subsections + 8 DESIGN_LANGUAGE subsections)**: §3.26.18.24-§3.26.18.27 + §14.12. Phone-triggered Decision Focus integration ships at canon-faithful depth — six canonical triage queue config types at September scope (`phone_compliance_review_triage` + `phone_action_item_review_triage` + `phone_outbound_attempt_review_triage` + `phone_voicemail_follow_up_triage` + `phone_cross_tenant_acknowledgment_triage` + `phone_cost_warning_triage` Phone-distinctive); Phase 5 triage primitive substrate enables implementation. **Phone-mediated Coordination Focus deferred** — five concrete signals; deferral is **symmetric across email-mediated AND calendar-mediated AND SMS-mediated AND phone-mediated Coordination Focus integration**; single Coordination Focus arc unblocks all four communication primitives' integration. **Phone Intelligence at seven-prompt September scope**: `phone.call_summarize` (Sonnet) + `phone.call_intent_classify` (Haiku) + `phone.call_action_items_extract` (Sonnet) + `phone.call_entity_resolve` (Haiku) + `phone.call_sentiment_classify` (Haiku) + `phone.compliance_review` (Haiku) + `phone.voicemail_summarize` (Haiku). Real-time prompt firing discipline canonical (intent first-pass at 30s; action items first-pass at 60s; sentiment ~500ms; compliance ~1s); post-call reconciliation re-execution against canonical transcript with `superseded_by_artifact_id` audit chain. Operator agency preserved via canonical anti-patterns. **Phone Workshop at seven-template-type granularity** (Q21 confirmation): seven distinct Workshop template types — `call_template` + `recording_rule_template` + `cost_policy_template` + `call_routing_rule_template` + `jurisdictional_config_template` + `intelligence_prompt_policy_template` + `provider_provisioning_template`. Meta-pattern continuity preserved — Workshop template granularity is determined by structural-overlap-of-composition-shapes per §3.26.16.24 calendar precedent. **Total cross-primitive Workshop template types canonical post-Session-5**: Email 1 + Calendar 3 + SMS 5 + Phone 7 = **16 Workshop template types**. Meta-pattern locks across all four communication primitives. **Phone Visual Canon extends §14 Communications Visual System** (Q20): §14.12 ships eight subsections — §14.12.1 Phone call list (call-workspace-shape distinction from Space architecture) + §14.12.2 Call detail rendering split-pane composition (transcript pane + intelligence pane co-presentation discipline) + §14.12.3 Real-time call surface (Phone-distinctive; live transcript ~250ms cadence + live intelligence WebSocket/SSE + minimized call pill + reconciliation discipline visualization) + §14.12.4 Call composition surface (operator-initiated outbound modal + Workshop-template-generated outbound modal + cost breakdown chrome) + §14.12.5 Cross-tenant call chrome + §14.12.6 Operational-action affordance chrome (5 action_types) + §14.12.7 Voice-mediated affordance chrome (Phone-distinctive; push-to-talk + cross-primitive consumption + audio waveform visualization) + §14.12.8 Cross-references. **Architectural model post-Session-5**: 5 primitives + fractal Pulse + universal Command Bar + Workshop primitive + comprehensive Email + Calendar + SMS + Phone primitive canon. Email + Calendar + SMS + Phone = four communication primitives canonicalized at canon-faithful depth. Together they form the **operational substrate four-part composition** — interpersonal coordination + temporal coordination + operational reach + operational intelligence. Phase W-4b Layer 1 implementation arcs (Calendar + SMS + Phone) all open with locked canon foundation. In-platform messaging primitive (Session 6 candidate) sequences forward — tightest scope of four communication primitives. Coordination Focus arc dependency tracked symmetrically across Email + Calendar + SMS + Phone. Voice input as platform substrate canonical (Phone primitive ships voice infrastructure consumed by other primitives; per-primitive cost attribution preserves clarity). Existing Call Intelligence implementation foundation explicitly preserved (no greenfield rebuild). **22 architectural questions Q1-Q22 deliberated and resolved across three phases**. **Canon doc updates this session**: BRIDGEABLE_MASTER §3.26.18 NEW (27 subsections); DESIGN_LANGUAGE §14.12 NEW (8 sub-sections including cross-references); AESTHETIC_ARC Session 5 entry; CLAUDE.md §14 entry. **No code shipped.** **Sequencing forward**: Session 6 candidate (in-platform messaging primitive canon) opens; Phase W-4b Layer 1 Phone implementation arc opens with locked canon foundation; existing Call Intelligence implementation maps cleanly to canonical Phone primitive entity model (no greenfield rebuild). Total canon work post-Session-5: Sessions 1 + 1.5 + 2 + 3 + 4 + 5 ≈ 22-26h dedicated canon work — substantial; bounded; architecturally justified per §3.26.7.5 ("MVP cuts compound as debt"). Four communication primitives canonicalized at canon-faithful depth before any implementation-time canon-vs-implementation drift surfaces.

- **Session 4 — SMS Primitive Canon (canon locked, 2026-05-12):** Pure architectural canon work matching Sessions 1 + 1.5 + 2 + 3 discipline pattern. NO CODE this session. SMS primitive canonicalized as third concrete Phase W-4b Layer 1 communication primitive (after Email §3.26.15 + Calendar §3.26.16); 30 canon subsections across BRIDGEABLE_MASTER + DESIGN_LANGUAGE locking SMS primitive against settled foundation. Total ~3.5 hours conversational/architectural work; canon doc batch write at session close. **§3.26.17 introduced as comprehensive SMS Primitive section** (24 subsections). SMS established as **third concrete Communications layer primitive** per Phase W-4b sequence step 3 (after Email Step 1 + Calendar Step 2; sequencing per §3.26.6.4). **Architectural framing — SMS as three-axis-distinct primitive**: SMS is **architecturally lighter than Email** on substrate (no HTML rendering; no threading complexity; no MIME parsing; tighter 1600-char body; single-image MMS canonical) and **architecturally narrower than Calendar** on provider abstraction (Twilio dominant US provider; Email shipped 4 providers, Calendar shipped 4, SMS bounded narrower) but **architecturally heavier on compliance discipline** (TCPA + 10DLC at primitive level distinct from Email's CAN-SPAM and Calendar's iCalendar standard) and **architecturally heavier on cost discipline** (per-message cost ~$0.0075 outbound per §3.26.17.9 distinct from Email's bulk send economics). Three-axis-distinct shape shapes canon — entity model is straightforward; compliance + cost integration is comprehensive; provider abstraction is narrower but high-fidelity Twilio integration. **Phase A — Entity Model + Storage + Compliance + Cost (9 subsections)**: §3.26.17.1-§3.26.17.9. Native-vs-integration deliberation reaffirmed verbatim — native SMS implementation requires SMPP carrier integration + direct CTIA registry participation + per-carrier 10DLC compliance attestation + per-carrier short-code or long-code provisioning + carrier-direct routing infrastructure + per-carrier deliverability monitoring + international roaming carrier negotiations (deferred indefinitely — US-only at canonical scope) + carrier-direct billing reconciliation. Integrate-now-make-native-later commitment canonical — Bridgeable owns architectural model; provider abstraction handles transport (Twilio canonical; Bandwidth + alternate providers deferred per §3.26.17.4 + concrete signal). Future native CTIA participation deferred indefinitely. **Entity model canonical** (§3.26.17.2): five core entities (3 primary `sms_conversations` + `sms_messages` + `sms_participants` + 2 supporting `sms_accounts` + `sms_account_access` junction + optional `sms_message_attachments` for MMS) + cross-tenant pairing via `cross_tenant_sms_pairing`. **TCPA + 10DLC compliance discipline at primitive level** (§3.26.17.8): per-conversation opt-in tracking via 4-value enum (`opted_in` / `opted_out` / `opt_in_pending` / `unknown`) + 5-value method enum (`provider_imported` / `customer_initiated_inbound` / `manual_attestation` / `web_form` / `paper_form`) + indefinite audit log + STOP/HELP carrier-side handling + revocation discipline. Compliance is operational protection, not bureaucratic burden. **10DLC hybrid registration model** (§3.26.17.3): platform-owned brand + tenant-owned campaign — Bridgeable's TCR brand registration shared across all platform tenants; per-tenant 10DLC campaign registration tenant-owned; tenant operators do NOT register their own 10DLC brand. Bridgeable provides Workshop-authored campaign-registration template per §3.26.17.24. **Per-message cost discipline at primitive level** (§3.26.17.9): per-tenant + per-account + per-conversation cost tracking + Workshop-authored cost policies + pre-send budget cap enforcement; per-message cost ~$0.0075 outbound (Twilio canonical); pre-send budget cap enforcement structurally prevents runaway-cost scenarios. **Q1-Q9 Phase A deliberations resolved with 4 refinements integrated**: Q1 hybrid 10DLC registration confirmed + short-code signal threshold canonicalized (sustained >50,000 outbound SMS/month per tenant + tenant willingness to fund short-code lease ~$1000/month bounded measurable trigger); Q2 Twilio canonical confirmed + concrete TCR lead-time discipline canonicalized (10DLC campaign registration takes 1-3 weeks; tenant onboarding accommodates lead-time via Workshop campaign-registration template); Q3 from/to + 30-day window canonical conversation reconstruction confirmed; Q4 comprehensive TCPA discipline confirmed; Q5 comprehensive cost discipline confirmed + concrete competitor comparison prose canonicalized (Twilio Studio + MessageBird Flow Builder + Zapier SMS workflows treat SMS as standalone tool requiring operator context-switch; Bridgeable's SMS-as-operational-primitive eliminates context-switch); Q6 hybrid 10DLC registration confirmed; Q7 industry-canonical opt-out keywords confirmed (STOP / UNSUBSCRIBE / END / QUIT / CANCEL); Q8 MMS bounded with image size discipline confirmed (single image per message; 1MB Twilio canonical max; multi-attachment MMS deferred per §3.26.17.20); Q9 7-year retention default confirmed (hybrid retention discipline; tenant-configurable to 90-day minimum). **Phase B — Surfacing + Composition + Strategic Vision Integration (11 subsections)**: §3.26.17.10-§3.26.17.20. Three-shape surface decomposition: unified messaging surface (SMS-workspace-shape at `/sms` distinct from Space architecture per §3.26.17.10) + Pulse SMS rendering across multiple layers (Communications + Operational layers per §3.26.17.11 hybrid contribution pattern) + Workshop-template send modal per §3.26.17.14. **Hybrid layer contribution pattern canonical** (§3.26.17.11, parallel to §3.26.16.10 calendar precedent): SMS contributes to MULTIPLE Pulse layers — Communications layer ("who needs me?" interpersonal SMS signals) + Operational layer ("what's my work right now?" operational-team SMS signals). NOT a dedicated SMS layer. Pattern locks for future Layer 1 communication primitives (phone, messaging) — pattern continuity across Email + Calendar + SMS established. **Customer-facing vs operational-team-facing conversation discipline** (§3.26.17.19): single primitive with `conversation_orientation` parameter (`customer_facing` | `operational_team_facing`); orientation is composition parameter, not primitive split; drives default Pulse layer assignment + default routing on outbound + default visual chrome surfacing per §14.11. **Five canonical action_types** (§3.26.17.18): `customer_confirmation` + `driver_check_in_confirmation` + `delivery_arrival_acknowledgment` + `service_day_acknowledgment` + `cross_tenant_operational_acknowledgment`. **Two action patterns canonical**: keyword-reply (strict matching: case-insensitive + trailing whitespace tolerant only) + magic-link (single-action commit; tokens reuse `platform_action_tokens` substrate per §3.26.16.17 substrate consolidation). Strict keyword-reply matching at canonical scope preserves operator agency per §3.26.14.14.5; fuzzy keyword matching via Bridgeable Intelligence (`sms.intent_classify`) deferred per §3.26.7.5 with concrete signal documented. **Communications + scheduling + reach = operations strategic framing locked** (§3.26.17.16, trifecta-completion): SMS primitive's strategic role completes operational substrate trifecta. Email primitive ships interpersonal coordination; Calendar primitive ships temporal coordination; SMS primitive ships **operational reach** — Layer 1 primitive that bypasses surface-navigation friction and reaches operators + customers directly in their pocket. Concrete competitor comparison canonicalized: Twilio Studio + MessageBird Flow Builder + Zapier SMS workflows treat SMS as standalone workflow tool — operators leave operational context to manage SMS workflows in separate tool; Bridgeable's SMS-as-operational-reach-primitive framing eliminates context-switch by treating SMS as platform primitive. **State-changes-generate-SMS canonicalized at depth** (§3.26.17.18): outbound-weighted operational-coupling more pervasive than Email's inverse coupling. Canonical state-change → SMS mappings for Delivery scheduling, Driver check-in, Delivery arrival, Service day, Cross-tenant operational acknowledgments. Drafted-not-auto-sent discipline preserves operator agency per §3.26.14.14.5. Inverse: keyword-reply or magic-link propagates state changes — bidirectional binding canonical. **Q10-Q14 Phase B deliberations resolved with 2 refinements integrated**: Q10 separate `/sms` route confirmed (SMS-workspace-shape distinct from Space architecture parallel to §3.26.16.9); Q11 hybrid layer contribution pattern confirmed (Communications + Operational layers split); Q12 5 action_types + 2 patterns with strict keyword-reply matching confirmed + strict matching refinement canonicalized (case-insensitive + trailing whitespace tolerant only; fuzzy matching deferred); Q13 single primitive with conversation_orientation parameter confirmed; Q14 SMS-as-operational-reach trifecta-completion framing confirmed + trifecta-completion strategic prose canonicalized (Email + Calendar + SMS together ARE the operational substrate of the platform). **Phase C — Focus + Workshop + Intelligence Integration + Visual Canon (4 BRIDGEABLE_MASTER subsections + 6 DESIGN_LANGUAGE subsections)**: §3.26.17.21-§3.26.17.24 + §14.11. SMS-triggered Decision Focus integration ships at canon-faithful depth — five canonical triage queue config types at September scope (`sms_compliance_review_triage` + `sms_response_review_triage` + `sms_budget_warning_triage` + `sms_action_review_triage` + `sms_opt_in_pending_triage`); Phase 5 triage primitive substrate enables implementation when Phase W-4b SMS Step ships triage integration (no Coordination-Focus-arc dependency). **SMS-mediated Coordination Focus deferred** — five concrete signals from Step 5b investigation; deferral is **symmetric across email-mediated AND calendar-mediated AND SMS-mediated Coordination Focus integration**; single Coordination Focus arc unblocks all three communication primitives. **SMS Intelligence at four-prompt September scope**: `sms.intent_classify` (Haiku) + `sms.priority_classification` (Haiku) + `sms.compliance_review` (Haiku) + `sms.response_draft` (Sonnet). Operator agency preserved via canonical anti-patterns; suggestion telemetry privacy-preserving. **SMS Workshop at five-template-type granularity** (Q16 confirmation): five distinct Workshop template types — `sms_template` + `sms_opt_in_flow_template` + `sms_campaign_registration_template` + `sms_cost_policy_template` + `sms_response_router_template`. Meta-pattern continuity preserved — Workshop template granularity is determined by structural-overlap-of-composition-shapes per §3.26.16.24 calendar precedent. SMS's five template shapes are structurally different (opt-in flow ≠ campaign registration ≠ cost policy ≠ response router ≠ message template) → per-template-type appropriate. Future Workshop integrations (phone, messaging) apply same heuristic. **SMS Visual Canon extends §14 Communications Visual System** (Q15): §14.11 ships six subsections matching §14.10 Calendar precedent + §14.9 Email precedent — §14.11.1 SMS conversation list (SMS-workspace-shape distinction from Space architecture) + §14.11.2 SMS conversation detail (chronological message sequence + Pattern 2 sub-cards distinct from Email's Pattern 2 cards) + §14.11.3 SMS composition surface (new conversation modal + inline reply + Workshop-template send modal) + §14.11.4 Cross-tenant SMS conversation chrome (cross-tenant indicator + bilateral consent state + MMS visual chrome + compliance audit chrome) + §14.11.5 Operational-action affordance chrome (keyword-reply + magic-link + MMS photo proof affordance) + §14.11.6 Cross-references. **Architectural model post-Session-4**: 5 primitives + fractal Pulse + universal Command Bar + Workshop primitive + comprehensive Email + Calendar + SMS primitive canon. Email + Calendar + SMS = three communication primitives canonicalized at canon-faithful depth. Together they form the **operational substrate trifecta** — interpersonal coordination + temporal coordination + operational reach. Phone (Session 5 candidate) + Messaging (Session 6 candidate) sequence forward. Coordination Focus arc dependency tracked symmetrically across Email + Calendar + SMS — single Coordination Focus arc unblocks all three communication primitives' integration. Six canonical platform action_types: `quote_approval` + `service_date_acceptance` + `delivery_date_acceptance` + `joint_event_acceptance` + `recurring_meeting_proposal` + `event_reschedule_proposal` (Calendar) + 5 SMS-specific action_types per §3.26.17.18 (substrate consolidation: SMS magic-link tokens reuse `platform_action_tokens` substrate). **16 architectural questions Q1-Q16 deliberated and resolved across three phases**. **Canon doc updates this session**: BRIDGEABLE_MASTER §3.26.17 NEW (24 subsections); DESIGN_LANGUAGE §14.11 NEW (6 sub-sections including cross-references); AESTHETIC_ARC Session 4 entry; CLAUDE.md §14 entry. **No code shipped.** **Sequencing forward**: Session 5 candidate (Phone primitive canon) opens; Phase W-4b Layer 1 SMS implementation arc opens with locked canon foundation. Total canon work post-Session-4: Sessions 1 + 1.5 + 2 + 3 + 4 ≈ 19-22h dedicated canon work — substantial; bounded; architecturally justified per §3.26.7.5 ("MVP cuts compound as debt"). - **Session 3 — Calendar Primitive Canon (canon locked, 2026-05-09):** Pure architectural canon session matching Sessions 1 + 1.5 + 2 discipline pattern. NO CODE this session. Calendar primitive canonicalized as second concrete Phase W-4b Layer 1 communication-temporal primitive (after Email §3.26.15); 30 canon subsections across BRIDGEABLE_MASTER + DESIGN_LANGUAGE locking Calendar primitive against settled foundation. Total ~3.5 hours conversational/architectural work; canon doc batch write at session close. **§3.26.16 introduced as comprehensive Calendar Primitive section** (25 subsections matching §3.26.15 Email primitive depth). Calendar established as **second concrete Communications layer primitive** per Phase W-4b sequence step 2 (parallel-able with Email Step 1; sequencing per §3.26.6.4). **Architectural framing — Calendar as dual-character primitive**: Calendar is **architecturally lighter than Email** on novel architecture (RFC 5545 iCalendar standard; provider abstraction simpler; free/busy + attendee-status well-understood) but **architecturally heavier than Email** on operational coupling (calendar events are state-bound to operational entities by their nature; state-changes-generate-calendar-events more pervasive than email's inverse coupling). **Phase A — Entity Model + Storage Architecture (8 subsections)**: §3.26.16.1-§3.26.16.8. Native-vs-integration deliberation reaffirmed verbatim — ~3-4 months engineering scope for native-from-day-one (RFC 5545 iCalendar + RFC 5546 iTIP + CalDAV server + recurrence engine + IANA tzdb + iTIP processing + free/busy publication). Integrate-now-make-native-later commitment canonical — Bridgeable owns architectural model; provider abstraction handles transport (Google Calendar API + MS Graph + CalDAV deferred + local Bridgeable-native provider as canonical first-class citizen). Entity model canonical (§3.26.16.2): seven core entities (3 primary `calendar_events` + `calendar_event_attendees` + `calendar_accounts` + 4 supporting `calendar_account_access` + `calendar_event_instance_overrides` + `calendar_event_reminders` + `calendar_event_linkages`) + cross-tenant pairing via `cross_tenant_event_pairing`. RRULE-as-source-of-truth canonical recurrence (§3.26.16.4) — Bridgeable owns recurrence engine; provider is bridge; cross-tenant scheduling resolution + free/busy availability + operational-state-coupled-to-calendar functioning + Workshop scheduling-rule templates all benefit independent of provider availability. **Q1-Q7 Phase A deliberations resolved**: Q1 RRULE-as-source-of-truth confirmed; Q2 free/busy bilateral consent confirmed (privacy-preserving busy/free + duration default; both tenants explicitly opt in for full details; either unilaterally revokes — matches §3.26.11.10 cross-tenant Focus consent canon); Q3 hybrid shared + personal calendar account model confirmed; Q4 polymorphic linkage pattern-parallel + extension to include `quote` entity (calendar events naturally link to Quotes from §3.26.15.17 + Step 4c — quote follow-up call, quote presentation meeting, quote signing event, quote delivery scheduling); Q5 Google + MS Graph + local canonical scope; CalDAV deferred per §3.26.7.5 with concrete signals documented; Q6 RFC 5545 EXDATE + modified-instance shadowing canonical verbatim adoption; Q7 dual-surface reminder mechanics with **deduplication discipline** — canonical default `provider_default` only; passive Bridgeable-surface display distinct from reminder firing; explicit operator opt-in for additional Bridgeable-surface reminders (prevents canonical-default-double-notification). **Phase B — Surfacing + Scheduling UX + Strategic Vision Integration (13 subsections)**: §3.26.16.9-§3.26.16.21. Three-shape surface decomposition (Q8): unified calendar surface (calendar-workspace-shape at `/calendar` distinct from Space architecture per §3.26.x — clarification prevents canon-vs-implementation drift) + Pulse calendar widget (composed across Communications + Operational layers per Q9 hybrid contribution) + Calendar Period Pulse (scoped Pulse summonable per §3.26.12 fractal Pulse). **Hybrid contribution pattern canonical** (Q9 + extension): calendar contributes to MULTIPLE Pulse layers based on signal type — Communications layer ("who needs me?" interpersonal-scheduling signals) + Operational layer ("what's my work right now?" operational-scheduling signals) + scoped Period Pulse (full scheduling workspace). NOT a dedicated Schedule layer. **Pattern locks for future Layer 1 communication primitives** — SMS interpersonal-vs-operational signals, phone call-incoming-vs-alarm signals, messaging direct-mention-vs-system-event signals all decompose along same cognitive-question split. Pattern canonicalized as reference for §14.11 SMS + §14.12 Phone + §14.13 Messaging future canon. **Five canonical action_types** (Q10 + extension): `service_date_acceptance` + `delivery_date_acceptance` + `joint_event_acceptance` + `recurring_meeting_proposal` + **`event_reschedule_proposal`** (fifth added — post-confirmation reschedule has different semantics than counter-proposal in initial-negotiation; without `event_reschedule_proposal` canonical, reschedule falls back to ad-hoc email + phone calls, kill-the-portal anti-pattern). All reuse Step 4c email_action_tokens substrate; substrate consolidation direction note canonicalized (table semantically renamed `platform_action_tokens` to prevent architectural fragmentation). **Communications + scheduling = operations strategic framing locked** (Q11 + strengthening): Calendar primitive's strategic role completes operational substrate trifecta. Email primitive ships interpersonal coordination; Calendar primitive ships temporal coordination; together they ARE the operational substrate of the platform. **Concrete competitor comparison canonicalized**: Salesforce ships Calendly integration; HubSpot ships scheduling-link integration; both are bolt-ons — calendar is integration, not native primitive. Operators using these platforms context-switch between operational state (in CRM) and scheduling state (in calendar tool). Bridgeable's calendar-as-operational-substrate framing eliminates this context-switch by treating calendar as platform primitive — operators never leave operational context to manage scheduling. Sharpens September Wilbert demo positioning at canon level. **Cross-tenant native scheduling at canon-faithful depth** (Q12): full depth matching §3.26.15.20 cross-tenant native messaging precedent. Joint event schema + bilateral state synchronization + entity references + retroactive linkage handling + iterative-negotiation pattern + per-tenant masking + audit logs. Hopkins+Sunnycrest joint service-day coordination is canonical Wilbert demo scenario. **Magic-link parallel canonical** (Q13): non-Bridgeable attendee receives calendar invite with magic-link to accept/decline/propose-counter without portal login. Same kill-the-portal canon as §3.26.15.17. Token infrastructure reuses Step 4c email_action_tokens substrate. **State-changes-generate-calendar-events canonicalized at depth** (§3.26.16.18): bidirectional binding more pervasive than email's inverse coupling. Canonical state-change → calendar-event mappings for SalesOrder.scheduled_date, FHCase.service_date, Quote.delivery_date, WorkOrder.scheduled_date, Equipment.next_maintenance_date, ComplianceRequirement.expires_at, Disinterment.scheduled_date. Drafted-not-auto-sent discipline preserves operator agency per §3.26.14.14.5. Inverse: calendar acceptance propagates state changes — bidirectional binding canonical. **Phase C — Focus + Workshop + Intelligence Integration + Visual Canon (4 BRIDGEABLE_MASTER subsections + 5 DESIGN_LANGUAGE subsections)**: §3.26.16.22-§3.26.16.25 + §14.10. Calendar-triggered Decision Focus integration ships at canon-faithful depth — five canonical triage queue config types at September scope (`calendar_conflict_triage` + `calendar_counter_time_triage` + `calendar_capacity_overflow_triage` + `calendar_reschedule_cascade_triage`); Phase 5 triage primitive substrate enables implementation when Phase W-4b Calendar Step ships triage integration (no Coordination-Focus-arc dependency). **Calendar-mediated Coordination Focus deferred** — same five concrete signals from Step 5b investigation; deferral is **symmetric across email-mediated AND calendar-mediated Coordination Focus integration**; single Coordination Focus arc unblocks both communication primitives. **Calendar Intelligence at four-prompt September scope**: `calendar.conflict_detection` (Haiku) + `calendar.priority_classification` (Haiku) + `calendar.scheduling_suggest` (Sonnet) + `calendar.attendee_response_summarize` (Haiku). Operator agency preserved via canonical anti-patterns; suggestion telemetry privacy-preserving. **Calendar Workshop at three-template-type granularity** (Q15): three distinct Workshop template types — `event_template` + `recurring_meeting_template` + `scheduling_rule_template`. **Meta-pattern canonicalized**: Workshop template granularity is determined by structural-overlap-of-composition-shapes, not by primitive count. Email's three composition shapes (new/reply/forward) share substantial structural overlap → single template_type appropriate. Calendar's three template shapes are structurally different → per-template-type appropriate. Future Workshop integrations (SMS, phone, messaging) apply same heuristic. **Calendar Visual Canon extends §14 Communications Visual System** (Q14): §14.10 ships five subsections matching §14.9 Email Primitive Visual Canon precedent — §14.10.1 Calendar grid (month/week/day views; calendar-workspace-shape distinction from Space architecture) + §14.10.2 Agenda view (Pattern 2 card per row; status dot canon) + §14.10.3 Event detail (Pattern 2 card with internal sections; recurrence + attendees + linked entities + internal commentary) + §14.10.4 Event creation (quick event + detailed event modal + cross-tenant proposal modal) + §14.10.5 Operational-action affordance chrome (parallel to §14.9.5; inline action affordance + magic-link contextual surface + counter-proposal flow + decline flow + reschedule flow). **Eight refinements integrated at canon batch write time** parallel to Session 2 mid-session canon hygiene discipline (Phase A Q2 + Q4 + Q7 refinements; Phase B Q8 + Q9 + Q10 + Q11 + Q13 refinements/extensions). **Architectural model post-Session-3**: 5 primitives + fractal Pulse + universal Command Bar + Workshop primitive + comprehensive Email + Calendar primitive canon. Email primitive canon comprehensive (25 subsections; Steps 1-5 shipped); Calendar primitive canon comprehensive (25 subsections; Calendar implementation arc opens). Coordination Focus arc dependency explicitly preserved (deferred for both email-mediated AND calendar-mediated integration; single arc unblocks both). Six canonical platform action_types: `quote_approval` + `service_date_acceptance` + `delivery_date_acceptance` + `joint_event_acceptance` + `recurring_meeting_proposal` + `event_reschedule_proposal`. **15 architectural questions Q1-Q15 deliberated and resolved across three phases**. **Canon doc updates this session**: BRIDGEABLE_MASTER §3.26.16 NEW (25 subsections); DESIGN_LANGUAGE §14.10 NEW (6 sub-sections including cross-references); AESTHETIC_ARC Session 3 entry; CLAUDE.md §14 entry. **No code shipped.** **Sequencing forward**: Session 4 candidate (SMS primitive canon) opens; Phase W-4b Layer 1 Calendar implementation arc opens with locked canon foundation. Total canon work post-Session-3: Sessions 1 + 1.5 + 2 + 3 ≈ 16-19h dedicated canon work — substantial; bounded; architecturally justified per §3.26.7.5 ("MVP cuts compound as debt"). Two communication primitives canonicalized at canon-faithful depth before any implementation-time canon-vs-implementation drift surfaces.

- **Phase W-4b Layer 1 Email Step 5 — cross-surface email rendering (complete, 2026-05-08):** Three of five §3.26.15.10 canonical email rendering surfaces ship; canon-faithful scope adjusted from spec at investigation phase based on three concurrent canon-conflicts (full halt-and-report at session start; user approved scope adjustment). Three explicit deferrals tracked per §3.26.7.5 + Sessions 1-1.5-2 + Steps 1-4c discipline. **Surface 1 — `email_glance` widget (full Phase W-3 catalog citizen)**: widget definition seeded in `widget_registry.py` with `pulse_grid` + `spaces_pin` + `dashboard_grid` supported_surfaces, Glance-only variant per §14 Communications canon, `Mail` Lucide icon per §14.2. Backend service `app/services/widgets/email_glance_service.py` computing unread count + top sender + cross-tenant indicator across user's accessible accounts via EmailAccountAccess junction (mirrors `inbox_service._accessible_account_ids` access enforcement); `target_thread_id` set ONLY when single-thread surface so click navigation routes to specific thread; `ai_priority_count` placeholder zero today (W-4b sequence step 7-8 future signal — Haiku priority scoring lands with communications stream synthesizer). `GET /api/v1/widget-data/email-glance` endpoint matches existing widget budget. Frontend `EmailGlanceWidget.tsx` ships three density tiers per §13.4.1 + §14.4-14.5 (Default ≥121px / Compact 101-120 / Ultra-compact 80-100) — all three rendered simultaneously with CSS `@container piece` queries dispatching visibility via `email-glance-widget-pulse-{default,compact,ultra-compact}` classes (canonical pattern matching `anomalies-widget-pulse-*`); spaces_pin variant renders single-line Pattern 1 tablet (no density tiers). Click routes: single-thread → `/inbox?thread_id={id}`, multi-thread → `/inbox?status=unread`, empty + has access → `/inbox`, no access → `/inbox` graceful. **Surface 2 — Customer email threads composition source (data-layer-only; substrate-ready)**: backend service `app/services/email/customer_email_threads_service.py::get_threads_for_customer(customer_entity_id, user, limit)` computing recent threads via two-source resolution — (1) explicit `EmailThreadLinkage` rows with `linked_entity_type="customer"` + `linked_entity_id=<CompanyEntity.id>`, (2) `EmailParticipant.resolved_company_entity_id` matching the customer entity. Union deduped via Python set; sorted by `last_message_at DESC`; capped at `limit` (default 5, hard ceiling 50). Per-thread `linkage_source` provenance returned (`"manual"` / `"participant"` / `"both"`). Cross-tenant CompanyEntity probe returns existence-hiding empty payload (`customer_name: null`, `threads: []`, `total_count: 0`). Dismissed linkages (`dismissed_at NOT NULL`) filtered. Latest-message snippet bounded to 96 chars. New endpoint `GET /api/v1/pulse/email-threads-for-customer/{customer_entity_id}?limit=N` (`limit` validator: 1 ≤ N ≤ 50). **Canonical resource for future scoped Pulse Customer Pulse consumption** — when scoped Pulse summoning + per-template slot mapping infrastructure ships per §3.26.12.4, Customer Pulse template's email_threads slot consumes this endpoint rather than building parallel query patterns. Future Sonnet sessions building scoped Pulse infrastructure should find this endpoint and extend rather than duplicate. **Surface 3 — V-1c CRM activity feed integration (full implementation)**: new `app/services/email/activity_feed_integration.py::log_email_event_for_thread(message, thread, actor_user_id?)` writes activity log rows for every CompanyEntity that should surface the email event in their V-1c activity feed. **`master_company_id` resolver helper** handles four shapes — (1) explicit `EmailThreadLinkage(linked_entity_type="customer")` → linked_entity_id IS CompanyEntity.id directly; (2) `EmailParticipant.resolved_company_entity_id` → directly; (3) `EmailThreadLinkage(linked_entity_type="fh_case")` → indirect via `FHCase.customer_id → Customer.master_company_id`; (4) `EmailThreadLinkage(linked_entity_type="sales_order")` → indirect via `SalesOrder.customer_id → Customer.master_company_id`. Defensive try/except around each indirect path so model issues never block email ingestion. Wired at two write sites: `ingestion.py::ingest_provider_message` (step 10, after audit log) for inbound messages + `outbound_service.py::send_message` (step 10, after audit log) for outbound messages — both best-effort, logger.exception on failure, never block the caller. **Activity row body carries `thread_id={uuid}` reference** for click-through routing — existing `RecentActivityWidget` already maps `email` activity_type to "logged an email" verb (line 75); zero widget changes. Title format `"{Received|Sent} email — {subject}"`. Tenant isolation enforced — every activity write checks `tenant_id == thread.tenant_id`; cross-tenant CompanyEntity references NEVER written into the caller's activity feed (per §3.25.x masking — partner tenant's CompanyEntity surfaces only in their own activity log). **Three explicit deferrals tracked with §3.26.7.5 canon-faithful rationale**: (1) Communications layer composition (`communications_layer_service.py` + LayerName literal extension to `"communications"`) deferred to W-4b sequence step 6 per §3.26.6.4; concrete signal warranting canonicalization: all 4 communication primitives (email + SMS + phone + in-platform messaging) functional and ready for cross-primitive composition; (2) Communications cross-primitive intelligence stream (`communications_intelligence_service.py` synthesis) deferred to W-4b sequence step 7-8; concrete signal: communications layer service shipped + multiple primitive signal contributions available for synthesis; (3) Customer Pulse template extension (slot mapping for `email_threads` composition source) deferred until scoped Pulse infrastructure ships per §3.26.12.4; concrete signal: scoped Pulse summoning surface + per-template slot mapping infrastructure + composition resolution engine in place. **Halt-and-report discipline validated**: prompt's "concerns worth surfacing if encountered" matched 2 of 3 surfaces; investigation surfaced the canon-conflicts before any code was written; user approved scope adjustment; final scope ships canon-faithfully with explicit deferral catalog. Same discipline pattern as Steps 1-4c (Step 2 deferred IMAP IDLE; Step 4b deferred Workshop role-routing; Step 4c deferred SMS in DeliveryChannel stub). **Tests shipped Phase W-4b Step 5**: 24 backend in `test_email_primitive_step5.py` across 9 test classes (TestEmailGlanceWidgetDefinition × 1, TestEmailGlanceDataService × 6, TestEmailGlanceAPI × 2, TestCustomerEmailThreadsService × 6, TestCustomerEmailThreadsAPI × 3, TestMasterCompanyResolver × 3, TestActivityFeedWriteSites × 3) + 15 frontend in `EmailGlanceWidget.test.tsx` across 5 describe blocks (density tiers × 4, click navigation × 4, empty states × 2, sender display × 2, spaces_pin variant × 3). **Full Phase W-4b Email Steps 1-5 backend regression: 196/196 passing, no regressions**. Full vitest 971/971 passing (956 baseline + 15 new). tsc clean, vite build clean (4.69s). **Migration head unchanged**: r66_email_action_tokens — Step 5 is pure service-layer + frontend work, no schema changes.

- **Canon refinement (canonical decisions preservation, 2026-05-08):** §3.26.15.8 encryption interpretation prose (per-tenant key isolation = per-row FK-scoped isolation under platform master key per Step 2 investigation; future per-tenant key derivation deferred with documented signals) + §3.26.15.4 IMAP IDLE deferral prose (polling-only canonical via APScheduler; long-lived IDLE deferred with documented signals). Preserves yesterday's canonical decisions in canon prose; prevents re-litigation.

- **Session 2 — Email Primitive Canon (canon locked, 2026-05-07):** Pure architectural canon work. NO CODE this session. Email primitive emergence canonicalized as foundational Phase W-4b Layer 1 communication primitive; integrate-now-make-native-later framework formalized; operational-state-coupled-to-communication discipline established; cross-tenant native messaging architecture canonicalized; 25+ explicit deferrals tracked. Total ~4 hours conversational/architectural work; canon doc batch write at session close. **Phase A — Entity Model + Storage Architecture (8 subsections)**: §3.26.15 introduced as comprehensive Email Primitive section (25 subsections total). Email established as **first concrete Communications layer primitive** per Phase W-4b sequence step 1. **Native-vs-integration deliberation prose canonicalized verbatim** (§3.26.15.1): 4-6 months engineering scope for native-from-day-one (SMTP/IMAP/POP3 + MIME + attachments + sandboxed HTML + threading + spam + deliverability + bounce + DKIM/SPF/DMARC); ongoing operational infrastructure (24/7 deliverability monitoring + abuse handling + provider reputation management + queue management at scale); reputation risk (Bridgeable's domain reputation tied to platform health); regulatory dimensions (CAN-SPAM + GDPR data residency + jurisdictional sovereignty). The integrate-now-make-native-later commitment is canonical — Bridgeable's email primitive owns its **architectural model** (entity model + UI surfaces + Intelligence integration + operational coupling) while delegating **transport** to provider abstraction (Gmail API + MS Graph + IMAP fallback + transactional providers like Resend). Future native transport implementation lands behind provider abstraction without disturbing architectural model. **Entity model canonical** (§3.26.15.2-§3.26.15.4): four core entities — `email_threads` + `email_messages` + `email_accounts` + `email_user_status`. Per-user status independence (§3.26.15.13) load-bearing — two users in the same tenant see different status state for the same thread. **Provider abstraction canonical** (§3.26.15.6): `EmailProvider` protocol (similar to Phase D-7 `DeliveryChannel` Protocol) with implementations for Gmail API + MS Graph + IMAP + transactional providers. Native transport implementation slots in as additional provider when ready. **HTML body sandbox + outbound sanitization** (§3.26.15.5): inbound HTML messages render in sandboxed iframe (no scripts + no top-level navigation + no popups + sandboxed origin); inline images resolved via Content-ID; outbound HTML composer output sanitized via DOMPurify-equivalent. **Storage architecture canonical** (§3.26.15.4-§3.26.15.5): hybrid retention with tenant-configurable extension. Recent message body cached in Bridgeable storage (default 90 days); older messages reference provider via tombstone records. Tenant configuration extends cache window per tenant policy. Attachments are first-class VaultItems per §3.26.15.4. **Phase A refinement**: native-vs-integration deliberation rationale canonicalized verbatim so future canon sessions don't re-litigate the trade-off. **Phase B — Surfacing + Summoning + Composition + Strategic Vision Integration (13 subsections)**: §3.26.15.10 through §3.26.15.21 + strategic vision integration items. **Inbox surfacing canonical** (§3.26.15.12): list as default presentation mode (Q5 leaned-toward) — chronological thread list with per-user status indicators. Alternative presentation modes (kanban + calendar + grouped) deferred per §3.26.7.5. **Per-message inline reply + modal new/forward** (§3.26.15.13, Q3 canonical): reply to existing thread happens inline at thread bottom (preserves thread context flow); new email + forward open modal composer (discrete authoring tasks deserve focus-mode treatment). **Per-user status independence** (§3.26.15.13, Q1 canonical): per-user status primary; thread-level operational state for shared signals. **Cross-tenant native messaging** (§3.26.15.10, kill-the-portal architectural discipline): cross-tenant threads use Bridgeable-native messaging directly — no external email transport between platform tenants. Cross-tenant threads are first-class platform entities with cross-tenant masking per §3.25.x. **Each tenant has its own copy under its own ownership** (§3.26.15.18 — independent per-tenant cross-tenant copies); operational state changes don't mirror to the other tenant's copy. **Retroactive linkage canon detail** (§3.26.15.20, Q4 canonical): when partner tenant onboards mid-thread, prior thread copies are explicitly NOT auto-mirrored. Each tenant's copy starts at moment of cross-tenant relationship establishment. Architectural rationale: retroactive auto-mirror across tenant boundaries violates data sovereignty. **Entity linkage canonical** (§3.26.15.7): emails link to Bridgeable entities (cases + orders + invoices + tasks + contacts) three ways — explicit pre-link at compose time, explicit post-link via per-message action, or retroactive via Intelligence detection. **Front-style shared inbox UX** (§3.26.15.15): cross-tenant threads visible to multiple operators within a tenant — shared inbox mental model where operators collaborate on customer conversations. **Operational-state-coupled-to-communication** (§3.26.15.16): emails carry operational-action affordances binding email content to platform operations. Mark-as-task creates Task linked to message; Link-to-entity binds thread to entity; Generate-state-change surfaces Intelligence-detected candidate state mutations. Email is not a separate inbox island. **State-changes-generate-communications** (§3.26.15.17): inverse coupling — state changes in the platform automatically draft outbound communications. Drafted emails surface in Workshop-managed templates and require explicit operator confirm before send per §3.26.14.14.5 operator agency discipline. **Cross-primitive intelligence stream** (§3.26.15.19): communications intelligence stream synthesizes across email + SMS + phone + messaging at Pattern C composition. **Strategic vision deferral catalog** (§3.26.15.21): 25+ deferrals canonicalized including kanban + calendar presentation modes, real-time collaborative composition, native transport, advanced filtering UI, mass actions, email rules engine, signature management UI, vacation auto-responder, multi-account inbox unified view, threading algorithm tuning, attachment preview enhancements, search ranking refinement, archive policy customization, draft autosave conflict resolution, multi-tenant inbox aggregation, cross-tenant masking presentation refinements, AI compose assistance, AI thread summarization, follow-up reminders, recipient suggestions, link-preview rendering, calendar-event-from-email, contact-from-email auto-creation, conversational AI agent over email content, Workshop email-template advanced features (conditional logic + multi-language), Bridgeable Assist email integration. Each deferred per §3.26.7.5 architectural restraint discipline — concrete operator signal triggers future canonicalization. **Phase C — Focus + Workshop + Intelligence Integration (4 subsections)**: §3.26.15.22 through §3.26.15.25. **Email Focus integration** (§3.26.15.22): Email Focus type — Coordination Focus per §3.26.11. Triggered by thread escalation (multiple participants + cross-tenant + linked entity) OR explicit summon. Participants per §3.26.11.9 magic-link participant scope. Auto-closure semantics: Coordination Focus auto-closes when outcome flow completes; Push-back-to-Pulse if reopen needed. **Workshop email-template integration** (§3.26.15.25, Q2 canonical): single `email_template` type with `reply_to_account_id` parameter. Workshop manages email templates as templates-as-data per §3.26.14.5. Single template adapts per-user reply-to account routing without proliferation of per-account template copies. **Intelligence integration** (§3.26.15.23): seven new Intelligence prompts — `email.compose.draft_reply` + `email.thread.summarize` + `email.intent.extract_state_change` + `email.entity.detect_linkage` + `email.priority.score` + `email.briefing.synthesize_communications_layer` + `email.cross_primitive.communications_pattern`. Each prompt seeded as platform-global per Bridgeable Intelligence canonical pattern; tenants customize via Workshop intelligence-prompt template type per §3.26.14.5. **Mid-session canon discipline validated: Phase B completion canon hygiene** — Phase B initial draft was incomplete (strategic vision integration items §3.26.15.15-§3.26.15.21 missing). User-flagged via "Phase B draft acknowledged but incomplete... directing prompt directed Phase B canon to include strategic-vision integration. Those items are missing." Seven additional subsections drafted. Discipline established: prompts directing comprehensive canon coverage warrant verbatim coverage check before phase close. Pattern reused from Session 1 mid-session reframe (Pulse-fractal insight) and Session 1.5 mid-session primitive emergence (Workshop). **Architectural model post-Session-2**: 5 primitives + fractal Pulse + universal Command Bar + Workshop + comprehensive Email primitive canon. Email primitive is the **first concrete Communications layer primitive** — Phase W-4b Layer 1 implementation begins after canon foundation complete (Calendar primitive next, ~3-4 weeks parallel-able with Email implementation per Phase W-4b sequence step 2). Architectural elegance achievement: every email-specific surface unifies under existing primitive framework (Pulse for surfacing + Command Bar for summoning + Focus for coordination + Workshop for templates + Intelligence for synthesis); no speculative parallel primitives. **Six explicit deferrals from Sessions 1 + 1.5 + 25+ from Session 2 tracked.** Phase W-4b Layer 1 Email implementation unblocked; Calendar canon (Session 3 candidate) + SMS canon (Session 4 candidate) + Phone canon (Session 5 candidate) + Messaging canon (Session 6 candidate) sequence forward as Phase W-4b implementation arc proceeds. **Canon doc updates this session**: BRIDGEABLE_MASTER §3.26.15 NEW (25 subsections); DESIGN_LANGUAGE §14.9 NEW (5 sub-sections — Email inbox + Email thread rendering + Email composition surface + Cross-tenant thread chrome + Operational-action affordance chrome); AESTHETIC_ARC Session 2 entry; CLAUDE.md §14 entry. **No code shipped.** Architecturally locked foundation for Phase W-4b Layer 1 Email primitive implementation; integrate-now-make-native-later framework established; templates-as-data discipline reinforced (Workshop email-template type); operator agency discipline reinforced (Generate-state-change affordance never auto-applies); kill-the-portal discipline reinforced (cross-tenant native messaging direct). Implementation deferred to scoped session(s) starting with Phase W-4b Layer 1 Email primitive build per BRIDGEABLE_MASTER §3.26.6.4 sequence step 1.

- **Session 1.5 — Workshop Primitive Canon (canon locked, 2026-05-06):** Pure architectural canon work. NO CODE this session. Workshop primitive emergence canonicalized; templates-as-data discipline established as cross-cutting platform commitment; network library architecture as strategic moat; Intelligence-Suggested Customizations as closing-the-loop feature. **Phase A — Workshop Space + Customization Architecture (8 subsections)**: §3.26.14 introduced as comprehensive Workshop Primitive section (15 subsections total). Workshop established as canonical Space — distinct from operational Spaces and Custom Spaces. **Workshop is craft-work Space**: tenant operators visit deliberately to customize the platform, then leave. One Workshop instance per tenant (singleton); both side-nav top-level entry AND command-bar summon canonical. **Three-tier access model** — `workshop.view` default-permissive (operational visibility); `workshop.author` default-restricted with per-customization-area sub-roles (workflow.edit, space.admin, briefing.edit, document.edit, email.edit, focus.author, widget.author); `workshop.publish` admin-gated. **Workshop home rendering** as Workshop-specific Space layout (NOT fractal Pulse — different verbs require different shapes; architectural restraint corollary). Five-section composition: Your Customizations / Tenant Templates / Network Library / Intelligence Suggestions / Recent Changes. Visual continuity preserved via Pattern 2 chrome + section eyebrows + Pattern 1 tablet treatment. **Templates-as-data canonical commitment** established as cross-cutting platform discipline — every customizable platform surface is a TEMPLATE rendered against DATA. Same architectural depth as "every AI call routes through `intelligence_service`" + "every document goes through `document_renderer`". Retroactively canonicalizes pattern emerging across Phase 2 saved views + Phase 6 briefings + Phase 8a workflows + Phase D-2 document templates + Phase W-3 widget catalog. **Template registry infrastructure**: distributed storage matching existing canon; per-template-type registries with unified contracts; common template envelope (versioning + adoption lineage + visibility levels). **Tune mode + Compose mode canonical** authoring; **Code mode deferred indefinitely** per §3.26.7.5. Tune-mode covers Marmon 80/20 case; Compose-mode for power-user template composition (Notion-blocks-for-operational-data). **Version history + staging + collaborative editing**: immutable per-version retention; preview-only staging canonical; full staging environment + promotion gates + real-time collaborative editing deferred per §3.26.7.5. **Phase A refinement** (§3.26.14.4): legacy URL coexistence reframed from "eventual deprecation" speculative promise to "parallel canonical access paths" with consolidation decision deferred to post-Workshop production tenant signal. **Phase B — Network Library + Cross-Tenant Template Propagation (5 subsections + reciprocity discipline + adoption-with-publish-authority workflow)**: §3.26.14.9 through §3.26.14.13. Network library as cross-tenant browsable surface (Section 3 of Workshop home; NOT separate top-level Space). Multi-source curation ranking signals (adoption count + ratings + Bridgeable editorial + Intelligence relevance). **Editorial selection authority Bridgeable-team-only initially**; community-suggested editorial mechanism deferred per §3.26.7.5. **Cross-tenant template propagation** (CONCERN B resolved): copy-on-adopt default + subscribe-to-upstream-updates affordance opt-in. **Two-tier merge discipline** — Tune-mode parameter changes auto-accept non-conflicting + explicit-review conflicting; Compose-mode composition changes ALWAYS require explicit operator review regardless of conflict status. **Anonymization granularity** (CONCERN C resolved): three-tier model with **two-tier confidence within Tier 1** — high-confidence terms (≥0.90) auto-stripped; low-confidence terms (0.50–0.89) flagged for operator review without auto-strip; below-threshold preserved as pattern semantic. Tier 2 pattern semantics preserved. Tier 3 volume signatures configurable (stripped / bucketed / preserved). Cryptographic identifier replacement preserves within-template referential integrity while preventing cross-template fingerprinting. **Cross-vertical template applicability** (CONCERN D resolved): vertical_applicability field with explicit operator declaration required at publish time; **no default**. Intelligence flags-and-suggests at pre-publish review. Marmon 80/20 case canonical cross-vertical pattern. **Templates composing other primitives** (CONCERN E resolved): source references by canonical name; on adoption, references resolve against adopting tenant's primitive instances; missing references render per §13.4.3 agency-dictated error surface. **Reciprocity discipline canonicalized as encouragement, not requirement** — forced contribution rejected; pay-to-play rejected; arbitrary access barriers rejected. **Adoption-with-publish-authority workflow** with **3-level lineage canonical** — original publisher → adopting tenant → republisher. Deeper chains collapse intermediate links; 2-level chains display direct lineage without "republished derivative" framing. **Phase C — Intelligence-Suggested Customizations + Cross-References + Workshop Visual Canon**: §3.26.14.14 (Intelligence-Suggested Customizations as closing-the-loop feature) + §3.26.14.15 (Cross-References catalog covering 9 customizable primitives + forcing function for new customizable surfaces) + DESIGN_LANGUAGE §17 (Workshop Visual System NEW top-level section parallel to §14 Communications + §15 Briefing + §16 Triage Focus). **Intelligence learning loop**: per-tenant pattern detection via existing audit log + Phase 8e.1 affinity tracking; cross-tenant pattern signals anonymized; suggestion quality calibration. **Pattern-detection-vs-operator-initiated discipline** locked: "Intelligence augments operator agency, never replaces it." Suggestion is offering, not imposition; operator always controls accept / dismiss / customize-before-saving. No "Intelligence auto-creates templates without operator approval" pattern. Templates-as-data discipline preserves operator authorship. Load-bearing canon preventing future drift toward Intelligence-auto-creation patterns. **Architectural model post-Session-1.5**: 5 primitives + fractal Pulse + universal Command Bar + Workshop. Architectural elegance compounded — every customizable surface unifies under existing primitive framework; templates-as-data commitment makes every "feature" a template + data + intelligence composition; network library compounds operational wisdom across years. **Six explicit deferrals tracked across Phase A + B + C** per §3.26.7.5 architectural restraint discipline: Code mode (Phase A Q4); Real-time collaborative editing (Phase A); Full staging environment (Phase A); Promotion gates (Phase A); vertical_applicability default (Phase B Q3); Community-suggested editorial (Phase B Q5). Each deferred per concrete-need-emerges discipline; each future canonicalization path documented; Workshop primitive ships at canonical quality without speculative scope expansion. **Canon doc updates this session**: BRIDGEABLE_MASTER §3.26.14 NEW (15 subsections); DESIGN_LANGUAGE §17 NEW (5 sub-sections) + §16.8 cross-reference touch ("Triage Focus Canonical Pattern" → "Focus Primitive Types — Triage is core element of Decision Focus type"); AESTHETIC_ARC Session 1.5 entry; CLAUDE.md §14 entry. **No code shipped.** Architecturally locked foundation for Session 2 Email primitive canon + Phase W-4b email primitive implementation. Sequencing forward: Session 2 (Email primitive canon, ~3-4h) → Phase W-4b implementation begins after Session 2 ships. Total canon work pre-email-implementation: Session 1 (Focus + Pulse + Command Bar canon) + Session 1.5 (Workshop canon) + Session 2 (Email canon) ≈ 11-13h. Substantial; bounded; architecturally justified per §3.26.7.5 ("MVP cuts compound as debt").

- **Session 1 — Focus Primitive Generalization + Pulse Scope Architecture + Command Bar Elevation Canon (canon locked, 2026-05-05):** Pure architectural canon work. NO CODE this session. Three architectural areas canonicalized as load-bearing foundation for Phase W-4b email primitive design (Session 2) + Phase W-4b implementation. **Phase A — Focus Primitive Types**: §3.26.11 expanded from "Triage Focus Canonical Pattern" to comprehensive "Focus Primitive Types" canon with four types (Decision / Coordination / Execution / Review) distinguished by lifecycle trigger + participant pattern + outcome flow + core element catalog. 13 unique core element catalog citizens across types. Sub-Focus hierarchy as canonical platform feature with per-template declaration + cascade closure semantics. Notification routing as hybrid 3-layer (deterministic baseline template-registered + Intelligence-learned per-tenant amendments stored as tenant operational config + prompt-tier fallback for novel events). SMS thread routing as 4-layer (most-recent-active-Focus default + intent classification + operator escalation + Intelligence learning). Magic-link participant scope as template-declared with §3.25.x cross-tenant masking inheritance + tenant-tightening affordance. Cross-tenant Focus scope as consent-based per §3.26.9 inheritance. Per-type auto-closure semantics. **Triage-as-core-element-not-primitive resolution**: §3.26.11 amended from "Phase 5 Triage primitive composing inside Focus" to "Decision Focus with triage core element"; Phase 5 task_triage + ss_cert_triage queues re-frame without code change. Core element catalog discipline: per-Focus-type catalogs; cross-type sharing permitted when architectural fit warrants but not built speculatively. **Phase B — Pulse Scope Architecture (fractal-Pulse reframe)**: §3.26.12 introduced as "Pulse Scope Architecture" canon. Pulse is fractal primitive — same architecture (Pattern A/B/C composition + chrome budget + viewport-fit math + intelligence layer + composition cache) renders at multiple scopes. Home Pulse = default-scope instance; scoped Pulses = scope-parameterized instances of same primitive. Three canonical scopes: Operation + Customer + Period (Person + Location + Material deferred per §3.26.7.5 until concrete need emerges). Reframe collapses pre-draft "Overview Primitive" to single Pulse primitive with scope parameter — single render-stack canon, single visual canon, single intelligence integration, single storage model. Composition sources canonicalized (entity cards + widgets + saved views + activity timeline + intelligence patterns + communications + sub-Focus references); scope is composition parameter, not surface variant. No widget supported_surfaces extension needed (`pulse_grid` covers all scopes by inheritance). Persistence model: composition templates persistent (Workshop-housed per Session 1.5); data ephemeral. **Mid-session reframe discipline**: pre-draft canon was treating Overview as third class of summonable surface parallel to Pulse; user-surfaced fractal-Pulse insight collapsed framing mid-flight rather than locking wrong-architecture canon (matches §3.26.7.5 canonical-quality discipline). CONCERN 1 (Briefings ↔ Pulse Scope distinct primitives), CONCERN 2 (Saved Views composed inside scoped Pulses; not replaced), CONCERN 4 (hybrid persistence), CONCERN 6 (widget catalog stays intact + composes into scoped Pulses) all resolved. **Phase C — Command Bar Architecture + Spatial Workspace**: §3.26.13 introduced. Command Bar elevated to universal summoning verb handling 8 summon types (entities + scoped Pulses + Focuses + communications + Workshop + actions + saved views + briefings). Forcing function on Command Bar maturity — every summon type must be handled gracefully. Spaces are NOT summoned (architectural exception); Workshop is exception-summonable as canonical craft-work surface. Natural-language summoning grammar extended with type-discriminator candidates. Intelligence-driven autocomplete as 3-layer hybrid (recent+frequent + Intelligence-suggested + Workshop-authored shortcuts). Spatial workspace canonicalized as transient command-bar-bound surface (NOT persistent overlay primitive per CONCERN 5 pre-resolution). Render-stack: underlying page foundation → backdrop dim + blur on ⌘K → command bar input foreground + summoned tablets in spatial workspace area → ⌘K exit disposes everything; underlying page restored. Multi-object composition canonical (cascade default + drag-to-position; no magnetic snapping per §3.26.7.5). Pulse-launched Focus (modal-overlay, Phase A canon) vs Command-bar-summoned Focus (spatial workspace tablet, Phase C canon) — same instance, coherent state, both paths canonical. **Tablet-tier viewport-fit math canonicalization** (§3.26.13.5a + DESIGN_LANGUAGE §13.10.4): scoped Pulse rendering inside spatial workspace tablet uses tablet container dimensions instead of underlying viewport for §13.3.4 density-tier dispatch. Single canon discipline; both rendering paths share viewport-fit math, parameterized per render context. Prevents broken-layout failure mode in implementation when scoped Pulse renders at smaller dimensions than viewport. **Architectural model post-Session-1**: 5 primitives + fractal Pulse + universal Command Bar + Workshop (Session 1.5). Architectural elegance achievement: every customizable surface unifies under existing primitive framework; no speculative parallel primitives. **Workshop primitive emergence flagged for Session 1.5** dedicated session (~3-4h conversational/architectural) before Session 2 (Email primitive canon) per Option B sequencing per canonical-quality discipline. **Canon doc updates this session**: BRIDGEABLE_MASTER §3.26.11 fully rewritten as Focus Primitive Types (11 subsections); §3.26.12 introduced as Pulse Scope Architecture (8 subsections); §3.26.13 introduced as Command Bar Architecture (9 subsections + §3.26.13.5a tablet-tier viewport-fit math integration); cross-references at §3.26.6.4 (3 line-item replacements) + §3.26.9.7 (parenthetical) + §3.26.10 (cross-reference touch). DESIGN_LANGUAGE §13.10 NEW (Spatial Workspace + Pulse-at-Scope Visual Canon — 5 subsections: spatial workspace visual treatment + Pulse-at-scope rendering parity + visual language consistency across summon types + viewport-fit math parameterization + cross-references); §13.9 cross-references extended. AESTHETIC_ARC Session 1 entry added per drafted prose. **No code shipped.** Architecturally locked foundation for Phase W-4b email primitive design + implementation; Workshop canon (Session 1.5) lands next; email canon (Session 2) designs against settled Focus + Pulse + Command Bar + Workshop foundation.

- **Phase W-4a Step 6 — Pulse Viewport-Responsive Architecture (complete, 2026-05-04):** Six-commit implementation arc closes. Viewport-responsive Pulse infrastructure ships canonically, calibrated against §13.3.1 + §13.3.4 + §13.4.1 + §13.4.3 canon. **Commit 1 — Foundation (`f35e157`)**: chrome budget computation per §13.3.4 Step 1; grid math foundation; `--pulse-content-height` / `--pulse-cell-height` / `--pulse-scale` CSS variables on PulseSurface root; 350ms cubic-bezier ease-out transition discipline on grid-template-rows; `useViewportFitMath` hook implementing §13.3.4 math chain end-to-end with `__viewport_fit_internals` test-only export. 26 new tests, 770/770 vitest. **Commit 2 — Tier-based columns + container queries (`ffe14b9`)**: tier dispatch via `useViewportDimensions()` extracted hook; `--pulse-column-count` per tier (mobile=2 / tablet=4 / desktop=6 per §13.3.1); PulseLayer `grid-template-columns: repeat(var(--pulse-column-count, 6), minmax(0, 1fr))`; PulsePiece declares `containerType: "size"; containerName: "piece"` per §13.4.1; three opt-in widgets (anomalies / line_status / today) gain three-tier density rendering with `pulse-density.css` @container dispatch. Spec-vs-canon resolved: container-type=size (not inline-size) for height queries; JS-driven column count (not CSS @media) for single-source-of-truth. 24 new tests, 820/820 vitest. **Commit 3 — Scroll mode + tier-three threshold + boundary buffer (`9c4fb1a`)**: `scroll_mode_active` composed from `tier === "mobile" || tier_three_threshold_breached`; PulseSurface writes `data-scroll-mode` + `--pulse-cell-height: auto` sentinel; pulse-density.css scroll-mode override flips PulseLayer to `flex-column`; `min-height: 200px` floor on pieces in scroll mode (closes empty-piece collapse from `h-full` → 0 in flex-column-without-parent-height); sub-pixel + Pattern-2-border buffer 97.5/117.5 (canon 100/120 minus 2px border minus 0.5px sub-pixel) — fixes Commit 2's 101px → ultra-compact bug. Canonical `console.warn` per §13.3.4 Step 5 on tier-three breach (debounced via key tuple). 22 new tests, 842/842 vitest. **Commit 4 — Empty slot filter + CI parity test (`91df9a4`)**: §13.4.3 agency-dictated error surface canonical implementation; `isItemRenderable(item)` predicate in `utils/renderability.ts` shared between PulseSurface measurement walk + PulseLayer rendering (cell-height denominator equals rendered piece count); canonical `[pulse] missing widget renderer; skipping piece` warn at PulseLayer site debounced per `${layer}:${component_key}`; CI parity test at canonical `frontend/src/__tests__/widget-renderer-parity.test.ts` reads `widget_registry.py` via fs + asserts every pulse_grid-eligible widget_id resolves to registered renderer; `KNOWN_DEFERRED` Map permits documented drift (`scheduling.ancillary-pool` → Path 3 deferral) with stale-entry detection. Spec-vs-canon resolved: filter at PulseLayer site (canon §13.4.3 placement) but predicate threaded through both consumers via shared util; console.warn fires once per session per layer:key. 25 new tests, 867/867 vitest. **Commit 5 — Workspace-core scroll-within-piece (`db15200`)**: vault_schedule Brief variant `overflow-hidden` → `overflow-y-auto` for both empty kanban-frame + non-empty paths per §13.3.2.1 + §13.4.1 canon; Detail variant already correct (regression guards added). 4 regression tests, 871/871 vitest. **Option A scope locked** per §3.26.7.5 canonical-quality discipline: urn_catalog_status / recent_activity density-tier opt-ins deferred until widgets emerge in active compositions; saved_view per-presentation density logic deferred to SavedViewRenderer session; briefing / operator_profile / Path 3 / pre-existing approvals_waiting drift documented as separate sessions. **Commit 6 — §13.8 reference implementations (this commit)**: 10 viewport-matrix screenshots captured (5 viewports × 2 modes) under `docs/design-references/pulse-step-6/` via Playwright capture script `frontend/scripts/capture-pulse-step-6-references.mjs`. DESIGN_LANGUAGE §13.8 augmented with 7 new subsections (13.8.9–13.8.15) preserving existing 13.8.1–13.8.8 W-4a foundation references. New subsections: tier-based column dispatch (Commit 2), container-query density tiers + opt-in widget renderings (Commit 2), sub-pixel + border boundary buffer canon prose (Commit 3), scroll mode dispatch + min-height fix canon prose (Commit 3), empty slot filter + agency-dictated error surface (Commit 4), workspace-core scroll-within-piece (Commit 5), viewport matrix reference implementations (Commit 6 itself — 5 sub-subsections per viewport with embedded screenshots + per-viewport tier/cols/cell_height/density-tier verification points). Canonical Sunnycrest dispatcher composition State B (D5 — work_areas set, banner suppressed) is the captured composition; chrome budget math verified live at desktop common 1440×900 (chrome=272, available=628, scale=0.875 floor, cell_height=205.3px) — matches §13.3.4 formulas exactly. **Cumulative for Step 6: 17 net new files + ~17 modified, ~5,200 LOC, 97 new tests, 871/871 vitest.** Step 6 implementation arc closes; Phase W-4a ships canonically. Sequencing forward: Phase W-4b implementation begins with Step 1 (Layer 1 email primitive, ~5-6 weeks) per BRIDGEABLE_MASTER §3.26.6.4. Aesthetic Arc Sessions 5-6 unblocked; Phase 4.4.4 (slide animation + multi-day Focus rendering) unblocked.

- **Phase W-4b Canon Expansion Session (canon locked, 2026-05-04):** **Canon-only session. No code shipped.** User-directed expansion of Phase W-4b scope. Canonicalizes four architectural shifts: Communications layer replaces Personal layer; Pattern C composition pattern (stream + supporting widgets); per-primitive 4-aspect decomposition for email/SMS/phone/messaging; Triage Focus migration pattern. **Strategic discipline locked: build at canonical quality.
