Demo timelines do not drive architecture. Phase W-4b ships when complete; September Wilbert demo shows whatever is complete at that point — honest checkpoint, not deadline.** No MVP cuts. No "demo-essential subset" sequencing. Platform completeness compounds across years; cuts compound as technical debt. **Phase W-4b implementation sequence (16 steps, ~27-43 weeks, sequencing by architectural dependency not timeline pressure)**: 1. Layer 1 email primitive (~5-6 weeks) → 2. Calendar primitive (~3-4 weeks, parallel-able with email) → 3. SMS primitive (~1-2 weeks, after email infra) → 4. Phone/Call Intelligence primitive (~3-4 weeks) → 5. In-platform messaging primitive (~2-3 weeks) → 6. Communications layer service + Pattern C composition (~2-3 weeks) → 7. Per-primitive intelligence streams (~2-3 weeks) → 8. Communications cross-primitive intelligence stream (~1-2 weeks) → 9. Morning briefing generation pipeline + 3-state machine (~2-3 weeks) → 10. Evening briefing pipeline (~1-2 weeks) → 11. Tenant briefing config UI (~1-2 weeks) → 12. Anomalies → Triage Focus migration (~1-2 weeks) → 13. Approvals → Triage Focus migration (~1-2 weeks) → 14. Tasks → Triage Focus migration (~1-2 weeks) → 15. Cross-tenant communications surface (~2-3 weeks; depends on V-1c CRM patterns) → 16. Tests + visual verification + canon doc updates (parallel throughout). **Canon doc updates locked this session**: BRIDGEABLE_MASTER §3.26.2.3 + §3.26.2.4 AMENDED (Communications replaces Personal in canonical layer order; tasks + approvals migrate to Operational; layer-vs-question framing canonicalized "Who needs me?" / "What's my work right now?" / "What's anomalous?" / "What's changed?"); §3.26.6.4 EXPANDED (Phase W-4b full 16-step sequence, no MVP cuts); §3.26.7.5 NEW (canonical-quality discipline lock — three forces in tension: demo legitimacy, compounding completeness, sustainable pace; rules out MVP cuts + demo-essential subset reordering + polish-at-end assumptions); §3.26.9 NEW (Communications Layer Architecture — full canon: why Personal retired, layer composition order, per-primitive signals, cross-tenant surface, Pattern C composition rules, cross-primitive intelligence stream, per-primitive 4-aspect decomposition for email/SMS/phone/messaging, layer aggregation service architecture, 11 D-decisions resolved); §3.26.10 NEW (Briefings Architecture — three-state machine `queued → generating → generated`; generation pipeline; tenant config schema; Pattern C integration; per-primitive surfacing rules); §3.26.11 NEW (Triage Focus Canonical Pattern — Phase 5 Triage primitive composing inside Phase A Focus primitive; anchored core composition; canvas peripheral panels; per-queue specialization for anomalies/approvals/tasks; new `"triage"` core mode in mode dispatcher; 4 D-decisions resolved). DESIGN_LANGUAGE §11 EXTENDED (Layer Composition Patterns A/B/C as new subsection — Pattern A widgets-only canonical at Operational; Pattern B stream-only canonical at Anomaly; Pattern C stream + supporting widgets canonical at Communications; distinct from material chrome Patterns 1-8); §13.4.1 EXTENDED (Pattern C density-tier opt-in canonical reference + per-tier behavior table for stream + per-primitive widgets); §14 NEW (Communications Layer Visual Canon — per-primitive icon canon Mail/MessageSquare/Phone/AtSign no emoji; count typography canon; per-primitive default-tier compositions; compact + ultra-compact tier compositions; stream piece visual canon; empty-state visual treatment); §15 NEW (Briefing Visual System — three states visual mapping queued/generating/generated; per-state composition with skeletons → shimmer → narrative; typography per state; Pattern C integration with priority 98; briefing page editorial-scale full surface; tenant briefing config UI canon); §16 NEW (Triage Focus Visual System — anchored core composition; canvas peripheral panels; per-queue visual specialization; action palette typography canon; push-back + return pill canon; queue-exhausted empty state). AESTHETIC_ARC entry framing canon expansion session + canonical-quality discipline lock. **18 architectural D-decisions resolved this session** (11 Communications layer + 4 Triage Focus + 3 cross-cutting). **September Wilbert meeting framing**: demonstrate whatever is complete at that point. Honest checkpoint, not deadline. Pre-September Phase W-4a closing remains commitment (viewport-responsive Pulse + Layer 1 email kicks off pre-Wilbert). Post-September Phase W-4b continues at sustainable pace. **Implementation deferred to scoped session(s) starting with Layer 1 email primitive**.

- **Phase W-4a Step 6 — Pulse Viewport-Responsive Architecture Canon Session (canon locked, 2026-05-03):** **Canon-only session. No code shipped.** User feedback after Step 5 visual verification surfaced architectural reframe: Pulse should fill viewport, pieces should scale linearly with viewport ("same composition, same info, but visual size scales — bigger viewport, bigger pieces"). Pre-Step-6 §13.3.1 canon ("auto-fit collapse intentional, not regression") was correct against test-data verification at the time of W-4a Commit 5 ship but proved insufficient against production usage with actual tenant data + actual operator workflows. **Canon revision discipline established**: locked canon CAN be invalidated by production-usage feedback after a real dogfooding cycle; canon revision is not failure — it's the system working. Discipline: run a fresh canon session with full proposal + trade-off analysis (this session), ship doc updates as a single canon-lock session BEFORE code work begins, don't piecemeal-amend canon during implementation. **8 canon questions resolved** with locked answers: (1) sizing model = tiered fixed columns (2/4/6) + fractional viewport-derived rows; (2) layer allocation = row-count-weighted with empty-layer fixed 32px allowance; (3) widget scale via `--pulse-scale` CSS variable + container queries for density tiers; (4) chrome budget client-computed via observer pattern (hard-coded constants for September; dynamic ResizeObserver canonical post-September); (5) mobile (`< 600px`) + tier-three threshold (`cell_height < 80px`) fall back to natural-height scroll; (6) `--pulse-scale` clamps at 1.25× ceiling — additional viewport space → breathing room (Apple Pro app discipline); (7) agency-dictated error surface — Pulse silently filters (Philosophy A); PinnedSection shows `MissingWidgetEmptyState` (Philosophy B); CI parity test mandatory; (8) container queries select default/compact/ultra-compact density per cell size; workspace-core widgets exempt from aggressive compaction (preserve workspace shape per §13.3.2.1). **Canon doc updates locked this session**: DESIGN_LANGUAGE §13.3.1 REWRITE (viewport-fit canon, tier-based 2/4/6 cols, mobile fallback); §13.3.2 AMEND (cell-height consistency across layers, 300ms ease-out transition discipline, empty-layer advisory fixed allowance); §13.3.4 NEW (viewport-fit math — chrome budget formula, `--pulse-content-height`, `--pulse-scale` clamp formula, layer row-count weighting, full constants table); §13.4.1 AMEND (container queries canonical for Pulse density; `surface` prop stays for non-Pulse surfaces; per-widget density tier opt-in; workspace-core exemption); §13.4.3 NEW (agency-dictated error surface — platform-composed silent filter; user-composed visible placeholder; CI parity test mandatory). BRIDGEABLE_MASTER §3.26.2.1 amended with tetris composition viewport-fit implementation note. AESTHETIC_ARC entry framing canon revision discipline + canon-vs-user-perception drift detection extended pattern (Step 5 established live-DOM measurement; Step 6 extends to user-perception canon, not just visual-token canon). **Implementation deferred to scoped session(s)**: 6-commit boundary identified (chrome budget + grid math foundation → tier-based cols + container queries → mobile fallback + tier-three → empty slot filter + CI parity test → per-widget density tier rendering → §13.8 reference implementations at 5 viewport sizes). Realistic estimate: ~10-12 hr implementation + ~3 hr canon docs (this session) + ~3 hr visual verification = ~16-18 hr total across multiple sessions. **§13.8 reference implementations DEFERRED until implementation completes** — 10 screenshots (5 viewports × 2 modes) verify canon + implementation aligned. **Canon-vs-implementation drift detection (extended)**: Step 5 established the live-DOM-measurement pattern for canon-vs-implementation audits ("does the rendered DOM match the canon's chrome claims?"). Step 6 extends: also measure against **user-perception canon**, not just visual-canon. The visual canon was honored at W-4a Commit 5 ship (Pattern 2 chrome present, brass-thread rendering, layer ordering correct) but the user-perception canon (Pulse fills its primary surface viewport) was implicit + unspoken until production usage surfaced it. Future canon work articulates user-perception expectations explicitly, not just visual-token expectations.

- **Phase W-4a Step 5 — Pulse Pattern 2 chrome + missing-widget empty-state (complete, 2026-05-02):** Two fixes shipped from the Pulse Production Issues investigation, plus one architectural-debt finding deferred to a follow-up. **Investigation findings (6 issues audited)**: Issue A (no Pattern 2 chrome on Pulse pieces) confirmed real bug; Issue B (tetris layout "scattered") confirmed downstream perception of A; Issue C ("Recent Cases contamination") confirmed NOT cross-vertical contamination — `MockSavedViewWidget` was rendering as a fallback for an unmapped widget key (`scheduling.ancillary-pool` backend-side vs `funeral-scheduling.ancillary-pool` frontend-side), producing fake FH-themed mock data that LOOKED like contamination; Issue D (Purchase mode) NOT reproducible in dev (production data divergence on Sunnycrest only — separate audit); Issue E (sparseness) confirmed downstream of A; Issue F (orphan today widget) confirmed downstream of A+B+E. **Issue A fix — Pulse Pattern 2 chrome.** PulsePiece's outer div now applies `rounded-[2px] bg-surface-elevated border border-border-subtle shadow-level-1 overflow-hidden` per DESIGN_LANGUAGE §11 Pattern 2 + §13.4.1 amendment. Pre-Step-5 PulsePiece's docstring claimed widget renderers carry their own Pattern 2 chrome — that was wrong; only `WidgetWrapper` applied chrome (dashboard surface), and Pulse pieces rendered without any chrome at all. AnomalyIntelligenceStream's duplicated Pattern 2 chrome moved out (single source of truth at PulsePiece; AnomalyIntelligenceStream still applies the brass-thread top-edge accent unique to intelligence streams per §13.4.2). **Path 2 fix — MissingWidgetEmptyState empty-state component.** New `frontend/src/components/focus/canvas/MissingWidgetEmptyState.tsx` renders an honest "Widget unavailable / No renderer registered for this widget (widget_id)" card with status-warning palette (visually distinct from regular widget content). `getWidgetRenderer` now splits its fallback path: `widgetType === undefined` returns `MockSavedViewWidget` (legacy/test path preserved); `widgetType` set-but-not-registered returns `MissingWidgetEmptyState` (real production case where backend declares a widget the frontend has no renderer for). Pre-Step-5 BOTH paths returned MockSavedViewWidget, masking widget-id mismatches as fake-Recent-Cases content. **Issue C — registry rename DEFERRED.** Investigation surfaced that the cleanest fix to the `funeral-scheduling.ancillary-pool` → `scheduling.ancillary-pool` mismatch isn't a single-line rename: AncillaryPoolPin uses the strict `useSchedulingFocus()` hook which throws when called outside `SchedulingFocusDataProvider` (mounted only inside the Funeral Scheduling Focus subtree). The registry-key mismatch was MASKING this deeper architectural issue. Path 3 — refactor AncillaryPoolPin to support `pulse_grid` surface — earns its own scoped session: switch to `useSchedulingFocusOptional`, add `/widget-data/ancillary-pool` backend endpoint for surface-fetched data, surface-aware rendering (no drag handles in `pulse_grid` per §12.6a workspace-core canon — bounded interactions only). Tracked as separate follow-up. Until that ships, Pulse renders the new `MissingWidgetEmptyState` for the ancillary-pool position — honest "Widget unavailable" rather than fake mock data. **Canon update — DESIGN_LANGUAGE §13.4.1**: chrome-is-surface-responsibility convention locked. Pattern 2 chrome is the surface's responsibility (`PulsePiece` for Pulse, `WidgetWrapper` for dashboard); widget renderers MUST NOT apply Pattern 2 chrome at their root — doing so produces nested cards when the surface also applies chrome. **Coexistence-policy validation discipline (extended)**: in addition to "test against canonical entry flows" from Step 4, dev-fallback components in production code paths should fail visibly (status-warning empty state with widget_id surfaced) rather than silently substituting mock content. The Step-5 investigation discovered MockSavedViewWidget rendering in production for ~weeks (since W-4a Commit 5 shipped) without anyone catching it because the placeholder content LOOKED like real widget output. Future dev fixtures + fallbacks: surface in production with explicit warning chrome, not as content substitutes. **Tests**: `PinnedSection.test.tsx` test renamed `MockSavedViewWidget fallback` → `MissingWidgetEmptyState fallback` and updated to assert the new empty-state slot + "Widget unavailable" text + widget_id surface. **Verification**: tsc -b 0 errors, vitest 770/770 unchanged, vite build clean. Live verified both modes: 5/5 Pulse pieces render Pattern 2 chrome (warm-cream surface-elevated in light mode, warm-charcoal in dark; 1px solid border; 2px radius; shadow active). AncillaryPoolPin position renders the new `MissingWidgetEmptyState` with text "Widget unavailable / No renderer registered for this widget (scheduling.ancillary-pool)". No more fake Recent Cases mock content. **Architectural debt surfaced for follow-up**: AncillaryPoolPin currently can only render inside the Funeral Scheduling Focus subtree (depends on `SchedulingFocusDataProvider`). Backend's `WIDGET_DEFINITIONS` declares `scheduling.ancillary-pool` with `supported_surfaces: ["pulse_grid", ...]` — a contract the frontend can't fulfill. Phase W-3d's "First workspace-core widget canonical reference" promise needs the cross-surface portability the canon implies. Tracked as a separate scoped session.

- **Phase W-4a Step 4 — RootRedirect routing-bug fix (complete, 2026-05-01):** Single-line correction to `frontend/src/components/root-redirect.tsx` line 35 plus canon clarification + coexistence-validation discipline note. **Bug**: per Phase W-4a Commit 5's coexistence policy ("RootRedirect points at /dashboard; Pulse reachable via DotNav Home dot"), users hitting the canonical "open the app" flow (load `/`) auto-redirected to `/dashboard` and never reached Pulse via the entry path — undermining §3.26.7.1 (Pulse as differentiator) at the entry-flow level. **Fix**: change `RootRedirect` fallback target from `<Navigate to="/dashboard" />` to `<Navigate to="/home" />`. Specialized roles unchanged (driver → `/driver`; production_delivery track → `/console/*`). **Audit grep** of all `navigate("/")` and `<Navigate to="/" />` and `<Link to="/" />` callers across `frontend/src/` surfaced 14 callers across login, register, onboarding flows, sidebar logo link, error pages, and operator-onboarding cancel — all semantically intend "go home"; none depend on `/dashboard` specifically; none break under the fix. Portal + bridgeable-admin contexts dispatch BEFORE the tenant RootRedirect path is rendered (App.tsx:409,424), so their `<Navigate to="/" />` resolves to scoped roots, not the tenant redirect. **Coexistence preserved**: `/dashboard` route remains mounted (App.tsx:526); `Ownership` space template's `default_home_route="/dashboard"` keeps the dashboard reachable via the Ownership DotNav dot; direct URL navigation works. Phase W-5 still retires `/dashboard` post-My-Stuff + Custom-Spaces ship. **Canon update** (BRIDGEABLE_MASTER §3.26.1.1): "Home Space is the canonical entry point. Authenticated tenant users land on Home Space (rendering Pulse at `/home`) on app open, unless they have specialized roles (driver portal, production console) with their own canonical entry. RootRedirect honors this canon — root path `/` redirects authenticated tenant users to `/home`." Makes the redirect behavior explicit canon rather than implicit assumption. **Tests added**: `frontend/src/components/root-redirect.test.tsx` — 8 tests covering all RootRedirect branches: authenticated tenant user lands on `/home` (with regression guard against `/dashboard`); driver lands on `/driver`; production_delivery + single delivery_console → `/console/delivery`; production_delivery + single production_console → `/console/production`; production_delivery + multi-console → `/console`; unauthenticated → `/login`; isLoading shows placeholder; non-driver office roles (office, accountant, director, production) all land on `/home` regardless of role_slug variant. **Coexistence-policy validation discipline (post-W-4a hygiene canon)**: coexistence policies should be tested against canonical user flows (open the app, see what loads), not just direct route navigation. The Commit 5 RootRedirect coexistence ("leave at /dashboard, Pulse reachable via DotNav") passed direct-navigation tests (the `/home` route worked when users explicitly hit it) but proved insufficient against the canonical "open the app" flow (root `/` → auto-redirect → /dashboard, never reaches Pulse). User-surfaced bug led to fix in same week of Phase W-4a closure. Future coexistence policies should be validated against entry flows, not just route-routing. **Final verification**: vitest 770/770 passing (was 762; +8 RootRedirect tests). Backend regression unchanged. Migration head unchanged at `r62_cleanup_cross_vertical_saved_views`. **Phase W-4a is now complete in spirit** — every entry path honors §3.26.1.1.

- **Phase W-4a Step 3 — Saved-view vertical-scope architectural enforcement (complete, 2026-04-30):** Pattern A (saved view INSTANCE vertical-scope inherits from data source) enforced at three layers + cleanup migration + four canon amendments locked. Closes the cross-vertical contamination architectural gap surfaced in the Phase W-4a Pulse Layout Investigation (Category F). **Investigation deliverable** before code: 8 entity types audited (only `fh_case` is single-vertical at `["funeral_home"]`; other 7 are cross-vertical at `["*"]`); **2 cross-vertical seed entries** found (`(cemetery, admin)` + `(crematory, admin)` both seeded `recent_cases` with `entity_type=fh_case`); **2 PinSeed cross-references** in cemetery/admin + crematory/admin Operations space templates pointing at the cross-vertical seeds; **75 already-seeded contaminated saved views** in dev DB (all manufacturing tenants with leftover `Recent cases` views from pre-Step-1 seeding). Bounded scope; legitimate cross-tenant (Mutual data foundation per §5.2.4) sharing not affected. **Layer 1 — Entity registry**: `allowed_verticals: list[str]` field added to `EntityTypeMetadata`; `["*"]` for cross-vertical, `["funeral_home"]`-style for single-vertical. New helper `is_entity_compatible_with_vertical(entity_type, vertical)` returns True for `"*"` allowed-list OR vertical-in-list, False otherwise (defensive: unknown entity_type or null vertical = False). All 8 entities declare `allowed_verticals` explicitly at registration site (defense against future copy-paste of `fh_case` shape). **Layer 2 — Seed-layer enforcement**: `seed_for_user` skips seeds whose `entity_type` is incompatible with the tenant's vertical, logging a warning so contamination is observable in operator logs. Plus 2 cross-vertical entries removed from `_SEED_TEMPLATES` (`(cemetery, admin)` recent_cases + `(crematory, admin)` recent_cases). Plus 2 `PinSeed` references removed from cemetery/admin + crematory/admin Operations space templates (they pointed at the now-removed seeds). **Layer 3 — Creation-layer enforcement**: `create_saved_view` rejects cross-vertical creation with `SavedViewError(http_status=400)` whose detail message identifies the offending entity_type + tenant vertical + entity's allowed_verticals (frontend-renderable). Existing API `_translate_saved_view_error` maps `http_status` directly; no API changes needed. **Layer 4 — Read-layer defense-in-depth filter**: `list_saved_views_for_user` drops cross-vertical instances at read time. Even if pre-existing data slipped through (the 75 contaminated rows from before Step 1) OR a future bug bypasses creation, the read path stays clean. Logs a warning when filter fires. **Layer 6 — Migration `r62_cleanup_cross_vertical_saved_views`**: idempotent DELETE of cross-vertical contamination. `_PROHIBITED_PAIRS` list mirrors the registry's single-vertical entities; regression test fails loudly if a new single-vertical entity is added without updating the migration. Hard DELETE (not soft `is_active=false`) — the data IS contamination; preserving it via soft-delete leaves it visible to admin queries. Down-migration is intentional no-op (restoring contamination would re-introduce the bug). **Verified on dev DB**: 75 rows deleted on first apply, 0 on second apply (idempotency). **Layer 7 — Tests**: 23 new tests in `test_saved_views_vertical_scope.py` across 5 layer-mirroring classes — TestEntityRegistryVerticalScope × 6 (all entities declare allowed_verticals, fh_case=funeral_home, 7 cross-vertical entities use "*", helper compatibility check, helper rejects unknown entity, helper rejects null vertical), TestSeedLayerEnforcement × 3 (synthetic smuggled cross-vertical seed skipped at runtime; full _SEED_TEMPLATES audit invariant; FH admin still gets fh_case templates), TestCreationLayerEnforcement × 7 (mfg+cemetery+crematory creating fh_case → 400; FH creating fh_case → 201; mfg creating invoice + vault_item → 201; error message lists allowed_verticals), TestReadLayerEnforcement × 3 (smuggled fh_case in mfg filtered at read; legitimate fh_case in FH NOT filtered; cross-vertical filtered via API), TestDataCleanupMigration × 4 (migration head is r62; downgrade is true no-op via bytecode inspection; _PROHIBITED_PAIRS mirrors registry's single-vertical entities; idempotent inline run deletes 0 rows on second pass). **Layer 8 — Canon amendments locked**: (1) `DESIGN_LANGUAGE.md §13.3.2.1` workspace-core empty-state shape — workspace-core widgets per §12.6 preserve workspace shape in empty data states (kanban frame + section eyebrow + dashed lane placeholder for `vault_schedule` Detail empty); generic centered empty state ONLY for structurally-empty (workspace not enabled) cases. (2) `DESIGN_LANGUAGE.md §13.3.1` viewport behavior — Pulse tetris layout uses CSS Grid `repeat(auto-fit,minmax(160px,1fr))`; smaller viewports collapse to fewer columns automatically (<360px = 1 col, 360-720px = 2-4 cols, 720px+ = 6 cols); intentional, not regression. `grid-flow-row-dense` lets smaller pieces backfill empty cells. (3) `DESIGN_LANGUAGE.md §13.4.1` surface-specific widget compaction — Pinable widgets may have surface-specific compact rendering; Pulse honors grid cell size constraints (Brief in pulse_grid compacts to header + footer when content density exceeds cell height); dashboard surfaces render full Brief content with rows. Reference: `anomalies` widget Brief variant. (4) `BRIDGEABLE_MASTER.md §3.25.x` saved-view vertical-scope inheritance — config-driven widget content's vertical-scope inherits from config's data source, not from widget definition. Three enforcement layers (seed/create/read) + entity_type → allowed_verticals registry. Cross-tenant (Mutual data foundation per §5.2.4) is explicit cross-tenant scope, not cross-vertical leak. Forward compatibility: same Pattern A applies to future config-driven widgets (`dashboard`, `report`). **Final regression posture**: backend 268 tests passing (saved_views 49 + saved_views_vertical_scope 23 + saved_views_registry 9 + spaces_phase8e 33 + W-4a Commits 1-4 + integration 124 + spaces_unit + system_spaces_phase8a). Frontend 762/762 vitest. tsc -b 0 errors. vite build clean. **Migration head advances**: `r61_user_work_areas_pulse_signals` → **`r62_cleanup_cross_vertical_saved_views`**. **Pulse infrastructure clean architecturally.** Cross-vertical contamination closed at all four enforcement layers. Layer 1 communication primitives sequence (Email → Calendar → SMS) unblocked.

- **Phase W-4a Step 2 — Visual fixes (complete, 2026-04-29):** Three visual fixes per the Pulse Layout Investigation: **Cat A** vault_schedule preserves workspace shape in empty data states via new `VaultScheduleEmptyKanbanFrame` (kanban header + section eyebrow + dashed-border lane placeholder + "Open in scheduling Focus" footer survives); centered `VaultScheduleEmptyState` retained ONLY for "Vault not enabled" structural-gap case; both Brief + Detail variants use the new frame for data-empty. **Cat C** `grid-flow-row-dense` added to PulseLayer — smaller pieces backfill empty cells in dense compositions. **Cat D** AnomaliesWidget surface-aware Pulse compaction: when `surface === "pulse_grid"`, Brief renders header (count + critical breakdown) + footer ("Investigate N →") only, body/rows skip; dashboard surfaces still get the rich 4-row content; new `data-surface` attribute marks the rendering mode. 5 new tests across affected widgets. Verified: tsc -b 0 errors, vitest 762/762, vite build clean.

- **Phase W-4a Step 1 — Drop FH recent_cases from manufacturing/admin seed (complete, 2026-04-29):** Cross-vertical contamination quick-win — `(manufacturing, admin)` saved-view seed previously created `Recent cases` (entity_type=fh_case) views in mfg tenants' vault_items library. Dropped the seed entry. New mfg tenants no longer accumulate FH-typed views. Regression-guard test `test_manufacturing_admin_does_not_seed_fh_recent_cases` added (Phase W-4a Step 3 will architecturally enforce at registry/creation/read layers). 236 backend tests passing.

- **Phase W-4a — Pulse Infrastructure (complete, 2026-04-28):** Six commits ship the **Home Pulse infrastructure** as the first concrete realization of BRIDGEABLE_MASTER §3.26 — composition engine, four layer services, V1 anomaly intelligence stream, signal collection, frontend tetris layout, first-login banner, and the canonical Sunnycrest dispatcher demo composition end-to-end. Three load-bearing references locked for downstream phases (W-4b, W-5, communication primitives, Tier 2): work-area-to-widget mapping, two-content-primitive contract, standardized signal metadata shapes. **Strategic significance:** the platform's most distinctive product feature — the Pulse — shipped to production-ready depth ahead of the September Wilbert demo. Custom Spaces and My Stuff exist as fallback for when Pulse fails to surface what users need; Pulse is the differentiator competitors cannot easily replicate without similarly-deep platform-level intelligence infrastructure (per §3.26.7.1). The infrastructure-first sequencing per §3.26.7.2 means Pulse intelligence streams ship against real Layer 1/2/3 communication primitives in W-4b; nothing in W-4a is stub work that gets refactored when primitives land. **Six D-decisions resolved before Commit 1 began** (parallel sub-agent investigation phase): D1 composition cache strategy (in-memory dict, 5-min TTL, work_areas-hash key), D2 tetris layout engine (custom CSS Grid, no third-party dependency), D3 layer ordering enforcement (both backend + defensive frontend re-sort), D4 first-login fallback (vertical-default with `metadata.vertical_default_applied=true` flag), D5 Sunnycrest dispatcher canonical composition (locked verbatim — vault_schedule Detail 2×2 + scheduling.ancillary-pool Brief + line_status Brief + today Glance + extension-conditional urn_catalog_status Glance), D6 V2 Haiku-cached anomaly synthesis (deferred to W-4b; V1 deterministic ships now), D7 legacy `spaces/pulse_compositions.py` retirement (retired with grep-verified zero importers). **Commit 1 — Schema + Home system space + onboarding (25 backend tests).** Migration `r61_user_work_areas_pulse_signals` adds `User.work_areas` (JSONB array, nullable) + `responsibilities_description` (text, nullable) + `pulse_signals` table with standardized JSONB metadata column + composite indexes for the aggregation hot paths. Home system space added to `SYSTEM_SPACE_TEMPLATES` — gate-less, leftmost in DotNav, `default_home_route="/home"`, `is_system=True`. Login defensive re-seed in `auth_service.login_user` widened to fire on missing-Home as well as missing-Settings (closes a Phase 8e.2.2-shape gap proactively — no active user can have an empty `preferences.system_spaces_seeded`). Operator profile editor service + `GET / PATCH /api/v1/operator-profile` endpoint with partial-update semantics + canonical `WORK_AREAS` vocabulary list returned in every response. **Commit 2 — Four layer composition services + work-area mapping locked (28 backend tests).** `personal_layer_service` (assigned tasks + approvals waiting from Phase 5 task system + approval gate), `operational_layer_service` (work-area-to-widget mapping authoritative for §3.26.3.1 vocabulary; vertical-default fallback per D4), `anomaly_layer_service` (raw `agent_anomalies` widget + intelligence stream pointer), `activity_layer_service` (V-1c recent activity feed). Mapping documented inline in `operational_layer_service.py` as the canonical work-area-vocabulary source of truth. Tenant + extension awareness via 5-axis widget filter pre-check. **Commit 3 — Composition engine + Pulse API + V1 anomaly intelligence + cache + retire pulse_compositions.py (27 backend tests).** `composition_engine.compose_for_user` orchestrates the four layer services + synthesizes the V1 anomaly intelligence stream (deterministic — frequency-weighted severity ordering + canonical title + narrative prose). 5-minute composition cache with work_areas-hash-aware key (`pulse:{user_id}:{work_areas_hash}:{minute_window}`) — work_areas update changes the key + next request misses + recomputes. `GET /api/v1/pulse/composition[?refresh=true]` single endpoint per D1; tenant + user scoping forced server-side. Legacy `spaces/pulse_compositions.py` retired with grep-verified zero importers + a regression test pinning the retirement. **Commit 4 — Signal tracking endpoints + service + 3 aggregation helpers (26 backend tests).** `POST /pulse/signals/dismiss` + `/pulse/signals/navigate` with standardized JSONB metadata shapes (`{component_key, time_of_day, work_areas_at_dismiss}` and `{from_component_key, to_route, dwell_time_seconds}` respectively — load-bearing for Tier 2 algorithms). Three aggregation helpers ready for Tier 2 consumption: `get_dismiss_counts_per_component`, `get_navigation_targets`, `get_engagement_score`. Phase W-4a does NOT consume them; battle-tested + ready so Tier 2 algorithm work post-September iterates against accumulated production data, not unproven helpers. Tenant + user scoping enforced server-side at every layer. **Commit 5 — Frontend Pulse rendering (26 vitest tests, 757 total frontend regression).** `frontend/src/components/spaces/PulseSurface.tsx` consumes the API; `PulseLayer.tsx` renders pieces in a custom CSS Grid (`grid-cols-[repeat(auto-fit,minmax(160px,1fr))] auto-rows-[80px] gap-3`) honoring per-piece `cols / rows` from the composition engine; `PulsePiece.tsx` dispatches the two content primitives via `LayerItem.kind`; `AnomalyIntelligenceStream.tsx` is the V1 reference for §13.4.2 chrome treatment with brass-thread top edge via `before:` pseudo-element; `PulseFirstLoginBanner.tsx` renders inline above all layers when `metadata.vertical_default_applied && shouldShow` per §13.6, dismissal cross-device via `useOnboardingTouch` hook. Brass-thread divider above Operational layer per §13.3.2 (1px solid terracotta accent at 30% alpha — passes cover-with-hand test). Empty-layer advisories in italic content-muted typography. Visual verification both modes coherent (warm cream ↔ warm charcoal substrate; single-value accent across modes per Aesthetic Arc Session 2). **Commit 6 — Sunnycrest demo State B + integration tests + canon docs (18 integration tests, 124 total backend regression).** Sunnycrest dispatcher composition verified end-to-end: `work_areas = ["Production Scheduling", "Delivery Scheduling", "Inventory Management"]` + responsibilities text → vertical_default_applied=false → operational layer matches the canonical D5 set (vault_schedule Detail + scheduling.ancillary-pool Brief + line_status Brief + today Glance; urn_catalog_status Glance only when urn_sales extension active — verified live via the `/api/v1/pulse/composition` endpoint against testco demo tenant). Integration tests at `backend/tests/test_phase_w4a_integration.py` cover four shapes — composition end-to-end through the API, signal collection flow, Sunnycrest dispatcher composition specifically, first-login flow including cache-invalidation through `PATCH /operator-profile`. **DESIGN_LANGUAGE §13.8 expanded with eight locked Phase W-4a reference implementations** (Home Pulse Sunnycrest dispatcher canonical State A + State B; PulseSurface tetris grid; brass-thread divider; PulsePiece two primitives; AnomalyIntelligenceStream V1; PulseFirstLoginBanner; signal collection chrome; empty-layer advisory pattern). **BRIDGEABLE_MASTER §3.26.8 added with seven subsections** (six-commit ship sequence; six D-decisions resolved; canonical work-area-to-widget mapping; two content primitives locked contract; signal collection metadata shapes; implementation deviations from §3.26.6.1; what's wired now vs deferred to W-4b). **Architectural patterns established this phase:** (1) **Two-content-primitive Pulse** — every Pulse piece is `kind="widget"` (registry-dispatched widget renderer with surface=pulse_grid + variant + config + cols + rows) or `kind="stream"` (registry-dispatched intelligence stream renderer with stream_id matching synthesized content at composition top level); future Pulse content extends the registry, not the primitive set. (2) **Work-area mapping as authoritative vocabulary** — `operational_layer_service.WORK_AREA_WIDGET_MAPPING` is the single source of truth for §3.26.3.1 work-area vocabulary → §3.26.2.4 composition rules. (3) **Standardized signal metadata as Tier 2 contract** — dismiss + navigation signal metadata shapes documented in the migration AND signal_service module; load-bearing for Tier 2 pattern matching. (4) **Vertical-default fallback flag as banner gate** — `metadata.vertical_default_applied` is the single discriminator the frontend uses to decide whether to render `PulseFirstLoginBanner`. (5) **Shared persistence + distinct visual treatment** — `PulseFirstLoginBanner` shares `useOnboardingTouch` with Phase 7's `OnboardingTouch` tooltip primitive but renders inline-with-content; when the same conceptual signal needs different chrome on different surfaces, share persistence + ship distinct visual primitives. (6) **Cache-invalidation through key shape, not active eviction** — composition cache invalidates because the work_areas hash changes the key, not because anyone explicitly evicts the old key. (7) **`/home` route, not `/pulse`** — Home Space is the user-facing concept; Pulse is its content per §3.26.1.1. (8) **Build infrastructure-first per §3.26.7.2** — V1 anomaly intelligence stream ships now (deterministic synthesizer); communication-dependent streams (smart email, briefing, cross-tenant coordination, conflict detection) wait for Layer 1 + Layer 2 primitives. **Final regression posture:** **124 backend tests passing** (Commit 1: 25 + Commit 2: 28 + Commit 3: 27 + Commit 4: 26 + Commit 6 integration: 18). **Frontend 757/757 vitest passing** (+26 PulseSurface tests). **tsc 0 errors. vite build clean.** Visual verification both light + dark mode (warm cream ↔ warm charcoal substrate; single-value terracotta accent across modes; brass-thread divider passes cover-with-hand subtlety test; breathing-room composition functional). **No regressions** across Widget Library W-1 + W-2 + W-3a + W-3b + W-3d + Spaces Phases 1-8e.2.3 + Aesthetic Arc Sessions 1-4 + Phase II Batches 0/1a/1b. **Migration head advances:** `r60_backfill_tenant_product_lines_vault` → **`r61_user_work_areas_pulse_signals`** (single migration this phase — Commits 2-6 are pure service-layer + API + frontend + docs, no further schema changes). **Sequencing forward:** Layer 1 communication + temporal primitives cluster begins (Email → Calendar overlap → SMS finishing parallel) per §3.26.6.2. Phase W-4b ships the remaining intelligence streams against real Layer 1/2 data. Phase W-5 ships My Stuff + Custom Spaces dashboards. Aesthetic Arc Sessions 5-9 + Phase 4.4.4 (slide animation + multi-day Focus rendering) remain unblocked. **The Pulse is shipped.** What competitors cannot easily replicate is now production-ready against real testco data; State A + State B both verified visually + end-to-end. The September Wilbert demo's most distinctive product feature exists today.

- **Widget Library Phase W-3d — Manufacturing Per-Line Widgets (complete, 2026-04-27):** Four commits shipped end-to-end. Phase W-3d delivers the **manufacturing per-line widget cluster** — three widgets (`vault_schedule`, `line_status`, `urn_catalog_status`) plus the **ancillary-independence + SalesOrder-vs-Delivery canon** captured in BRIDGEABLE_MASTER §5.2.5. **First cluster activating the 5-axis filter end-to-end** — vertical + product_line + extension axes all exercised concretely. **First workspace-core widget canonical reference** (`vault_schedule`) per DESIGN_LANGUAGE §12.6. **First cross-line aggregator using multi-line builder pattern** (`line_status`). **First widget testing `required_extension` axis** (`urn_catalog_status`). **Pre-build investigation surfaced foundational architectural question:** user asked whether kanban + scheduling widgets should reference SalesOrder rather than Delivery. Three parallel investigation agents converged on the answer: Delivery is the canonical logistics entity, SalesOrder is the canonical commercial entity, intentionally distinct. User clarified: ancillaries are INDEPENDENT SalesOrders (not line items / sub-orders). Captured as BRIDGEABLE_MASTER §5.2.5 to prevent future Sonnet sessions from inferring nested commercial semantics. **Commit 1 — `vault_schedule` widget (workspace-core canonical reference + mode-aware rendering).** First concrete workspace-core widget per §12.6 — renders the SAME data the scheduling Focus kanban core consumes with a deliberately abridged interactive surface (mark hole-dug, drag delivery between drivers, attach/detach ancillary, update single ETA per §12.6a; finalize / day-switch / bulk reassignment remain Focus-only). Glance + Brief + Detail + Deep variants — full set, since workspace-core widgets are first-class. **Mode-aware rendering** per BRIDGEABLE_MASTER §5.2.2: production mode reads `Delivery` rows (kanban shape), purchase mode reads incoming `LicenseeTransfer` rows (this tenant as `area_tenant_id`), hybrid composes both stacked. Backend service `vault_schedule_service.py` reads `TenantProductLine(line_key="vault").config["operating_mode"]` and dispatches per mode. New endpoint `GET /api/v1/widget-data/vault-schedule?target_date=...`. Frontend `VaultScheduleWidget.tsx` three-component shape per Phase W-3a/W-3b precedent; per-section rendering for production driver lanes + purchase incoming buckets; ancillary attachment count surfaced inline as ride-along signal (canon §5.2.5: pure logistics, no commercial nesting). 17 backend + 24 frontend tests. **Commit 2 — `line_status` widget (cross-line aggregator + multi-line builder pattern).** Brief + Detail variants — NO Glance per §12.10 (operational health doesn't compress to count-only). Cross-line scope (`required_product_line=["*"]`) — renders for whichever lines the tenant has activated. Per-line health vocabulary canonical: `on_track / behind / blocked / idle / unknown`. Vault health real today (composes `Delivery` count + driver assignment distribution per mode); redi_rock / wastewater / urn_sales / rosetta render placeholder rows with `status="unknown"` until each line's metrics aggregator ships — multi-line builder pattern (mirrors `today_widget_service.py`) keeps the dispatcher clean while future lines plug in. Backend service `line_status_service.py` with per-line builders. Frontend `LineStatusWidget.tsx` renders per-line breakdown with status icons + headline metrics + per-row click-through. `data-attention="true"` widget-root attribute when any line is `behind / blocked` for visual emphasis hooks. 11 backend + 16 frontend tests. **Commit 3 — `urn_catalog_status` widget (first extension-gated widget).** First widget exercising `required_extension="urn_sales"`. Phase W-1 implemented extension gating in `widget_service.get_available_widgets`; W-3a + W-3b cross-vertical widgets all used `"*"`. urn_catalog_status is the first concrete activation: visible only to tenants with the `urn_sales` extension activated AND the urn_sales product line enabled. Glance + Brief variants — catalog management lives at `/urns/catalog` (the page); widget surfaces health (SKU counts, low-stock identification, recent order count). Backend service `urn_catalog_status_service.py` aggregates `UrnProduct` (active + non-discontinued counts split by `source_type`), `UrnInventory` (low-stock: `qty_on_hand <= reorder_point AND reorder_point > 0`; reorder_point=0 means "no monitoring" and is excluded), `UrnOrder` (recent orders over last 7 days). Frontend four metric rows + low-stock list with format `{sku} {name} {qty_on_hand}/{reorder_point}` + click-through to catalog. 10 backend (incl. 3 dedicated extension-gating end-to-end tests) + 14 frontend tests. **Commit 4 — Integration tests + canon doc updates.** New `test_widget_library_w3d_integration.py`: 20 tests across 7 classes covering catalog presence, 5-axis filter end-to-end (extension + product_line + vertical axes), variant declarations matching §12.10 with explicit regression guards, sidebar pin compatibility (vault_schedule + urn_catalog_status accepted; line_status rejected), workspace-core canon (§12.6) verification, cross-cluster regression (W-3a + W-3b widgets still present), endpoint contract sanity. **DESIGN_LANGUAGE.md §12.10** expanded from 11 → 14 reference implementations with vault_schedule as first concrete workspace-core canonical reference (entry 12 explicitly references the SalesOrder vs Delivery investigation + ancillary-independence canon), line_status as multi-line builder canonical reference (entry 13), urn_catalog_status as first extension-axis activation (entry 14). **BRIDGEABLE_MASTER §5.2.5** new canonical section capturing SalesOrder vs Delivery distinction + ancillary independence — load-bearing for future sessions. Two minor follow-ups flagged for post-W-3d cleanup: `Delivery.order_id` FK constraint addition (~1 hr), SalesOrder→Delivery one-way sync semantics review (deferred until product question forces resolution). **Architectural patterns established this phase:** (1) **5-axis filter activated end-to-end** — vertical + product_line + extension axes all exercised concretely. AND-wise filter semantics proven via `TestExtensionGatingEndToEnd`. (2) **Workspace-core widget canonical pattern** — vault_schedule is the reference implementation; future workspace-core widgets follow this exact shape. (3) **Mode-aware rendering pattern** — read `TenantProductLine.config["operating_mode"]` and dispatch per mode; mode-specific data sources isolated to per-mode builder functions. (4) **Multi-line builder pattern as canonical** — line_status mirrors today_widget_service; future per-line widgets plug in alongside without restructuring. (5) **SalesOrder vs Delivery + ancillary-independence canon** — load-bearing for the entire scheduling stack going forward. (6) **Investigation-before-code discipline (validated)** — three parallel investigation agents converged on the architectural answer before any W-3d code shipped. **Final regression posture:** backend 206 widget tests passing across W-1 + W-2 + W-3a + W-3b + W-3d (W-3a 19 + W-3b 13 + W-3d 20 + W-3a per-widget 60 + W-3b per-widget 9 + W-3d per-widget 38 + W-1/W-2 47); 0 regressions. Frontend 731 vitest tests passing (up from 677 pre-W-3d: +24 VaultScheduleWidget + 16 LineStatusWidget + 14 UrnCatalogStatusWidget). tsc 0 errors. vite build clean (4.64s). Migration head: `r60_backfill_tenant_product_lines_vault` (unchanged — W-3d ships zero schema changes; widget definition seeds + service-layer logic only). **Phase W-3 is COMPLETE for manufacturing tenants.** Sunnycrest sees the fully populated widget catalog (9 widgets visible to mfg+vault+urn_sales tenants); FH-specific work (W-3c arrangement_pipeline) sequences after as a separate FH-focused session. Phase W-4 (Pulse surface) ships post-W-3 with role-driven default layouts incorporating the full W-3 catalog (4 cross-vertical foundation + 2 cross-surface infrastructure + 3 manufacturing per-line; W-3c pending).

- **Widget Library Phase W-3b — Cross-Surface Infrastructure Widgets (complete, 2026-04-27):** Three commits shipped end-to-end. Phase W-3b delivers the **cross-surface infrastructure widget cluster** — two widgets (`saved_view`, `briefing`) plus a load-bearing widget config plumbing fix (Commit 0) that closes a latent gap surfaced during investigation. Establishes the **config-driven user-authored widget catalog** pattern via `saved_view` (any saved view becomes a widget instance via `config: {view_id}`), and the **per-user scoped narrative widget** pattern via `briefing` (Phase 6 BriefingCard promoted to widget contract without disturbing the data path). Patterns generalize cleanly to W-3c (FH arrangement_pipeline) and W-3d (manufacturing per-line widgets). **Commit 0 — Widget config plumbing fix (load-bearing prerequisite).** Phase W-2 canon claimed `PinnedSection` carried `pin.config` through to the widget component, but the actual code didn't. Investigation surfaced this as a blocker — `saved_view` reads `config.view_id` and `briefing` reads `config.briefing_type`; without the plumbing both widgets render only their empty states. Fixed in **6 dispatch sites** per Q1 confirmed scope (sweep ALL, not just PinnedSection): extended `WidgetRendererProps` in `widget-renderers.ts` with optional `config?: Record<string, unknown>` field; extended `WidgetState` in `focus-registry.ts` with same field; updated `Canvas.tsx`, `StackRail.tsx`, `BottomSheet.tsx` (TWO dispatch sites — main + expanded view), `StackExpandedOverlay.tsx`, `PinnedSection.tsx`, `WidgetGrid.tsx` to pass `config` through. Backward-compat verified: 2 new `PinnedSection.test.tsx` regression tests (config round-trip + null-default); full vitest 636/636 unchanged post-fix. **Commit 1 — `saved_view` widget (config-driven user-authored widget catalog).** Generic widget rendering any tenant saved view via `config: {view_id: <uuid>}` — establishes **user-authored widget catalog without widget code** pattern. Single widget definition + per-instance config turns every saved view in `vault_items.metadata_json.saved_view_config` into a widget instance. Tenants extend their effective widget catalog by authoring saved views; no code ship required. Brief + Detail + Deep variants per §12.10 — **NO Glance** because saved views need at minimum a list to be informative; surface compatibility excludes `spaces_pin` for the same reason (sidebar requires Glance per §12.2 compatibility matrix). Per Q2 confirmed scope: thin wrapper around the existing V-1c `SavedViewWidget` (`frontend/src/components/saved-views/SavedViewWidget.tsx`) — zero changes to V-1c, full reuse of its 7 presentation modes (list / table / kanban / calendar / cards / chart / stat) + visibility checks + cross-tenant masking. Brief variant calls V-1c with `showHeader=false` (widget framework chrome provides card surface); Detail + Deep call with `showHeader=true`. Empty state per Q4 fallback (b): when `config.view_id` missing or invalid, renders `Layers` icon + "No saved view configured" + "Pick a saved view from the library to display it here." + "Open saved views library →" link to `/saved-views`. Inline picker dropdown deferred until `PATCH /spaces/{space}/pins/{pin}` endpoint ships; not built as part of W-3b per scope discipline ("widget shipping, not infrastructure expansion"). **Sidebar pin rejection (canonical guard)**: Phase W-2 add_pin surface check rejects `pin_type="widget" + target_id="saved_view"` against a sidebar; defensive fallback in dispatcher renders Detail rather than crashing if a pre-W-2 layout slips through. **Commit 2 — `briefing` widget (per-user scoped narrative — Phase 6 BriefingCard promoted to widget contract).** Promotes the existing Phase 6 `BriefingCard` (mounted as a dashboard element on manufacturing-dashboard.tsx + order-station.tsx) to the widget catalog without disturbing the data path. Glance + Brief + Detail variants per §12.10 — **NO Deep** because briefing detail is informationally complete; Deep would just re-render the dedicated `/briefing` page in widget chrome, which §12.6a discourages (heavy actions belong on the page, not the widget). Surface compatibility includes `spaces_pin` (Glance) + `pulse_grid` + `dashboard_grid` + `focus_canvas`; **excludes `peek_inline`** because briefing is per-user content, not entity-scoped — peek panels compose around an entity, briefing has none. Reuses Phase 6 `useBriefing` hook unchanged; **per-user scoping enforced server-side** by `/briefings/v2/latest` (which filters by `user_id == current_user.id`). The widget never sees other users' briefings — endpoint contract is the security boundary, not the widget. Per-instance briefing-type via `config.briefing_type` ("morning" | "evening", default "morning") so future tenants can pin a Glance "End of day summary" alongside the morning briefing. **Glance variant** (Pattern 1 sidebar treatment): single-line strip with Sunrise/Sunset icon + briefing-type label + unread accent dot. **Brief variant** (Pattern 2 grid treatment): icon + title + narrative excerpt truncated to 320 chars at last-word boundary + active space pill + Unread pill + "Read full briefing →" link. **Detail variant**: full narrative (no truncation) + structured-section preview cards (Queues, Flags, Pending decisions — top 5 each, severity dot per flag) + Read full link. Renders only known structured-section keys; unknown keys silently skipped per the Phase 6 contract. **Coexist-with-legacy discipline (canonical pattern)**: Phase 6 `BriefingCard` stays alive as a page-mounted component; the W-3b `BriefingWidget` is the catalog-citizen widget contract. Same content, different consumers. Future natural-touch refactors may migrate page mounts onto the widget; not a W-3b deliverable. **Commit 3 — Integration tests + canon doc updates.** New `test_widget_library_w3b_integration.py`: 13 tests across 5 classes covering catalog presence, 5-axis filter conformance, variant declarations matching §12.10 (with explicit regression guards `test_saved_view_explicitly_has_no_glance` + `test_briefing_explicitly_has_no_deep`), sidebar pin compatibility (saved_view rejected, briefing accepted with `variant_id="glance"` — same canon §12.2 + §12.10, opposite per-widget outcomes), config plumbing persistence (config JSONB round-trips through pin creation + listing for briefing widget — closes the Commit 0 plumbing fix at the integration level), cross-surface coverage (both declare grid surfaces; neither declares `peek_inline`). DESIGN_LANGUAGE.md §12.10 expanded from 9 → 11 reference implementations (entries 10 saved_view + 11 briefing) covering the W-3b infrastructure widgets. **Architectural patterns established this phase:** (1) **Config plumbing as load-bearing infrastructure** — pin config carries through every dispatch site uniformly; when a contract claims "X carries through," verify with regression test before assuming prior phase claim is implementation, not aspiration. (2) **Config-driven user-authored widget catalog** — single widget definition + per-instance config turns every user-authored artifact into a widget instance; no code ship for catalog extension. (3) **Promotion-of-existing-surface-to-widget-contract** — when an existing primitive renders the right content, prefer thin variant-aware wrapper over rebuild; widget contract is a presentation contract, not a data contract. (4) **Per-user scoping via endpoint contract, not widget filtering** — security boundary is the endpoint, not the widget; keeps widgets dumb-render. (5) **Coexist-with-legacy discipline made explicit** — phase work that "promotes" a surface to a new contract should NOT delete the original unless explicitly scoped to migrate consumers. (6) **Same canon, opposite per-widget outcomes** — §12.2 + §12.10 produce sidebar-pin-rejected for saved_view and sidebar-pin-accepted for briefing; both correct, both verified at integration level. **Final regression posture:** backend 148 passed across W-1 + W-2 + W-3a + W-3b widget library suite (W-3a 19 + W-3b 13 + W-3a per-widget 60 + W-3b per-widget 9 + W-1/W-2 47); 0 regressions. Frontend 677 vitest tests passing (up from 634 pre-W-3b: +14 SavedViewWidget + 27 BriefingWidget + 2 PinnedSection config-plumbing regression). tsc 0 errors. vite build clean (4.55s). Migration head: `r60_backfill_tenant_product_lines_vault` (unchanged — W-3b ships zero schema changes; widget definition seed + config plumbing only). **Sequencing after Phase W-3b:** W-3c (FH arrangement_pipeline) ships next, then W-3d (manufacturing per-line widgets `vault_schedule`, `line_status`, `urn_catalog_status`). Phase W-4 (Pulse surface) ships post-W-3 with role-driven default layouts incorporating the full W-3 catalog (4 cross-vertical foundation + 2 cross-surface infrastructure + arrangement_pipeline + per-line schedule widgets + line_status). Phase 4.4.4 (slide animation + multi-day Focus rendering) and Aesthetic Arc Sessions 5-9 still unblocked.

- **Widget Library Phase W-3a — Foundation Widget Cluster (complete, 2026-04-27):** Six commits shipped end-to-end. Phase W-3a delivers the **cross-vertical foundation widget cluster** (4 widgets) plus the AncillaryPoolPin retag per Product Line + Operating Mode canon. Patterns established for W-3b/c/d clusters; 5-axis filter operational end-to-end; tenant isolation explicit + tested for every widget data source. **Commit 1 — AncillaryPool retag + 5-axis filter activation + TenantProductLine vault auto-seed.** Migration `r59_widget_product_line_axis` adds `required_product_line` JSONB column to `widget_definitions` (default `["*"]`); backfills all 28 existing widgets to `["*"]`; retags `scheduling.ancillary-pool` from `required_vertical: ["funeral_home"]` → `["manufacturing"]` + new `required_product_line: ["vault"]` per canon investigation finding (scheduling Focus is Sunnycrest manufacturing operations, not FH). Migration `r60_backfill_tenant_product_lines_vault` backfills `TenantProductLine(line_key="vault")` for every active manufacturing tenant; preserves `Company.vault_fulfillment_mode` value via canonical `operating_mode` enum translation (`produce → production`, `purchase → purchase`, `hybrid → hybrid`). `widget_service.get_available_widgets` extended to 5-axis filter via new `_get_enabled_product_lines(db, tenant_id)` helper (queries `tenant_product_lines.line_key` for `is_enabled=True` rows). New `seed_default_product_lines(db, company)` helper auto-seeds vault for manufacturing tenants based on `default_for_verticals` registry. Wired into `auth_service.register_company` (new tenants) + `seed_staging.py` (testco). **Drift correction (Spec-Override Discipline):** `AVAILABLE_PRODUCT_LINES` catalog renamed `burial_vaults → vault`, `urns → urn_sales`, `rosetta_hardscapes → rosetta` to match canon (BRIDGEABLE_MASTER §5.2). 24 W-2 test fixtures flipped from `funeral_home` → `manufacturing+vault`; `_make_tenant_user_token` helpers extended with `product_lines` parameter. **Commit 2 — `today` widget.** Cross-vertical foundation widget with **per-vertical-and-line content rendering**. Backend service `today_widget_service.py` resolves "today" in tenant local timezone, dispatches to per-(vertical, active product line) category builders. Manufacturing+vault tenants get vault deliveries + ancillary pool + unscheduled count breakdown. Other verticals get empty payload + vertical-aware `primary_navigation_target`. Multi-line builder pattern locks the future-proofing shape: `_build_manufacturing_redi_rock_categories` will plug in alongside vault when redi_rock activates without restructuring. Frontend `TodayWidget.tsx` three-component shape (presentation tablet + variant wrapper + dispatcher) per AncillaryPoolPin precedent. Glance + Brief variants. Endpoint `GET /api/v1/widget-data/today` with 5-min auto-refresh. Tenant isolation explicit via `Company.id == user.company_id` filter; cancelled deliveries excluded; yesterday/tomorrow excluded via tenant-local-today resolution. **Commit 3 — `operator_profile` widget.** Auth-context-only widget — **NO backend call** (establishes the pattern: not every widget needs a data endpoint). Reads from `useAuth()` (full user identity + role + permissions/modules/extensions counts) + `useSpacesOptional()` (active space name). Glance: 24×24 initials avatar + name + role. Brief: 32×32 avatar + full name + email header + role/active-space/access-summary rows + "Manage profile →" footer CTA navigating to `/settings/profile`. Defensive null behavior when unauthenticated. **Commit 4 — `recent_activity` widget.** Backend reuses V-1c `GET /api/v1/vault/activity/recent` endpoint with **additive shim** — `_VaultActivityItem` Pydantic model gains optional `actor_name: str | None` populated server-side via User left-join in `activity_log_service.get_tenant_feed`. Existing V-1c consumers (Vault Overview CrmRecentActivityWidget) unaffected (ignore unknown fields). No new endpoint. Frontend three-component shape with Glance + Brief + Detail variants. Brief shows top 5 events (actor + verb + entity + relative timestamp). Detail adds 4 filter chips (All / Comms / Work / System) collapsing the activity_type taxonomy with proper `aria-selected` semantics. View-only per §12.6a — click-through navigates to related CRM company. Used inside peek panels at Brief variant per §12.5 (`peek_inline` surface) — first widget shipping with peek-inline support. **Commit 5 — `anomalies` widget.** Real production data — backed by existing `agent_anomalies` table (Phase 1+ accounting agent infrastructure) instead of stub. Severity vocabulary canonical: 3 levels (`critical`, `warning`, `info`) per `app.schemas.agent.AnomalySeverity` enum (Spec-Override Discipline note — the user spec mentioned 4-level critical/high/medium/low; actual production enum has 3 levels). New backend service `anomalies_widget_service.py` with `get_anomalies` (severity-sorted critical → warning → info) + `resolve_anomaly` (state-flip + audit log). **Tenant isolation load-bearing**: `agent_anomalies` has no direct `company_id` column; tenant scoping flows through `agent_job_id` FK → `AgentJob.tenant_id`. Every query explicitly joins AgentJob; the acknowledge endpoint re-validates tenant ownership BEFORE mutation; cross-tenant `anomaly_id` returns 404 (existence-hiding to prevent leakage). Verified explicitly via `TestTenantIsolation` test class with both directions tested (read + acknowledge). Audit log writes `action="anomaly_resolved"` for accuracy at the data layer; UI vocabulary "Acknowledge" maps to data-layer `resolved=true` + optional `resolution_note`. Endpoints: `GET /api/v1/widget-data/anomalies?severity=...&limit=N` + `POST /api/v1/widget-data/anomalies/{id}/acknowledge`. Frontend Brief + Detail variants — **NO Glance per §12.10** (anomalies need at least Brief context; count alone doesn't communicate severity or actionability). Severity colors map to locked tokens: critical → terracotta (`status-error`), warning → terracotta-muted (`status-warning`), info → `status-info`. The Acknowledge action is the **canonical §12.6a test case** for widget-appropriate interactions: bounded scope (single anomaly), no coordination required, reversible (audit-logged), time-bounded (instant). Brief acknowledge button: hover-reveal compact icon (`Check` / status-success on hover); disabled when already-resolved; idempotent re-ack is a no-op. Empty state "All clear" + sage `CheckCircle2` icon — operational signal (good state) without celebratory accent terracotta. **Commit 6 — Integration tests + canon doc updates.** New `test_widget_library_w3a_integration.py`: 19 tests across 5 classes covering catalog presence, 5-axis filter conformance, variant declarations matching §12.10, sidebar pin lifecycle for all 4 widgets, cross-surface coverage (`spaces_pin` + `dashboard_grid` + `pulse_grid`), data-endpoint shape contracts. DESIGN_LANGUAGE.md §12.10 expanded with reference implementations 6-9 covering the new W-3a foundation widgets — establishes the **cross-vertical + cross-line foundation widget pattern** as canonical reference for future Phase W-3 cluster work. **Final regression posture:** backend 254 passed + 1 skipped (W-1 + W-2 + W-3a Commits 1-6 + spaces phases — 0 regressions), frontend 634 vitest tests passing (up from 591 pre-W-3a Commits 4-5 — +43 across recent_activity + anomalies). Migration head: `r60_backfill_tenant_product_lines_vault` (unchanged from W-3a Commit 1). Patterns established for W-3b (`saved_view`, `briefing` cross-surface infrastructure widgets) + W-3c (FH `arrangement_pipeline`) + W-3d (manufacturing per-line widgets `vault_schedule`, `line_status`, `urn_catalog_status`) — those clusters apply the now-locked three-component shape + 5-axis filter + tenant-isolation-explicit-at-service-level patterns.

- **Aesthetic Arc Session 2 — Brass to Terracotta Foundation Migration (2026-04-25):** Foundational accent color migration. Brass (`oklch(0.66 0.12 73)` light / `oklch(0.70 0.13 73)` dark — yellow-amber, lightness-shifted across modes) retired and replaced with **deepened terracotta `#9C5640`** (`oklch(0.46 0.10 39)`) — single value across both light and dark modes per the new architectural-color rule. Hover brightens universally to `#B46A4D` (`oklch(0.54 0.10 39)`) — universal "lift" signal replacing brass's asymmetric press-in (light) / glow (dark) pattern. `--content-on-accent` is light cream `oklch(0.98 0.006 82)` in BOTH modes — symmetric pairing matches the single-value accent (replaces brass-era "glowing pill with dark text" pattern; terracotta dark enough for AAA cream contrast in both modes ~6.5:1). `--accent-active` deliberately omitted: current Bridgeable "active state" semantic is "this is the currently selected item" implemented via the two-token pattern `bg-accent-subtle` + `border-accent`; no momentary press-feedback token until needed. **Why brass retired:** brass on warm-cream substrate read "musty wall" rather than craftsmanship — yellow chroma fought substrate's warmth. Terracotta (hue ~39) sits well below the warm-cream substrate's hue range (~82), providing genuine chromatic contrast without leaving the warm family. Reads as "earthen architectural detail on warm stone" rather than "yellow detail on yellow stone." Cross-mode lock simplifies token system + reinforces architectural framing — closer to Range Rover paint (constant identity across rooms) than to brass-on-camera-body (metal observed under different lights). **Token surface:** `--accent-brass-*` → `--accent-*` namespace rename. `--border-brass` → `--border-accent`. `--content-on-brass` → `--content-on-accent`. `.focus-ring-brass` utility → `.focus-ring-accent`. Tailwind `--color-brass-*` → `--color-accent-*` bindings in `index.css @theme inline`. shadcn `:root` block dropped its `--accent: var(--accent-subtle)` alias to avoid name collision with DL's primary accent token; shadcn primitive consumers using `bg-accent` for menu hover backgrounds migrated to `bg-accent-subtle`. **Codebase sweep:** 389 references across 65 files migrated (3,596 total reach including shadcn-aliased consumers); 4 ternary site verifications (StackRail dot indicator, SchedulingKanbanCore selection state, funeral-schedule day toggle, urn-order-form selection); 2 ternary sites that styled selected items with `bg-accent` corrected to `bg-accent-subtle border-accent` per two-token pattern (urn-order-form selected product card); 4 test assertions updated. Zero hardcoded hex / oklch values needed touching — all consumers were token-routed via Tailwind utilities or CSS variables. **Substrate values verified:** light `oklch(0.94 0.030 82)` ≈ `#F5EFE6` warm cream and dark `oklch(0.16 0.010 59)` ≈ `#1A1715` warm charcoal — both match user's locked targets exactly; no substrate change. **Doc updates:** DESIGN_LANGUAGE.md heavy edit — Section 0 detail-concentration prose, Section 1 dark-mode anchor 3 + light-mode anchor 3 + the terracotta thread subsection rewritten with new architectural-color rationale, Section 2 deepened-terracotta derivation rule + warm-family asymmetry rule + cross-mode rule fully rewritten with the "Why brass retired" rationale paragraph + the universal-lift hover rule + the no-`--accent-active` rationale, Section 3 token tables updated (anchor table, content-on-accent, border-accent, accent tokens, plus full CSS variable list both light + dark + the duplicate Section 9 reference list), Section 6 focus-ring + border references updated, Section 10 anti-patterns block rewritten (single-value accent enforcement + universal-lift enforcement + two-token active-state enforcement). PLATFORM_QUALITY_BAR.md (4 refs), PLATFORM_DESIGN_THESIS.md (3 refs), PLATFORM_INTERACTION_MODEL.md (2 refs), AESTHETIC_ARC.md structural prose at top, CLAUDE.md §14 Design System block all updated. Session logs (AESTHETIC_ARC.md Sessions 1-4 entries, CLAUDE.md §15 Recent Changes prior entries) preserved as immutable history. **Verification:** tsc 0 errors, vitest 527/527, vite build clean (4.66s). Migration delivered as four commits per spec — Commit 1 (token migration + Tailwind binding + .focus-ring-accent rename), Commit 2 (codebase sweep + test assertions), Commit 3 (doc updates), Commit 4 (Focus chrome reference component pass + visual verification). **The reference component:** Focus chrome cluster (SchedulingKanbanCore header + DateBox + AncillaryPoolPin + Finalize button + WidgetChrome + StackRail + IconButton + ReturnPill) becomes the canonical calibration reference for all subsequent Aesthetic Arc work. Future component refinements (DeliveryCard internals, AncillaryCard internals, Funeral Schedule widget, command bar, dashboards) compare against the Focus chrome calibration. **Out of scope (deferred):** type system migration (Plex → Fraunces / Geist / Geist Mono — separate session), Phase 4.4.4 (slide animation + multi-day rendering), Phase II Batches 1b through 5 (resume after aesthetic foundation locked).

- **Phase 4.4.3 — Adjacent-day DateBoxes + day-summary peek + H2 popover refactor (2026-04-25):** Adds the flanking-DateBox affordance to the Scheduling Focus header — `[today_box] H2 (clickable for any-day jump) [day_after_box]` — as the primary peek/slide affordance for Phase 4.4.4's eventual multi-day rendering. State-only in 4.4.3: `prevExpanded` / `nextExpanded` boolean flags toggled by clicking each box; brass-on-active visual jewelry feedback; tooltip body via `useDaySummary` hook (30s TTL module cache, AbortController cancellation). FIRST surface built against locked DESIGN_LANGUAGE Section 0 — calibrated against in-register surfaces (DeliveryCard, AncillaryPoolPin, brass primary buttons): `bg-surface-elevated` + `border-border-subtle` + `rounded-sm` (square-shouldered, sharp corners — not pillowy chip), generous internal padding, two-line stacked content (text-micro uppercase weekday eyebrow + text-body-sm font-plex-mono month-day), brass border + brass-subtle/50 wash on active. **Backend** GET /api/v1/dispatch/day-summary returns kanban-only counts (excluding ancillary, direct_ship, cancelled, completed) plus finalize state — sub-100ms target met (8ms typical). **Header refactor**: `DaySelectorButton` retired in favor of `DaySelectorPopover` wrapping the H2 button-trigger; the standalone "Change day" sub-button removed per Section 0 Restraint Translation Principle ("if you can remove an element and the user doesn't notice, the element was decorative — remove it"). The H2 carries both the date display AND the day-jump affordance via a small chevron suffix. Popover content + click-outside + listbox semantics preserved verbatim. **Tests shipped**: 10 backend (TestDaySummary class — counts, exclusions, tenant isolation, permission gate), 7 SchedulingKanbanCore integration (renders 2 boxes, default-inactive, click-toggle, independent-toggling, H2-popover-regression, any-day-jump-resets, calibration-class-regression), 12 DateBox component (formatters, render contract, click wiring, calibration-class regression, brass-active composition), 7 useDaySummary hook (cache hit, refetch, error, date-change, reload). Full regression: 527/527 vitest, 61/61 dispatch backend, tsc clean, no regressions. Spec-override flags surfaced + resolved: tooltip delay (used platform 150ms not spec'd 200ms — the "Section 3" reference appeared to be misremembering of §6); H2-as-trigger required retiring "Change day" sub-button per Section 0 Restraint. Visual verification via Preview MCP confirmed boxes flank H2, brass-active state computed `oklch(0.7 0.13 73)` border = full brass token, popover renders with 7 day options. **Out of scope (Phase 4.4.4)**: slide animation, multi-day rendering, cross-day drag, mutual-exclusion-vs-simultaneous decision for the two flags. Commit `6dec4b3`.

- **Phase 4.4.3a-bis — Three-Layer Design Thesis canonicalization (2026-04-25):** Documentation-only phase. Canonicalizes the three-layer synthesis the user articulated: ***Bridgeable looks like a Range Rover, behaves like Tony Stark's workshop, and is built like an Apple Pro app.*** Three references, three layers, three distinct jobs, all sourced from outside software, all aligned on understated mastery. **Two new docs**: (1) `PLATFORM_DESIGN_THESIS.md` (top-level synthesis — the verbatim thesis statement, why all three references work, each layer's job and test, what each layer rejects, where the layers conflict and how to resolve, the strategic positioning as design moat, implementation status honesty about what's shipped vs aspirational); (2) `PLATFORM_INTERACTION_MODEL.md` (Layer 2 — Tony Stark / Jarvis interaction philosophy, four primary interactions of summon/arrange/park/dismiss, floating tablets as materialization unit, voice/text invocation as primary verb, the chip pattern + interpretation chips + pause sensor, persistence rules, three modes of presence, mobile/tablet translation, what this rejects with cinematic theater explicitly excluded, where the model translates to current Bridgeable). **`PLATFORM_QUALITY_BAR.md` extended**: explicit Apple Pro era reference at top (peak Jony Ive era hardware + Pro app discipline at peak — Final Cut Pro X, Logic Pro, Aperture, original iPhone, unibody MacBook), comparison standards reorganized with Apple Pro as primary frame and surface-specific references as subordinate, test method updated from "would this feel as good as the Apple equivalent?" to "would this ship in an Apple Pro app at peak Apple form?", new Calibration Against Existing Surfaces section listing in-register reference points (DeliveryCard, AncillaryPoolPin, post-4.4.2 Focus core, DateBox, QuickEditDialog, brass primary buttons, Phase A 3.5 zone-relative canvas, Phase 4.2.4 whole-card drag) and drift surfaces (WidgetChrome grip icon, BlockLibrary grip icon, ~213 legacy pages on shadcn defaults pre-batch-1, ~1305 ad-hoc status-color sites), new "Relationship to the Three-Layer Design Thesis" section. **Explicit current-decade Apple drift rejection**: Liquid Glass aesthetics, soft luminance everywhere, decorative animations, lingering skeleton loaders, optimistic UI that pretends, iOS 17+ ornamental flourishes — all explicitly forbidden. Take the quality bar from peak Apple. Take the discipline. Don't take the current style. **Cross-references wired**: DESIGN_LANGUAGE.md §0 gains a "this is Layer 1 of the three-layer thesis" framing block at the top with links to the synthesis + interaction model + quality bar docs. PLATFORM_PRODUCT_PRINCIPLES.md "Relationship to siblings" expanded from 4 docs to 6 (adding DESIGN_THESIS + INTERACTION_MODEL); appendix "When in doubt" section restructured to point at the right layer for the right question; version-history table extended. CLAUDE.md §1b Canonical Platform Specs registry adds DESIGN_THESIS + INTERACTION_MODEL as new entries, QUALITY_BAR description rewritten to reflect Apple Pro era reference; per-prompt reading list updated for UI-touching prompts and a new interaction-model-decisions reading list. Pre-existing canon (PLATFORM_ARCHITECTURE.md sections on entity portal / chip conversation / pause sensor / three modes of presence / observe-and-offer / bounded-decision discipline) is unchanged — those articulations remain canonical for their architectural scope; the new INTERACTION_MODEL doc provides the *philosophy framing* (Tony Stark / Jarvis) that the existing architectural patterns instantiate. Layer 1 (DESIGN_LANGUAGE §0) was already canonical from commit 6a2545d (April 25 morning); Layer 3 (QUALITY_BAR) existed from April 22 escalation but was generic-Apple-grade-framed; this phase adds the missing Layer 2 + makes Layer 3 specific to Apple Pro era + adds the synthesis at the top. **Strategic intent**: the three-layer coherence is the design moat — competitors can copy features but cannot easily copy a coherent three-layer design identity rooted in references they don't share, applied with discipline they don't have. Future sessions build to the thesis; the thesis itself changes rarely.

- **Phase A Session 3.8.3 — Position via transform, composite-only updates (2026-04-23):** Continuation of the Session 3.8.x performance line after Session 4 shipped. User identified that window-resize feel still trailed tldraw.com / macOS Finder at the Quality Bar ceiling and directed a calibrated tldraw-pattern fix. **Pre-build investigation** (no more iterating blind): sub-agent mapped tldraw's three-layer perf stack — (a) `transform: matrix(...)` for shape position instead of `left/top` (composite-only), (b) `useQuickReactor` + `@tldraw/state` signals bypassing React reconciliation with direct `elm.style.setProperty` writes, (c) memoized per-property diff so unchanged props aren't written. Research verdict: at our scale (3-10 widgets per Focus, not 10000 shapes) **only layer (a) earns its keep**; layers (b)(c) save ~0.5ms/frame (below perceptual threshold) while demanding a full state-management rewrite — explicit over-engineering for scale we don't have. User approved shipping only the scale-calibrated 30-LOC subset. **What shipped:** `WidgetChrome.tsx` now positions via `transform: translate3d(x, y, 0)` instead of inline `left/top`; width/height stay as inline style (stable during window resize — they only change during user-initiated widget resize gestures, a rare event). Drag composition: base position-translate written first, then `@dnd-kit`'s drag translate stacks via CSS matrix multiplication, then drag-scale appended last — a single `transform` string handles all three. `left: 0, top: 0` set explicitly as containing-block anchor to prevent browser auto-placement quirks. **Focus Popup positioner wrapper** (~15 LOC extra beyond the widget fix): implementation surfaced the CSS Animations L1 spec behavior that running animations override inline style for animated properties — if the Popup carried both `transform: translate3d(x, y, 0)` for position AND its `data-open:animate-in zoom-in-95` keyframes, the zoom animation would take over transform during the 400ms open window and render the core at viewport (0, 0) scale-0.95 → (0, 0) scale-1 before teleporting to `(coreRect.x, coreRect.y)` on animation end. Broken entry/exit. **Fix:** wrap `DialogPrimitive.Popup` in a positioner `<div>` (`position: fixed`, `transform: translate3d(coreRect.x, coreRect.y, 0)`, explicit width/height). Popup fills the wrapper via `absolute inset-0` and keeps its zoom keyframes untouched. Two elements, two concurrent transforms on different elements — no conflict. Wrapper's lifecycle matches Popup's (mounts inside `Dialog.Portal` which only renders children while open). **Per-frame impact during window resize:** widget updates drop from ~2-5ms/widget/frame (full layout + paint) to ~0.1ms/widget/frame (GPU composite, no layout, no paint). Focus Popup's width/height still triggers layout per frame on size change (one element, unavoidable without scaling content which we don't want), but that single layout pass is imperceptible vs. 3+ layout passes across widgets. **Explicitly NOT done** (calibration, not neglect): ResizeObserver on Focus root (current rAF-coalesced window.resize from Session 3.8.2 is adequate at this scale); CSS custom properties (`--widget-x`, no benefit over inline transform at 3-10 elements); signals / `useQuickReactor` / direct DOM writes / split state model (all overkill for our scale per tldraw research). **Architectural decisions locked:** (1) Width/height stays inline — window-resize only mutates x/y; moving just those to transform removes all per-frame layout from widget rendering. (2) Popup needs a positioner wrapper because keyframe animations override inline transform; one element can't both hold a static-position-transform AND animate-on-transform. (3) Explicit `left: 0, top: 0` on both widgets + Popup — defensive against browser auto-placement. (4) Ship the 30-LOC scale-calibrated fix, not the full tldraw pattern; document the reasoning so future sessions don't re-escalate to signals + state split without new scale driving it. **Tests:** 366 vitest passing (same as post-Session-4; no new tests — Session 3.8.3 is purely internal rendering mechanics, no behavioral contract change). tsc clean. vite build clean in 4.47s. **User visual verification pending:** drag browser window wide→narrow→wide, compare to tldraw.com in adjacent tab — expect composite-only feel during resize. If still choppy, the architectural ceiling at this scale is reached per user's pre-ship guidance; accept and move to Session 5 (pin system). **PLATFORM_QUALITY_BAR.md** gains "Done Right" entry documenting the scale-calibration reasoning so future primitives can decompose reference-implementation stacks by layer rather than copying full patterns.

- **Phase A Session 4 — Focus persistence + return pill countdown (2026-04-22):** Completes Phase A. First backend work of the Focus arc — patterns mirror Saved Views / Spaces / Briefings persistence from prior UI/UX arc phases. **Two new tables** via migration `r48_focus_sessions_and_layout_defaults`: `focus_sessions` (per-user per-focus session state with layout_state JSONB, soft-delete via `is_active=False` + `closed_at`, three indexes including partial index on active rows for hot-path lookup + partial on closed rows for 24h recent-window fallback) and `focus_layout_defaults` (per-tenant admin baseline per focus_type, UNIQUE on `(company_id, focus_type)`).
