Migration head `r47_users_template_defaults_retrofit` → `r48_focus_sessions_and_layout_defaults`. **Service layer** at `app/services/focus/focus_session_service.py` — 8 functions: `get_active_session` + `create_or_resume_session` (idempotent, seeds new sessions from tenant default) + `update_layout_state` (no-op on closed sessions) + `close_session` (idempotent, soft-delete) + `get_recent_closed_session` / `list_recent_closed_sessions` (24h default window, tunable) + `get_layout_default` / `set_layout_default` (tenant admin path) + `resolve_layout_state` (3-tier cascade: active user session → recent closed session within 24h → tenant default → None). Frontend calls via API and doesn't know about tiers. **5 new API endpoints** at `/api/v1/focus/*`: GET `/{focus_type}/layout` (3-tier resolve with `source` field reporting which tier answered), POST `/{focus_type}/open` (create-or-resume with resolved layout in one round-trip), PATCH `/sessions/{id}/layout`, POST `/sessions/{id}/close`, GET `/recent`. Ownership enforced via `_get_owned_session` which 404s on cross-user access (existence-hiding, not 403). **Frontend API client** `services/focus-service.ts` — plain axios via `apiClient`, no query library per CLAUDE.md §2 convention. **Optimistic loading in FocusContext**: on Focus open, seed `layoutState` immediately from registry default (first paint instant per Quality Bar §1); in parallel fire POST /open; swap to persisted layout when resolved. Persistence errors never block UX (console.warn + continue with optimistic default). Layout writes debounced 500ms via `scheduleLayoutWrite` — most-recent-wins cancellation, matches saved-view convention. Pending write flushes before close fires. New `FocusState.sessionId` field gates PATCH firing on having a server session. **Return pill countdown** — new `useReturnPillCountdown` hook encapsulates full re-arm semantics per Session 4 user decision: 15-second countdown (tunable totalMs), hover pauses + mouse-leave resumes, tab visibility hidden pauses + visibility return RE-ARMS to full 15s (not resume-from-paused — spec intent is "welcome back, here's 15 more seconds"), resetKey change re-arms, onExpire fires once (latched via `expiredRef`), resetKey=null halts countdown. ReturnPill gets a 2px-tall brass countdown bar at bottom, width `(remainingMs/totalMs)*100%`, CSS transition `width 100ms cubic-bezier(0.32, 0.72, 0, 1)` — approximates spring-decay feel without a physics library. **Deferred re-arm trigger**: Intelligence-backbone data subscription (requires real-time infrastructure — WebSocket/SSE — that doesn't exist in the codebase). Documented in FEATURE_SESSIONS.md Session 4 entry; candidate for future session alongside Phase E cross-tenant personalization which would benefit from similar infrastructure. **Tests shipped Session 4:** 23 new backend tests in `test_focus_session.py` across service layer (3-tier resolve precedence, create-or-resume idempotency, layout roundtrip, close semantics, recent window, tenant isolation) and API layer (auth required, empty-state null, open-then-update, close, cross-user 404 ownership, recent history list, recent history cross-user isolation) — all 23 passing. 14 new frontend tests: 7 in `useReturnPillCountdown.test.ts` (initial state, tick decrement, hover pause/resume, onExpire single-fire latch, resetKey re-arm, resetKey=null halt, tab visibility pause/re-arm), 5 in `focus-service.test.ts` (contract for each endpoint), 2 new in `ReturnPill.test.tsx` (countdown bar renders at 100%, auto-dismiss after 15s with fake timers). Full frontend suite: 366 passing. tsc clean. vite build clean in 4.76s. **Preview verification**: POST /open → DB row created, GET /layout source=null initially, PATCH /layout → layout persisted (verified via deep equality on returned JSONB), GET /layout source=active after write, POST /close → is_active=false + closed_at stamped in DB with layout_state preserved, reopen → GET returns prior layout with source="recent" (3-tier fallback working). Return pill renders with countdown bar, 100ms cubic-bezier transition set, pause-on-tab-hidden confirmed (preview tab is backgrounded, hook correctly marks isPaused=true). **Completes Phase A** — Focus primitive foundation. Next: Sessions 5-6 pin system, Session 7 Focus Chat.

- **Phase A Session 3.8.2 — Viewport-synchronous layout, no transition lag (same-day fix on 3.8.1, 2026-04-22):** User verification of Session 3.8.1 confirmed the three layering fixes landed but flagged that window resize still felt choppy relative to macOS Finder. **Root cause (profiled):** CSS transitions on `left/top/width/height` for WidgetChrome (`duration-settle`, 300ms) and Focus Popup (`duration-arrive`, 400ms) were adding second-order interpolation on top of the viewport's first-order motion. Each viewport resize event set a new target left/top/width/height, and CSS started a fresh transition FROM mid-animation state TO the new target. At 60Hz resize, widgets + core were always ~100-200ms behind where they should be, easing into new positions — the "choppy chase" feel. macOS Finder doesn't do this: content follows the window per-frame, no transition layer. **Fix:** layout properties now update synchronously with viewport — no CSS transitions on `left/top/width/height`. Three coordinated changes: (1) WidgetChrome's `transition-[left,top,width,height] duration-settle ease-settle` removed, plus the `data-[chrome-active=true]:transition-none` guard no longer needed (nothing to suppress). Widget chrome still has `transition-shadow` (box-shadow is cheap, non-layout). (2) Focus Popup's `transition-[width,height,left,top] duration-settle ease-settle` removed AND `transition-none` added explicitly. Without the explicit disable, the remaining `duration-arrive` sets `transition-duration: 0.4s` while `transition-property` defaults to `all` (browser default), which would still animate layout props. `transition-none` guarantees no CSS transitions on the element. Enter/exit keyframe animations (`animate-in`, `fade-in-0`, `zoom-in-95`) use CSS `animation`, not `transition`, so they remain unaffected. (3) `useViewportTier` resize handler rAF-throttled — multiple resize events within one animation frame collapse to one `setDims` call. Browsers fire resize events during drag faster than a render frame can complete; without throttling, React re-renders per event, stacking work. With the throttle, re-renders match the browser's paint cadence. **Preserved transitions** (all GPU-composited or cheap): tier-renderer wrapper `transition-opacity` + asymmetric `duration-settle`/`duration-quick` from 3.8.1; WidgetChrome `transition-shadow`; chrome children (grip + dismiss X) `transition-opacity`. **Trade-off accepted:** tier-boundary core size changes now snap instantly instead of interpolating over 300ms. The tier-renderer opacity crossfade (stack fading out as canvas widgets fade in) carries visual continuity at the boundary; the core size step-change underneath is imperceptible because the eye is tracking the renderer change, not the backdrop container. This is the macOS-Finder model: layout is viewport-driven, animations are reserved for state changes that the viewport itself can't carry (opacity transitions between alternate content presentations). **Preview verification (Chromium, live):** post-fix core `transitionProperty: "none"` (confirmed via `getComputedStyle`); widget `transitionProperty: "box-shadow"`; renderer `transitionProperty: "opacity"`. Programmatic resize from 2560×1400 → 1400×900: core width snapped to final value 1072 within 100ms and stayed stable at 350ms (pre-fix would show width mid-animation at 100ms). **Tests:** 352 passing (same as 3.8.1 — no new tests, the only changes are removing CSS transition classes which weren't asserted on directly; the Canvas/geometry/Focus behavioral tests all continue to pass). tsc clean. vite build clean in 4.84s.

- **Phase A Session 3.8.1 — Layering and transition coordination (same-day fix on 3.8, 2026-04-22):** User verification of Session 3.8 surfaced three quality-bar failures that shared one root cause. (1) **Issue 1 — widgets never exited stack at wide-but-short viewports.** At 2560×1300 (typical 27" monitor minus browser chrome), the three seeded Kanban widgets stayed in stack mode even though they'd fit comfortably in canvas reserved space. Diagnosis: `widgetsFitInCanvas` used AND logic for corner anchors — a 320×240 top-left widget failed because height 240+16 > reservedTop=200, even though it had 580px of horizontal slack and could obviously sit in the left band alongside the core. The AND check was over-strict for corner anchors, which have an L-shaped reserved area (top band × full width OR left band × full height). **Fix:** rewrote `widgetsFitInCanvas` with anchor-aware per-class logic. Corner anchors now use OR logic — fit if widget's `offsetX + width + buffer ≤ horizontalBand` OR `offsetY + height + buffer ≤ verticalBand`. Rail anchors check horizontal band only (vertical is unbounded). Center anchors check vertical band only (horizontal is unbounded). Fit check also now respects stored `offsetX`/`offsetY` — a widget pushed toward core via large offset correctly fails fit where pre-3.8.1 it passed. Live-verified: 2560×1300 now returns tier=canvas, all three widgets render without core overlap (widget 1 at x=32-352, core at x=580+ — no horizontal overlap; widget sits in left band extending into left-band area alongside core). (2) **Issue 2 — stack appeared "under" core during stack↔canvas transition.** As core grew into the space where stack rail had been, fading stack rail visibly sat inside the growing core for ~100-200ms. (3) **Issue 3 — icon button hidden behind core in icon tier.** Hit-test at icon center returned the core's FOOTER child instead of the icon — the icon was in DOM but painted under core. **Root cause for Issues 2 + 3:** both Canvas and Popup at `z-index: var(--z-focus)`; DOM order decides paint order at equal z-index; Canvas rendered BEFORE Popup, so Popup won paint order and covered everything inside Canvas (widgets, stack rail, icon). **Fix:** swapped DOM order so `<Canvas />` renders AFTER `<DialogPrimitive.Popup>` in `Focus.tsx`. Canvas + its three tier-renderer wrappers now paint in front of core in every tier — correct because icon floats over core by design, and canvas widgets + stack rail don't overlap core in final state but need to paint above during transitions. Canvas still sibling-to-Popup (not parent); `pointer-events: none` on Canvas wrapper + active subtree's `pointer-events-auto` preserves backdrop-click-to-dismiss and core interactivity. **Fix for Issue 2 polish:** asymmetric crossfade duration. Inactive renderer (fading-out) now uses `data-[active=false]:duration-quick` (200ms) while active renderer (fading-in) keeps `data-[active=true]:duration-settle` (300ms). The fading-out renderer reaches opacity 0 before the core has moved appreciably through its own 300ms width/height transition — stale spatial position doesn't linger long enough to be visible under the growing core. **Preview verification (Chromium, live):** 2560×1300 tier=canvas with 3 widgets rendered, no core overlap; 900×800 tier=icon with hit-test at icon center returning icon (hitInIcon=true, hitInCore=false — pre-3.8.1 returned core's FOOTER); DOM order check `core.compareDocumentPosition(canvas) & DOCUMENT_POSITION_FOLLOWING` = truthy; inactive renderer computed `transitionDuration: "0.2s"`, opacity 0. **Tests:** 352 passing (346 baseline + 6 new — 3 new `widgetsFitInCanvas` tests for OR logic (horizontal-only clears, vertical-only clears, both fail), 1 offset-pushes-widget test, 1 wide-but-short regression guard in `determineTier`, 1 Canvas fade-class asymmetric-duration assertion, 1 Focus DOM-order regression guard via `compareDocumentPosition`). `widget()` helper extended with optional `offsetX`/`offsetY` parameters. Existing tests updated from "corner checks BOTH dimensions" to "corner OR logic." tsc clean. vite build clean in 5.01s.

- **Phase A Session 3.8 — Continuous geometric layout cascade (2026-04-22):** Layout is continuous, not tier-based. Three-tier components (canvas / stack / icon) preserved, but transitions now happen at geometric constraints — not viewport pixel thresholds. Architectural correction identified during Session 3.7.2 verification — user observed that the responsive cascade felt like discrete mode switches rather than macOS-grade continuous flow. **Chunk A — geometric icon gate.** New `stackFitsAlongsideCore(vw, vh)` derives the icon-tier floor from `CORE_MIN_WIDTH(600) + STACK_EDGE_MARGIN(16) + STACK_CORE_GAP(16) + STACK_RAIL_WIDTH(280) + STACK_EDGE_MARGIN(16) = 928` wide and `CORE_MIN_HEIGHT(400) + 2×STACK_EDGE_MARGIN(16) = 432` tall. `determineTier` replaced the `vw < TIER_ICON_MAX_WIDTH(700)` hard threshold with the geometric check: if even a minimum-sized core + rail can't fit, the tier drops to icon. Ordering tightened — stack-floor check is now first so canvas (structurally stricter than stack) can never return at a viewport where stack itself wouldn't fit. `TIER_ICON_MAX_WIDTH` constant retained for reference; no longer a direct gate. **Chunk B — simultaneous renderer crossfade.** Canvas.tsx restructured: all three tier subtrees (canvas widgets, stack rail, icon button / bottom sheet) now render simultaneously in the DOM, inside `[data-tier-renderer]` wrappers with `data-active="true"|"false"`. Visibility switches via `transition-opacity duration-settle ease-settle` + `data-[active=true]:opacity-100 data-[active=false]:opacity-0` + matching pointer-events gating so dimmed renderers can't eat a click mid-fade. Simultaneous (not sequential) crossfade — old tier fades out AS new tier fades in at the same time — matching macOS window-resize feel rather than stepped "mode switch" feel. **Chunk C — widget-position transition on viewport resize.** WidgetChrome wrapper gains `transition-[left,top,width,height] duration-settle ease-settle` so on viewport resize, widgets glide to their new anchor-resolved positions. Suppressed during drag/resize via existing `data-[chrome-active=true]:transition-none` guard — during active gesture, the widget follows the cursor in real time, not through an ease. **Boundary matching.** Stack `computeCoreRect` formula gains `CORE_MAX` caps (previously uncapped on the upper bound). Canvas and stack now both cap core at 1400×900 on wide viewports, so the canvas↔stack boundary converges there with zero core-size pop. At narrower viewports the formulas differ inherently (canvas reserves 100 per side for widgets, stack reserves 280+ for rail) — the existing `transition-[width,height,left,top]` on the Focus Popup smooths that step-change over `--duration-settle`. New extracted constants `STACK_EDGE_MARGIN = 16` + `STACK_CORE_GAP = 16` are shared between `stackFitsAlongsideCore`, `computeCoreRect`, and `computeStackRailRect` so the numbers stay consistent across call sites. **Crossfade timing = `--duration-settle`** (token-first per Aesthetic Arc). Reduced-motion users inherit the global `prefers-reduced-motion` retrofit in `base.css`. **Preview verification (Chromium, live):** tier="canvas" at 3840×2160 with core 1400×900 (CORE_MAX capped); tier="stack" at 1800×1200 with core 1400 (CORE_MAX capped — boundary match ✓); tier="stack" at 1400×900 with core 1072×868; tier="stack" at 928×700 (exact floor, core 600×668); tier="icon" at 927×700 (one px below floor correctly drops); tier="icon" at 900×800 (was stack in 3.7, correct geometric icon now). All three `[data-tier-renderer]` wrappers mount simultaneously with `transition-opacity / 0.3s`. Widget chrome carries `transition: left, top, width, height / 0.3s` with `chrome-active=false`. **Tests:** 346 passing (336 baseline + 10 new — 6 `stackFitsAlongsideCore` tests across derived-floor/below-floor/above-floor + mobile landscape / iPad mini, 1 CORE_MAX cap test on stack formula, 3 new Canvas crossfade tests asserting all-three-mounted + active-tier-via-data-active + opacity-transition-token-contract, and 1 geometric-floor-wins test for oversized widgets below the stack floor). Existing determineTier tests updated from the pre-3.8 `vw ≥ 700 → canvas` assumption to the post-3.8 `vw ≥ 928 AND vh ≥ 432` geometric floor. Canvas tier-dispatch tests rewritten from presence/absence assertions to `data-active` assertions (all three mount always; visibility is via data attribute). tsc clean. vite build clean in 4.83s.

- **Phase A Session 3.7.2 — Stack rail Smart Stack behavior (same-day fix on 3.7.1):** User visual verification of stack tier at medium viewport surfaced widgets rendered as a plain scrollable vertical list instead of iOS Smart Stack. Root cause: [canvas/StackRail.tsx:145](frontend/src/components/focus/canvas/StackRail.tsx:145) sized each tile to `state.position.height` (200-320px per widget — the stored canvas-mode heights). In a 70vh rail container (~504-525px at test viewports), three 200-320px tiles fit simultaneously — scroll-snap had no overflow to snap through. Fix: tile height changed from `state.position.height` to inline `height: 100%` + `minHeight: 100%` + `flexShrink: 0`. Each tile now fills the rail exactly; three tiles = 3× rail height scrollable; `scroll-snap-type: y mandatory` + `scroll-snap-align: start` force scroll to settle on exactly one tile at a time. Matches iOS Home Screen Smart Stack pattern. Why `flexShrink: 0` alongside `height: 100%`: inside a flex column with `overflow-y-auto` the default `min-height: auto` on flex children would collapse them to content height, defeating the 100% height. `flexShrink: 0` + explicit `minHeight: 100%` keeps each tile at full rail height. IntersectionObserver root changed to the rail element (was viewport default) so intersection ratio is computed against the rail's visible area — dots indicator now tracks the currently-visible tile accurately regardless of where the rail sits in the page (fixed center via `top-1/2 -translate-y-1/2`). **Preview verification (Chromium at 900×750 and 800×720):** tier="stack"; rail 280 × 525/504px; scrollHeight 1569/1506px (3× tile); `scroll-snap-type: y mandatory` computed; 3 dots rendered; `aria-selected` flips correctly across the 3 dots as `rail.scrollTop` steps through `0 / railHeight / 2×railHeight` (dots tracking IntersectionObserver confirmed). DOM introspection via `document.elementFromPoint(740, 375)` returns `MockSavedViewWidget` content — rail is rendered, visible, and in the correct stacking position at the right edge of the viewport. **Tests:** 336 passing (335 baseline + 1 new `StackRail.test.tsx` "each tile fills full rail height" regression guard asserting `height / minHeight / flexShrink` inline styles). tsc clean. vite build clean in 5.11s. User-side visual verification pending: scroll the rail on a medium-width viewport — each scroll should snap to exactly one widget; dots indicator column (brass filled for active, subtle outlined for inactive) updates in lockstep; tap a dot scrolls smoothly to that widget.

- **Phase A Session 3.7.1 — Content-aware tier detection (same-day fix on 3.7):** User visual verification of Session 3.7 surfaced widgets clipping at viewport edges in canvas mode — three 320×240/280×320/280×200 seeded widgets at vw=1400 need 296-336px of reserved space per side, but canvas reserved only 100px per side. The Session 3.7 initial threshold used fixed viewport dimensions (`vw < 1000 OR vh < 700 → stack`) under the assumption "viewport wide enough → canvas fits widgets," which breaks at every intermediate viewport because reserved margins shrink linearly with viewport while widget sizes stay fixed. **Fix:** tier detection is now content-aware. New pure `widgetsFitInCanvas(widgets, vw, vh, buffer=16)` in `geometry.ts` does per-anchor fit checks — `*-left`/`left-rail` widgets need width+16 ≤ reservedLeft; `*-right`/`right-rail` need width+16 ≤ reservedRight; `top-*` need height+16 ≤ reservedTop; `bottom-*` need height+16 ≤ reservedBottom. Corner anchors check both dimensions (stricter — anchor reserves corner overlap region). Empty widget list fits vacuously. `determineTier(vw, vh, widgets?)` rewritten: icon tier still viewport-only at `vw < TIER_ICON_MAX_WIDTH = 700` (canvas structurally unusable at that width regardless of content), but canvas-vs-stack now driven by `widgetsFitInCanvas`. Matches Apple Freeform behavior — if objects don't fit cleanly in the workspace, adapt the workspace presentation rather than clipping. **Removed constants** `TIER_STACK_MAX_WIDTH`, `TIER_STACK_MIN_WIDTH`, `TIER_CANVAS_MIN_HEIGHT` (no longer referenced). **Threading:** `useViewportTier(widgets?)` hook accepts optional widget set; Canvas.tsx + Focus.tsx + dev focus-test.tsx all thread `currentFocus?.layoutState?.widgets ?? {}` so content-aware detection runs everywhere the viewport is observed — critical that Canvas + Focus agree on tier or Popup sizing would drift out of sync with widget rendering. Tier derives fresh on every render (dims + widgets); resize listener still the only viewport-side reactive dependency, avoiding useEffect churn on widget mutation. **Tests:** 335 passing (318 baseline + 14 new `widgetsFitInCanvas` tests + 6 rewritten content-aware `determineTier` tests + 1 new Canvas content-aware transition regression guard; 4 stale viewport-only `determineTier` assertions removed; dev-mode-grid test switched to `test-single-record` fixture since test-kanban's seeded widgets now force stack at jsdom default viewport and dev-grid only renders in canvas tier). tsc clean. vite build clean in 5.19s. **Quality bar learning canonicalized:** PLATFORM_QUALITY_BAR.md "Almost But Not Quite" gains the 3.7-initial viewport-only-threshold entry + "Done Right" gains the 3.7.1 content-aware entry — responsive tier logic must consider what's being placed, not just available space. Viewport dimensions are necessary but not sufficient; reserved space shrinks linearly with viewport but widget sizes stay fixed, so "viewport is wide enough" isn't a reliable proxy for "content fits." **User-side visual verification pending**: at 1920×1080 Focus should now render stack tier (not clipped canvas); ultra-wide 2560+ should land canvas; narrow <700 stays icon; live transitions smooth without flicker.

- **Phase A Session 3.7 — Three-tier responsive cascade (canvas → stack → icon) (complete):** Focus primitive becomes responsive. Replaces the original Session 3.6 "hide widgets + indicator" plan with iOS Smart Stack pattern; scope expanded to three tiers per user direction so Focus is architecturally mobile-ready in one coherent pass rather than as a separate mobile polish session. **What shipped:** 14 files touched, ~1,400 LOC added across new components + tests; 32 new vitest assertions (318 passing, 286 baseline). Widgets now collapse through three render paths as viewport shrinks — same canonical state, three presentations. **Tier breakpoints** in new `geometry.ts::determineTier(vw, vh)` pure function: **canvas** (vw ≥ 1000 AND vh ≥ 700) = free-form placement, drag/resize, 8 anchors, WidgetChrome map (Session 3.5 behavior preserved); **stack** (700 ≤ vw < 1000 OR vh < 700 with vw ≥ 700) = right-rail iOS Smart Stack analog, 280px × 70vh scroll-snap column, dots indicator, tap-to-expand overlay; **icon** (vw < 700) = floating 56×56 brass button bottom-right, tap opens bottom-sheet with 2-column widget tile grid. **`useViewportTier` hook** subscribes to window resize + returns `{width, height, tier}` — single source of tier truth. **`computeCoreRect(tier, vw, vh)`** extended with tier-aware sizing: canvas reserves 100px margin, stack subtracts right-rail width + margins, icon fills viewport minus 16px padding (no floor — narrow viewports define icon mode). **New primitives:** `StackRail.tsx` (native CSS `scroll-snap-type: y mandatory` + `scroll-snap-align: start` per widget; dots indicator column to the left with IntersectionObserver tracking active widget via threshold 0.55 — jsdom-guarded via `typeof IntersectionObserver === "undefined"` early return so tests stay green in non-browser environments; chrome affordances disabled — widgets scroll in place, not drag); `StackExpandedOverlay.tsx` (bespoke floating overlay — NOT a nested base-ui Dialog, which would compete with the parent Focus Dialog's focus trap + create tricky dismiss layering; outside-click + Esc + tap-widget dismiss); `IconButton.tsx` (floating button with count badge, zero-renders when `widgetCount === 0`, `bottom-[max(1rem,env(safe-area-inset-bottom))]` for iOS home-indicator clearance); `BottomSheet.tsx` (slide-up sheet with drag-handle + two-level modality — tap tile opens full-screen expanded widget view, Esc cascade closes expanded first then sheet; `touchAction: none` on handle to prevent scroll hijack; `bg-black/60` backdrop); `useSwipeDismiss` hook (PointerEvent-based swipe-down detector, 150px threshold, uses window-level pointer listeners + `latestRef` pattern from `useResize`). **Canvas.tsx rewritten as tier dispatcher** using `useViewportTier`; renders WidgetChrome map (canvas) OR StackRail (stack) OR IconButton + optional BottomSheet (icon); `data-focus-tier` attribute for test assertions; `handleDragEnd` only relevant in canvas tier. **Focus.tsx** uses `useViewportTier` + tier-aware `computeCoreRect` for Popup sizing via inline style; `transition-[width,height,left,top] duration-settle ease-settle` smooths tier transitions; `data-focus-tier` attribute on Popup. **Pure render-time override, no state mutation across tiers** — widget position state (`{anchor, offsetX, offsetY, width, height}`) is canonical and tier-independent. Tier change re-renders different components reading the same state. User drags a widget in canvas mode, narrows the browser to stack tier, widens back to canvas — widget is exactly where they left it. No tier-specific position storage. **Kanban stub seeded with 3 widgets** (top-left 320×240, right-rail 280×320, bottom-right 280×200) so stack mode has enough content to exercise scroll-snap + dots indicator, and icon-tier BottomSheet has a populated tile grid. **Dev test page** at `/dev/focus-test` gains viewport-tier indicator with color coding (canvas=success, stack=info, icon=warning). **Native CSS scroll-snap, not spring physics** — per PLATFORM_QUALITY_BAR.md §2 spring physics is the ideal for iOS-grade feel; Session 3.7 ships native `scroll-snap-type: y mandatory` + momentum scrolling for close-to-Smart-Stack feel on iOS/Safari without a physics library. Spring physics + velocity-weighted bottom-sheet dismissal deferred to mobile polish session (documented in PLATFORM_QUALITY_BAR.md "Almost But Not Quite"): shipping a physics library for one primitive when the rest of the overlay family uses CSS-based `data-open:animate-*` would fork the animation pipeline. **Architectural decisions locked**: (1) Three tiers, not two — original plan was canvas + stack only; user expanded to make Focus mobile-ready in one pass. (2) Pure render-time override, no state mutation across tiers. (3) Bespoke `StackExpandedOverlay` instead of nested Dialog — Focus owns the modal container; everything inside is bespoke. (4) Native CSS scroll-snap is the pragmatic "80%" ship; spring physics deferred. (5) IntersectionObserver jsdom guard keeps tests green without polyfill. (6) Safe-area-inset-bottom via `max()` fallback — no conditional rendering for iOS vs desktop. **Tests:** 318 vitest passing (286 + 32 new across `Canvas.test.tsx` 4 tier-dispatch tests, `StackRail.test.tsx` 6, `IconButton.test.tsx` 6, `BottomSheet.test.tsx` 7, `geometry.test.ts` tier tests, + test fixture updates for 3-seeded-widget Kanban). tsc clean. vite build clean in 5.00s. **Preview verification (Chromium via preview_eval):** at 483×1298 narrow viewport `{tier: "icon", chromeCount: 0, stackRail: false, iconBtn: true}` ✓. User-side visual verification pending for stack tier at medium viewport (scroll-snap feel, dots indicator tracking, tap-to-expand), canvas tier at wide viewport (drag/resize preserved), live tier transitions, and iOS Safari device behavior. **PLATFORM_ARCHITECTURE.md §5.15** extended with three-tier cascade paragraph documenting breakpoints + state-canonicality rationale + deferred spring physics. **Next session (Phase A Session 4)**: persistence tier — `focus_sessions` table + per-user layout preferences + 15-second return-pill countdown with hover-pause + re-arm on state change. Per Session 1's deferred scope. Layout state is currently session-ephemeral; Session 4 makes it per-user durable and per-tenant defaulted.

- **Phase A Session 3.6 — Chrome restraint during active drag/resize (complete):** Small polish fix surfaced during Session 3.5 user verification. Chrome affordances (grip icon + dismiss X) now hide during active drag/resize instead of forcing visible. CSS class flipped — `group-data-[chrome-active=true]:opacity-0` replaces the Session 3 / 3.5 `opacity-100`. Dismiss X additionally gets `pointer-events-none` during active interaction so it can't intercept an in-flight gesture. Rationale per PLATFORM_QUALITY_BAR.md §4 visual restraint + §6 affordance-through-interaction: during drag/resize the cursor already tells the user what's happening (`grab` → `grabbing` during drag, `nwse-resize`/etc during resize); additional in-widget visual indicators are noise — same reasoning that removed the static resize-corner icon in 3.5. **Tailwind class order matters:** `group-data-[chrome-active=true]:opacity-0` must appear AFTER `group-hover:opacity-100` in the class list so source-order specificity resolves correctly during concurrent conditions (cursor IS over widget during drag ∧ drag IS active → hide wins). **Verification:** 288 vitest passing (286 baseline + 2 new restraint assertions in `WidgetChrome.test.tsx`); tsc clean; vite build clean 4.78s. Chromium preview-verified: grip + dismiss class strings contain the correct restraint selectors. **PLATFORM_QUALITY_BAR.md** Done Right list gains the 3.6 entry. **Session 3.7 takes iOS Smart Stack path** for narrow-viewport widget handling — replaces the original 3.6 "hide widgets + indicator" plan after user noted Smart Stack is the canonical iOS responsive pattern. 3.6 is therefore scoped to chrome restraint only.

- **Phase A Session 3.5 — Canvas refactor: zone-relative positioning + 8-zone resize + drag-from-anywhere (complete):** Three architectural improvements surfaced during Session 3 user verification. Quality > session ceremony — foundational canvas primitives warrant doing this right before Session 4 adds persistence. **Refactor 1 — Zone-relative positioning:** Replaced `WidgetPosition = {x, y, width, height}` with `{anchor: WidgetAnchor, offsetX, offsetY, width, height}`. Eight anchors: `top-left / top-center / top-right / left-rail / right-rail / bottom-left / bottom-center / bottom-right`. Widget positioning is now viewport-independent — a right-rail widget tracks the right edge across viewport changes (verified: resolves to x=1568 at vw=1920, x=848 at vw=1200, x=448 at vw=800 — always `vw - width - offsetX`). Render-time resolution via `resolvePosition(pos, vw, vh): Rect` in [canvas/geometry.ts](frontend/src/components/focus/canvas/geometry.ts). Drag-end picks new anchor via `determineAnchorFromDrop(dropX, dropY, vw, vh)` (100px rail threshold from edges; top/bottom halves divided into left/center/right thirds), then re-projects drop rect to anchor offsets via `computeOffsetsForAnchor`. `clampPositionOffsets` keeps widget in viewport while preserving anchor. Inverse-of-resolve identity unit-tested across all 8 anchors. **Refactor 2 — 8-zone resize:** Replaced the single visible bottom-right resize icon with 8 invisible zones — 4 corners (8×8px, `nwse-resize` / `nesw-resize` cursors) + 4 edges (8px strips, `ew-resize` / `ns-resize` cursors). Cursor change on hover provides discoverability per DESIGN_LANGUAGE §6 restraint principle. `useResize` hook refactored to accept a zone parameter + new pure `applyResizeDelta(zone, startRect, dx, dy)` function — N/NW/NE move top edge; S/SW/SE move bottom; W/NW/SW move left; E/NE/SE move right. Resolve to absolute rect → apply delta → enforce min + canvas clamp → re-project to the ORIGINAL anchor (resizing doesn't change zones; only dragging does). **Refactor 3 — Drag from anywhere:** `useDraggable` listeners moved from the grip button to the wrapper element. Grip icon becomes decorative (`pointer-events: none`, `aria-hidden="true"`) — visual affordance only. Dismiss X + all 8 resize zones use `onPointerDown={e => e.stopPropagation()}` so they don't initiate drag. Wrapper carries `cursor-grab` + `role="button"` semantics from @dnd-kit's spread attributes. **Preview verification (Chromium):** widget resolves correctly from anchor-based seed (top-left/32/96 → screen (32, 96)); all 8 resize zones present with correct cursor mappings; wrapper has `role="button"` + `cursor-grab`; grip `aria-hidden="true"` + pointer-events-none; zone-relative math verified via direct module import. Manual user verification: drag from middle of body, hover each edge/corner for cursor change, resize from each zone, drag to viewport edges for anchor reassignment, resize browser window for zone persistence. **Tests:** 286 vitest passing (259 baseline + 27 new/rewritten across geometry + WidgetChrome + focus-context). tsc clean. vite build clean in 4.87s. **Architectural decisions locked:** (1) Anchor is first-class, stored explicitly, not derived. (2) Rails span vertically; top-*/bottom-* span horizontally. No center-center anchor — widgets cannot occlude core by design. (3) offsetX/offsetY are distances from anchor edges, always ≥ 0 after clamping, 8px-snapped. (4) Resize is zone-pure (preserves anchor); drag is anchor-reassigning (changes anchor based on drop point). Cognitive separation. (5) Cursor-only on resize (no icon); X + icon on dismiss (WCAG target-size + keyboard). (6) Drag listeners on wrapper; conflict guards via stopPropagation. **Freeform-quality-bar follow-up flagged** mid-session by user — Apple Freeform becomes comparison standard for canvas interactions (Sessions 5+, Phase B). Spring physics, 60fps drag, snap-to-widget guides, multi-select, undo/redo deferred to post-Session-3.5 polish. Separate `PLATFORM_QUALITY_BAR.md` commit follows Session 3.5 ship (quality bar escalated from canvas-only to platform-wide per later user decision).

- **Phase A Session 3 — Canvas + WidgetChrome + drag/resize (complete):** Third session of Phase A per [ARCHITECTURE_MIGRATION.md](ARCHITECTURE_MIGRATION.md). Ships the free-form canvas region surrounding the anchored core, the WidgetChrome primitive (drag/resize/dismiss affordances), drag wiring via `@dnd-kit/core`, custom pointer-event-based resize, and layout-state widget persistence end-to-end (session-ephemeral tier; database tiers arrive Session 4). Demo hero workflow's canvas contract is now verifiable end-to-end with a mock saved-view widget. **What shipped:** Geometry utilities at [canvas/geometry.ts](frontend/src/components/focus/canvas/geometry.ts) — pure `snapTo8px` (normalizes negative zero), `rectsOverlap` (exclusive boundaries), `clampToCanvas`, `enforceMinSize`, `computeCoreRect`, `findOpenZone` (smart positioning engine preferring top → left → right → bottom rails with 8px candidate scan). 22 unit tests. Canvas component at [canvas/Canvas.tsx](frontend/src/components/focus/canvas/Canvas.tsx) — fixed-inset `pointer-events: none` wrapper, `DndContext` with `PointerSensor` (8px activation distance matching WidgetGrid precedent), `onDragEnd` snaps delta → clamps to viewport → rejects core-overlap silently → persists via `updateSessionLayout`. Dev-mode `?dev-canvas=1` renders 8px grid overlay via `useSearchParams`. Canvas is a **sibling of Dialog.Popup** inside the Portal (not a parent) — avoids `pointer-events: none` blocking Popup interactivity while keeping DndContext accessible via React context. WidgetChrome at [canvas/WidgetChrome.tsx](frontend/src/components/focus/canvas/WidgetChrome.tsx) — drag handle (Lucide `grip-vertical`, top-left), dismiss X (top-right), resize corner (Lucide `arrow-down-right`, bottom-right). Chrome `opacity-0` default + `group-hover:opacity-100` on widget hover; state-driven `data-chrome-active="true"` keeps chrome visible during active drag/resize via `group-data-[chrome-active=true]:opacity-100`. Drag initiates from handle only — widget body is non-draggable. `@dnd-kit` inline `transform` applied with `scale(1.02)` during drag per PA §5.1 "subtle lift effect." `useResize` hook at [canvas/useResize.ts](frontend/src/components/focus/canvas/useResize.ts) — custom PointerEvent-based resize (@dnd-kit has no resize primitive). Real-time `liveSize` during drag drives visual feedback; on pointer-up, final size `snapTo8px → enforceMinSize (default 200×100) → clampToCanvas → updateSessionLayout`. MockSavedViewWidget at [canvas/MockSavedViewWidget.tsx](frontend/src/components/focus/canvas/MockSavedViewWidget.tsx) — placeholder "Recent Cases" table with 5 rows (name + status pill + date); deleted when Session 5 ships the real pin system. **Layout state reshape:** `WidgetPosition` changed from Session 2's placeholder grid shape (`row/col/span`) to viewport-pixel shape (`x/y/width/height`); new `WidgetState = { position: WidgetPosition }` wrapper future-proofs for per-widget fields (visibility, z-index). Focus-context seeds layoutState from `config.defaultLayout?.tenantDefault` on open (Session 4 resolves full 3-tier cascade). New `removeWidget(widgetId)` method on FocusContextValue — dismiss X calls it. Kanban stub seeded with mock widget at `(32, 96, 320×240)`; other 4 stubs open without widgets (smart positioning takes over when real pins land). Focus integration: `<Canvas />` mounted as sibling of `<Dialog.Popup>` inside `Dialog.Portal`. Canvas reads `currentFocus.layoutState?.widgets` + renders one `<WidgetChrome>` per widget. **Key architectural decisions locked**: (1) Canvas sibling-to-Popup, not parent — `pointer-events: none` parent would risk blocking Popup interactivity even with child-level override; sibling is safe by construction; DndContext still reaches useDraggable consumers via React context. (2) Drop-only layout-state updates — visual movement during drag via `@dnd-kit` inline transform, state update on drag end (PA Session 3 user decision 5). (3) Core-overlap guard rejects drop silently via early return; widget state never updated so visually snaps back. (4) `findOpenZone` preference order: top → left → right → bottom rails; scanning top-to-bottom left-to-right within each zone. Best-effort heuristic; Session 5 refines once data-relationship context exists. (5) `WidgetState` wrapper over `WidgetPosition` for extensibility. (6) Custom pointer-event resize via `useResize` hook (80 LOC). **Preview verification (Chromium):** Canvas present with `pointer-events: none`; mock widget renders at seeded position with all 3 chrome affordances; dismiss X click → widget removed from DOM (Focus stays open); ESC dismiss → dialog closes + push-back reverts + return pill appears — full Session 1 + 2 + 3 regression green. Drag + resize + boundary-enforcement + dev-canvas grid require manual user verification (jsdom can't emit realistic pointer events; preview tooling can't simulate drag gestures). **Tests:** 259 vitest passing (226 baseline + 33 new: `geometry.test.ts` × 22, `WidgetChrome.test.tsx` × 7, `Canvas.test.tsx` × 5; one Session 2 layout-state test updated for new WidgetState shape — all 5 Session 2 layout tests still green). tsc clean. vite build clean in 4.86s. **In-session debug hits:** 6 test failures resolved — viewport dimensions in `findOpenZone` tests (rails too small for test widgets; bumped to 2400×1400), `computeCoreRect` expected value (function correctly caps at `min(900, 918) = 900`), `snapTo8px(-3)` returning `-0` (normalized via `+ 0`), Canvas dev-mode test using MemoryRouter vs window.location (switched Canvas to `useSearchParams`). Also one structural refactor mid-session: Canvas originally designed as Dialog.Popup parent; refactored to sibling after `pointer-events: none` parent risk identified.

- **Phase A Session 2 — Anchored core + mode dispatcher + push-back scale (complete):** Second session of Phase A per [ARCHITECTURE_MIGRATION.md](ARCHITECTURE_MIGRATION.md). Session 1's placeholder core replaced with a registry-driven mode dispatcher routing to 5 stub core-mode renderers. Push-back scale (deferred from Session 1) now ships. Canvas + WidgetChrome + drag/resize deferred to Session 3 per user scope decision. **What shipped:** Focus registry at [contexts/focus-registry.ts](frontend/src/contexts/focus-registry.ts) — Pattern B. `CoreMode` type union (`kanban | singleRecord | editCanvas | triageQueue | matrix`), `FocusConfig` interface, `LayoutState<TCoreLayout = unknown>` + `LayoutConfig<TCoreLayout = unknown>` generic layout types. `registerFocus / getFocusConfig / listFocusConfigs` API. Five stub Focuses seeded at module load, one per mode. Mode dispatcher at [components/focus/mode-dispatcher.tsx](frontend/src/components/focus/mode-dispatcher.tsx) uses `Record<CoreMode, ComponentType<CoreProps>>` — TypeScript's exhaustive-record check catches forgotten map entries at compile. Unknown focus id renders an error state, not a crash. Five stub cores in [components/focus/cores/](frontend/src/components/focus/cores/): KanbanCore (3 columns with 2-3 cards each), SingleRecordCore (6-row form stack with labels), EditCanvasCore (Bold/Italic/Underline/Image toolbar + ~600px centered canvas), TriageQueueCore (5 rows with keyboard-shortcut badges 1-5), MatrixCore (4×4 table with row/column headers). `_shared.tsx` holds the `CoreProps` contract + `CoreHeader` + `EscToDismissHint` primitives reused across all cores. **Layout-state scaffold:** `FocusState` extended with `layoutState: LayoutState | null`. `FocusContextValue` gains `updateSessionLayout(patch)` that merges widget records; no-op when no Focus is open; resets to null on close-and-reopen (session-ephemeral tier). Session 4 adds per-user + tenant-default persistence via `focus_sessions` + `focus_layout_defaults` tables. **Push-back scale — SHIPPED:** `<main>` in AppLayout carries `data-focus-pushback={isOpen ? "true" : "false"}`. CSS rule in `base.css`: `main[data-focus-pushback="true"] { transform: scale(0.98) }` + transition via `--duration-arrive` + `--ease-settle`. Scope safe by construction: sidebar + header + mobile tab bar are siblings of `<main>`, NOT descendants, so CSS transform's containing-block effect on fixed descendants cannot reach them. Reduced-motion users inherit instant transition from the global retrofit. Chromium verified (scale 0.98 matrix applied to main; sidebar + header transforms stay `none`). Safari verification user-side — scope boundary holds regardless of browser-specific containing-block behavior. **Dev test page** at `/dev/focus-test` updated: 5 mode-labeled buttons (`Open Kanban Focus` / `Open Single-Record Focus` / `Open Edit-Canvas Focus` / `Open Triage-Queue Focus` / `Open Matrix Focus`) + live state readout (isOpen / currentFocus.id / resolved mode / layoutState widget count / lastClosedFocus.id). **PLATFORM_ARCHITECTURE.md §5.15** updated: push-back paragraph flipped from "deferred" to "ships in Session 2" with the scope-safety explanation. **Architectural decisions locked**: (1) Mode lives in registry (Pattern B); consumers call `open(id)` without passing mode. (2) Open-closed mode dispatch — adding a new mode = type-union entry + map entry + new core component + `registerFocus()` call, no existing mode code changes. (3) Naming = camelCase for core modes; verified no backend precedent before choosing; the backend `triage_queue` pin_type literal lives in a distinct namespace (pinnable-target-kinds vs rendering patterns). (4) Canvas + WidgetChrome deferred to Session 3 for coherent drag/resize scope. (5) Generic `LayoutState<TCoreLayout = unknown>` chosen over plain `unknown` — modes can specialize without breaking base contract. (6) Push-back ships because scope is safe by construction. **Tests:** 226 vitest passing (206 baseline + 20 new: `focus-registry.test.ts` × 8, `mode-dispatcher.test.tsx` × 7, `focus-context.test.tsx` +5 layout-state tests). tsc clean. vite build clean in 5.18s. **Orphan blocker resolved mid-session:** user's localStorage had stale `platform_mode="true"` from an earlier session routing the app to BridgeableAdminApp regardless of path; cleared manually, not a Session 2 code issue.

- **Phase A Session 1 — Focus primitive scaffolding (complete):** First session of Phase A per [ARCHITECTURE_MIGRATION.md](ARCHITECTURE_MIGRATION.md). Establishes the Focus primitive's foundational scaffolding — later sessions layer core-mode dispatch (Session 2–3), free-form canvas (Session 3), pin system (Sessions 5–6), `focus_sessions` database persistence (Session 4), and Focus Chat (Session 7) on top. **What shipped:** `FocusContext` at [contexts/focus-context.tsx](frontend/src/contexts/focus-context.tsx) — React context with URL-as-source-of-truth discipline (`?focus=<id>` param drives state via `useSearchParams`; browser back/forward naturally dismisses/reopens). Public API: `open(id, options?) / close() / dismissReturnPill()` + state `currentFocus / lastClosedFocus / isOpen`. Only one Focus active at a time (PA §5.1). `Focus` component at [components/focus/Focus.tsx](frontend/src/components/focus/Focus.tsx) — full-screen overlay atop `@base-ui/react/dialog`. Backdrop `bg-black/40 supports-backdrop-filter:backdrop-blur-md` (heavier blur than Dialog's 4px for push-back signal per PA §5.2). Anchored core `bg-surface-raised rounded-lg shadow-level-3 p-6` at `w-[90vw] max-w-[1400px] h-[85vh] max-h-[900px]`. Animation via `data-open:animate-in / data-closed:animate-out` classes + `duration-arrive ease-settle` on enter / `duration-settle ease-gentle` on exit — identical pattern to Dialog + rest of overlay family. `aria-modal="true"` set explicitly on Popup (base-ui doesn't emit it automatically). Placeholder content only — Session 2 adds core-mode dispatcher. `ReturnPill` at [components/focus/ReturnPill.tsx](frontend/src/components/focus/ReturnPill.tsx) — UI-only scaffolding. Fixed bottom-center, rounded-full `bg-surface-raised shadow-level-2`. Renders when `lastClosedFocus !== null && currentFocus === null`. Click body → reopen; click X → dismiss without reopening. 15s countdown + hover-pause + re-arm-on-state-change deferred to Session 4. **Layering tokens** — `--z-base / --z-elevated / --z-dropdown / --z-modal / --z-focus / --z-command-bar / --z-toast` added to `DESIGN_LANGUAGE.md §9` (new "Layering tokens" subsection) + `tokens.css` in same commit per tokens.css header discipline ("edit DESIGN_LANGUAGE.md first, then port"). New overlay code consumes via `style={{ zIndex: "var(--z-focus)" }}` inline. Existing `z-50` literals in the overlay family (Dialog, Popover, DropdownMenu, Tooltip) NOT retrofit — natural-touch refactor. **Command Bar integration** — `core/CommandBarProvider.tsx` consumes `useFocus()` (FocusProvider mounts above CommandBarProvider in App.tsx). When Focus is open: Cmd+K handler short-circuits + `<CommandBar>` is not rendered + defensive effect closes bar if already open when Focus opens. Bounded-decision discipline in implementation: Act and Decide are distinct primitives with distinct shapes; mixing them inside one screen breaks the boundary. Information-lookup needs inside a Focus are answered by Focus Chat (Session 7), not by escaping to Command Bar. **Dev test page** at `/dev/focus-test` (not in nav, authenticated tenant route) with three Open-Focus buttons, live state readout, and manual-test copy for URL restoration / browser back / Cmd+K suppression. **Key architectural decisions locked**: (1) State pattern = React Context + URL sync. Database persistence deferred to Session 4. (2) Animation infrastructure = `@base-ui/react/dialog` + `tw-animate-css`. Plan said "framer-motion (existing dependency)" — verified against `package.json` + `grep src/` that framer-motion is NOT installed; switched to base-ui Dialog per user decision after flag. Rationale: the Aesthetic Arc's overlay family (Dialog, Popover, DropdownMenu, Tooltip, SlideOver, Select, PeekHost) all use base-ui + `data-open:animate-*` Tailwind classes with `--duration-*` / `--ease-*` tokens — Focus adopts the same pattern for consistency + inherits Dialog's focus trap / ESC / role="dialog" / portal rendering for free. Choice documented in PA §5.15 to prevent future sessions from rebuilding primitive Dialog functionality. (3) Push-back scale on underlying app = DEFERRED from Session 1. CSS `transform: scale` creates a containing block for `position: fixed` descendants on Safari + some Chromium builds; scaling app shell would break DotNav + ModeToggle (both fixed-positioned). Backdrop blur alone provides push-back signal for Session 1. Session 2 revisits with a scoped wrapper element. (4) Command Bar hidden while Focus open (primitive-boundary choice, not UX). (5) Tests colocated (`.test.tsx` next to `.tsx`) per existing codebase convention — overrode plan's `frontend/tests/focus/` subdirectory. (6) `aria-modal="true"` set explicitly on Popup (base-ui's `Dialog.Popup` does not emit it automatically even when `Dialog.Root` is modal). **PA §5.15 "Implementation Foundation"** documents the base-ui Dialog choice + the push-back scale deferral + the Command-Bar-Focus mutual-exclusion decision. **Verification:** 206 vitest passing (185 baseline + 21 new across 3 test files — `focus-context.test.tsx` 11 tests, `Focus.test.tsx` 5, `ReturnPill.test.tsx` 5). tsc clean. vite build clean in 4.88s.

- **Tier-4 Measurement-Based Correction — Dark-mode card chrome calibrated to sampled reference (complete):** Supersedes Tier 2 + Tier 3 (both inference-based). After three sessions of inferring tuning values from §1 anchor prose hadn't closed the visual gap to the approved reference (IMG_6085.jpg), sampled the reference directly via PIL. Measurement revealed three drift points the prose-inference path missed entirely. **Measurement methodology**: `Image.open(path).convert('RGB')` + `getpixel()` at strategic coordinates (page, card body, left-edge transition at 1-pixel horizontal resolution, top-edge transition at 1-pixel vertical resolution, bottom shadow halo), converted sRGB to OKLCH via Ottosson 2020 formulas. Stable samples across dozens of pixels in flat regions validated method accuracy; light-mode samples matched existing tokens to within 0.005 OKLCH lightness (confirming the measurement method is accurate and the dark-mode discrepancies are real drift). **Findings (measurement-based)**: **(G1) Dark-mode hue progression missing from prior tokens.** Reference shifts hue warmer as surfaces elevate — page at h=59 (cool-amber foundation), card body at **h=81** (warmer-amber, catches-more-lamplight), raised at h=85, sunken at h=55. Prior tokens used static h=65 across the elevation stack. **This is the root cause of the visual gap** — neither Tier 2 (shadow composition) nor Tier 3 (lightness bump) touched hue. **(G2) Tier-2's canonical perimeter border was over-specified.** Reference shows NO discrete border pixel in either mode — edge transitions are shadow-halo-mediated. Tier-2 read §1 anchor 4 prose ("the cue may be a top-edge highlight, surface gradient, OR warm hairline border") as "apply all three"; the reference uses only two. Card primitive `border border-border-subtle` removed in both modes. **(G3) Top-edge highlight was wrong shape.** Reference shows a **3-pixel band at L=0.30**, not a 1-pixel inset at L=0.48. Wider-dimmer band delivers ~2.3× more visible-light integral than the thinner-brighter line — reads as "focused light pool catching the top edge" per §1 anchor 2, whereas a 1-pixel hairline doesn't. `--shadow-highlight-top` calibrated to `oklch(0.32 0.010 61 / 0.9)` + `--shadow-level-1/2/3` inset width changed from 1px to 3px across all three elevation levels. **(M1) Tier-3 lightness bumps reverted.** Reference card body measures L=0.202; Tier-3 had set L=0.22 (over by 0.018). Pre-Tier-3 L=0.20 was already correct. Surface-elevated / surface-raised return to L=0.20 / L=0.24 (pre-Tier-3 values). **Tier-4 token changes (dark mode only)**: `--surface-base` `oklch(0.16 0.012 65)` → `oklch(0.16 0.010 59)`; `--surface-elevated` → `oklch(0.20 0.011 81)`; `--surface-raised` → `oklch(0.24 0.013 85)`; `--surface-sunken` → `oklch(0.13 0.010 55)`; `--shadow-highlight-top` → `oklch(0.32 0.010 61 / 0.9)`; `--shadow-level-1/2/3` inset 1px → 3px. **Card primitive**: removed perimeter `border border-border-subtle`, restored CardFooter `border-t border-border-subtle` (since the parent no longer carries a border to mark the footer-boundary). **WidgetWrapper**: inline `border border-border-subtle` removed for parity with Card primitive — both now render with identical chrome discipline. **DL prose updates (mirror discipline, same commit)**: §1 dark-mode anchor 4 **rewritten from "three cues" to "two cues"** with explicit exclusion of perimeter border + explanation of light-vs-dark asymmetry (morning is ambient/diffuse, evening is directional) + Tier-4 history note. §3 Surface tokens rationale **updated with hue-progression paragraph** explaining the second material dimension (hue warms with elevation) + reverted "0.05–0.06 dark-mode step" language to "0.04" (measurement-accurate) + Tier-history note. §3 Full CSS variable list + §9 CSS block synchronized with new dark-mode values. §6 Shadow specifications table updated (inset 3px) + full Tier-history notes + **measurement-first learning note** canonicalized. §6 Border treatment **"Card perimeter border (canonical)" subsection REMOVED and replaced** with "Card perimeter: no border" canonical statement + "Overlay perimeter: no border" + "Where borders DO apply" (inputs, table rules, focus indicators, status callouts, internal section dividers) + updated "What NOT to add borders to." **Methodology canonicalized** in CLAUDE.md Design System section: "Reference images win over prose — for diagnosis, not just authority. When canonical reference images exist in docs/design-references/, sample them directly via PIL before inferring tuning values from prose anchors." Three inference-sessions missed the gap; one measurement-session calibrated it. **Blast radius**: every `bg-surface-elevated` consumer inherits the warmer hue (h=65→81) + reverted lightness (L=0.22→0.20); every `bg-surface-raised` consumer inherits warmer hue + reverted L=0.24; every `shadow-level-*` consumer inherits the 3px inset highlight + the new dimmer-warmer `--shadow-highlight-top` color; every `<Card>` consumer loses perimeter border (~100+ sites); WidgetWrapper loses perimeter border (all dashboard widgets + Vault Overview widgets); overlay family (Dialog/Popover/DropdownMenu/SlideOver/mobile bottom-sheets) inherit updated shadow composition but are unaffected structurally (no primitive changes to overlays). **Aesthetic reconciliation arc status after Tier 4**: measurement-calibrated — values match reference within measurement tolerance. If user visual verification confirms match, arc complete; if residual gap, targeted follow-up for composition-level items (radius — reference shows ~16px vs current `rounded-md` 8px; typography cadence — reference shows specific micro-caps eyebrow → serif display → mono metadata → body sans → brass CTA rhythm). Both flagged for Phase III Final QA, not token scope. Tests: tsc clean, vitest 185/185 unchanged, vite build clean.

- **Tier-3 Spec Reconciliation — Dark-mode surface-lift matches approved reference (complete):** Follow-up to Tier 2 after user provided the canonical approved reference images (saved to `docs/design-references/`). IMG_6084.jpg = John Michael Smith case card **light mode**; IMG_6085.jpg = same card **dark mode** — both are the ground truth from the design-language creation session and become the canonical visuals for card chrome. Per DL §1 canonical-mood-references clause ("images win over prose"), Tier 2 closed most of the gap but the card body still read slightly less-lifted than the reference. Tier 3 bumps dark-mode surface lightness to match. **Changes** (same-commit mirror discipline: tokens.css + DL §3 + §9): `--surface-elevated` dark `oklch(0.20 0.014 65) → oklch(0.22 0.015 65)`; `--surface-raised` dark `oklch(0.24 0.016 65) → oklch(0.27 0.017 65)`. New dark-mode deltas: **base→elevated = 0.06** (was 0.04), **elevated→raised = 0.05** (was 0.04), **base→raised = 0.11** (was 0.08). Chroma eased proportionally per §3 "elevated surfaces carry more warmth, catching more implied lamplight." **DL §3 rationale prose updated**: "Each step of elevation lifts lightness by approximately 0.025 in light mode and **0.05–0.06 in dark mode**. Dark mode needs a larger step because low-lightness differences are perceptually compressed — the approved canonical reference (`docs/design-references/design-ref-dark-card.jpg`) demonstrates cards lifting visibly from the page via a 0.06 OKLCH lightness lift, supplemented by top-edge highlight, shadow composition, and hairline border. The three-cue composition (surface lift + top-edge highlight + perimeter border) is what §1 dark-mode anchor 4 'Material, not paint' prescribes; the 0.06 lift is the surface-lift component." **Tier-3 reconciliation history note** added to §3 Surface tokens documenting the pre→post values and rationale. **Composite verification**: top-highlight at L=0.48/α=0.65 over Tier-3 surface-elevated L=0.22 produces composite L≈0.389 (Tier-2 was 0.382; +0.007 shift). Highlight alpha stays 0.65 — imperceptible shift means no α tune-down needed. **Overlay family impact verified**: `bg-surface-raised` has 25 consumer sites (Dialog, Popover, DropdownMenu, Tooltip, SlideOver, Sonner, Tabs, Select/Input/Textarea backgrounds, Switch, Radio, plus Phase-II Batch-1b scheduling bottom-sheets + FABs). Dark-mode delta vs page shifts from 0.08 to 0.11 OKLCH — distinct-not-harsh range. Strengthened Tier-2 shadow composition (atmospheric halos at level-2/3) mediates any perceived hard-edge on overlays. **docs/design-references/ now populated** with IMG_6084.jpg + IMG_6085.jpg + README.md manifest. The README describes what each reference represents, how future Claude instances should use them (inspect via Read tool before tuning tokens — Read renders JPG content visually), includes per-image observation notes for quick-context scanning, and documents the reconciliation history. **Incidental observation flagged (not in Tier-3 scope)**: the reference cards show generous corner radius (~16px, `rounded-lg` range) whereas Card primitive's default is `rounded-md` (8px). The current default is the dense-card variant; signature detail cards should opt up to `rounded-lg` via className override. Larger-radius card variant is a future consideration, not a Tier-3 change. **Blast radius**: every `bg-surface-elevated` / `bg-surface-raised` consumer inherits the lifted values. For `bg-surface-elevated`: Card primitive body + WidgetWrapper + many ad-hoc consumers (hundreds of sites). For `bg-surface-raised`: 25 sites across overlay family + form input backgrounds + Phase-II Batch-1b bottom-sheets. **Light-mode values untouched** — Tier-3 is dark-mode only per DL §3 asymmetric-step rationale. Tests: tsc clean, vitest 185/185 unchanged, vite build clean 5.06s. **Arc-reconciliation series complete after Tier 3** (Tier 2 + Tier 3 together deliver the full three-cue material composition prescribed by §1 dark-mode anchor 4). User visually verifies dashboard cards / case-detail / widgets / overlay family in dark mode against IMG_6085.jpg. If still under-delivering: escalate to Tier 4 (larger radius, perimeter border weight tweak, other refinements) as separate session. If matches reference: spec-reconciliation complete, proceed to Phase II Batch 1c-i.

- **Tier-2 Spec Reconciliation — Card chrome in dark mode (complete):** Documentation-evolution fix between Aesthetic Arc Phase II Batch 1b and Batch 1c-i. **Problem:** user visual verification of Batch 1b noted that live dashboard cards in dark mode rendered less-distinct from the page than the canonical approved reference (a design-language-session mockup showing a Bridgeable funeral home case card for "John Michael Smith at Hopkins Funeral Home"). The reference shows clear card-vs-page separation via focused light-catch + grounding shadow + warm perimeter edge. Current implementation delivered only a subtle surface lightness lift + one-layer shadow + top-edge highlight whisper. **Diagnostic** (no code changes) identified **mirror discipline intact** (tokens.css byte-for-byte matched DESIGN_LANGUAGE.md §3) but **spec-to-reference drift** — the captured DL values were transcribed more conservatively than the approved reference visually demonstrated. Per DL §1 canonical-mood-references clause ("**when in doubt about warmth, density, or material character, the images win over prose**"), the fix is to evolve the spec + tokens to match what the reference shows. Three convergent adjustments approved as **Tier 2** of three proposed tiers: **(1) `--shadow-highlight-top` strengthened** — lightness L 0.42 → 0.48 (brighter), alpha α 0.45 → 0.65 (more opaque). Supports §1 dark-mode anchor 2 ("catches warm light pools") — reads as focused light caught on top edge rather than whisper of reflection. **(2) Dark-mode `--shadow-level-1/2/3` gain tight grounding shadow**: level-1 prepends `0 1px 3px var(--shadow-color-strong)`; level-2 prepends `0 2px 4px var(--shadow-color-strong)`; level-3 prepends `0 3px 6px var(--shadow-color-strong)`. Each level's existing soft halo + inset top highlight preserved. Dark mode now uses **three-layer composition** (tight ground + soft halo + inset highlight) at every level — necessary because low-lightness surface deltas are perceptually compressed in dark mode. Light mode composition unchanged. **(3) Card primitive gains `border border-border-subtle` perimeter** — canonical "warm hairline border that reads as metal edge" option from §1 dark-mode anchor 4 promoted from optional cue to default card treatment. Light mode renders as atmospheric-weight whisper (near-invisible on cream); dark mode renders as warm metal edge (L=0.35 @ 50% alpha composited over surface-base L=0.16 → ~L=0.26, visible 1px perimeter). Card body inherits the new three-cue material treatment: surface lift + top-edge highlight + perimeter border. **CardFooter explicit `border-t border-border-subtle` removed** — parent Card's new perimeter border + footer's existing bg-surface-base color shift carry footer separation without doubled-border effect. **DESIGN_LANGUAGE.md updates (mirror-discipline commit)**: §1 dark-mode anchor 4 "Material, not paint" rewritten to promote hairline border from one-of-three-options to canonical three-cue card treatment; §3 shadow tokens table updated `--shadow-highlight-top` dark value `0.42 / 0.45` → `0.48 / 0.65`; §3 Full CSS variable list dark-mode block updated; §6 Shadow specifications dark-mode table updated to three-layer composition at all levels; §6 Shadow specifications notes rewritten to document three-layer rationale + Tier-2 reconciliation history; §6 Border treatment gains new **"Card perimeter border (canonical)"** subsection documenting the light-mode whisper vs dark-mode metal-edge behavior + "Overlay perimeter border" subsection + "What NOT to add borders to" guardrails. §9 CSS block synchronized with §3 (existing status-muted drift in §9 lines 1694-1701 noted but not fixed — separate cleanup). **Blast radius**: every `shadow-level-1/2/3` consumer inherits the strengthened highlight + tight grounding (Card, Dialog, DropdownMenu, Popover, SlideOver, tooltips, WidgetWrapper). Every `<Card>` consumer inherits the perimeter border (~100+ sites including all dashboard cards, case-detail cards, accounting-reminder-banner hosts, etc). `WidgetWrapper` was already rendering with `border border-border-subtle + bg-surface-elevated + shadow-level-1` inline (pre-aligned with Tier 2) — post-fix, dashboard cards and widget chrome converge visually. **Tier 3 held in reserve**: surface-elevated dark-mode lightness bump (L 0.20 → 0.22) + surface-raised bump (L 0.24 → 0.27) is the next escalation if Tier 2 visual verification still under-delivers. Escalation path documented in AESTHETIC_ARC.md as separate follow-up session. **Design-reference directory created** at `docs/design-references/` (currently empty; per approval user will provide the canonical mood reference (cocktail lounge) + approved card-treatment reference (John Michael Smith case card) so future Claude instances can compare implementation to approved visuals directly). **Tests**: tsc clean, vitest 185/185 unchanged (no vitest-level tests for surface tokens), vite build clean 5.27s. **User visually verifies**: (a) dashboard cards in dark mode match reference, (b) dashboard cards in light mode remain subtly-bordered per restraint anchor, (c) overlay family (Dialog, DropdownMenu, SlideOver) renders correctly with strengthened shadow-level-2 / shadow-level-3, (d) case detail page cards, (e) widget cards (WidgetWrapper). If Tier 2 matches reference, complete; if still under-delivering, escalate to Tier 3. **Verification posture**: this is a **documentation-evolution fix**, not a bug fix in Phase II work. Phase II Batch 1a + 1b correctly implemented the spec as it was transcribed; this session corrects transcription imprecision vs. the approved visual ground truth.

- **Aesthetic Arc Phase II Batch 1b — Scheduling Board Family (complete):** 4 files, ~3,100 file LOC, ~590 LOC touched. Closes the user-reported scheduling-board bugs surfaced post-Batch-1a visual verification — unreadable right-panel wrapper in dark mode, blinding bg-white cards throughout kanban + ancillary + direct-ship panels, "Failed to load schedule" hardcoded red error state, 16 emoji/HTML-entity icons, 4 inline hand-rolled SVGs, 4 different "primary action" button color tracks (emerald/blue/violet/slate), indigo droppable-over state from DnD library convention. **Files touched:** (1) `pages/delivery/scheduling-board.tsx` — right panel wrapper `bg-slate-50/50 → bg-surface-sunken` (user-reported unreadable in dark mode because slate-50-at-50%-alpha over dark page reads as "lighter wash" inverting the intended recessed meaning; surface-sunken is mode-aware); calendar toggle + prev/next buttons migrated to border-border-base + surface-raised + brass focus rings; Weekend Planning Badge `bg-indigo-100 text-indigo-700` → `<Badge variant="info">`; new-driver warning banner → status-warning-muted/status-warning; collapsed-panel toggles for Ancillary + Direct Ship migrated to status-warning-muted/status-info-muted count pills with `<Package>` + `<Mailbox>` Lucide replacing `&#128230;` + `&#128236;` HTML entities; `<Undo2>` + `<ChevronLeft>` + `<ChevronRight>` Lucide replacing `&#8617;` / `&#8592;` / `&#8594;` / `&#9664;` arrow entities; "Back to today" pill gains brass-family info-muted rounded affordance with Lucide Undo2 leading icon. (2) `components/delivery/kanban-panel.tsx` — **InlineError primitive adoption**: the "Failed to load schedule" ad-hoc bg-white panel with hardcoded text-red-600 + manual Retry Button replaced with `<InlineError message="Couldn't load the delivery schedule." hint="Check your connection, then retry." onRetry={fetchSchedule}>` — exactly the Phase-7 pattern InlineError was designed for. OrderCard chrome `bg-white` → `bg-surface-elevated`; critical `border-red-400 bg-red-50` → `border-status-error bg-status-error-muted`; warning `border-amber-400 bg-amber-50` → `border-status-warning bg-status-warning-muted`; DnD `ring-indigo-400` → `ring-brass` during drag; **droppable-over state `border-indigo-300 bg-indigo-50/50` → `border-brass bg-brass-subtle`** per approved platform interaction language (brass = active surface signal, consistent with focus rings); 4 raw unicode emoji `⛪ 🏛 ⚰ 📍` replaced with Lucide `<Church>`, `<Landmark>`, `<Box>` (with aria-label="Graveside" — no direct Lucide casket match), `<MapPin>` — each with fallthrough rendering via DotIcon-style conditional chain; panel-header chrome migrated to surface-sunken family; inline SVG chevron → `<ChevronRight>` Lucide with rotation transform; hours-countdown Badge class map migrated from `border-red-400 text-red-700` / `border-amber-400 text-amber-700` to `border-status-error text-status-error` / `border-status-warning text-status-warning`; "Time TBD" / "ETA: TBD" amber text → text-status-warning; unassigned-count conditional → text-status-warning/text-content-muted; Unscheduled Badge destructive → error; Driver-lane over-warning destructive → warning variant. (3) `components/delivery/ancillary-panel.tsx` (largest — 1,131 LOC) — 5 card chromes `rounded-lg border border-slate-200 bg-white` → `rounded-lg border border-border-subtle bg-surface-elevated`; 3 dropdown menus (tenant Move picker, Driver Assign picker, Reassign picker) migrated from `bg-white shadow-lg` → `bg-surface-raised shadow-level-2` per approved Q4 (no DropdownMenu primitive migration this batch — raw div + position:absolute retained; deferred to natural refactor); **ALL action buttons unified on brass `<Button>` primary variant** per Q1 — emerald-600/violet-600/blue-600/slate-700 Mark-Pickup/Mark-Delivered/Assign/Confirm-as-Pickup buttons collapsed to `<Button size="sm">` default variant; secondary actions to `<Button variant="outline" size="sm">`; checkmark-labeled actions gain leading `<Check>` Lucide icon per spec item #2 (scannability in dense card UI); "Funeral home pickup" inline form `border-violet-200 bg-violet-50` → `border-status-info bg-status-info-muted`; "Assign to driver + date" info card → status-info-muted; FloatingCard wrapper `border-dashed border-slate-300 bg-slate-50/80` → `border-dashed border-border-base bg-surface-sunken/80`; Floating-Orders section border `border-amber-200` → `border-status-warning/30`; Floating count/section-header Badge+text `text-amber-600 bg-amber-50` → `variant="warning"`/text-status-warning; completed-items checkmark `&#10003;` → `<Check className="h-3 w-3 text-status-success">` Lucide; 5 HTML-entity icons migrated (`&#128230;` → Package; `&#10003;` → Check; `&#9662;` → ChevronDown; `&#128230;` empty-state → Package as `h-6 w-6 text-content-subtle` icon); mobile FAB `bg-white border-slate-300 shadow-lg` → `bg-surface-raised border-border-base shadow-level-2`; mobile drawer migrated to `bg-surface-raised shadow-level-3` with `bg-black/40` canonical scrim (Dialog convention); hand-rolled SVG close button → `<X>` Lucide; hand-rolled chevron SVG → `<ChevronDown>` Lucide with `rotate-90` transform. (4) `components/delivery/direct-ship-panel.tsx` — 4 card chromes migrated to surface-elevated; ShippedCard nested shows-recency state wrapper `bg-slate-50/50` → `bg-surface-sunken`; "Order placed with Wilbert" info card `border-blue-200 bg-blue-50` → `border-status-info bg-status-info-muted`; "Mark as shipped?" confirmation card `border-emerald-200 bg-emerald-50` → `border-status-success bg-status-success-muted`; "Mark as complete?" confirmation card `border-slate-200 bg-white` → `border-border-subtle bg-surface-elevated`; ALL action buttons (Mark-as-Ordered blue-600 / Mark-as-Shipped emerald-600 / Done slate-700) unified on brass primary `<Button>`; secondary outlines via `<Button variant="outline">`; 6 HTML-entity icons migrated (`&#10003;` x4 checkmarks → `<Check>` with leading-icon Button pattern; `&#128236;` x2 mailbox → `<Mailbox>` Lucide); `getUrgencyClass()` helper internals migrated per Q8 — retained helper structure, swapped `text-red-600` / `text-amber-600` / `text-slate-500` for `text-status-error` / `text-status-warning` / `text-content-muted` with `font-bold` preserved for critical urgency signal; "Shipped N days ago" suggestion text `text-amber-600` → text-status-warning; completed-list checkmark emoji → `<Check>` Lucide; Needs-to-be-Ordered Badge destructive → error; Ordered Badge `border-blue-300 text-blue-600` → `variant="info"`; Shipped Badge `border-emerald-300 text-emerald-600` → `variant="success"`; collapsed panel + mobile FAB + drawer migrated to surface-raised family. **Primitive adoption**: `<Card>` pattern intent (card-chrome migrations treated as inline refactors since these cards are tightly-coupled with DnD drag handles — full `<Card>` + `<CardHeader>` + `<CardContent>` subcomponent wrapping would require restructuring DnD hit targets; surface tokens applied inline preserve hit-target fidelity while getting the mode-aware surface treatment — accepted deviation from the audit's primitive plan), `<InlineError>` (1 — kanban Failed-to-load), `<Badge variant="info/warning/success/error">` (10+ — driver lane counts, section counts, plan-ahead, weekend planning, floating), `<Button>` (all action buttons in ancillary + direct-ship, ~12 sites), **dueDateColor helper migrated to status tokens**. **Lucide icon migrations (16 total)**: `⛪→Church`, `🏛→Landmark`, `⚰→Box` (aria-label graveside), `📍→MapPin`, `&#128230;→Package` (x3 sites — scheduling header collapsed pill + ancillary empty state + ancillary FAB), `&#128236;→Mailbox` (x3 sites — scheduling + direct-ship empty state + direct-ship FAB), `&#10003;→Check` (x10 sites — Mark-as-Ordered button, Mark-as-Shipped, Done, Picked Up, Delivered, plus 4 completed-list entries across panels), `&#9662;→ChevronDown` (Assign to Driver), `&#8617;→Undo2` (back to today), `&#8592;/&#8594;→ChevronLeft/ChevronRight` (nav arrows), `&#9664;→ChevronLeft` (panel expand toggle). **4 inline SVGs migrated**: kanban panel-header chevron path → `<ChevronRight>` with rotation; ancillary collapse button → `<ChevronDown>` with rotation; ancillary close-drawer X → `<X>`; direct-ship collapse toggle → `<ChevronDown>` with rotation; direct-ship close-drawer X → `<X>`. **0 hardcoded Tailwind bypass patterns remain** across all 4 files (excluding migration-history comments and the canonical `bg-black/40` backdrop scrim per DESIGN_LANGUAGE §9 which Dialog also uses). Platform "primary action = brass" invariant now consistent with Batch 1a's AgentDashboard treatment — users re-learn that color-rotation isn't action-category signal anymore (Q5 approved). **Deferred to post-arc natural refactor**: BottomSheet primitive (Q3 — only 2 usages today, not yet 3+ threshold); DropdownMenu primitive migration (Q4 — 3 raw `absolute`-positioned dropdowns in ancillary retained as raw divs with surface-raised tokens; full primitive adoption is a separate slot). Tests: tsc clean, vitest 185/185 (baseline unchanged — no vitest-level tests for these 4 files; user visually verifies post-commit in both light + dark mode), vite build clean 5.13s. **Ready for Phase II Batch 1c-i** (order-station standalone).

- **Workflow Arc Phase 8e.2.3 — Invariant Widening + DotNav Bug Fixes (complete):** Follow-up micro-phase to 8e.2.2 after user visual verification surfaced that James (production Sunnycrest admin) was still missing template defaults despite 8e.2.2's ship. **Root cause:** 8e.2.2's seed hooks + defensive login re-seed both gated on "is `preferences.spaces` empty?" — James had 2 manually-created spaces (Accounting + Operations) pre-dating every hook, so he triggered neither. His `spaces_seeded_for_roles` marker was `null`; template defaults (Production + Sales + Ownership for mfg admin) were never applied. Audit identified 2,196 active users in this "James-shape" state (738 admins). **Invariant widened:** "seeded" now means BOTH `preferences.spaces` non-empty AND `preferences.spaces_seeded_for_roles` contains current role slug(s). See `SPACES_ARCHITECTURE.md §12`. **Six-item bundle:** (1) **Migration `r47_users_template_defaults_retrofit`** — one-shot data migration; filter `is_active AND spaces_seeded_for_roles IS NULL/empty` (no filter on spaces); idempotent via `seed_for_user`; per-user try/except + batch commit every 100; end-of-migration summary with per-vertical breakdown + james_shape vs empty_shape split. Dev DB result: 491 candidates → 491 retrofitted → 0 failed on second pass (first pass surfaced malformed Phase-6 fixture data; see lenient loader below). (2) **Cap-breach guard in `_apply_templates` + `_apply_system_spaces`** — checks `len(spaces) >= MAX_SPACES_PER_USER` before each append. If breach, skip with structured WARNING (user_id + company_id + role_slug + template_name + current_spaces + max) and continue. Partial-seed INFO summary line (`added=N/M skipped_names=[...]`) at end of pass. Manual spaces + earlier-iteration templates win; later-iteration templates drop. User agency preserved (umbrella principle). (3) **Login defensive re-seed gate widened** in `auth_service.login_user` — now fires when `spaces` empty OR `spaces_seeded_for_roles` empty. James-shape users retrofitted at first login even if r47 somehow missed them (belt-and-suspenders self-heal). (4) **DotNav tooltip state-aware** — `DotNav.tsx:264` branches on `active`: "Active: X" for current state, "Switch to X" for alternates. Includes `(system)` suffix appropriately. Pattern documented in `DESIGN_LANGUAGE.md` as "describe state, not action, when state matters." (5) **DotNav active-state visual feedback strengthened** — ring `1px → 2px`, added `ring-offset-1` with `ring-offset-surface-sunken` for raised-chip effect, tightened inactive `opacity-60 → hover:opacity-100` so the active/inactive delta is readable even when user-created spaces share the colored-dot fallback rendering. (6) **Backend user-created-space icon default flipped** — `crud.create_space` + API `_CreateRequest`: `icon="layers" → icon=""`. DotNav's ICON_MAP doesn't contain `""`, so new user-created spaces fall through to the colored-dot fallback branch (matching component name semantics: "DotNav = dots for user spaces, icons for platform-owned templates"). Template spaces (with explicit `factory`, `store`, `trending-up` icons) unaffected. NOT retroactive — existing `icon="layers"` spaces preserved per approved spec ("just flip the default for new creates"). **Lenient loader helper** (`_load_spaces_lenient`) added defensively — pre-Phase-8e test fixtures + some legacy migrations wrote minimal `{id, name, accent}` shapes that fail `SpaceConfig.from_dict`. First r47 run produced 120 `KeyError: 'space_id'` failures from fixture residue. Loader skips malformed entries with WARNING; retrofit proceeds. Prod data always canonical; this is dev-DB safety net + forward-compat for future schema additions. **James-shape sanity check (proxy)**: post-r47 dev DB invariant — 0 active users have empty `spaces_seeded_for_roles` (filtering out test-fixture domains). Fresh mfg admin who previously had 2 custom spaces now receives Production + Sales + Ownership + Settings appended — 6 total dots including 2 custom, within `MAX_SPACES_PER_USER = 7`. Production James on next login will see the same 6-dot composition with visible accent + icon + landing-route distinctions between dots. **Tests shipped Phase 8e.2.3**: 10 new in `backend/tests/test_spaces_phase8e23.py` across 5 classes — TestCapBreachGuard × 2 (6-manual + 3-template user gets 1 template + 2 WARNINGs + partial-seed INFO; happy path no WARN), TestJamesShapeRetrofit × 2 (manual+templates both present + marker populated; second call idempotent no-op), TestDefensiveReseedWidenedGate × 2 (login retrofits James-shape; fully-seeded login no-op), TestIconDefaultFlip × 3 (crud default "", API schema default "", explicit icon preserved), TestWidenedInvariant × 1 (platform-wide 0 users with empty seed marker, filters test-fixture domains).
